rule: In frontend files, always place absolute (library/package) imports before relative imports and apply this consistently across all changed files.
Never consume fontSizes, colors, or other design tokens directly; always access them through the themeHelper utility to ensure theming consistency and whitelabel support.
mode_rationale: Single convention: always use themeHelper for design tokens

rule: When a store (e.g., useStore) is globally accessible, consume it directly in the component that needs it rather than threading it through router or parent props.
mode_rationale: State management convention for direct store access

rule: Disabled states and error messages shown to users should explain both why the state exists and what the user can do to resolve it, so they can self-serve without contacting support.
mode_rationale: Single UX convention for disabled state messaging

rule: Add descriptive comment sections to test scenarios (e.g., linking to test case IDs) to improve readability and make failure triage easier.
mode_rationale: Single convention about adding descriptive comments to tests

rule: API specification fields should include example values to clarify expected formats and usage, especially for non-obvious types like stringified values or comma-delimited arrays.
mode_rationale: Single convention: include example values in API spec fields

rule: Always add explicit error handling around API calls in frontend code so failures are caught and surfaced to users rather than silently ignored.
mode_rationale: Single rule: always add error handling around frontend API calls

rule: Add explicit type annotations to vals/variables that hold complex expressions (tuples, mapped collections) to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Readability convention for complex type expressions

rule: Add explicit TypeScript type annotations even when types can be inferred, especially for function parameters and variables used across boundaries.
mode_rationale: Single convention: add explicit TypeScript type annotations

rule: When adding environment variables or annotations to Kubernetes workloads, also add corresponding labels so administrators and tooling can easily filter and query workloads.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence Kubernetes labeling convention

rule: Include logging statements in methods that perform significant operations (e.g., project forking, template creation) so that failures and execution flow can be traced.
mode_rationale: Simple best practice to include logging in significant methods

rule: When introducing a new parameter, default value, or behavioral branch, add tests that explicitly demonstrate the new behavior is exercised and produces the expected result.
mode_rationale: Passive convention to always test new behavioral branches

rule: When fixing a bug in a class that lacks tests, create a test file and add tests covering the fix rather than leaving the class untested.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to add tests alongside bug fixes

rule: When a hardcoded value or workaround exists because upstream functionality is not yet available, add a TODO comment referencing what needs to change and ideally the tracking ticket.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for tracking workarounds

rule: Parameter names should be aligned with their corresponding type names for readability and consistency.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming convention

rule: Every user-facing operation that can fail must display error feedback to the user; never silently swallow errors in UI code.
mode_rationale: Single convention about never swallowing UI errors

rule: Always wrap if expression bodies in curly braces for readability; do not use shorthand single-line if statements without braces.
mode_rationale: Simple code style rule about brace usage

rule: API documentation should describe behavior abstractly rather than referencing current implementation details (such as specific upsert keys or internal identifiers) that may change as the system evolves.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for API docs

rule: In REST API models, make new fields optional to maintain backward compatibility with existing clients; plan to make them required in a future API version.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: make new API fields optional for backward compatibility

rule: API filter parameter names should match the corresponding domain model attribute names so the mapping is easy to understand; fix naming mismatches early because they become harder to change later.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for API-to-model alignment

rule: When an API schema marks fields as required, do not silently map missing values to empty strings or defaults; either ensure the values are present or make the schema reflect the actual optionality.
mode_rationale: Single API correctness convention

rule: API endpoint summaries and descriptions must accurately reflect what the endpoint does; update them when the implementation changes.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for API endpoint summaries

rule: When removing entities that are referenced by other records (e.g., resource configurations referenced by model deployments), archive them instead of deleting to preserve referential integrity.
mode_rationale: Single rule about archiving vs deleting referenced records

rule: Use human-readable, screen-reader-friendly names for aria-label values (e.g., 'chart metric selector') rather than dash-separated ID-like strings (e.g., 'chart-metric-selector').
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: use human-readable aria-label values

rule: API tests should explicitly assert HTTP status codes rather than only checking response bodies, to catch unexpected error responses early.
When exposing configuration or internal data through API endpoints, review whether sensitive values (secrets, credentials) could leak outside their intended security boundary.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence security review reminder

rule: Every new API endpoint must include an authorization check, especially when the code will be deployed in cloud environments.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: every endpoint needs an auth check

rule: Methods in Authorizer classes should return a proper AuthorizationResult type rather than a boolean or other ad-hoc type to maintain consistent authorization patterns.
mode_rationale: Simple type convention for authorization method return types

rule: When designing APIs that support multiple providers or backends, accept generalized parameter names from the user and map them internally to provider-specific fields, reducing user-facing complexity.
Access store-provided values directly from useStore rather than extracting them through intermediate custom hooks to keep the data source clear.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention about data access patterns

rule: In styled-components, access theme colors directly in the component definition via the theme object rather than passing color values as props from the parent.
mode_rationale: Style convention for accessing theme directly in styled-components

rule: When introducing a default page size or limit for a list endpoint, research actual production data volumes (e.g., number of environments, EDVs, snapshots) to ensure the default serves the majority of users without requiring extra pagination.
mode_rationale: Design guideline to research production data when setting defaults

rule: When wrapping a component, only declare prop types you actually forward; use Pick to select forwarded props, or use the rest operator to pass through remaining props.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence typing convention for component prop forwarding

rule: Error messages should accurately reflect the actual error condition; do not use misleading messages even if the HTTP status code is correct.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about error message accuracy

rule: Name methods to reflect their actual behavior — use 'update' when the entity must already exist, not 'upsert'.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention about matching behavior

rule: Name telemetry and audit events to precisely describe the action they capture; rename ambiguous event names that could be confused with related but different operations.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for telemetry events

rule: Log messages and status labels must use terminology that accurately reflects the operation being performed (e.g., 'Stopped' not 'Delete' when stopping a run).
mode_rationale: Simple rule about accurate log message wording

rule: useEffect dependency arrays must only include values actually used inside the effect; do not add extra dependencies that are not referenced.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence React convention about dependency array correctness

rule: Variable and map names must precisely describe their key-value semantics; rename when the actual key type differs from what the name implies (e.g., containerName vs imageName).
mode_rationale: Naming convention: names must match actual key-value semantics

rule: Name authorized actions without the 'can' prefix since capability is implicit in the authorized actions endpoint (e.g., 'project.override_dataset_limits' not 'project.can_override_dataset_limits').
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for authorized actions

rule: When configuration values fail to resolve, make downstream error messages actionable so developers can trace failures back to the missing configuration.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about error message quality

rule: Service adapter classes that wrap external APIs or infrastructure should be declared as singletons to avoid unnecessary instantiation and ensure consistent state.
mode_rationale: Simple instantiation convention for service adapters

rule: API controllers should include a catch-all exception handler that returns a 500 status code to prevent unhandled exceptions from leaking implementation details.
mode_rationale: Single error handling convention for controllers

rule: When adding new files that must be present in Docker images (e.g., .env files, config), add a corresponding container-structure-test entry to verify the file exists in the built image.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to add structure tests for new Docker artifacts

rule: When a method has non-obvious behavior that could be mistaken for a bug, add a warning comment so future debuggers can quickly identify the root cause.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for non-obvious behavior

rule: Add exception handling around operations that interact with external systems or can fail at runtime; do not leave them unguarded.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention for external calls

rule: Message consumers and event processors must include exception handling to prevent unhandled errors from crashing the consumer loop or losing messages silently.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention for message consumers

rule: When code behaves differently for specific cases (e.g., skipping redaction for one entity type but not others), add an inline comment explaining why to preserve context for future readers.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for special-case logic

rule: When adding a new metric or event type, ensure it is also added to all related tracking or reporting locations in the codebase.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence consistency rule for metrics/events

rule: When adding a new feature path (e.g., customer template creation), add corresponding observability counters to track usage.
mode_rationale: Single rule to add observability counters with new feature paths

rule: When testing authorization changes, include negative test cases that verify users cannot perform actions outside their allowed scope (e.g., transferring to an org they don't belong to).
mode_rationale: Single convention: include negative auth test cases

rule: When adding a new use case to an existing API, prefer adding a new code path rather than modifying existing behavior, to reduce the risk of breaking current consumers.
mode_rationale: Convention to prefer additive changes over modifications

rule: When removing a behavior or skipping a code path (e.g., disabling a connection check), add a regression test that verifies the old behavior does not resurface.
mode_rationale: Testing convention when removing code paths

rule: When a service method performs a side-effecting operation (e.g., updating a K8s custom resource), add a comment warning that subsequent code additions should include a rollback for that operation.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention for side-effecting code

rule: Actor system instances should register shutdown hooks to allow clean and graceful shutdowns, even if not needed during normal operation.
mode_rationale: Single infrastructure convention for actor systems

rule: All CRUD service methods should include telemetry events for observability; plan telemetry from the start even if it ships in a follow-up PR.
mode_rationale: Single observability convention

rule: When adding a new actions or service class, add a corresponding test class following the existing naming convention (e.g., ProjectGoalActionsSpec for ProjectGoalActions).
mode_rationale: Simple convention to add tests alongside new classes

rule: When adding new data retrieval functions or integration points, include unit and/or integration tests covering the new code paths.
mode_rationale: Passive convention to include tests for new data retrieval code

rule: New service methods and functionality must be accompanied by corresponding unit tests in the same PR, not deferred to a follow-up.
mode_rationale: Expectation that tests ship with new code

rule: When adding a new filtering or query parameter to an existing endpoint, add integration or unit tests that verify the filtering behavior.
When adding new service actions or permission checks, add corresponding test cases in the relevant spec file.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence testing convention

rule: When modifying repository or data access layer code, add or update corresponding integration tests to cover the new behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple expectation to add tests with data layer changes

rule: When adding a new public method or changing an interface (e.g., a new handlesEvent function), add corresponding unit tests — especially when fixing a bug that the test would catch in regression.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to add tests alongside new public methods

rule: When forced to use `any` due to pending backend API changes, add a TODO comment referencing when it should be fixed.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation rule for tracking temporary type workarounds

rule: When a design system token cannot be used due to component context limitations, leave the hardcoded value with a TODO comment explaining why and what needs to be refactored.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for when tokens can't be used

rule: Add trace-level log statements at service method entry points to aid debugging without polluting info-level logs.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to add trace logging at entry points

rule: When code branches on a feature flag, add a TODO comment with consistent text across all affected locations so the code can be easily found and removed when the flag is retired.
mode_rationale: Single convention about feature flag cleanup tracking

rule: When business logic is complex, invest in stronger TypeScript types to catch errors at compile time rather than relying on runtime behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to invest in stronger types for complex logic

rule: When a common operation (like executing an aggregation pipeline and converting from BSON) is repeated across persistence methods, extract it into a typed helper method to eliminate repetitive conversion boilerplate.
mode_rationale: Single DRY guideline for repeated conversions

rule: When a field must be unique (e.g., vanity URLs, slugs), add a unique database index rather than relying solely on application-level checks to prevent race conditions.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture rule: enforce uniqueness with DB indexes, not app-level checks

rule: When code has fallback behavior (e.g., returning 'Unknown' when a lookup fails), add explicit unit tests covering those edge cases.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention for fallback paths

rule: When adding new fields to a data model or event, add at least one unit test that verifies the new fields are correctly extracted and populated, not just left as None.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to cover new data model fields

rule: Utility functions and hooks with non-trivial logic should have unit tests, even if added in a follow-up ticket.
mode_rationale: Simple rule that utility functions need tests

rule: When implementing paginated API responses, use the codebase's standardized pagination traits (e.g., PaginatedResponse) rather than creating ad-hoc pagination wrappers.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use the codebase's standardized pagination traits

rule: Critical operational failures (like failed rollbacks) should trigger active admin notifications via supportAlerter.error(), not rely solely on log output which will likely go unnoticed.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about critical failure notification

rule: When defining new API endpoints, align field names and path segments with terminology already used in related internal systems (e.g., Vault config, other services).
mode_rationale: Naming convention to align with existing internal systems

rule: Keep terminology in code (function names, variable names, test steps) aligned with the current product naming conventions, updating references when product terms change.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention to match product terms

rule: When adding or modifying methods on an interface, use the same parameter types and ordering as existing methods on that interface to maintain consistency and reduce cognitive load.
mode_rationale: Consistency convention for parameter types and ordering on interfaces

rule: When adding Prometheus metrics or structured log fields, use the same field names as existing events/telemetry for the same concept so engineers can correlate data across systems without confusion.
mode_rationale: Single convention: reuse existing field names for cross-system correlation

rule: When wrapping or mirroring a well-known library's functionality, use the same parameter names that library uses so callers can transfer existing knowledge.
mode_rationale: Naming convention to match upstream library terminology

rule: Test files should have the same code ownership (CODEOWNERS) as the source files they test, so the owning team is notified of changes.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention for CODEOWNERS alignment

rule: Ensure the terms used in the UI, API, and backend code are consistent; if concepts diverge (e.g., 'owner' vs 'publisher'), resolve the naming before shipping.
mode_rationale: Simple naming consistency convention

rule: Before renaming shared configuration files or resources, get explicit alignment from the domain owners to ensure the new name is semantically correct and agreed upon.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about getting alignment before renaming

rule: When wrapping SDK types, adopt the naming conventions of the target SDK version (e.g., use 'Response' instead of 'Result' when migrating to AWS SDK v2).
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for SDK wrapper types

rule: Every interactive form control (radio buttons, selects, checkboxes) should be managed through the form library (e.g., Ant Design Form) rather than with standalone local state, to ensure consistent validation, submission, and reset behavior.
mode_rationale: Convention to use form library for all controls

rule: Keep dependencies in package.json alphabetically sorted to maintain consistency and ease scanning.
mode_rationale: Ordering convention for package.json

rule: Every data table must include an empty state component that explains what the table shows, why it is empty, and what the user can do — either inline or tracked via a TODO for a follow-up PR.
mode_rationale: Single rule: every data table must include an empty state component

rule: Always include optional components or plugins in the configuration payload and control their behavior via an enabled flag, rather than conditionally omitting them entirely.
mode_rationale: Single configuration convention: include with enabled flag

rule: When using `themeHelper` to resolve color tokens, always pass the theme object; omitting it causes the color to silently fall back to `inherit`, producing incorrect styling.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: always pass theme object to themeHelper calls

rule: When a component conditionally renders based on an API response, always handle and surface errors to the user (e.g., via a toast notification) — otherwise a failed request results in a blank screen with no explanation.
mode_rationale: Single rule: always surface errors to the user

rule: When catching exceptions to return error responses, always set an explicit error code — don't leave it unset even for generic Exception types.
mode_rationale: Single rule: always set error codes

rule: When tracking previous values or derived state, always update the tracked value unconditionally rather than gating the update behind a condition, to prevent stale state and logic bugs.
mode_rationale: Single rule: update tracked values unconditionally to prevent stale state

rule: Analytics event properties should contain aggregatable, non-sensitive values (e.g., OS name) rather than raw URLs or host-specific data that hinders aggregation and risks leaking sensitive information.
mode_rationale: Convention about analytics event property values

rule: When adding or modifying mutable state, explicitly analyze whether concurrent access paths exist and document the reasoning, even if you conclude synchronization is not needed.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to analyze and document concurrency reasoning

rule: If code must be left commented out, add a TODO comment explaining why it is retained and under what conditions it should be restored or removed.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for commented-out code

rule: Use Ant Design Select's `options` array prop instead of manually rendering `<Option>` children for a simpler, more declarative approach.
mode_rationale: Simple preference for declarative options prop over manual children

rule: Boolean fields in API response schemas should be marked as required with a default value in the transformer, so omitted fields are not silently treated as false.
mode_rationale: Single rule about boolean field schema design

rule: Name API client classes to reflect the actual API version they communicate with, not an internal or legacy version, to avoid confusion.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for API client classes

rule: API parameter descriptions must accurately describe the filtering or sorting behavior of the implementation; a mismatch between 'or greater' vs 'or less' in a version filter description can cause users to pass incorrect values.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence accuracy rule for API parameter descriptions

rule: API documentation and payloads should be semantically accurate, future-proof, and include human-readable fields. Avoid implementation-specific details in docs, ensure required fields are never silently empty, and use consistent PATCH semantics.
trigger: When writing or modifying API schemas, documentation, PATCH endpoints, or audit event payloads
rationale: Implementation-specific docs break when internals change. Silently empty required fields confuse API consumers. Missing human-readable names force users to do extra lookups. Reviewers consistently flag these across nucleus, apps, and extensions.
mode_rationale: General convention about API payload/doc quality, not a multi-step procedure

rule: Name API endpoints for their specific purpose rather than using broad names that could be confused with other functionality (e.g., 'cost activation status' instead of 'cost status').
mode_rationale: Naming convention for API endpoint specificity

rule: Actions exposed through an 'authorized actions' endpoint should actually check authorization; if an action is configuration-based rather than auth-based, expose it through a separate configuration API.
mode_rationale: Single API design principle about semantic correctness

rule: Every public API endpoint must include summary, tags, and other OpenAPI annotations so it appears correctly in generated API documentation.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation requirement for endpoints

rule: API enum string values should not contain spaces; use machine-friendly identifiers (e.g., BuildFailed) and let the UI map them to human-readable display strings.
mode_rationale: Single convention: API enum values should be machine-friendly identifiers

rule: All API error responses (including BadRequest) must return a JSON body, not plain text, to ensure clients can parse error details consistently.
mode_rationale: Single rule about error response format

rule: Place API response fields at the abstraction level where the underlying data is stored; if data belongs to a version-level entity, expose it on the version response rather than the parent entity.
mode_rationale: Single design principle about response field placement

rule: API filter parameters should default to returning all results (no filter) when no filter value is specified, rather than requiring explicit opt-in to see everything.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention for filter defaults

rule: Pure API call functions should throw errors and let the caller handle error messaging; API layers should have no concern about user-facing error messages.
mode_rationale: Single separation-of-concerns rule for API layers

rule: Ensure API response metadata (counts, totals, pagination) accurately reflects the filtered or visible data, not the underlying unfiltered dataset, to avoid confusing clients.
mode_rationale: Single API correctness convention

rule: Ensure API route parameter names accurately describe what they represent to avoid confusion from copy-paste errors.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for API route parameters

rule: API endpoint paths should describe the resource being returned, not imply a boolean check when the endpoint actually returns data (e.g., use `/bundle/permissions/view` instead of `/bundle/can-view`).
mode_rationale: Naming convention for API endpoint paths

rule: API route descriptions should elaborate on the type of artifact or entity the endpoint expects, not just the action it performs.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for API route descriptions

rule: Use RESTful resource-oriented routes for CRUD operations and /rpc/ prefixed routes for RPC-style actions; choose the style that matches the API's semantics.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention: REST for CRUD, /rpc/ for actions

rule: OpenAPI schema fields should include description, nullable annotation, and regex validation patterns for ID fields to ensure clear contracts and input validation.
mode_rationale: Single convention about OpenAPI schema field annotations

rule: API service functions should accept raw key-value objects rather than pre-built query strings, matching the pattern used by domino-api for easier future migration.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention about parameter formats

rule: When adding a trait or mixin that changes behavior, apply it at the individual class level rather than a shared parent trait, to avoid unintended side effects on sibling classes.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture convention about trait/mixin granularity

rule: When a service makes repeated lookups for the same data, apply a caching pattern to avoid redundant calls and improve performance.
mode_rationale: Single guideline: cache repeated lookups

rule: When adding defensive handling (e.g., empty-string checks) for one parameter, review and apply the same treatment to other parameters of similar type and risk.
mode_rationale: Convention to apply defensive checks uniformly

rule: When gating a feature behind a feature flag, ensure all API endpoints and code paths that expose or depend on that feature are also filtered, not just the primary path.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to gate all paths, not just the primary one

rule: When fixing or updating logic in one code path, search for and apply the same change to all analogous code paths in the codebase.
mode_rationale: Convention: propagate fixes to all analogous code paths

rule: When renaming or reformatting identifiers, apply the same convention uniformly across all related files to avoid inconsistency.
mode_rationale: Single consistency convention for naming

rule: When submitting user-generated content (e.g., markdown with mentions), apply all required formatters (like formatMentions) before sending data to the backend, consistent with existing usage patterns.
mode_rationale: Single data formatting convention

rule: When fixing a security vulnerability, identify and patch all other services that handle the same input vector to ensure consistent protection.
mode_rationale: Multi-step procedure: identify the vulnerability, find all services with same input vector, patch each one

rule: When converting numeric types (e.g., `.toDouble`, `.toLong`) in pagination or comparison logic, apply the same conversion consistently across all code paths to avoid subtle bugs.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about consistent numeric type conversions

rule: Return 404 (not 400) when a referenced resource does not exist, even on POST endpoints, because it more precisely describes the problem than a generic bad request.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to return 404 instead of 400 for missing resources

rule: Use the appropriate log level for the situation: expected recovery operations (like rollback after an exception) should log at info, not warn; actual failures should log at error level.
mode_rationale: Logging convention for choosing correct log levels

rule: Always add aria-label attributes to icon-only buttons to maintain accessibility score.
mode_rationale: Single accessibility rule for icon-only buttons

rule: Set aria-label values to accurately describe the component's current function, not the broader workflow it belongs to (e.g., 'select collaborators' for a selection input, not 'invite collaborators').
mode_rationale: Simple accessibility naming convention

rule: Add the appropriate ARIA role attribute (e.g., role="button") to non-semantic HTML elements like divs that act as interactive controls.
mode_rationale: Accessibility convention for interactive non-semantic elements

rule: Array.prototype.sort() mutates the original array in place; when sorting data that may be referenced elsewhere, sort a copy (e.g., [...items].sort()) to avoid silent bugs.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about sorting copies to avoid mutation

rule: Repository names should match the artifactId (not the groupId) so that developers can trace dependency coordinates back to their source repository without ambiguity.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for repository-to-artifact traceability

rule: In E2E tests, verify the absence of an element by first confirming the page has fully loaded and then asserting the element is not present, rather than using a wait-to-not-see timeout which is slow and unreliable.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about how to assert element absence

rule: In tests, assert against directly known values (e.g., a specific entity's field) rather than values derived through the same code path being tested, which can mask regressions if those paths diverge.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention for assertion targets

rule: In tests, assert the expected size of a collection before accessing individual elements to catch unexpected additions or omissions.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence testing best practice

rule: When a test checks for an error condition, use explicit assertion mechanisms (e.g., pytest.raises) so the test actually fails when the error is absent, rather than silently passing.
mode_rationale: Testing convention for explicit error assertions

rule: Integration tests for API endpoints should assert on the actual response body content, not just HTTP status codes or response metadata, to catch subtle data-handling bugs.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to assert on response body content in integration tests

rule: When a function must return one of a known set of valid values, test assertions should verify the result is a member of that set, not just that it is non-null or non-zero.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about set membership assertions

rule: E2E tests should assert the semantically correct HTTP status code (e.g., 403 for forbidden access) rather than accepting any 4xx error.
mode_rationale: Testing rule to assert specific HTTP status codes

rule: When verifying signed payloads, assert that critical fields in the signed payload match the corresponding fields in the request to prevent tampering or mismatch.
mode_rationale: Single security assertion rule

rule: When replacing system dependencies or tools (e.g., switching from wget to curl), verify that the new dependency is available in all supported environments to avoid backward incompatible changes.
mode_rationale: Single check: verify dependency availability across environments

rule: When allowing users to define or reuse tokens across resources, evaluate whether sharing a token could grant unintended cross-resource access to collaborators on only one of those resources.
mode_rationale: Security consideration to evaluate during token design, not a multi-step procedure

rule: When changing a field's type (e.g., from required to optional), explicitly assess the impact on downstream event consumers and integration tests before merging.
mode_rationale: Single review checkpoint before merging type changes

rule: When changing a shared component's styles or behavior, verify that existing usages are not adversely affected.
mode_rationale: Single guideline to verify impact on existing consumers

rule: When changing data schemas, permission models, or stored values, explicitly assess whether existing data or permissions will be affected and document the migration path.
mode_rationale: Multi-step procedure: identify affected data, assess impact, document migration path

rule: Before modifying a shared/common component, assess whether the change will cause behavior changes in all consumers, not just the immediate use case.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to assess consumer impact before modifying shared components

rule: Before adding values to shared enums or type definitions, verify the change does not alter behavior in other screens or features that consume them.
mode_rationale: Single check before modifying shared types

rule: Use a loading wrapper component to resolve all async data before rendering, rather than allowing components to render with partially loaded state that may cause race conditions.
mode_rationale: Single pattern preference for resolving async data before rendering

rule: When a UI wizard creates a parent entity with its children (e.g., a deployment target with resource configurations), use a single creation endpoint rather than multiple sequential calls to avoid partial success states.
mode_rationale: Single design principle about atomic creation endpoints

rule: When a reviewer identifies a misuse of a utility function, systematically review all call sites for the same mistake rather than fixing only the commented instance.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence thoroughness rule about fixing all call sites

rule: After moving code out of a module, audit BUILD files to identify and remove dependencies that are no longer needed, reducing unnecessary coupling.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence reminder to clean up BUILD files after moves

rule: When adding configuration values to a service, verify whether existing config-fetching code could pull and override those values from a remote source like Nucleus, and ensure the override precedence is correct.
mode_rationale: Reminder to check config override precedence when adding values

rule: When using withScalarChange for audit events, respect the parameter order (fieldName, before, after, unit) and use None for the 'after' value on deletions.
mode_rationale: API usage convention for parameter order

rule: Always include a human-readable name alongside entity IDs in audit trail events so that audit logs are meaningful without requiring additional lookups.
When adding audit trail v2 events, use normalized fields (target, affecting, location) instead of flat metadata keys for entities with IDs and names.
mode_rationale: Single convention about audit event field structure

rule: Only audit events that represent user-initiated actions; system-driven status changes (e.g., job status transitions not triggered by a user) should be excluded from audit trails.
mode_rationale: Scoping rule for what qualifies as an auditable event

rule: Use sequential changes for entity-related fields (anything with an ID tied to a collection) and scalar changes only for primitive values in audit trail events.
mode_rationale: Single convention for audit trail field types

rule: Place authorization checks before database queries or other expensive operations to avoid unnecessary work when the user lacks permissions.
mode_rationale: Single performance/security convention about authorization ordering

rule: Authentication redirect logic must be scoped to the specific routes that require it; do not place auth guards in always-mounted wrapper components where they can inadvertently redirect anonymous-accessible routes to login.
mode_rationale: Simple architectural convention about auth guard placement

rule: Add explicit tests that verify authorization checks are enforced, ensuring unauthorized users cannot access protected resources.
mode_rationale: Single testing requirement for authorization checks

rule: Include at minimum a basic authorization check (e.g., rejecting anonymous users) even in stubbed or incomplete implementations to prevent security gaps from shipping.
mode_rationale: Single security convention about stub implementations

rule: When manually converting values one by one (e.g., enum members to constants), create a utility function to automate the conversion instead of doing it individually.
mode_rationale: Simple convention preferring utility functions over manual repetition

rule: TypeScript state declarations (e.g., useState) should be explicitly typed rather than using `any`, even in legacy code — prefer adding proper types when touching the code.
mode_rationale: TypeScript typing convention

rule: Instead of casting to `any`, use TypeScript utility types like `ReturnType<typeof fn>` to derive proper types from existing functions and maintain type safety.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence TypeScript typing convention

rule: Limit the use of `as any` type casts in TypeScript; prefer proper typing or generics to maintain type safety.
mode_rationale: Simple type safety rule against as-any casts

rule: Do not use the generic `object` type; define or generate a specific type from the API schema (e.g., Swagger) so consumers get type safety.
mode_rationale: Type safety convention to avoid generic object types

rule: Only use @ts-ignore when there is a genuine type system limitation — investigate the root cause of the type error first and fix it properly when possible.
mode_rationale: Convention to investigate type errors before suppressing

rule: Do not call `.get` on Option values; use `.getOrElse`, `.fold`, pattern matching, or throw a descriptive exception to handle the None case explicitly.
mode_rationale: Scala Option handling convention

rule: Avoid nested ternary expressions; use early returns, if/else blocks, or helper functions for multi-branch conditionals to preserve readability.

Use variables or configuration references for environment-specific values (image tags, versions, URLs) in test files instead of hardcoding them, to prevent silent test failures when those values change.
mode_rationale: Testing convention against hardcoded env values

rule: Avoid asserting exact values for environment-dependent quantities in tests; instead assert relative conditions (e.g., greater than a baseline) or check units to avoid brittle tests that break across different infrastructure configurations.
mode_rationale: Testing convention about assertion style

rule: When aggregating data across entities, consolidate into a single query instead of issuing per-entity queries; create a follow-up ticket if immediate optimization is not feasible.
mode_rationale: Single rule to consolidate per-entity queries into batch queries

rule: Use styled-components or CSS classes instead of inline style attributes to maintain consistency and leverage the project's theming system.
mode_rationale: Single styling convention

rule: Do not nest class selectors inside styled-components unless styling third-party components (e.g., Ant Design); prefer gap, direct component styling, or wrapping the target in its own styled component.
mode_rationale: Simple CSS convention except for third-party component styling

rule: Do not spread-clone an entire props object just to inject a single additional property; pass the original props through and add the extra prop individually.
mode_rationale: Coding style rule: don't spread-clone props just to add one property

rule: When the same data is needed at multiple layers (e.g., controller and service), fetch it once and pass it as a parameter rather than making redundant database calls, both for performance and to avoid inconsistency if the data changes between calls.
mode_rationale: Single principle: fetch once and pass rather than re-fetching

rule: Avoid making additional database queries solely for auditing purposes; capture audit data from context already available at the call site rather than fetching it after the fact.
mode_rationale: Performance guideline to use existing context for audit data

rule: Be cautious when adding feature-specific traits or changes to shared infrastructure files (e.g., telemetry events); ensure the additions are generic enough to be reused by other consumers of that module.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about keeping shared code generic

rule: Don't repeat parent context in nested configuration key names; if a setting is already scoped under 'workflows.storage', don't prefix its children with 'flows' or 'storage' again.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention against redundant prefixes

rule: Service layer interfaces should not return REST API DTOs — this couples all service consumers (including internal ones) to the API definition and forces changes in the API layer to propagate to unrelated consumers.
mode_rationale: Layer separation convention about service return types

rule: Avoid using `let` with subsequent value overwriting; instead, extract the logic into a function that returns the final value to promote immutability and clarity.
mode_rationale: Simple immutability convention against let reassignment

rule: Do not add explicit imports for classes that are already covered by a wildcard import in the same file.
mode_rationale: Simple import hygiene rule

rule: Avoid using prototype method calls directly on objects; prefer static methods like Object.keys(obj).forEach() instead.
mode_rationale: Simple style rule preferring static methods over prototype calls

rule: Do not hardcode behavior or settings for a specific compute provider type; design abstractions that are provider-agnostic to avoid adding new settings or branches for every new provider.
mode_rationale: Architecture principle about provider-agnostic design

rule: Avoid using singleton/global state patterns unless there is a clear need and thread-safety implications have been considered for the test runner or runtime environment.
mode_rationale: Convention to avoid global state without thread-safety analysis

rule: Avoid hardcoding dimension constants (e.g., pixel widths) in child components that the parent knows nothing about; instead derive state from a boolean or enum at the parent level to prevent fragile implicit coupling.
mode_rationale: Single architecture principle about prop design

rule: Prefer explicit boolean props or render props over using optional function props to conditionally show or hide UI elements, as optional function props obscure intent and reduce readability.
mode_rationale: React API design convention about explicit boolean props

rule: Do not explicitly set style properties that already match the theme defaults; remove redundant declarations like fontSizes.medium or fontWeights.normal when they duplicate the default values.
mode_rationale: Single rule to not re-declare default theme values

rule: API schemas should define a type in exactly one place; having the same concept defined at multiple levels (e.g., top-level and inside a validationSchema) creates ambiguity.
mode_rationale: Simple API schema convention about single-source type definitions

rule: Use the simplest accurate name (e.g. datasetIds) and only add qualifiers like toInclude/toExclude when a complementary parameter is actually introduced.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming simplicity rule

rule: Never retrieve all records from a datastore on every request; apply filters, pagination, or scoping at the query level to avoid fetching too much data.
mode_rationale: Performance convention to always scope queries

rule: E2E tests should not assert on excessive specific UI copy; reduce text-matching assertions to only what is essential for validating behavior, so tests are resilient to copy changes.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about minimizing text assertions in E2E tests

rule: When a legacy template system (e.g., Scala HTML templates) is being phased out, avoid adding new content to it; instead, place new content in the replacement system (e.g., React components).
mode_rationale: Single migration-awareness guideline

rule: Do not duplicate the same data across multiple React state variables; if a single state object (e.g., editedLauncher) already tracks the full form state, derive values from it rather than maintaining separate parallel state.
mode_rationale: Convention against duplicating state

rule: When naming API fields, avoid reusing names that already have established meaning in other parts of the product to prevent confusion.
mode_rationale: Naming convention to avoid reusing established terms

rule: Do not maintain separate boolean state variables (e.g., loading and hasLoaded) when one can be derived from the other — consolidate into a single source of truth.
mode_rationale: State management convention to avoid derived state

rule: Do not store an InputStream as a class attribute because it can only be read once; instead hold a factory lambda that produces a fresh stream on each call, or use a stream-plus-metadata pattern like AWS ResponseInputStream.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence design rule about InputStream handling

rule: Choose clearly distinct names for types and interfaces; if a name looks like a typo of another type in the same file, rename it to reflect its actual purpose to avoid confusion.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for type distinctness

rule: When the same TypeScript type is needed across packages, define it once in a shared package and import it rather than duplicating the definition. Prefer interface with extends over type aliases for extensibility.
mode_rationale: Single rule: define shared types once in a shared package

rule: Do not force-fit a complex abstraction (like a generic data-fetching hook) when the resulting workarounds outweigh the benefits; prefer simpler, direct implementations when the abstraction adds more complexity than value.
mode_rationale: General design principle about abstraction tradeoffs

rule: Do not use the TypeScript non-null assertion operator (!) unless you can guarantee the value is defined; use optional chaining or explicit null checks instead.
mode_rationale: Type safety rule about the non-null assertion operator

rule: Do not encode implicit context (e.g., principal, tenant) into method names when all methods in the class already operate under that context.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention

rule: Do not use the __mocks__ directory convention for manual component mocks; prefer co-located jest.mock() calls with explicit factory functions.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence testing convention for mock placement

rule: Do not use the `.styled` suffix in file names for styled component files.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention: do not use .styled suffix in filenames

rule: Use full, descriptive words in type and file names instead of abbreviations — names should be self-explanatory without requiring team knowledge.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention

rule: Do not embed version prefixes like /v1 in API base URLs when they refer to a third-party API version rather than your own, as users may confuse it with the Domino API version.
mode_rationale: Simple URL design guideline

rule: Do not use hardcoded pixel heights to vertically center content — use flexbox or grid instead, as fixed heights break on smaller viewports and cause unnecessary scrolling.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use flexbox/grid instead of hardcoded heights

rule: Do not add new entries to monolithic configuration classes like Settings; find or create a more scoped location for new configuration.
mode_rationale: Architecture rule against monolithic config classes

rule: Variable names should clearly indicate their type; avoid names that sound like booleans (e.g., prefixed with 'is' or 'has') when they hold collections or other non-boolean values.
mode_rationale: Naming convention about type-name alignment

rule: Avoid using --break-system-packages for pip installs; use virtual environments instead to isolate Python dependencies from the system Python.
mode_rationale: Single rule against a specific pip flag

rule: Avoid using numeric indexes to select UI elements in E2E tests; instead, use stable selectors (data-testid, ARIA roles, or meaningful text) and file UI tickets to improve element testability when needed.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about stable selectors

rule: Do not call onChange callbacks inside useEffect hooks, as unstable references will cause the effect to fire repeatedly; instead, lift state changes to event handlers or use stable callback refs.
mode_rationale: Single React anti-pattern to avoid

rule: Avoid chaining multiple useEffects to trigger data refreshes; instead extract the fetch function and call it imperatively from the event handler that requires the refresh.
mode_rationale: Single React convention about avoiding chained useEffect for data fetching

rule: Do not introduce circular dependencies between build targets; co-locate test specs with their corresponding implementation to keep the dependency graph clean.
mode_rationale: Single convention about dependency graph hygiene

rule: When assigning ports for internal sidecar or proxy services, check that the port number does not collide with common defaults used by user-facing frameworks and languages (e.g., 8180, 8888) to prevent conflicts in user workloads.
mode_rationale: Single convention: avoid port collisions with common framework defaults

rule: When boolean parameters interact such that one silently overrides another, extract separate methods with clear contracts instead of adding flags that create unintuitive behavior.
mode_rationale: Convention to avoid flags that silently override each other

rule: Do not place conflicting version constraints for the same dependency in a single requirements file; use a single combined constraint (e.g., `>=1.0,<2.3`) or separate requirements files per context.
Session-scoped pytest fixtures must have unique names across the test suite; duplicate names with different implementations cause unpredictable behavior across modules.
mode_rationale: Single naming uniqueness rule for pytest fixtures

rule: Define local DTOs with only the fields you need rather than depending on DTOs owned by another module, to avoid coupling to upstream changes.
mode_rationale: Simple decoupling convention for DTOs across modules

rule: Do not add default values to data model fields when other fields in the model have none, as defaults can mask places where the field should be explicitly set.
mode_rationale: Single data modeling convention

rule: Before encoding a URL or path, check whether downstream utilities already apply encoding to avoid double-encoding bugs.
mode_rationale: Simple defensive check before encoding

rule: Before adding an analytics tracking call, verify the event is not already being tracked by another handler or component to avoid duplicate event firing.
mode_rationale: Simple check-before-adding convention

rule: Do not import the same module twice, and prefer named imports over wildcard ("* as") imports in React/TypeScript files.
mode_rationale: Import style convention

rule: Do not check the same condition twice in contiguous lines — consolidate the logic into a single conditional block.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: consolidate duplicate conditionals

rule: Instead of rendering the same component twice with different props based on a condition, use a single instance with a conditional prop value.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use conditional props instead of duplicate components

rule: Before adding a new configuration flag, search for existing flags with similar intent; if one exists, extend or properly deprecate it with release notes and a removal timeline rather than creating a near-duplicate.
mode_rationale: Multi-step procedure: search for existing flags, then decide to extend or deprecate with release notes and removal timeline

rule: Before adding a new dependency injection binding, check whether the same binding already exists in another injection module to prevent duplicate registrations.
When adding items to a navigation sidebar or tabbed view, verify the same page is not already reachable from another menu point to prevent confusing duplicate entries.
mode_rationale: Simple UI architecture check

rule: Do not copy-paste utility functions across modules; either fix dependency issues to reuse the existing utility or write a minimal inline version if only a fraction of the logic is needed.
mode_rationale: Single convention: reuse existing utilities instead of copy-pasting

rule: Avoid listing implementation details (method names, endpoints, etc.) in design documents when those details are likely to change, as it creates an additional maintenance burden.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention to avoid listing volatile details

rule: Do not use an empty collection to mean 'no filter' or 'all values'; instead define an explicit constant (e.g., ALL_TYPES) so the intent is clear at every call site.
mode_rationale: Single API design rule about explicit intent over implicit conventions

rule: Remove or reduce log statements that produce excessive output; verbose logging in hot paths degrades signal-to-noise ratio and can impact performance.
mode_rationale: Logging convention about signal-to-noise ratio

rule: Do not use type casts to work around loose or incorrect type definitions; instead, fix the underlying types so TypeScript can provide actual safety — excessive casting is equivalent to using `any`.
mode_rationale: Single type safety convention

rule: Do not use explicit `return` statements in Scala; instead, restructure control flow (e.g., invert the if condition) so the last expression is the return value.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence Scala idiom convention

rule: Do not hardcode default values (e.g., severity levels) when the actual value should come from an API response; let the API be the source of truth.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about API as source of truth for default values

rule: Route paths like '/admin' should be defined as constants or derived from configuration, not hardcoded inline in components.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention: route paths as constants

rule: Use generic terms like 'A user' instead of 'A Domino user' in user-facing copy to support white-labeling.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence white-labeling convention

rule: Avoid hardcoding validation rules for third-party models and endpoints that change frequently; prefer open-ended validation or keep the list externally configurable.
mode_rationale: API design convention: keep third-party validation configurable

rule: When using string-based identifiers with embedded metadata (e.g., 'dataset-<name>-<id>'), ensure the naming scheme cannot collide with real user-created names.
mode_rationale: Single convention about collision-safe identifier schemes

rule: API documentation should not reference current implementation details (like specific upsert keys) that may change as new types or features are added.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention for API docs

rule: Prefer explicit constructor parameters over Scala implicits because implicit constructors create hidden nullary constructors that cause surprising instantiation errors and obscure the dependency graph.
Avoid CSS !important; increase specificity through selector nesting or repetition instead.
mode_rationale: Single convention: increase specificity instead of using !important

rule: Do not migrate code to an intermediate pattern that is itself scheduled for replacement; instead, use the simplest direct approach (e.g., pass values as arguments) to avoid compounding tech debt.
mode_rationale: Single principle about avoiding intermediate migration steps

rule: Do not use deprecated or legacy product terminology (e.g., 'detwirl') in new code, variable names, or route definitions — use current domain language instead.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention

rule: In end-to-end tests, avoid placing long-running API steps before browser interactions, as the browser session may time out and cause unrelated failures.
mode_rationale: E2E testing convention about browser timeout avoidance

rule: Do not introduce base classes or traits that add little value or that each subclass must immediately override — they obscure intent and introduce bugs.
mode_rationale: Design convention against unnecessary base classes

rule: Do not implement manual undo/rollback logic for non-transactional multi-step operations — concurrent requests can cause the rollback to undo another request's work. Instead, make operations idempotent and easy to retry.
mode_rationale: Single convention: prefer idempotent retry over manual rollback

rule: Do not memoize table column definitions as it can break column resizing behavior; verify resize still works if memoization is added.
mode_rationale: Performance gotcha for table columns

rule: In meta-schemas (schemas that define schemas), avoid field names like 'description' or 'type' that conflict with the actual data fields they constrain, as this creates ambiguity.
mode_rationale: Naming convention to avoid ambiguity in meta-schemas

rule: Use schema-validated fields instead of the metadata catch-all in AuditableEvents; reserve metadata only for event-level details that cannot be captured any other way.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about structured vs catch-all fields

rule: Do not render a separate modal instance for every table row; use a single shared modal at the table level and pass the selected row's data to it.

Instead of changing the behavior of a widely-used method (e.g., adding case-insensitivity), create a new method variant and use it only where needed, with a comment explaining why the variant is necessary.
mode_rationale: Design guideline to create variants instead of modifying shared methods

rule: Prefer idiomatic Scala patterns like returning tuples or multiple values from expressions instead of using mutable variables.
mode_rationale: Idiomatic Scala style convention

rule: Avoid using negative margins in CSS; prefer restructuring layout with proper spacing, padding, or flexbox/grid alignment instead.
mode_rationale: Single CSS convention about layout approach

rule: Do not match on null in Scala code; use Option types and idiomatic Scala constructs instead of null checks carried over from Java interop.
mode_rationale: Language idiom rule: use Option instead of null checks

rule: When matching elements between two collections, convert one to a Set for O(1) lookups instead of nested iteration that produces O(n²) runtime.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention about collection lookups

rule: Do not wrap a field in Option when an empty string is a valid value; use a plain String type instead to avoid ambiguity between None and Some("").
mode_rationale: Simple type design convention

rule: When a boolean field should only be true or false, make it required with a default of false rather than optional, to avoid an unnecessary None/Some(true)/Some(false) three-state representation.
mode_rationale: Convention to use required boolean with default over optional

rule: Do not override Ant Design (or other library) CSS classes directly from custom components; instead use styled-components wrapper with specificity selectors (e.g., &&&) and pass configuration through component props like btnType and size.
mode_rationale: Single styling convention for third-party components

rule: Before writing system-generated configuration files, consider whether users may have made their own modifications that should not be silently overwritten.
mode_rationale: Single principle: check for user modifications before writing config

rule: Do not conditionally render entire pages based on auth state in a way that causes a visible flash or blink when the auth check passes; prefer showing a loading state or keeping the page mounted.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: show loading state instead of flashing content during auth checks

rule: Instead of passing null for required parameters, use a proper placeholder or sentinel value (e.g., an 'uncorrelated' context) to maintain type safety and avoid null pointer issues.
mode_rationale: Type safety convention for required parameters

rule: Avoid the "previous value + useEffect" pattern for tracking state changes in React; instead follow the React team's recommended approach of computing derived values during rendering or using event handlers.
mode_rationale: Single anti-pattern to avoid in React

rule: Child components should not bubble errors up to parent containers via prop drilling; use context providers or localized error boundaries instead so failures are scoped to the affected widget.
mode_rationale: Single convention about error boundary scoping

rule: Prefer hand-crafted test cases with explicit corner cases over random data generators, which are a major source of test flakiness and do not reliably cover edge cases like zero, negative numbers, or empty inputs.
mode_rationale: Single convention: prefer explicit test cases over random generators

rule: Define static configuration objects (such as table column definitions) outside the render path or memoize them to avoid unnecessary recomputation on every re-render.
mode_rationale: Performance convention: hoist or memoize static config

rule: Before adding a color, spacing, or other design token, check whether the default theme/palette already defines it — avoid duplicating values that are already provided.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: check for existing theme tokens before adding new ones

rule: Summarized or aggregated API data should be fetched once and cached in the provider, not re-fetched on every render or component mount.
mode_rationale: Performance convention: fetch once and cache in provider

rule: In aggregation endpoints that span multiple projects, authorize the project list once upfront and then query child resources in bulk, rather than re-authorizing each project individually.
mode_rationale: Single performance guideline about batching authorization

rule: Do not combine useEffect-triggered fetches with explicit refetch calls, as this causes duplicate simultaneous requests for the same data.
mode_rationale: Single convention: don't combine useEffect fetches with explicit refetch calls

rule: Do not create multiple DTOs or case classes that represent the same domain concept; consolidate into a single model and reuse it.
mode_rationale: Single code organization rule about DTO consolidation

rule: Do not add logging to intermediate steps (e.g., per-chunk upload) when the concluding step already logs the complete action; log at the boundary that represents the completed operation.
Extract the result of a method call into a variable when it is used multiple times; use pattern matching on the extracted value instead of calling the method again.
mode_rationale: Convention: extract repeated method calls into a variable

rule: Remove redundant words from field names when the context already conveys the meaning (e.g., use `pvcName` instead of `persistentVolumeClaimName` when the type is already a PVC).
mode_rationale: Naming convention to remove redundant words from field names

rule: Do not add explicit null/undefined checks when the falsy value already produces the correct result (e.g., `undefined !== 'blank'` is already false).
mode_rationale: Code simplification convention

rule: Do not use the nullish coalescing or optional chaining operator on values that are already typed as optional, as it adds unnecessary noise.
mode_rationale: Single convention: don't use nullish operators on already-optional types

rule: Do not set both `data-test` and `testId` props on the same component; use only the one that your test framework requires to keep test selectors consistent and avoid confusion.
mode_rationale: Single rule to not duplicate test selectors

rule: Do not add explicit cleanup steps in tests when the test framework or setup already handles that cleanup automatically.
trigger: When adding teardown or cleanup logic (e.g., deleting records, closing connections) in a test method or afterEach block
rationale: Without checking what the framework already handles, redundant cleanup adds noise, slows test execution, and can interfere with framework-managed lifecycle (e.g., double-closing a connection).
mode_rationale: Simple convention to not duplicate framework-provided cleanup
steps: Step 1: Check if the test fixture, base class, or framework (e.g., @Transactional, withDatabase) already handles cleanup
Step 2: Verify by reading the fixture's afterEach/afterAll or teardown method
Step 3: Only add explicit cleanup for resources the framework does NOT manage
Step 4: If you must add cleanup, add it in the fixture/base class so all tests benefit

rule: Review test scenarios for steps that duplicate assertions or setup already performed by prior steps, and remove them to keep scenarios concise.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to remove duplicate test assertions

rule: When a test library target (e.g., tests-lib) already bundles common dependencies like the core library and test frameworks, do not re-declare those dependencies in consuming BUILD targets.
Do not cast a value to a type it has already been cast to; check if an earlier cast already covers the needed type assertion.
Pass context methods directly to child components when the context already memoizes them, instead of wrapping in an additional useCallback.
mode_rationale: Single convention: pass already-memoized context methods directly

rule: Do not copy data from a custom hook (e.g., useRemote) into local state via useEffect; use the hook's returned data directly to avoid unnecessary re-renders and state synchronization bugs.
mode_rationale: Single convention about not copying hook data into local state

rule: When caller context (e.g., a Principal) is already available, pass it through the call chain rather than performing a redundant lookup downstream.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention about passing context through

rule: Acknowledge that regex-based parsing of shell-like syntax will have edge cases (e.g., escaped quotes); document known limitations and prefer a proper parser when complexity grows.
mode_rationale: Simple architectural guideline about parser choice

rule: Import from specific module paths within a package instead of the root index to prevent circular dependency cycles.
mode_rationale: Single convention: import from specific module paths to prevent circular deps

rule: Before naming API schemas or models, check for existing names in related services to avoid collisions (e.g., use 'ModelEndpoint' instead of 'Endpoint' when 'NewEndpointV1' already exists in AI Gateway).
mode_rationale: Single check-before-naming convention

rule: Do not use setTimeout to defer state changes in order to bypass form validation timing issues; find a proper solution such as conditional validation or explicit state flags instead.
mode_rationale: Single anti-pattern prohibition

rule: Use descriptive variable names instead of single-character abbreviations, even in short callbacks or lambdas, to maintain readability.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention about descriptive variable names

rule: Do not store computed values in the database that can be derived from other fields; compute them on read instead.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence data modeling guideline

rule: Never add test library dependencies to production build targets; restructure imports to use the production library instead.
mode_rationale: Single rule about build target dependency hygiene

rule: Do not write tests for components where all functionality is mocked, as the tests validate the mocks rather than real behavior.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence testing principle about mock validity

rule: Do not call toString on strongly-typed identifiers; use the appropriate accessor or conversion method to preserve type safety.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use proper accessors on typed IDs

rule: Do not add DI framework dependencies (e.g., Guice) directly in library classes; initialize bindings in injection modules to prevent transitive dependency leakage into downstream services.
mode_rationale: Single architecture rule about DI framework isolation

rule: Avoid accessing tuple elements via positional indices (_1, _2); use case classes or named fields instead for readability.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention preferring named fields over positional access

rule: Do not default query limits or page sizes to Int.MaxValue or similar unbounded values; use a reasonable finite default to prevent future performance issues.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use finite defaults for query limits

rule: Do not create a container/wrapper component when there is no significant business logic or backend calls to justify the separation from the presentational component.
mode_rationale: Architecture guideline against unnecessary wrapper components

rule: Return the most specific type directly from service methods instead of adding unnecessary conversion methods when the service already produces the correct type.
mode_rationale: Single guideline to return specific types directly

rule: Avoid default parameter values (e.g., `= None`) unless genuinely needed, as they can mask bugs by allowing callers to omit required arguments silently.
mode_rationale: Simple parameter design convention

rule: Do not import utility libraries like Ramda for operations that native JavaScript handles equally well (map, filter, etc.); prefer built-in array methods to reduce bundle size and cognitive overhead.
mode_rationale: Single convention: prefer native JS methods over utility libraries like Ramda

rule: Do not wrap JSX in a React Fragment when only a single child element is returned; fragments should only be used to group multiple siblings without adding a DOM node.
mode_rationale: Simple JSX cleanup convention

rule: Do not use `useMemo` for simple calculations like counts or string concatenations; reserve memoization for expensive computations or objects that would trigger unnecessary re-renders.
mode_rationale: Single rule about when memoization is appropriate

rule: Do not wrap a field in Option when the unwrapped type already has a valid empty/default value (e.g., empty string for String); prefer the simpler type unless None carries distinct semantic meaning.
mode_rationale: Simple type design convention about Option usage

rule: Do not use useEffect to synchronize state that can be handled by passing a callback prop directly; prefer prop-based handlers over effect-driven state synchronization.
mode_rationale: Single convention preferring prop callbacks over effect-driven state sync

rule: Do not wrap expressions in template string backticks when the value is already a string; template strings should only be used in styled-component interpolations or when concatenation is needed.
mode_rationale: Simple syntax convention about unnecessary template literals

rule: When a function's return type is explicitly declared, avoid redundant type casts on the returned value — the type annotation already provides type safety.
mode_rationale: TypeScript convention to avoid redundant casts

rule: Do not create type definitions or intermediate variables for values that are hardcoded inline — pass them directly as object literals.
mode_rationale: Style convention to avoid redundant type definitions

rule: Do not add extra wrapper div elements in React components unless they serve a structural or styling purpose; unnecessary nesting hurts readability and can affect performance.

Instead of using useEffect to synchronize state with changing props (which can cause extra renders and missing-dependency warnings), use the React pattern of tracking the previous prop value and updating state during render.
Never use the `any` type in TypeScript; use specific types or type aliases to maintain type safety.
mode_rationale: Single type safety rule

rule: When calling an async function inside a try-catch block, you must `await` the call; otherwise the catch block will not intercept errors from the async operation.
mode_rationale: Single coding convention about async/await in error handling

rule: When checking or retrieving data for multiple items, use a single batch query (e.g., `findByIds`) and filter the results in memory, rather than making a separate database call for each item.
mode_rationale: Simple performance guideline to prefer batch queries over per-item queries

rule: When consuming a stream of events that each triggers a database write, consider batching writes (e.g., update after N events or within a time window) to reduce write churn.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention for write batching

rule: When setting multiple fields on the same form or state object, combine them into a single call rather than making multiple sequential calls.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention for state updates

rule: Every BDD/Gherkin scenario must include explicit assertion steps using 'Then' keywords that verify expected outcomes, not just action steps using 'And' or 'When'.
mode_rationale: Single BDD convention about Then steps

rule: In React/TypeScript files, leave a blank line between type/interface declarations and component definitions, and between successive styled component definitions, for visual separation.
mode_rationale: Formatting convention for visual separation in React files

rule: Boolean-returning methods should use names that clearly convey both the condition being checked and its specificity (e.g., `areMultipleStoragesRegistered` instead of ambiguous alternatives).
mode_rationale: Naming convention for boolean methods

rule: Name boolean variables and props with verb prefixes like `should`, `is`, `has`, or `can` to clearly communicate their purpose (e.g., `shouldDisableHwPicker` instead of a generic name).
mode_rationale: Naming convention for boolean variables and props

rule: When handling process termination or state transitions, branch on the specific signal that caused the transition (e.g., stop properties being set) rather than the current broad state, to avoid catching unrelated exit reasons.
mode_rationale: Coding convention about precise conditional branching

rule: When fixing a bug, add a unit test that would have caught the bug to prevent regressions.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention for bug fixes

rule: When upstream data changes rarely (e.g., once every few months), avoid performing heavy IO on every endpoint call; use caching or a check-before-fetch strategy to reduce database load.
mode_rationale: Performance guideline about caching strategy

rule: When processing a stream of events that each trigger a database or service lookup, introduce a low-complexity cache to avoid redundant lookups that scale linearly with event volume.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about caching redundant lookups in event streams

rule: Call React hooks at the top level of a component and assign results to variables before using them in arrays, objects, or other data structures — never invoke hooks inline within data structure definitions.
mode_rationale: Single rule about hook call placement

rule: In user-facing text, capitalize Domino-specific entity names (Workspace, Model API, Artifacts, Datasets) but use lowercase for generic terms (published apps, docs).
mode_rationale: Simple capitalization rule for UI text

rule: Capitalize domain-specific terms (e.g., Dataset, ID) and proper abbreviations in client-facing error messages and API route descriptions for consistency and professionalism.
mode_rationale: Simple naming/capitalization convention

rule: Capitalize product feature names (e.g., 'Exports', 'Feature Store') when referring to the named feature, but use lowercase for generic actions (e.g., 'export files from a project').
mode_rationale: Style rule distinguishing feature names from generic actions

rule: Use case-insensitive comparison (e.g., `.toLowerCase()`) when matching user-facing strings like labels, names, or identifiers that may vary in casing.
mode_rationale: Simple comparison convention for user-facing strings

rule: Async operations that can throw (API calls, mutations) must be wrapped in try/catch with user-facing error feedback so failures are not silently swallowed.
mode_rationale: Single convention: wrap async ops in try/catch with user feedback

rule: In Scala, catch NonFatal(ex) instead of RuntimeException to avoid swallowing fatal errors like OutOfMemoryError while still handling all recoverable failures.
mode_rationale: Single coding convention for Scala exception handling

rule: Color values should be defined in the centralized colors/theme list, not hardcoded or hidden in individual component files.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about defining colors in centralized theme files

rule: When the same transformation or sanitization logic appears in multiple places, move it onto the relevant domain object so consumers get the behavior for free.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence DRY principle for domain object transformations

rule: Centralize repeated encoding/transformation logic (such as double URL encoding) into a single shared utility function so it can be updated in one place when the underlying framework changes.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to centralize repeated encoding logic

rule: When a magic value (e.g., version string) is used across multiple files, extract it into a single shared constant rather than duplicating literals.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to extract shared constants
