rule: Do not hardcode shared values (URLs, support links, config strings) in feature components; place them in the appropriate shared utility module (e.g., supportUtil.ts) so they can be maintained in one place.
mode_rationale: Code organization rule: shared values go in utility modules

rule: Define URL patterns in dedicated route objects (e.g., FrontendRoutes) instead of hardcoding URL strings inline, so routes are maintained in one place.
mode_rationale: Simple architectural rule about route definition location

rule: Use full terms or define abbreviations on first use in test descriptions and feature files; do not assume readers know domain-specific acronyms.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention about acronym clarity

rule: When using numeric values in styled components (e.g., z-index 99, pixel sizes), ensure the value is intentional and documented or use a named constant to clarify its purpose.
mode_rationale: Style convention to name or document numeric values

rule: Do not add dependencies that are no longer actively maintained; prefer actively maintained alternatives to reduce risk from unpatched bugs and vulnerabilities.
Do not use state flags that unmount and remount components just to trigger a data refresh; show a loading indicator within the existing component instead.
mode_rationale: Performance convention: don't unmount/remount to refresh data

rule: Do not use `position: absolute` for main screen content that can be laid out with normal document flow — absolute positioning should be reserved for overlays, tooltips, and floating elements.
mode_rationale: Single convention: prefer normal flow over absolute positioning for main content

rule: Do not route simple operations through complex machinery like state machines when a direct implementation would suffice; choose the simplest approach that meets actual requirements.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture guideline about choosing simplest approach

rule: When a function's only async operation is the return value, return the promise directly instead of marking the function async and awaiting it.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention for returning promises directly

rule: Only use dangerouslySetInnerHTML when the content is actually HTML; if it is plain text, render it as a normal React child to avoid XSS risk.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence security convention about XSS risk

rule: Do not render empty or meaningless DOM elements (e.g., empty spans) when the underlying data is absent; conditionally render only when there is content to display.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about conditional rendering

rule: Only create a separate DTO when there is a real structural difference from the existing model (e.g., a Create DTO that omits the server-generated ID); do not duplicate types that are identical or nearly identical.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to avoid redundant type definitions

rule: Do not add eslint-disable comments when the violation can be resolved by restructuring code; for example, omit unused function parameters instead of suppressing the lint warning.
mode_rationale: Single lint hygiene rule

rule: Do not mark a value as lazy if it is referenced on the very next line; the deferred initialization adds complexity with no benefit.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence Scala performance convention

rule: Avoid unnecessary intermediate nesting levels in data model field names; prefer flatter, clearer naming when the context is already established by the parent structure.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for flat data models

rule: Don't manually pick individual properties from an object to pass as props when the extra properties would simply be ignored by the receiving component; pass the object directly to reduce boilerplate.
mode_rationale: Code style rule against redundant prop picking

rule: If a boolean or derived value is only used by one child component, compute it inside that child rather than passing it down as a prop from the parent.
mode_rationale: Single rule about where to compute derived values

rule: Do not clone or spread an entire props object just to inject one additional value; pass the original props through and add the extra prop individually.
mode_rationale: Simple convention against cloning props to inject one value

rule: Do not wrap child components in render functions when the parent is not injecting anything through the render prop; use the component directly as a child instead.
mode_rationale: Simple React convention

rule: Do not use module-level singletons or global-like variables unless there is a clear, justified need; prefer passing dependencies explicitly.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architectural guideline against global state

rule: Do not create special-case handling for entities that can be treated uniformly with other entities of the same type.
mode_rationale: Simple design principle about uniform treatment

rule: Keep test code inline and straightforward; do not extract test logic into separate helper functions unless it is reused across multiple test cases.
mode_rationale: Testing style rule: keep tests inline unless reused

rule: Don't introduce traits or interfaces solely for API DTOs; traits are justified when decoupling dependencies on full domain or DB objects, not when types are only used at the API layer.
mode_rationale: Convention about when traits/interfaces are justified

rule: Do not add ts-ignore or ts-expect-error directives when the type error can be resolved by providing the correct arguments or types.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to fix types instead of suppressing errors

rule: Do not create an intermediate function or variable when an inline arrow function or direct reference achieves the same result with less indirection.
Eliminate unnecessary Some() wrappers that force downstream .get calls on Options; restructure code so the Option is handled cleanly without calling .get, which can throw at runtime.
mode_rationale: Single convention: handle Options cleanly without .get

rule: Do not use type assertions (e.g., `as SomeType`) to bypass TypeScript's null/undefined checks — verify the value exists at runtime or use proper type narrowing.
mode_rationale: Single type safety convention

rule: When a configuration value controls a limit (e.g., max file size), do not default to 0 if undefined — a zero default can silently block all operations unless explicitly handled as a special case.
mode_rationale: Single convention about safe default values

rule: Do not add @ts-ignore without a justifying comment; audit existing @ts-ignore directives and resolve them via dedicated tech-debt tasks.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about requiring justification for ts-ignore

rule: Avoid installing packages from upstream repositories during every e2e test run; prefer pre-built Docker images or cached environments to prevent flakiness from upstream outages or breaking changes.
mode_rationale: Single principle about test environment isolation

rule: Do not add all component props as dependencies to useCallback, as this defeats the purpose of memoization — the function will be regenerated on every re-render just as if useCallback were not used.
mode_rationale: Single convention: don't list all props as useCallback dependencies

rule: Do not create catch-all 'Utils' classes; split distinct responsibilities into their own well-named classes or files.
mode_rationale: Convention to split responsibilities instead of catch-all Utils

rule: Do not reuse a variable name that shadows an outer-scope variable — use a distinct name to avoid confusion and subtle bugs.
mode_rationale: Single naming/scoping convention

rule: Instead of wrapping components in the withTheme HOC to access theme colors, use IconResolver (which accepts palette color keys directly) or component color props to eliminate the need for the HOC.
mode_rationale: Single convention to avoid withTheme HOC in favor of direct props

rule: When calling an async callback (like onSelect), await its resolution before triggering dependent operations (like refetch) to avoid race conditions and redundant calls.
mode_rationale: Single convention: await async callbacks before dependent operations

rule: When a function like hasAccessToResource can return a Promise, always await it before using its result in a boolean expression; an unresolved Promise is truthy and will bypass the check.
mode_rationale: Single rule about awaiting async results before boolean checks

rule: In Scala tests, explicitly await all Futures (via Await.result or equivalent) to ensure the test framework waits for completion and assertions are evaluated.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to explicitly await Futures

rule: Never use truthiness checks (!! or Boolean()) directly on a Promise object; always await or resolve the promise first, since !!Promise is always true.
mode_rationale: Simple correctness rule about promise truthiness

rule: Place data detection and validation logic on the backend where the source of truth exists, rather than reimplementing complex matching logic on the frontend.
mode_rationale: Architecture guideline placing validation logic on the backend

rule: When backporting a bug fix to a release branch, ensure a corresponding fix is also opened against the main development branch.
mode_rationale: Simple branching convention, not a multi-step procedure

rule: Do not rename analytics or telemetry event names that are already in production, as downstream consumers (e.g., Mixpanel dashboards) may depend on the existing names.
mode_rationale: Single convention about not renaming production event names

rule: When sending notifications or making API calls for multiple targets, combine them into a single request with a list of targets rather than looping and firing individual requests.
mode_rationale: Single performance principle: batch requests instead of looping

rule: When the frontend needs state for multiple items, provide a batch endpoint instead of requiring multiple individual API calls.
mode_rationale: Simple API design guideline preferring batch endpoints

rule: When sending a large number of items to an external service, split into configurable batches to avoid overwhelming the client or exceeding request size limits.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention about splitting large payloads

rule: When a client needs both a list of entities and a related aggregate (e.g., total count), return them together in a single API response rather than requiring a separate call to the service and database.
mode_rationale: Single API design principle about response bundling

rule: When mounting Kubernetes secrets with restrictive file modes (e.g., 0400), verify that the pod's fsGroup security context does not broaden permissions; if it does, copy the file to a writable path and chmod it.
mode_rationale: Security convention for Kubernetes secret file permissions

rule: Service implementations in Guice injection modules should be bound as eager singletons using .asEagerSingleton() to ensure only one instance is created.
mode_rationale: Simple coding convention for Guice binding style

rule: Name binder or helper objects/functions after the parameter they actually bind, not after unrelated concepts.
mode_rationale: Naming convention: name binders after the parameter they bind

rule: Add a blank line between logically distinct code blocks (e.g., before an if statement following variable assignments) to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Simple code formatting convention

rule: Store binary data (images, bundles, files) in blob storage rather than directly in MongoDB, using the GlobalBlobStore interface which supports all deployment types.
mode_rationale: Single architectural rule for binary data storage

rule: Use a boolean type for binary on/off states instead of a string enum or flavor prop.
mode_rationale: Type convention for binary on/off states

rule: When passing a boolean prop with value true in JSX, use the shorthand form (e.g., `<Route exact>` instead of `<Route exact={true}>`).
Boolean properties should use names that clearly indicate a true/false value (e.g., isPlaceholder); a bare noun like 'placeholder' implies a string value.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for boolean properties

rule: When adding boolean props to shared UI components, make them optional with a default value of false so callers only need to pass them when opting in.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention for boolean props

rule: Recursive functions that make external API calls must enforce a maximum depth or iteration limit to prevent unbounded API usage, rate limit errors, and UI hangs.
mode_rationale: Single rule to enforce depth/iteration limits on recursive API calls

rule: When reading files from untrusted sources (user code, external input), enforce a size limit to prevent oversized payloads from affecting downstream systems like message queues.
mode_rationale: Single defensive-coding rule about enforcing size limits

rule: Add test cases for implicit type coercion boundaries (e.g., integer-to-double conversion) in serialization and deserialization logic.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention for edge cases

rule: When a line of code (especially string formatting or assertions) is too long to read at a glance, break it into multiple lines rather than leaving it as one long horizontal scroll.
mode_rationale: Simple formatting convention for line length

rule: When permissions vary based on conditions, construct a single permission collection by conditionally appending entries rather than duplicating the authorization check with different argument lists.
mode_rationale: Convention to construct permissions by appending rather than duplicating

rule: When implementing HTTP response caching, cache based on the presence of cache-control headers rather than hardcoding status code checks, to avoid confusing conditional logic that implies impossible states.
mode_rationale: Single implementation guideline for HTTP caching

rule: Cache project authorization contexts to reduce redundant computation, especially when the same auth checks are needed across multiple features.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention to cache auth contexts

rule: Extract repeated lookups on mutable collections into a single val to eliminate time-of-check-to-time-of-use inconsistencies under concurrent access.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence performance/safety rule about caching lookups

rule: Reserve PascalCase names for React components; use initial camelCase for non-component exports like utility functions, hooks, and constants.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for exports

rule: Use camelCase for all JavaScript/TypeScript variable and prop names in React components.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for JS/TS variables and props

rule: When using setTimeout as a fallback, store the timeout ID and cancel it with clearTimeout if the primary operation completes first, to prevent stale side effects.
mode_rationale: Single cleanup convention for setTimeout fallbacks

rule: Capitalize domain entity names consistently in user-facing strings and enum values (e.g., 'Bundle' not 'bundle').
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for domain entities in UI strings

rule: Constants that are meant to be used across modules should follow capitalized naming conventions to signal they are constants.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for shared constants

rule: Error messages, validation messages, and other user-facing strings should start with a capitalized word.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence copy style convention

rule: When a feature flag controls infrastructure behavior (e.g., creating an ingress), capture the flag value into the resource record at creation time and use that persisted value for all subsequent operations, rather than re-reading the current flag value.
mode_rationale: Single principle: persist flag value at creation time

rule: If fields in a Scala class require @unused annotations because they are private, convert to a case class to make them public getters and eliminate the suppression.
mode_rationale: Single Scala convention about case classes and annotations

rule: Use enumeratum's withNameInsensitiveOption for parsing enum values from external input instead of manually converting to uppercase, to handle mixed-case input safely.
mode_rationale: Enum parsing convention

rule: When adding try/catch blocks, ensure the catch path provides visible feedback to the user (e.g., a toast or error message) rather than silently swallowing the error.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: catch blocks must show user-visible feedback

rule: When an async hook makes a request to determine a value (e.g., a navigation link), catch errors and return a sensible fallback rather than letting the error propagate unhandled.
mode_rationale: Single convention: catch errors in async hooks and return sensible fallbacks

rule: Catch specific exception types rather than matching on error message strings, to avoid brittle error handling that breaks when messages change.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention to catch specific types

rule: Third-party SDK initialization and app-level setup utilities should be consolidated into a dedicated bootstrap component rather than scattered across the app entry point.
mode_rationale: Architectural convention for SDK initialization placement

rule: Incorporate feature flags and access control checks into centralized auth contexts rather than scattering them across individual service methods, to ensure consistent enforcement across all access paths.
mode_rationale: Single architectural guideline about where to place auth checks

rule: When multiple action classes need to extract the same values from a JSON context (e.g., projectId), define a shared case class with Json macros in a companion object rather than duplicating extraction logic across each Actions class.
mode_rationale: Single DRY rule for shared JSON extraction

rule: Validation logic that applies to all code paths (e.g., billing tag validation for all project creation scenarios) should be centralized rather than duplicated across different branches.
mode_rationale: Single principle about where to place validation logic

rule: Use the `useDate` hook for all date operations instead of calling date libraries (dayjs, moment) directly, so the underlying library can be swapped in one place.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to use a specific hook for date operations

rule: Define entity types in a single shared location (e.g., a shared library) rather than scattering definitions across individual services.
mode_rationale: Single organizational convention about type placement

rule: Group custom exception classes in a shared location (e.g., an exceptions file in the domain layer) rather than defining them inline in individual services, to promote reuse and discoverability.
mode_rationale: Single organizational convention about exception placement

rule: Extract hardcoded values (ports, resource limits, service names) into central configuration to make them easily tunable and prevent them from becoming the root of operational issues.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about extracting hardcoded values into config

rule: When the same fallback or default-resolution logic (e.g., falling back to local data plane when an ID maps to None) is duplicated across multiple call sites, centralize it in a dedicated service method.
mode_rationale: Convention against duplicated fallback logic

rule: Shared route definitions should live in a centralized package location (e.g., packages/ui/src/core/routes) rather than within individual app modules.
mode_rationale: Single convention about where routes should live

rule: Place reusable test helpers in the shared tests-lib module rather than duplicating them in individual service test directories.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization convention for test helpers

rule: Place state mutation logic (e.g., status transitions with success/error messages) inside the relevant context provider or RPC layer rather than standalone utility functions, and parameterize by target state.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architecture convention for state management

rule: When one API response depends on another, chain the requests so that all related data updates atomically rather than rendering stale combinations.
mode_rationale: Single convention for dependent API calls

rule: When throwing or wrapping exceptions, always include the original cause to preserve the full error chain for debugging.
mode_rationale: Single convention: always include original cause when wrapping exceptions

rule: Chart and data visualization colors should come from the design system palette/tokens rather than hardcoded values, to ensure dark theme compatibility.
mode_rationale: Simple style rule: use design system palette for charts

rule: When renaming or modifying a shared configuration file, search for all services that include or reference it and update them in the same PR.
mode_rationale: Single verification rule for config changes

rule: Before adding or re-adding code, check whether concurrent or recently merged PRs have intentionally removed or modified the same code to avoid reverting others' changes.
mode_rationale: Single practice: check concurrent PRs before adding/re-adding code

rule: When adding new configuration values, verify whether existing code pulls and overrides these values from a remote source (e.g., nucleus), and ensure the override precedence is correct.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to verify config override precedence when adding values

rule: Before adding a dependency, verify it is not deprecated and follow the recommended migration path if it is.
mode_rationale: Single rule: verify a dependency is not deprecated before adding it

rule: When modifying or removing metric fields, verify that existing monitoring dashboards (NewRelic, Grafana, etc.) that query those fields are not broken by the change.
mode_rationale: Single verification rule when modifying metrics

rule: Always check error return values from fallible operations; do not silently ignore error variables.
mode_rationale: Simple error handling convention

rule: Verify that a directory or resource does not already exist before attempting to create it to avoid errors or unintended overwrites.
mode_rationale: Simple defensive coding guideline

rule: When fixing a bug caused by a specific code pattern, scan the same file and nearby files for identical patterns that may have the same issue.
mode_rationale: Simple practice to scan for duplicate bug patterns

rule: Before building a new UI element, check whether a global/shared component already exists for that pattern (e.g., run status indicators) and reuse it.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to check for existing components before creating new ones

rule: When introducing new log markers or event names, choose names that will not collide with planned features to avoid confusion when both coexist in production logs.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming convention for log markers

rule: CI shell scripts should use `set -exuo pipefail` for debuggability and fail-fast behavior, and consolidate related commands into a single step to reduce complexity.
mode_rationale: Simple CI scripting convention

rule: A class name must accurately reflect its actual behavior; if a class named 'Logger' also enforces authorization or throws exceptions, rename it to reflect its broader responsibility (e.g., 'Checker' or 'Enforcer').
mode_rationale: Single naming convention about accuracy

rule: When removing a feature or module, trace and remove all references including config bindings, injection modules, and test fixtures — don't leave hollowed-out shells.
mode_rationale: Multi-step procedure: trace references through config bindings, injection modules, and test fixtures

rule: Review and remove stale TODO comments that are no longer valid before merging; leftover TODOs create confusion about whether the work is actually incomplete.
Tests should clean up resources they create (e.g., archive projects, delete temporary data) to avoid polluting the test environment for subsequent runs.
mode_rationale: Testing convention: clean up created resources after tests

rule: CSS for React components should be co-located with the component (CSS modules, styled-components, or CSS-in-JS), not placed in global stylesheets, unless it genuinely applies globally.
mode_rationale: Convention about CSS co-location with components

rule: Define table column configurations in or near the table component file rather than in a parent view, to keep rendering logic co-located with the component that uses it.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline about code colocation

rule: When moving a class to a different module, move its corresponding test spec to the same module to keep tests co-located with the code they exercise.
mode_rationale: Convention to keep tests co-located with their code

rule: Place utility functions in the module that owns the domain concept they operate on, not in a shared generic utils file, especially when they are specific to one feature (e.g., URL parsing for file browser).
mode_rationale: Single code organization convention

rule: Keep related abstractions (interfaces, wrappers, models) in the same package/directory even when build-system module boundaries differ from language package boundaries.
Place new configuration settings alongside existing related settings and follow the established pattern rather than creating new configuration locations.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about config placement

rule: Keep UI state as close as possible to the component that uses it to avoid unnecessary prop drilling and reduce coupling between components.
mode_rationale: Single principle: keep state close to its consumer

rule: Add a comment explaining why a value is intentionally discarded (e.g., `val _ = ...` in Scala) so future developers do not accidentally remove the side-effecting call.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation rule for discarded values

rule: When a method's return value is not immediately obvious from the signature, add a comment above it explaining what is being returned.
When a variable is an alias for another concept (e.g., selectedRevision aliasing activeRevision), add a clarifying comment to prevent confusion for future readers.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention for aliases

rule: Comments on configuration fields and methods must describe the complete behavior, not just part of it; if a timeout triggers both deduplication and forced termination, both must be documented.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention about comment completeness

rule: Components should inject their own data into callbacks rather than receiving pre-curried functions from parent levels; keep data binding close to the source.
mode_rationale: Single architecture guideline about data binding in callbacks

rule: A styled component's name must describe what it actually renders; do not name a component 'Span' if it renders an icon or other non-span element.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming convention

rule: When injecting helpers or dependencies via dynamic module loading, evaluate whether race conditions can occur between modules loading and the helpers becoming available.
Use a consistent, short parameter name (e.g., `q`) for free-text search across all API endpoints; internally map it to a descriptive name like `searchQuery`.
mode_rationale: API naming convention: use consistent short name for free-text search

rule: When multiple async operations exist in the same component, handle their side effects (e.g., page reload, refetch, notifications) consistently unless there is an explicit reason to differ.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about consistent side-effect handling

rule: Use the same CSS unit system (px or rem) consistently throughout the application; do not mix units unless there is an established convention for when each is appropriate.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: don't mix CSS unit systems

rule: When adding new dependency injection bindings, follow the same pattern used by existing modules (e.g., @provides with named annotations) rather than introducing a different abstraction for the same purpose.
mode_rationale: Convention to follow existing DI patterns

rule: When adding documentation to one function or command in a group, add equivalent documentation to all sibling functions in the same file.
mode_rationale: Documentation consistency convention

rule: Keep API response DTO structures consistent across similar endpoints, even if slightly verbose, so frontend consumers can reuse TypeScript model definitions.
mode_rationale: Consistency guideline for API response shapes across endpoints

rule: When designing audit/domain events, choose a consistent convention (unified event with a changes field vs. separate events per action) and apply it across the codebase to ensure queryable and coherent audit trails.
mode_rationale: Single convention to use consistent audit event patterns

rule: Ensure CODEOWNERS entries for test files and supporting code match the team that owns the production code being tested, so that the correct team reviews changes.
mode_rationale: Single rule: CODEOWNERS entries should match production code ownership

rule: Verify a resource exists before attempting deletion and return an appropriate error if it does not, rather than silently succeeding or throwing an unexpected error.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to verify resource existence before deletion

rule: Review filenames for spelling errors before committing, especially for story files and test files that may not cause build failures.
mode_rationale: Single convention: review filenames for spelling errors before committing

rule: Before creating new feature flags or scoped identifiers, check if an established naming scope or prefix already exists for that domain area.
mode_rationale: Simple check-first naming convention

rule: When runtime behavior depends on infrastructure configured at instance creation, check the instance's persisted spec (e.g., ingressSpec) rather than the current feature flag value, since the flag may have changed after the instance was created.
mode_rationale: Single principle: use persisted instance spec for runtime decisions

rule: When migrating between SDK versions, verify that string representations and serialized outputs match between old and new versions, especially if those outputs are consumed downstream.
mode_rationale: Single verification rule during migrations

rule: Before reading a stream fully into memory, check or limit its size to prevent out-of-memory kills from unexpectedly large payloads.
mode_rationale: Performance/safety convention: limit stream size before full read

rule: Use DEBUG for detailed operational messages that would be noisy in production; reserve INFO for significant events that operators should see.
mode_rationale: Single convention: DEBUG for detailed messages, INFO for operator-visible events

rule: Pick one approach for composing event behavior — either override methods directly or use trait mixins — and apply it consistently; do not mix both in a way that cancels inherited behavior.
mode_rationale: Consistency convention: don't mix inheritance approaches

rule: When using Option[List], document whether None and Some(List.empty) have different semantic meanings, or prefer using plain List with a default empty value if they are equivalent.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for Option[List] semantics

rule: When a class is hard to name because it represents multiple concerns, it is a signal the class should be split into separate classes with clear single responsibilities.
mode_rationale: Naming/design heuristic expressible in one sentence

rule: Files that render JSX are components and should be named and organized as such; only put pure logic helpers (formatters, parsers, validators) in utils files.
mode_rationale: Simple file organization rule

rule: Interfaces (e.g., authorizers) should accept canonical types like Principal; callers that only have a User should perform the conversion themselves rather than polluting the interface with convenience overloads.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about keeping interfaces clean of convenience overloads

rule: When moving code into shared/common modules, extract a clean trait or interface that is free of external library dependencies so consumers are not forced to pull in transitive dependencies.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architecture guideline about dependency isolation

rule: After refactoring, verify that no dead code, unused imports, or orphaned test dependencies remain — these are easy to miss and create confusion for future readers.
mode_rationale: Simple hygiene rule after refactoring

rule: When deleting or archiving an entity, ensure all related resources (e.g., vanity URLs, references, child records) are also cleaned up to prevent orphaned state.
mode_rationale: Convention to clean up orphaned state on deletion

rule: When a TODO comment is resolved or removed, also remove any associated @ts-ignore directives that were only present because of the unfinished work.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup rule to remove ts-ignore when TODOs are resolved

rule: Remove test data, fixtures, and helper entities that are no longer referenced by any test to keep the test suite lean and avoid confusion about what is actively tested.
mode_rationale: Simple test hygiene rule about removing unused fixtures

rule: When the old version of a module is removed, rename the V2/V3 successor to drop the version suffix from interface names and file names.
mode_rationale: Simple naming cleanup rule after removing old versions

rule: Remove unused variables from useEffect dependency arrays; dependencies should only include values actually referenced in the effect body.
mode_rationale: Simple dependency array hygiene rule

rule: Always return a cleanup function from useEffect to unregister event listeners when the component unmounts.
mode_rationale: Simple React convention about useEffect cleanup

rule: When transitioning to a loading state, clear any previous error state so stale errors are not displayed alongside a loading indicator.
mode_rationale: Single convention: clear stale errors on loading transition

rule: Never mutate original arrays or objects passed as props or stored in state; clone data before performing filtering or other mutations to prevent React rendering issues.
mode_rationale: Simple immutability rule for React props/state

rule: Date and time formatting functions should live in the shared useDate utility so they respect locale settings, rather than being defined ad hoc in individual component utils.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use shared useDate utility for date formatting

rule: When a utility function is almost always invoked alongside the same side effect (e.g., logging), encapsulate that side effect inside the function to reduce repetitive call-site boilerplate.
Code should be readable and understandable without requiring the author to explain it; refactor complex logic until a reviewer can follow it unaided.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence readability guideline

rule: System tests and integration tests should automatically collect debug artifacts (e.g., support bundles, logs) when jobs or operations fail, to aid post-failure investigation.
When introducing a new configuration dimension (e.g., deployment flavor), ensure it is captured in observability and reporting data so results can be traced back to their source.
mode_rationale: Observability convention for new config dimensions

rule: Place configuration settings in the component that owns the decision they control, not in lower-level classes that merely execute the behavior.
mode_rationale: Configuration placement convention

rule: Fetch data in the component that renders it, not in a parent; use tab lifecycle hooks (e.g., destroyOnInactiveTab) so data refreshes when the component becomes active.
When extracting a new service to break cyclic dependencies, move all related operations (not just the new one) into the extracted service to maintain coherent responsibility boundaries.
mode_rationale: Architectural guideline about service extraction responsibility

rule: Define component prop types in the same file as the component rather than creating separate `.types.ts` files for simple prop interfaces.
mode_rationale: Convention to keep prop types in the same file

rule: Group related logic (e.g., all variables for a single feature/flag) into one block rather than scattering it across the function, to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention: group related logic together

rule: Move complex serialization logic to the model's companion object or a dedicated serializer in the same namespace, and add serialization tests to verify the output format.
mode_rationale: Simple convention for serialization placement

rule: Move TypeScript types and interfaces to a dedicated `types` file rather than defining them inline in utility or component files.
mode_rationale: File organization convention for TypeScript types

rule: When transforming a collection, prefer a single map/traversal that produces the final result rather than chaining separate map, zip, and toMap calls that create unnecessary intermediate collections.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention about collection traversals

rule: Combine related happy-path tests into a single test that exercises both the positive and negative cases together, verifying correct behavior is applied to the right entities and not applied to others.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to verify positive and negative cases together in one test

rule: Add explanatory comments when initialization or default-value logic involves multiple conditions or non-obvious rules, so future maintainers can understand the intent without reverse-engineering.
mode_rationale: Single convention: comment non-obvious init logic

rule: When a boolean parameter value appears counter-intuitive in context (e.g., isSnapshot=false for a snapshot mount), add a comment explaining the reasoning.
mode_rationale: Single convention: explain surprising boolean parameter values

rule: When code relies on an implicit assumption that may change in the future (e.g., only one active instance), add a comment documenting the assumption and what would need to change.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for assumptions

rule: When intentionally omitting dependencies from a React useEffect/useCallback dependency array, leave a comment explaining why to prevent future confusion.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to comment omitted React deps

rule: When code intentionally deviates from the expected flow (e.g., preserving stale data during a close animation), add an inline comment explaining why.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention about inline comments

rule: When a conditional check encodes domain knowledge that is not self-evident from the code, add a comment explaining the business reason and its relationship to the broader system.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for domain-specific conditionals

rule: Add explanatory comments or improved logging for non-obvious control flow, especially around retry logic, race conditions, or multi-step shutdown sequences where the 'why' is not self-evident from the code.
mode_rationale: Single guideline to document non-obvious logic

rule: When a default or fallback value depends on implicit context (e.g., undefined means 'everyone' in create mode vs. a real value in edit mode), add a code comment explaining the reasoning for future maintainers.
mode_rationale: Single convention: add comments explaining implicit default value reasoning

rule: When filtering logic depends on non-obvious domain behavior (e.g., one list only containing active items while another contains all), add a comment explaining why the filter exists.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention about explaining non-obvious filters

rule: Add inline comments explaining the purpose and meaning of parameters or expressions when the code is non-obvious, especially for future-proofed or indirectly mapped values.
Add comments explaining the rationale behind non-obvious priority or ordering logic in data structures like maps and lists, even if the code is somewhat self-documenting.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for non-obvious logic

rule: Add inline comments explaining what non-obvious parameters do, especially for aggregation or query configurations, so future developers can understand intent quickly.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for query configs

rule: Add comments explaining why retry or reconnection loops exist, including the failure scenarios they handle (e.g., cursor closed due to node failover), so future developers don't accidentally remove them.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for resilience logic

rule: Add explanatory comments for test steps whose purpose is not immediately clear, especially workarounds for previous bugs or non-deterministic behavior.
When using setTimeout or other timing hacks, add a comment explaining why the timeout is needed.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for timing hacks

rule: Add a comment explaining tuple field meanings when the types alone do not make the values obvious (e.g., tuples of ID and name).
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for tuple fields

rule: When adding code whose purpose is not self-evident (e.g., data remapping workarounds), leave a comment explaining why it exists so future developers can maintain or safely remove it.
mode_rationale: Single convention about explaining non-obvious code

rule: When combining multiple test cases into a single scenario, add section comments that label what each block verifies so readers can quickly understand the test structure.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention for test structure

rule: Sample or example deployment configurations should include comments warning where production configs need to be more restrictive (e.g., network policies, RBAC).
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for example configs

rule: When code intentionally differs from a nearby established pattern, add an inline comment explaining why the divergence exists to help future developers.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention

rule: Do not leave commented-out code without explanation; either remove it or add a TODO comment explaining why it is kept and when it should be restored.
mode_rationale: Single convention about handling commented-out code

rule: Comments should explain why code exists or the business reasoning, not restate what the code already clearly expresses.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention about comment purpose

rule: Ensure inline comments accurately describe the actual runtime behavior; a comment saying 'initial onChange' is misleading if the effect runs on every dependency change.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about keeping comments accurate

rule: Global/shared components must never depend on feature-scoped components; if a feature-specific component is needed by a shared component, extract it into the common layer first.
mode_rationale: Single convention: shared components never depend on feature-scoped code

rule: Prefer comparing entire case class instances (result shouldEqual ExpectedClass(...)) over checking individual fields, so new fields added in future PRs are automatically covered by existing tests.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about assertion granularity

rule: When comparing sizes or measurements, convert the limit to the base unit and compare directly (e.g., file.size > limitInMB * MB_IN_BYTES) rather than dividing and rounding, which introduces precision errors.
mode_rationale: Single convention about precision in comparisons

rule: Include all referenced variables in useEffect/useCallback/useMemo dependency arrays; missing dependencies cause stale closures and subtle bugs.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about React hook dependency completeness

rule: When adding ScalaDoc to a method, document all parameters and include a summary of what the method does — partial documentation is worse than none because it implies the undocumented parts are unimportant.

A component's callback functions should use the component's own props/context values rather than accepting overriding parameters, to prevent inconsistency between what the component displays and what it emits.
mode_rationale: Single rule about callback functions using component's own props

rule: Component callback props like onClick should be marked optional if the component can function without them, rather than requiring callers to always provide a handler.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about prop optionality

rule: Components should define sensible default values for props (e.g., orientation defaulting to 'horizontal') while allowing consumers to override via props.
mode_rationale: Single convention about providing default prop values

rule: Move visibility and rendering logic inside the component itself rather than keeping it in the parent; the component should decide whether it renders.
mode_rationale: Single convention about where rendering logic lives

rule: When a component is a specialization of another, place it in its own file rather than co-locating it inside the base component's file.
mode_rationale: Simple file organization convention

rule: Modal and form components should expose generic callbacks like onSuccess or onEntityCreated rather than directly calling parent-specific functions like refetch, so consumers can customize behavior.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence decoupling principle for component APIs

rule: Modals and child components that need global state should consume the store directly rather than having parents pass it down as props.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture rule: child components should consume store directly

rule: Style components by composing styled-components and forwarding className to the root element, rather than duplicating components or passing style override props.
mode_rationale: Simple styling convention about composing via className forwarding

rule: When making changes for a cloud fix or hotfix, prefer creating a wrapper/composition component rather than modifying a shared base component to minimize risk to other consumers.
mode_rationale: Single architecture principle for hotfixes

rule: When a component needs additional data for a new use case, create a specialized wrapper that composes the original component rather than modifying it to fetch the data internally.
mode_rationale: Single architectural preference for composition

rule: Compute derived boolean values in the component that uses them rather than passing them as props from a parent, unless multiple children need the same value.
mode_rationale: Simple component design principle about derived values

rule: Keep class and type names concise; avoid redundant qualifiers that duplicate the package namespace, and maintain consistent suffixes (e.g., Dto for API models) across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for class/type brevity

rule: Configuration key names must exactly match the names referenced in the code that reads them; mismatches cause silent failures.
When an API endpoint can return a large number of items, use a configurable page size (with a sensible default) and document known cases where the default may be insufficient.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence API design guideline about pagination

rule: Ensure ESLint and Prettier are configured in your local development environment so formatting issues (e.g., extra whitespace) are caught automatically before code review.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: ensure local linting/formatting tools are configured

rule: When a PR includes changes that appear unrelated to the stated purpose, reviewers should explicitly confirm intentionality to prevent accidental regressions.
mode_rationale: Single review practice about confirming unrelated changes

rule: Before modifying interactive UI behavior (e.g., hover states, disabled styling), confirm the intended behavior with the UX team to avoid unintended regressions.
mode_rationale: Single guideline: confirm UI behavior changes with UX team

rule: When modifying a shared API endpoint, identify all consumers (UI, other services) and ensure they are updated to match the new contract.
mode_rationale: Simple discipline rule about checking downstream consumers

rule: When removing a feature from one surface, audit all downstream consumers (e.g., PDF reports, exports, APIs) to ensure the removal is complete and consistent.
mode_rationale: Single convention: audit all consumers when removing a feature

rule: Before adding audit events to an operation, evaluate how frequently it fires in practice — high-frequency triggers (e.g., file preview clicks) can create excessive noise.
mode_rationale: Single convention: evaluate frequency before adding audit events

rule: When changing the encoding or format of data written to persistent storage, assess backward compatibility — determine whether old data needs migration or if it is regenerated on each use.
mode_rationale: Convention to assess backward compatibility when changing data formats

rule: When changing URL routes or API endpoints, ensure backwards compatibility by implementing redirects from old routes and updating all dependent components.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about redirects when changing routes

rule: When performing synchronous file operations (especially downloads or reads), evaluate whether the operation could block the main thread or lock resources, particularly with large files.
mode_rationale: Awareness guideline about blocking operations

rule: When transforming identifiers by replacing characters (e.g., replacing '/' with '--'), analyze whether distinct inputs can produce the same output and handle or document the collision risk.
mode_rationale: Design consideration when transforming identifiers

rule: When renaming or removing a configuration flag or central config key, evaluate whether a database migration is needed for existing persisted values.
mode_rationale: Single convention: evaluate DB migration needs when renaming/removing config flags

rule: When adding a new route, explicitly evaluate which controller owns the domain concept and discuss alternatives before defaulting to the most convenient location.
mode_rationale: Single architectural convention about evaluating controller ownership for routes

rule: When soft-deleting entities that have associated secrets or credentials, explicitly decide whether those credentials should be purged or retained, since users may expect their stored credentials are fully removed when they delete a resource.
When renaming a field in BSON or database storage, consider writing a migration rather than just changing the field name in code, to avoid breaking reads of existing documents.
mode_rationale: Simple reminder to migrate data when renaming stored fields

rule: Before fixing a long-standing API bug, assess whether any consumers may have built workarounds that depend on the buggy behavior, and plan for a safe migration path.
mode_rationale: Single API change management principle

rule: Before adding a new API endpoint, evaluate whether an existing endpoint (e.g., GET /:projectId) can be extended with an additional field to serve the same purpose, reducing API surface area.
mode_rationale: Single convention: evaluate extending existing endpoints before adding new ones

rule: When adding a configuration or feature for one entity type, evaluate whether the same capability should be extended to similar entity types for consistency.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence design consideration for consistency across entity types

rule: In React components, consider memoizing expensive computed values or derived data to prevent unnecessary recalculations on re-render.
mode_rationale: React performance convention for derived values

rule: Verify that new UI layouts render correctly at the minimum supported viewport dimensions (1366x768) before merging.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: verify layouts at 1366x768 minimum

rule: Before adding new traces or instrumentation, evaluate the volume of events that will be generated and the cost implications in the observability platform (e.g., New Relic data ingest costs).
mode_rationale: Single-sentence reminder to evaluate cost before adding traces

rule: When adding client-side sorting or grouping (e.g., pinning) on top of server-side paginated results, verify that the feature works correctly across page boundaries — items may not appear on the expected page if pagination was applied before reordering.
mode_rationale: Single principle: verify reordering works across page boundaries

rule: When adding or changing feature flags, configuration, or shared resources in tests, verify that the change does not create side effects for other tests running in parallel.
mode_rationale: Awareness guideline about side effects in parallel test environments

rule: Set pagination defaults based on real-world data volumes rather than arbitrary numbers, considering the largest known customer deployments.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about pagination defaults

rule: When adding new API calls to a rate-limited external service, evaluate whether other services or pods share the same rate limit and could cause contention.
mode_rationale: Simple performance consideration when adding API calls

rule: Analytics/tracking event names should follow a consistent naming pattern across the codebase; do not add ad-hoc qualifiers to some events that are absent from others.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for analytics event consistency

rule: Use a consistent approach for constructing API base URLs across the codebase (e.g., always use `window.location.origin` or never use it) rather than mixing patterns.
mode_rationale: Simple consistency convention for URL construction

rule: Use consistent phrasing across similar API endpoint descriptions (e.g., if one list endpoint says 'based on the provided filters', all similar list endpoints should use the same wording).
mode_rationale: Single style rule: use consistent phrasing across similar endpoint descriptions

rule: When building new API endpoints or schemas for a feature that parallels an existing feature, use the same field names and conventions to maintain cross-API consistency, even if a more accurate name exists.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to reuse field names across parallel APIs

rule: Do not mix API version prefixes (e.g., v1 and v4) in the same endpoint path; keep versioning consistent within each API surface.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention

rule: Use a dictionary or mapping for aria-label values so the same icon always gets the same label, enabling reliable QA/automation selectors.
mode_rationale: Single convention about using a dictionary for aria-labels

rule: Use a consistent async style within a function — either async/await with try-catch or promise chains with .catch(), but do not mix both patterns.
mode_rationale: Style rule about not mixing async patterns

rule: Do not mix authenticated and unauthenticated operations in the same service; keep authorization at a consistent layer so new contributors know where auth is enforced.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture principle about consistent auth layering

rule: When adding a new implementation of an existing capability, replicate the same validation rules and edge-case handling as the existing implementation to avoid inconsistent user experiences.
mode_rationale: Convention to replicate validation across implementations

rule: When designing a client wrapper, choose a consistent configuration strategy: either make the client a singleton with per-call parameters, or make one client instance per configuration set; avoid mixing constructor-level and method-level configuration for the same concern.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence API design convention about configuration consistency

rule: Configuration files of the same kind (e.g., ESLint configs) should use a consistent file format and extension across all packages in a monorepo.
mode_rationale: Simple consistency convention for config files

rule: When changing an infrastructure reference (e.g., username, path, host), audit and update all related configuration points in the same PR.
mode_rationale: Single convention: update all config points in one PR

rule: Keep default parameter values consistent across similar method signatures in the same class or module to avoid confusing callers.
mode_rationale: Simple API consistency convention

rule: Use a consistent version pinning strategy (e.g., caret ranges like ^29.7.0 vs exact pins) across all dependencies in a package manifest to avoid confusion about update behavior.
mode_rationale: Package manifest convention for version pinning

rule: Maintain consistent formatting conventions (e.g., punctuation, capitalization) across method descriptions and documentation comments within a codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple formatting convention for doc comments

rule: Use the same domain term across backend and frontend layers — do not introduce an alternate name in one layer (e.g., 'tasks' in the UI vs 'goals' in the backend) without a planned migration to rename everywhere.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming convention for cross-layer consistency

rule: When methods operate on specific domain resources (e.g., Kubernetes resources), include the resource type name in the method name for consistency with related methods.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for method names involving domain resources

rule: Domain terms (e.g., Dataset, Workspace, Model API) should be capitalized consistently across all API specification files to avoid confusion.
mode_rationale: Naming consistency convention for domain terms in API specs

rule: When the codebase uses synonyms (e.g., 'run' and 'execution'), keep naming consistent within a bounded context; if a DTO uses 'Run', the corresponding domain class should too, unless the name is scoped to a different local context.
mode_rationale: Single naming consistency convention

rule: Use a single, consistent encoding strategy (e.g., encodeFilePath vs encodeURIComponent) across all routes; do not mix approaches without justification.
mode_rationale: Consistency convention for encoding strategy

rule: Use consistent field names across related entities in the codebase; prefer the naming convention already established by sibling entities (e.g., use `name` instead of `title` if other event entities use `name`).
mode_rationale: Simple naming consistency convention

rule: Use consistent entity names across API endpoints and models; do not mix similar terms (e.g., 'app instance' vs 'app version instance') for the same concept.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for consistent entity terms across APIs

rule: Always use canonical entity types (e.g., `EntityType.User`) in audit events, not context-relative names like `"assignee"`, to enable consistent global filtering and querying.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for entity types

rule: Error handling behavior (which exceptions are logged, suppressed, or surfaced) should be consistent across all modules that serve the same role, even when they live in different packages.
mode_rationale: Consistency guideline for error handling behavior across same-role modules

rule: When multiple methods perform similar operations (e.g., parsing API responses), ensure error handling is uniform across all of them rather than having ad-hoc handling in individual methods.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to keep error handling uniform across similar methods

rule: Within a module, use a single error handling strategy (exceptions OR Either/Try) consistently across analogous methods rather than mixing approaches.
mode_rationale: Single convention: don't mix error handling approaches within a module

rule: Use a consistent format and casing for error messages and exception strings across the codebase (e.g., 'could not find project for Dataset ${dataset.id}').
mode_rationale: Single convention about consistent error message formatting

rule: Set a default human-readable error message and attempt to enhance it with details from the API response; do not replace standard response fields like statusText. Follow existing error handling patterns in the codebase.
mode_rationale: Single convention about default error messages with enhancement

rule: Maintain a consistent error response format (e.g., JSON with an 'error' key) across all endpoints; do not regress structured error responses to raw strings.
mode_rationale: Single API convention about error response structure

rule: Map the same exception types to the same HTTP status codes across all endpoints in a controller to avoid inconsistent behavior and confusing debugging.
mode_rationale: Consistency guideline for exception-to-status-code mapping across endpoints

rule: Use the same filtering approach (inclusion vs exclusion) across related code branches to avoid confusion and reduce the chance of subtle bugs.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence consistency rule for filtering approaches

rule: Use a consistent format for contextual help links across the product, such as 'Learn more about {feature}' without trailing punctuation.
mode_rationale: Simple formatting convention for help links

rule: Maintain consistent casing conventions for identifiers across a codebase (e.g., if existing code uses PascalCase like CustomSVG, new related identifiers should follow the same convention).
mode_rationale: Simple naming consistency rule

rule: Use a consistent import style for shared dependencies (e.g., React) across all files in a module to maintain codebase uniformity.
mode_rationale: Style rule: uniform import conventions per module

rule: Be consistent within a file about whether named parameters are used in function calls; don't mix named and positional styles for the same parameter across call sites.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence style rule about parameter consistency

rule: Use the same field/property name across all layers (API DTOs, service layer, database, permissions) so that read and write interfaces stay consistent and predictable.
mode_rationale: Single convention: same field names across API, service, DB layers

rule: Use consistent naming suffixes and verb choices across related type families (e.g., all event types should follow the same pattern like 'BulkUpdate' / 'BulkDelete' rather than mixing 'Change', 'Updated', 'UpdateChange').
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming consistency rule for type families

rule: Keep persistence logic (e.g., database saves) in a consistent location for all code paths (create, update, delete) within a service to avoid confusion about where state changes happen.
Use the established product terminology consistently in code identifiers — if the product calls something 'Apps', do not use 'Applications' in code names, as inconsistent naming creates confusion.
mode_rationale: Naming convention to match code identifiers to established product terminology

rule: Keep retry counts and backoff configurations consistent across the codebase; when a standard exists (e.g., 5 retries), new retry policies should match unless there is a documented reason to differ.
mode_rationale: Convention: match existing retry counts unless documented otherwise

rule: Keep all styled components in a single location per feature — either all in a dedicated styles file or all co-located in the component file, not split across both.
mode_rationale: Single convention about where to place styled components

rule: Validation logic (e.g., billing tags, permissions) should be applied consistently across all code paths that create or modify the same entity, not duplicated in different places with divergent behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to apply validation consistently across all code paths

rule: When multiple consecutive elements share the same condition, group them under a single conditional check rather than repeating the condition.
mode_rationale: Simple code style rule about grouping conditions

rule: When importing multiple items from the same module, combine them into a single import statement rather than using separate import lines.
mode_rationale: Simple code style convention

rule: When multiple permission check paths (e.g., All vs. ByName) have nearly identical logic, consolidate them into a single check to reduce duplication and maintenance burden.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline to reduce duplication in permission logic

rule: Place constants in companion objects rather than inline in class bodies, and reference existing constants from other modules instead of redefining them.
mode_rationale: Straightforward convention about constant placement

rule: When a type field in an API schema has a known fixed value, constrain it with an enum declaration to prevent invalid values.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention for constraining known values

rule: Use colors from the design system theme rather than hardcoding hex values; when the color palette is available in the theme, components should reference theme tokens.
mode_rationale: Theming convention: use tokens, not hardcoded hex values

rule: Place domain-to-DTO conversion logic in the API/DTO layer (e.g., `fromServiceDomain` on the DTO), not as `toApi` methods on internal domain models, to keep the dependency arrow pointing inward and avoid recompiling internals when API models change.
mode_rationale: Single convention: place DTO conversion in the API layer, not domain models

rule: When implementing object copy, fork, or clone operations, ensure all relevant attributes are transferred -- review the source model field-by-field to avoid missing properties.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: review all fields when implementing copy/clone operations

rule: Use the correct HTTP status code for each operation: 201 for resource creation, 200 for responses with a body, and 204 for delete operations with no response body.
mode_rationale: Simple convention mapping HTTP methods to status codes

rule: Ensure that exception types map to the correct HTTP status codes — user input errors (e.g., IllegalArgumentException) should return 400, not 500.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about mapping exceptions to correct HTTP status codes

rule: Column definitions, configuration objects, and other static data that do not depend on props or state should be defined outside the component to avoid unnecessary re-creation on every render.
mode_rationale: Performance convention about static definitions

rule: Use lowercase with hyphens (kebab-case) for data-test attribute values, not camelCase or PascalCase.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for data-test attributes

rule: Add debug logs at key checkpoints (e.g., readiness checks, polling loops) that include identifying context such as resource name, current count, and expected count.
mode_rationale: Single convention: include identifying context in debug log messages

rule: End-to-end test steps that verify backend or API behavior should not depend on being on a specific UI page; use API-based approaches or parameterized steps to avoid brittle coupling.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence test architecture convention

rule: When the same conditional logic appears in multiple places, extract it into a private helper function to avoid duplication and reduce the risk of the copies diverging.
mode_rationale: Simple DRY principle applied to conditional logic

rule: When two components across different modules perform nearly identical functions, consolidate them into a single shared component rather than maintaining parallel implementations.
mode_rationale: DRY convention for shared components

rule: When two utility functions have identical logic, combine them into a single shared function with a configuration flag rather than maintaining duplicates.
When a user's permission or role state is unknown (e.g., request pending or failed), default to the most restrictive access level rather than granting elevated privileges.
mode_rationale: Single security convention about default access levels

rule: Regex patterns and other compiled constants should be defined as static fields on companion objects so they are only compiled once, not on every method invocation.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention for compiled constants

rule: Define default or constant callback functions outside the React component body and pass them as references, rather than defining inline functions in component props.
mode_rationale: Single React performance convention

rule: Application route paths and link constants should be defined in the core routing module, not scattered across utility or feature files.
mode_rationale: Single rule about route definition placement

rule: When a component depends on asynchronously loaded data (e.g., locale configuration), consider delaying the render until the data is available to avoid rendering in an incomplete or incorrect state.
mode_rationale: Single coding convention: delay render until async data is ready

rule: When overriding a pattern match to customize handling for specific cases, delegate all other cases to the superclass or default implementation rather than duplicating or restricting the existing logic.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention: delegate non-custom cases to superclass

rule: When deleting a resource that has dependent external resources (e.g., Kubernetes objects), delete the database record only after the external cleanup succeeds so the record is preserved for retry if cleanup fails.
mode_rationale: Ordering convention for cleanup operations

rule: If a function is fully replaced by another (e.g., update replaced by patch), delete the unused function rather than keeping both to avoid confusion about which to use.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: remove replaced functions

rule: When a refactoring removes most of a file's contents, delete the file entirely rather than leaving a near-empty shell.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup rule after refactoring

rule: Use the project's shared form abstraction (e.g., DynamicField) consistently across a single form rather than mixing it with raw library inputs, which fragments state management and breaks UI consistency.
mode_rationale: Single convention about using the shared form abstraction consistently

rule: When adding action buttons (e.g., download) in a section that already has similar buttons, use the same icon collection and style for visual consistency.
mode_rationale: Visual consistency rule for action buttons in a section

rule: Use consistent types for ID fields across related parameters in the same domain (e.g., if other IDs use ObjectId, new IDs should too).
mode_rationale: Simple consistency rule for ID field types across a domain

rule: Maintain a consistent ordering of imports in Scala files, typically grouping by package hierarchy (java/scala stdlib first, then third-party, then internal packages) with alphabetical ordering within groups.
mode_rationale: Single convention about import group ordering

rule: Maintain consistent 2-space indentation in Scala files; fix spacing issues immediately rather than letting them propagate through the codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple formatting convention about indentation

rule: Ensure a state variable's initial value type is consistent with the type returned by its update handler to avoid runtime type mismatches.
mode_rationale: Type consistency convention for state variables

rule: Use consistent log levels across architectural layers — debug in services and trace in controllers — to provide distinct granularity when troubleshooting.
mode_rationale: Convention for log level assignment by layer

rule: When adding a new variant of an existing configuration or test target, follow the established naming convention to keep the system predictable (e.g., if standard has a vcluster variant, the new target should too).
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention to follow established patterns

rule: Use consistent casing and spacing in API route descriptions and doc comments — match the variable name's word boundaries (e.g., 'data plane' not 'dataplane') and capitalize acronyms like 'ID' consistently.
mode_rationale: Naming and casing consistency convention

rule: When a value is null-checked in one location, it must not be assumed non-null (via !) in another location that receives the same value — handle nullability consistently.
mode_rationale: Convention for consistent null handling across locations

rule: When a function accepts parameters that serve the same role (e.g., all are query params), pass them consistently through the same mechanism (params object vs positional args) rather than mixing styles based on whether they are required or optional.
mode_rationale: Single convention about parameter passing consistency

rule: Ensure return types are consistent and intentional across related methods; if callers discard a return value, the method should return Unit to avoid confusion.
mode_rationale: Single rule about return type consistency

rule: Use `q` as the query parameter name for search/filter endpoints across all APIs to maintain consistency, and use a more descriptive name like `searchQuery` only internally.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for search parameters

rule: Do not set a sort direction without a corresponding sort key; ensure sort-related state fields are always logically consistent.
mode_rationale: Simple invariant: sort direction requires sort key

rule: Use terminology that is consistent with URL patterns and codebase conventions (e.g., prefer 'allowlist' over 'whitelist' when URLs already use 'allowlist').
mode_rationale: Naming convention to keep terminology aligned with URL patterns

rule: Common UI components should use the dataTest prop convention (mapped internally) rather than data-testid for test selectors, to maintain consistency across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about using dataTest prop over data-testid

rule: Use consistent term ordering and phrasing in test step definitions so that steps for similar concepts are predictable and discoverable.
mode_rationale: Simple naming/phrasing convention for test steps

rule: When multiple providers implement the same interface, their validation logic should be consistent — don't reinvent validation in each provider when a shared approach already exists.
mode_rationale: Convention for shared validation logic

rule: When service-specific configuration closely mirrors a shared library's config structure, merge it into the common library to avoid duplication and drift.
mode_rationale: Architectural guideline to merge duplicated config into shared libraries

rule: Combine multiple filter conditions into a single filter function call instead of chaining multiple passes over the same collection.
mode_rationale: Single convention: combine multiple filters into one pass

rule: When multiple styled components share the same styles (e.g., icon wrappers with identical styling), consolidate them into a single reusable styled component.
Consolidate related parsing steps (e.g., splitting, field extraction, default handling) into a single function rather than scattering them across the call site.
mode_rationale: Code organization guideline to group related parsing steps

rule: Combine related log statements into a single informative line so it is easier to correlate results when reading logs.
mode_rationale: Single convention: combine related log lines into one

rule: Avoid proliferating near-identical DTO classes (e.g., StorageDto, StorageInfoDto, StorageViewDto); prefer adding optional fields to a single DTO over creating multiple overlapping types.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention: avoid DTO proliferation

rule: When testing multiple cases that share setup, group assertions inside the loop with their mocks to reduce duplication and improve readability.
mode_rationale: Testing style convention for grouping assertions with mocks

rule: When adding retry/wait logic for tests, extend the existing helper function rather than creating a new one, so all retry behavior lives in one place.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence DRY convention for test helpers

rule: Dependencies must be injected via class constructor arguments rather than instantiated inside the class, to enable unit testing.
mode_rationale: Single architectural convention about how to inject dependencies

rule: Before removing or merging code that appears duplicated, verify with the original author whether the duplication was intentional to avoid silent behavior changes.
mode_rationale: Simple social convention — check before removing apparent duplication

rule: When a store (e.g., useStore) is globally accessible, consume it directly in the component that needs it rather than prop-drilling it from a router or parent.
mode_rationale: Single state management convention

rule: When adding configuration files that must be present in a Docker image, add a corresponding container structure test to verify the file exists and update the Dockerfile COPY instructions.
mode_rationale: Multi-step procedure: add config file, add structure test, update Dockerfile COPY

rule: Split large components into container (business logic) and view (presentational) parts, using hooks or wrapper components to separate concerns.
mode_rationale: Single architectural convention about component structure

rule: Name section-level components with their parent context (e.g., `CostCenterHome` instead of `Home`) to avoid ambiguity with platform-wide concepts.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for section-level components

rule: Log messages should include contextual identifiers (e.g., controller name, operation name) and use trace-level logging before and after a block to aid debugging.
mode_rationale: Simple logging convention about including context

rule: A REST API controller (presentation layer) must depend only on its own domain service, never on external services directly; new logic belongs in the service layer to keep domain logic separate from REST concerns.
mode_rationale: Single layering rule for controller dependencies

rule: When reading optional collection fields from external sources (databases, APIs), convert them to non-optional with getOrElse(empty) at the domain boundary rather than propagating Option throughout the domain model.
mode_rationale: Boundary convention: normalize optionals at the edge

rule: Before renaming shared configuration files, align with the original authors or domain owners on the new name to ensure it accurately reflects the file's purpose.
mode_rationale: Simple communication convention

rule: When changing ports, constants, or configuration in one repo, identify and create matching PRs in dependent repos (e.g., workload operator, compute services) before merging.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to create matching PRs in dependent repos

rule: The core package should only define base routes for each domain; feature-specific inner routing belongs in the feature module, not in core.
mode_rationale: Architecture boundary rule for core vs feature routing

rule: Audit and telemetry events must reference the most specific entity (e.g., file paths, not the parent project ID) so events are traceable to the exact resource acted upon.
