mode_rationale: Convention for referencing the most specific entity in audit events

rule: Use the appropriate log level for the situation — use warn for concerning-but-recoverable conditions rather than info or error.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about log level selection

rule: Use NavTabs for full-page top-level navigation and Segment/toggle for in-context view switching within a section.
mode_rationale: Simple convention for choosing NavTabs vs Segment components

rule: Do not place internal service models in API model packages; keep non-API entities in the package where they are consumed (e.g., alongside the service interface).
mode_rationale: Code organization rule: keep internal models out of API packages

rule: Use the PRE flow (modal before action) only for actions that need user confirmation before proceeding; use the POST flow (confirmation after action) for inline forms and actions where the UI triggers after the user clicks — do not force a PRE pattern where POST is the natural fit.
mode_rationale: Single UX convention about confirmation modal timing

rule: Use `2>/dev/null` to suppress stderr output; do not use `2>&1` which merges stderr into stdout instead of discarding it.
mode_rationale: Single shell convention

rule: Use the most specific TypeScript type available instead of defaulting to `string`; review AI-generated code for type accuracy.
mode_rationale: Single rule about using specific types over string

rule: Only include dependencies that are actually used inside the useEffect callback; extra dependencies cause unnecessary re-runs and potential bugs.
mode_rationale: React convention for accurate dependency arrays

rule: When creating entities that span multiple systems, create the canonical record in the primary datastore first, then use its ID to propagate to external systems like identity providers.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence ordering convention for cross-system entity creation

rule: When removing a feature or capability, create a follow-up ticket to formally deprecate or replace it rather than silently dropping it.
mode_rationale: Process convention for feature removal

rule: When an existing role or entity has a semantically loaded name that doesn't exactly match the new use case, create a new purpose-specific one rather than repurposing it, to avoid future bugs when the original's permissions evolve independently.
mode_rationale: Single design guideline about role semantics

rule: When mocking data in tests, create generator functions in `frontend/packages/mocks/generators` so the mock data can be reused across test suites.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention: create reusable mock data generators

rule: Name creation request DTOs using the pattern 'New[EntityName]' (e.g., NewResourceConfiguration) to clearly distinguish them from read models and update DTOs.
mode_rationale: Single naming pattern for creation DTOs

rule: When cleaning up credentials, ensure all storage locations are covered — including credential files stored inside .git directories, not just the standard git-credentials path.
mode_rationale: Convention to check all credential locations

rule: Critical operational failures (such as rollback failures) should trigger admin-facing alerts via supportAlerter, not just log messages, because logs alone are likely to go unnoticed.
mode_rationale: Alerting convention for critical failures

rule: When adding a new API endpoint in one service, evaluate whether a corresponding endpoint needs to be created in related services that expose similar resources to maintain API parity.
mode_rationale: Single API design principle for service consistency

rule: When a PR touches code owned by another team, tag a specific accountable person from that team and do not merge until they explicitly approve.
mode_rationale: Single process rule about cross-team review

rule: When fixing layout or overflow bugs, ensure the solution works for all viewport sizes, not just the ones tested — avoid hardcoded values that only fix a specific screen width.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about viewport-agnostic CSS fixes

rule: Use cursor-based pagination (not offset-based) when aggregating results from multiple data sources that need cross-source sorting.
mode_rationale: Single architectural guideline about pagination strategy

rule: Custom exception classes should name the wrapped exception parameter 'cause' (not 'ex') and pass it to the parent Exception constructor so the JVM exception chain is properly maintained.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: name wrapped exception param 'cause' and pass to parent

rule: Handle display-level data masking (e.g., obscuring secret values with placeholder strings) in the frontend rather than the backend, to avoid confusing real values with mask sentinels and to keep the API response contract clean.
mode_rationale: Architectural convention about where to handle display-level masking

rule: Create database indexes via migration scripts, not in application startup code that runs every time the app starts.
mode_rationale: Convention for where to create indexes

rule: Add DEBUG or TRACE level log statements before key decision points and side-effecting operations to aid troubleshooting in production.
trigger: When writing code that branches on a condition, selects a strategy, or makes a routing decision that affects downstream behavior
rationale: Without logging at decision points, debugging production issues requires guessing which branch was taken; adding these logs after the fact requires a redeploy, delaying incident resolution.
mode_rationale: Logging convention for key decision points
steps: Step 1: Identify the key decision point (if/match/strategy selection) in your code
Step 2: Add a DEBUG-level log statement immediately before or after the decision, including the input values and the chosen branch
Step 3: Include enough context to reconstruct the decision (e.g., feature flag value, user attribute, config setting)
Step 4: Keep the message concise — one line per decision, not a paragraph

rule: Never use dependencies that are not listed in package.json; if a polyfill or library is needed, explicitly add it as a dependency (or devDependency) before importing it.
mode_rationale: Single dependency management convention

rule: Never skip required dependencies in useEffect; if a function references external variables, either declare them as dependencies or define the function inside the useEffect to keep the dependency array honest.
mode_rationale: Simple React hooks convention

rule: Even when transitive dependencies provide the required libraries, declare direct dependencies explicitly in BUILD files so the dependency graph is accurate and resilient to upstream changes.
mode_rationale: Single build convention: declare direct dependencies explicitly

rule: Separate unrelated changes (e.g., API client generation, library upgrades) into distinct PRs to keep each PR focused and easier to review and bisect in history.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: keep PRs focused on one concern

rule: Create separate DTO classes for API responses rather than reusing internal domain models, to prevent cross-team breakage and keep API contracts independent of internal refactors.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention to separate API DTOs from domain models

rule: Database persistence records should not reuse API DTOs for field types; define dedicated persistence types so that changes to the API layer do not break database serialization and vice versa.
mode_rationale: Architectural boundary rule separating persistence from API types

rule: Separate service-side mount points from container-side mount paths in configuration so they can be independently configured without coupling infrastructure concerns.
mode_rationale: Single configuration design convention

rule: When extracting values from a map or collection for use in a batch lookup or query, deduplicate them first to avoid redundant processing, even if each individual operation is inexpensive.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention for batch lookups

rule: When the same style override appears in multiple views, move it to the shared component that renders the styled element to keep styling DRY.
mode_rationale: DRY principle applied to style overrides — one-sentence guideline

rule: When a class or model exists in one module, do not recreate an identical version in another module; extract it to a shared location or reuse the existing one.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about avoiding duplication

rule: Default boolean component props (like autofocus) to false and require explicit opt-in; avoid defaulting behavioral props to true.
mode_rationale: API design convention for boolean prop defaults

rule: API list endpoints should default to sorting by creation timestamp in descending order (newest first) rather than having no default sort, providing a predictable and useful ordering out of the box.
mode_rationale: Single API convention for default sort order

rule: When adding new parameters to existing methods, provide default values to maintain backward compatibility so existing callers are not broken.
mode_rationale: Single API compatibility convention

rule: When a function takes an optional partial override object, default it to an empty object so callers are not forced to pass {} when using the defaults.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about defaulting optional parameters

rule: Provide sensible default values for component props (e.g., orientation defaults to horizontal) while allowing callers to override via props.
mode_rationale: Single convention about providing defaults with override capability

rule: When introducing new environment variables or config that changes routing or behavior, default to the safe/no-op value initially and change it in a follow-up PR to prevent breaking production.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to default new config to safe values

rule: Assign safe default values (e.g., empty array `[]`) to props or destructured parameters that may be undefined according to their type definition.
mode_rationale: Simple defensive coding convention

rule: Default parameter values should not silently override configuration settings; make parameters required when a default could mask a missing or incorrect config value.
mode_rationale: Single architectural principle about default parameter values

rule: Wrap API calls in try-catch to handle cases where intermediaries (proxies, gateways) return failures before the request reaches the target service, not just service-level error responses.
mode_rationale: Single guideline to wrap API calls in try-catch for proxy failures

rule: Use optional chaining with nullish coalescing (`arr[0]?.id ?? ''`) when accessing array elements, even when you expect the array to be non-empty.
mode_rationale: Single convention about safe array element access

rule: When accessing properties on a caught error, guard against the error not matching the expected interface to avoid throwing a secondary exception.
mode_rationale: Error handling safety convention

rule: Always wrap JSON.parse in a try-catch even when form validation exists, because optional or defaulted fields may still produce invalid JSON at runtime.
When accessing fields that may be absent during migrations or upgrades, use explicit pattern matching with a fallback branch and a warning log rather than assuming the field exists.
mode_rationale: Defensive coding convention for migration-sensitive fields

rule: Store raw identifiers in audit events and defer display formatting (e.g., fallback labels like "Unassigned") to read/display time, not write/storage time.
mode_rationale: Single data storage convention

rule: Don't execute all queries upfront when the results are only conditionally needed; defer data fetching to the point of use to avoid unnecessary work.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention about lazy data fetching

rule: Do not add API filtering, projections, or performance optimizations until all endpoints are implemented and real query patterns are known.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to avoid premature optimization

rule: Always define theme keys for both light and dark modes explicitly, even when the values are identical, to support future design divergence.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to always define both light and dark keys

rule: Component props should be typed so that downstream consumers do not need to cast values; if a prop is expected to have a specific type, define it in the component's prop interface rather than casting at the call site.
mode_rationale: Simple typing convention to avoid call-site casts

rule: Extract localStorage/sessionStorage key strings into named constants so they can be reused consistently and changed in one place.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for storage keys

rule: When a value can only be one of a known finite set (e.g., status filters like 'Open' | 'Resolved'), define a union type or enum rather than using raw strings, to get type safety and a single source of truth.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use union types/enums instead of raw strings for finite sets

rule: When a function or comparison operates on values of varying types, define explicit type parameters or type bounds rather than leaving them untyped.
mode_rationale: Single typing convention for generic parameters

rule: Define helper functions and variables in lexical order, placing them before the component that uses them for readability.
mode_rationale: Code ordering convention for helper functions

rule: Navigation identifiers and keys that may be needed for API calls should be defined in a separate data structure rather than inlined in UI components, enabling reuse across UI and API layers.
mode_rationale: Convention to externalize navigation keys for reuse

rule: In React components, define the props interface above the component definition and export the component as the default export.
mode_rationale: React component structure convention

rule: Layout components with fixed-width elements must define minimum supported viewport widths using breakpoint constants to prevent content overflow.
mode_rationale: Single rule about breakpoint definitions

rule: New routes for a feature area should be added to the existing module router rather than creating a parallel navigation entry, to avoid fragmenting routing logic.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention to avoid fragmenting routing

rule: Styled components must be defined outside the React component body, not inline within JSX.
mode_rationale: Single rule about styled component placement

rule: Define explicit TypeScript types for data records and function return values rather than using untyped objects or `any`.
mode_rationale: Simple typing convention

rule: When deleting a file, also remove all references to it (CI configs, imports, test steps) to prevent broken builds.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup convention when deleting files

rule: When re-enabling a previously skipped flaky test, include an actual code fix that addresses the flakiness and remove the skip comment; do not simply un-skip the test without evidence of a fix.
mode_rationale: Single rule: include a real fix when un-skipping tests

rule: When a class only needs a subset of a configuration object, accept that narrow type directly instead of depending on the full configuration, to reduce coupling and simplify construction.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention to reduce coupling

rule: Reference traits or interfaces rather than concrete service implementations to maintain loose coupling and testability.

Mark deprecated code paths with WARN-level log messages that explain the deprecation, so that anyone encountering the log in production knows to investigate and can help confirm whether the path is still in use.
mode_rationale: Logging convention for deprecated code paths

rule: Values that can be computed from existing state should be memoized (e.g., useMemo) rather than stored as separate state, to avoid synchronization bugs.
mode_rationale: Single rule about memoizing derived values instead of duplicating state

rule: Derive configuration values from existing data or context rather than adding new parameters; reduce the configuration surface area.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention to minimize configuration surface

rule: When a value can be computed from existing props or state, calculate it directly (or use useMemo) rather than syncing it through a useEffect into a separate useState, which adds unnecessary complexity and re-renders.
mode_rationale: Single React performance convention

rule: When looking up an entity by a unique field, use headOption.getOrElse with a descriptive NoSuchElementException rather than silently returning None or using unsafe .head.
mode_rationale: Single error-handling convention for entity lookups

rule: Choose field names that are unambiguous in context; when a name could be confused with existing fields, either pick a clearer name or add a comment explaining its purpose.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for unambiguous field names

rule: Name route-building or utility functions to reflect their specific purpose (e.g., `getProjectLauncherPath` instead of a generic name) so callers understand intent without reading the implementation.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention

rule: Log messages should describe the action and context (e.g. 'error creating model deployment secret, rolling back') rather than just echoing the exception message; pass the full exception object as a separate parameter for the stack trace.
mode_rationale: Logging convention about message content and exception passing

rule: Write test descriptions that describe expected behavior in plain language (e.g., 'should return empty list when no items match') rather than referencing method names (e.g., 'should testGetItems').
mode_rationale: Naming convention for test descriptions

rule: Name variables and config flags to precisely describe what they represent (e.g., use 'Count' suffix for counts, 'Size' for byte sizes).
mode_rationale: Single naming convention about precise suffixes

rule: When building generalized endpoints or services, ensure they support all planned use cases (e.g., multiple backing store types) from the start, not just the immediate one.
mode_rationale: Single API design principle

rule: Use a dictionary/object lookup instead of switch statements or if-else chains when mapping a static set of known keys to values.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to prefer object lookup over switch/if-else

rule: When a file contains methods serving multiple subsystems, prefix method names with the subsystem or domain they belong to (e.g., makeFlyteCommand instead of makeCommand) so readers can identify purpose without inspecting signatures or return types.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for methods in multi-concern files

rule: When a function name collides with or closely resembles an existing function in the codebase, use a more specific name that conveys scope, version, or return type to avoid confusion.
mode_rationale: Single naming clarity convention

rule: When a downstream service call can both fail and legitimately return empty results, only log warnings for actual failures — not for valid empty responses — to avoid noisy or misleading logs.
mode_rationale: Single logging convention to reduce noise from valid empty responses

rule: When an optional collection field has different semantic meanings for absence (None = unknown/legacy) vs empty (Some(empty) = explicitly none), preserve that distinction rather than collapsing both to None.
mode_rationale: Single type design principle for optional collections

rule: Exclude build artifacts, IDE-generated files, and temporary config files (e.g., Vite timestamp files) from version control.
mode_rationale: Simple version control hygiene rule

rule: Avoid merging commented-out code intended for future integration; instead, add the code when the dependency (e.g., API) is ready, or use feature flags.
mode_rationale: Simple code hygiene rule about not merging commented-out code

rule: Avoid adding eslint-disable comments when the code can be written in a way that satisfies the lint rule naturally; fix the code rather than silencing the linter.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: fix code instead of silencing linter

rule: When a code change breaks existing tests, fix the tests rather than disabling or deleting them; if a fix isn't immediately possible, document the plan to restore them.
mode_rationale: Single rule about maintaining test coverage

rule: Do not add fields, configuration, or dependencies to a data model until they are actually needed — defer additions until there is a concrete consumer.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence YAGNI principle about deferring additions

rule: Fields whose values are derived from other data (e.g., a path computed from a container config) should not be stored in the database; compute them at read time to avoid staleness and maintenance burden.
mode_rationale: Single data storage convention

rule: Before changing a component that has already been migrated to a new pattern, verify its migration status and consult the migration owner rather than rolling it back to the legacy approach.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about respecting migration status

rule: Code review comments should not be marked as resolved until the underlying question or concern has been actually addressed or answered.
mode_rationale: Single convention: don't mark comments resolved until the concern is addressed

rule: Avoid explicitly setting CSS properties to their default values (e.g., flex-direction: row) as it adds unnecessary code and can mislead readers into thinking a non-default value is intentional.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence CSS cleanliness rule

rule: Include all dependencies in React useEffect dependency arrays rather than using eslint-disable; refactor the effect or extract stable references to avoid infinite loops.
mode_rationale: React hooks convention

rule: API parameter documentation should enumerate the allowed or expected values, not just describe the parameter abstractly.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention for API params

rule: When an API schema field has implicit constraints (e.g., nesting depth limits), document those constraints explicitly in the schema description everywhere the assumption applies.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation rule for schema constraints

rule: Replace or annotate magic literals (like `true true`) with named variables or code comments so their purpose is immediately clear.
mode_rationale: Readability convention for unexplained literals

rule: When using API-imposed limits or magic numbers (e.g., max page size), add a comment explaining the constraint and its source so future developers understand why the value was chosen.
mode_rationale: Simple commenting convention

rule: When introducing new environment variables or setup requirements for running tests, document them in the README so developers know what to configure.
mode_rationale: Simple reminder to update docs when adding test prerequisites

rule: When an enum value's name no longer matches its semantic meaning due to backward compatibility, add a comment to the enum explaining the mapping (e.g., 'bitbucketServer' actually means 'datacenter').
mode_rationale: Single-sentence documentation convention for legacy enum values

rule: Add documentation to functions that use domain-specific numbers or non-obvious logic, explaining what the values represent and what the function returns.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for domain-specific logic

rule: When a method returns a generic type (e.g., String) that represents something domain-specific, add scaladoc or equivalent documentation explaining what the returned value actually is.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for generic return types

rule: When multiple derived lists or option sets come from the same source data, compute them all in a single iteration rather than using separate useMemo hooks that each traverse the full list.
mode_rationale: Single performance guideline: compute multiple derived values in one iteration

rule: When data is already present in the URL (route params or query string), read it from there rather than threading it through props or component state.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architecture guideline about state source of truth

rule: When a value has already been fetched from a service, derive dependent values from it rather than making a redundant service call to fetch the same data again.
mode_rationale: Performance convention to reuse already-fetched data

rule: API endpoint descriptions should describe the semantic behavior uniformly (e.g., 'absent fields are left unchanged') rather than calling out individual field behaviors that follow the same rule.
mode_rationale: Single documentation style rule for uniform descriptions

rule: Name constants to clearly indicate which domain or subsystem they belong to, so readers can understand their purpose without navigating to the definition.
mode_rationale: Naming convention: constant names should indicate their domain/subsystem

rule: Name feature flags and configuration parameters descriptively so their purpose is immediately clear (e.g., uploadFileBatchCount instead of a vague abbreviation).
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming convention for feature flags

rule: Name parameters that filter query results with a prefix like 'filterBy' to clearly communicate their purpose and distinguish them from identity or lookup parameters.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence parameter naming convention

rule: Name modules and packages with descriptive terms that convey their intent (e.g., 'utils' or 'commons') rather than generic names like 'lib'.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for modules

rule: Use explicit names like `pageNumber` instead of ambiguous short names for pagination parameters to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention

rule: Common/shared UI components should have descriptive, purpose-driven names rather than implementation-detail names like 'Styled' suffixes.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for shared UI components

rule: When designing schemas that may eventually be user-defined, minimize surface area now to reduce future validation burden.
mode_rationale: Simple schema design guideline

rule: When a parent and its children should be created as a unit (e.g., from a single UI confirmation), include child resources in the parent creation request rather than splitting across multiple endpoints to avoid partial success states.
mode_rationale: Single design principle: include children in parent creation to avoid partial success

rule: When implementing permission checks for a resource type, design the check to handle multiple instances of that resource rather than hard-coding for a single one.
mode_rationale: Single design guideline for permission checks

rule: When an error occurs in the UI, don't just display an error message — design a recovery path such as redirecting to an error page or offering a retry action, and coordinate with UX on the appropriate user experience.
mode_rationale: Principle about error UX design; guidance not a multi-step procedure

rule: Design DTOs and domain models so that invalid combinations of fields are unrepresentable at the type level, rather than relying on runtime validation.
mode_rationale: Single type-safety architectural principle

rule: When creating public API endpoints with generic paths, design the request schema to be polymorphic (e.g., using a discriminator field) so future types can be added without breaking changes.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use polymorphic schemas for extensible endpoints

rule: When creating a utility that operates on a single value (e.g., a theme color resolver), design its API to also accept multiple values to avoid forcing consumers to compose many single-value calls.
mode_rationale: Simple API design guideline: design utilities to accept multiple values

rule: Use a dictionary or mapping for aria-label values so the same icon always gets the same label, ensuring consistency for QA automation selectors.
mode_rationale: Simple accessibility/testing convention

rule: Create endpoints should produce deterministic results for the same payload; prefer upfront validation over silently skipping items, so two identical requests yield identical resources rather than different outcomes.
mode_rationale: Single API design principle: create endpoints should be deterministic

rule: Do not instantiate expensive resources like ActorSystem directly in class bodies; inject them via dependency injection to avoid cumulative latency in test suites that can cause hard-to-diagnose timeout failures.
mode_rationale: Single convention: inject expensive resources rather than instantiating them directly

rule: When a test needs to wait for an asynchronous operation, identify the root cause of the delay and use a targeted wait (e.g., wait_for_status) rather than a generous blanket timeout.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence test quality convention for async waits

rule: Include the exception type or error category in error/log messages so that different failure modes can be distinguished from each other.
mode_rationale: Simple error message convention

rule: Disable IDE 'align when multiline' formatting rules to avoid excessive whitespace that creates noisy diffs and makes side-by-side code review harder.
mode_rationale: Formatting convention: disable alignment rules to reduce noisy diffs

rule: Disable action buttons while their associated async operation is in progress to prevent duplicate submissions.
mode_rationale: Simple UI convention to prevent double-submit

rule: When embedding third-party scripts verbatim, disable ESLint and TypeScript checks at the file level rather than suppressing individual lines throughout the file.
mode_rationale: Convention for handling vendored files

rule: Disable service configuration options (like UsePAM in sshd) that are not actively needed to reduce attack surface and avoid environment-specific issues.
mode_rationale: Single security principle about reducing attack surface

rule: In API summaries and descriptions, qualify terms that overlap with existing domain concepts to prevent confusion (e.g., 'Gen AI endpoints' vs 'Domino Endpoints').
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline about qualifying overlapping terms in API descriptions

rule: When naming default values, include the entity they apply to so readers do not mistake them for generic or service-level defaults.
mode_rationale: Simple naming clarity rule for defaults

rule: Name audit event classes to clearly distinguish metadata changes (e.g., renaming a stage) from state transitions (e.g., moving to the next stage).
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for event classes

rule: When adding a new service or entity that shares a name with an existing package or concept, use a disambiguated name (e.g., prefix with the domain) to prevent confusion.
mode_rationale: Naming convention to prefix with domain when names collide with existing packages

rule: When naming variables that reference external resources like S3 buckets, include enough context to disambiguate which specific resource is being referenced.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention: include enough context to identify the resource

rule: When the same permission name exists at different scopes (e.g., project-level vs global), ensure the naming or documentation clearly distinguishes them to avoid confusion during code review and maintenance.
mode_rationale: Single naming clarity rule for permissions

rule: When an identifier contains words that are easily confused (e.g., 'Approval' and 'Approvers'), restructure the name to create clear separation between the similar terms.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: restructure names to avoid confusion between similar terms

rule: In BDD scenarios, clicking a button or performing a UI action is a 'When' progression step, not an assertion; use 'Then' steps to verify expected text or state after actions.
mode_rationale: Single BDD keyword usage convention

rule: Do not catch broad exception types (e.g., NonFatal) and map them all to a single HTTP status code; distinguish client errors (400) from server errors (500) by handling specific exception types.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention to map specific exceptions to appropriate status codes

rule: State enums used as 'target status' should only contain terminal/desired states, not transient states like 'starting' or 'updating' that represent in-progress transitions.
mode_rationale: Single enum design convention

rule: In Scala, distinguish between empty collections (Seq.empty) and null; they are not the same and mock setups must match the actual return types to avoid false test behavior.
Model query parameters according to their actual semantics: use sort order for ranking/ordering concerns and filters for inclusion/exclusion criteria; don't conflate the two.
mode_rationale: Single API semantics convention

rule: When auto-collapsing or hiding UI based on empty data, ensure the behavior only applies on initial render and not on every subsequent data change (e.g., after applying filters), as this can confuse users.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about limiting auto-collapse behavior to initial render

rule: Do not set a 'hasLoaded' or 'completed' flag in a finally block if it conflates successful loads with failed ones — loading state should accurately reflect whether data was successfully retrieved.
mode_rationale: State semantics convention for loading flags

rule: In microservice architectures, do not assume the authenticated principal is the end user — service-to-service calls may use a service principal, so preserve explicit user ID parameters when the caller is a service acting on behalf of a user.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture rule to preserve explicit user ID in service-to-service calls

rule: When catching a broad or non-fatal error, do not silently assume it was caused by one specific condition (e.g., uninitialized repo); the same error path may be triggered by unrelated failures.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about not over-interpreting broad errors

rule: Do not include empty files in a commit; either add the intended content or remove the file entirely.
mode_rationale: Simple commit hygiene rule

rule: Never use a user ID where an organization ID is expected or vice versa; mixing identity types across domain boundaries introduces authorization bugs and security vulnerabilities.
mode_rationale: Single security rule about identity type boundaries

rule: Configuration flags that control independent behaviors should remain independent; disabling one feature flag should not silently disable an unrelated feature, even if the code paths are nearby.
mode_rationale: Single principle: keep independent feature flags independent

rule: Do not define interfaces or types with no properties unless there is a concrete, immediate plan to populate them in a follow-up PR.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization rule

rule: Color tokens designated for font color must not be used for backgrounds, and vice versa — each token category has a defined purpose that must be respected.
mode_rationale: Single rule about respecting color token category boundaries

rule: Styled components should live in their own component files or alongside the component that uses them, not in shared utility files.
mode_rationale: File organization convention for styled components

rule: When roles are additive (e.g., projectContributor includes resultsConsumer), do not re-declare the same permissions on the parent role that are already granted by the inherited child role.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about role inheritance deduplication

rule: User-facing error messages should describe the issue and suggest actionable next steps in plain language, rather than referencing internal configuration keys or system internals.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about keeping error messages user-friendly

rule: Internal service dependencies should not be exposed as public vals on a class; instead, inject them directly into the class that actually uses them to preserve encapsulation.
mode_rationale: Encapsulation convention for dependency injection

rule: Keep retry/polling implementation details (e.g., backoff handlers) internal to helper functions rather than exposing them to callers, so the underlying retry library can be swapped without breaking consumers.
mode_rationale: Simple encapsulation guideline for retry logic

rule: Avoid adding new events or functionality to deprecated API versions; add new work to the current version only, even if the deprecated version has existing similar entries.
mode_rationale: Single convention about not adding features to deprecated API versions

rule: Never assume the default branch is called 'main'; use the API to fetch the repository's actual default branch.
mode_rationale: Single rule about always fetching the actual default branch

rule: Keep PRs focused by rolling back unrelated or accidental changes to files that are not part of the feature being implemented.
mode_rationale: PR hygiene rule: keep changes focused

rule: Do not infer the URL scheme (HTTP vs HTTPS) from the port number; instead, store and propagate the scheme explicitly in configuration.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about explicit URL scheme propagation

rule: Do not add classes, DTOs, or other code that has no current usages; introduce them in the PR where they are actually needed.
mode_rationale: Simple rule against adding code with no current usages

rule: If a React component prop is always passed by every caller, declare it as required rather than optional to accurately represent the component's contract.
When resolving user IDs or object IDs, log a warning or skip invalid values rather than silently falling through to send garbage data (e.g., emailing a raw ObjectId string).
mode_rationale: Error handling convention about invalid data

rule: Use either await or .then() consistently within a function; do not mix both async patterns as it creates confusion and potential double-promise issues.
mode_rationale: Simple style rule about async pattern consistency

rule: Pagination should be handled consistently either entirely on the backend or entirely on the frontend; hybrid approaches where some sort keys paginate server-side and others client-side create unpredictable behavior and should be avoided.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention: pagination must be handled in one layer

rule: Feature flags and configuration concerns should not be embedded in domain or data wrapper objects; keep them at the infrastructure or injection layer.
mode_rationale: Single separation-of-concerns convention

rule: When using styled-components, avoid combining them with CSS class selectors; convert elements to styled components to eliminate nested class-based styles.
mode_rationale: Single styling convention

rule: When writing tests, do not mock the component whose behavior you are trying to verify; mocking the expected answers means the test feeds itself correct results and provides no evidence the real code works.
mode_rationale: Single testing principle about what not to mock

rule: Preserve the existing correct import ordering when making changes; do not reorganize imports that were already correctly placed as part of an unrelated change.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about not reorganizing unrelated imports

rule: Avoid naming a function parameter the same as the enclosing function name, as it causes confusion and potential shadowing issues.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: avoid parameter/function name collision

rule: Keep status enums for durable lifecycle states (active, expired, revoked) and represent transient or computed conditions like 'expiring soon' as separate boolean fields.
mode_rationale: Single API design convention for status enums

rule: When writing new code, do not carry over lint ignores or suppressions that originated from bulk updates; fix the underlying issue instead.
mode_rationale: Single rule: fix lint issues in new code

rule: View and download events should not use fieldChanges or scalar change tracking, since the entity was not modified — only observed.
mode_rationale: Simple constraint: read-only events should not track field changes

rule: When a type extends a base interface, do not redundantly specify properties already defined in the parent type.
mode_rationale: Simple type inheritance rule

rule: Import from the directory path, not from the explicit index file — write `import X from './module'` not `import X from './module/index'`.
mode_rationale: Import style convention

rule: When refactoring or restructuring code, ensure existing tests are preserved or re-added to maintain coverage.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to preserve coverage during refactoring

rule: When removing code that behaves differently based on a feature flag, verify the behavior is correct in both the flag-on and flag-off states before removing the mapping.
mode_rationale: Single safety rule about feature flag removal

rule: Avoid rendering empty or unnecessary DOM elements (e.g., empty spans) when the underlying data is null or empty; conditionally render only when there is content to display.
mode_rationale: Single rule: conditionally render only when content exists

rule: Do not explicitly assign a property to its default value; it adds noise without changing behavior.
mode_rationale: Code cleanliness rule about avoiding noise from default assignments

rule: Do not catch and suppress errors behind a warning log without surfacing any indication to the user; provide degraded responses or error hints so failures are visible.
mode_rationale: Simple error-handling convention about error visibility

rule: When using immer-based reducers (e.g., Redux Toolkit or useImmerReducer), mutate the draft state directly instead of spreading it — spreading defeats immer's proxy mechanism and can cause subtle bugs.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention for correct immer draft mutation

rule: Do not disable or ignore accessibility lint rules; fix the underlying issue to maintain the accessibility score.
mode_rationale: Simple rule against disabling a11y lint rules

rule: Avoid using async linking patterns (e.g., token get/expire) when the work is synchronous — use simpler constructs like transaction naming instead.
mode_rationale: Simple coding convention against unnecessary async patterns

rule: Avoid using deprecated API keys (e.g., the `margins` key in styled components); use the current recommended approach instead.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to avoid deprecated API keys

rule: Avoid marking a value as lazy when it is used on the very next line; the deferred evaluation adds overhead with no benefit.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention about lazy evaluation

rule: Ensure docstrings accurately describe what the method returns; do not copy docstrings from similar methods without updating the return type description.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence documentation convention about keeping docstrings accurate

rule: Scaladoc and code comments must accurately describe the class or method semantics; do not add documentation that attributes behavior or constraints that are actually imposed by callers rather than the type itself.
mode_rationale: Simple convention for accurate documentation

rule: Public API documentation should describe how to use filter parameters to retrieve specific subsets of data, not just list the parameters.
mode_rationale: Single convention: describe how to use filter params, not just list them

rule: When code relies on a current authorization model (e.g., admin-only access), add a comment noting the assumption so future developers know what to revisit if permissions change.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation rule about noting auth assumptions

rule: When using batched MongoDB queries with $in, document the chosen batch size, the reasoning behind it, and the implicit BSON document size limits that constrain it.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for batch queries

rule: Add comments explaining behavioral invariants and design decisions (e.g., why a value is not updated once set) so future developers understand the intent.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention about commenting design decisions

rule: When using a Provider instead of direct injection to break a circular dependency, document the reason and file a tech debt ticket for the proper fix.
mode_rationale: Convention to document Provider usage and file tech debt tickets

rule: When authorization or access-control logic involves non-obvious rules (e.g., role-based exemptions, cross-organization restrictions), add inline comments explaining the business rationale and who is affected.
mode_rationale: Simple commenting convention for non-obvious auth rules

rule: API schema fields with conditional behavior (e.g., presence determines create vs. update) must include documentation explaining the semantics and when each value is expected.
mode_rationale: Single convention: document fields with conditional behavior

rule: When configuration files are written to user-visible locations (e.g., workspaces), document the reasoning behind non-obvious settings in external documentation rather than inline comments, and remove commented-out defaults.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for user-visible config files

rule: When introducing new abstractions or frameworks (e.g., custom query builders), document them with tutorials and examples in Confluence so the team can maintain them independently.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for new abstractions

rule: When introducing configurable timeouts or limits that affect user-facing behavior (e.g., post-run script execution windows), ensure public documentation exists so users understand the constraints.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention about user-facing constraints

rule: If console.log statements are intentionally left in production code, add a comment explaining why they are needed.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to comment intentional console.log statements

rule: When an API parameter is optional, the API description must specify the default behavior when the parameter is omitted.
mode_rationale: Single API documentation rule for optional parameters

rule: When commenting out code or disabling tests, include a reference to the tracking ticket (e.g., Jira link) explaining why the code is disabled and when it should be re-enabled.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for commented-out code

rule: When using undocumented or hard-to-find external API endpoints, add a comment explaining where the endpoint came from and where to find related documentation.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for undocumented endpoints

rule: When introducing novel operational features with potential failure modes, document known issues, configuration knobs, and troubleshooting steps in the runbook before shipping.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about documenting failure modes before shipping

rule: Add documentation that describes what a function does (its intent and purpose), not how it does it; the code already shows the how.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation style guideline

rule: When intentionally omitting dependencies from a React useEffect dependency array, leave a comment explaining why to prevent future developers from mistakenly adding them.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation rule: comment omitted useEffect dependencies

rule: When implementing soft limits with known race conditions (e.g., count-then-act without locks), document why the race is acceptable and reference stronger enforcement mechanisms (e.g., existing quota checkers or distributed locks) for cases where strict limits are required.
mode_rationale: Convention to document race conditions when they exist

rule: When code makes assumptions that will change (e.g., local storage always being ready), add a comment explaining the current assumption and what will change in the future.
mode_rationale: Single convention about commenting on temporary assumptions

rule: When implementing a pattern that must be updated when the code is extended (e.g., adding a new sortable column requires updating sort persistence logic), leave a comment explaining the maintenance requirement.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention for extensible code

rule: When implementing status update logic that depends on message ordering, document the delivery guarantees (synchronous RPC vs async streams) and explain why timestamps are or are not needed to prevent out-of-order processing.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for ordering assumptions

rule: Add docstrings to methods that return tuples or composite values where the meaning of each element is not self-evident; prefer adding named boolean fields to case classes over returning ad-hoc tuples.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for complex return types

rule: When using nested Option types like `Option[Option[T]]` to represent distinct states (no change vs. explicit unset vs. new value), add scaladoc or inline comments explaining the semantics of each case.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention for complex types

rule: When a variable is an alias for another concept (e.g., selectedRevision is actually activeRevision), add a comment explaining the relationship to prevent confusion.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention: comment variable aliases to prevent confusion

rule: When an API uses non-obvious parameter encodings (e.g., boolean true for ascending, false for descending, undefined for none), add an inline comment documenting the mapping so future readers do not have to guess.
mode_rationale: Single convention: comment non-obvious encodings

rule: When authorization is deliberately delegated to another layer (e.g., a proxy), add a code comment explaining the delegation and why the current layer does not perform the full check.
mode_rationale: Single convention: add a comment when delegating auth

rule: When deviating from the expected pattern (e.g., not reusing an existing client), add a docstring explaining the rationale so future readers understand why the seemingly redundant code exists.
mode_rationale: Single convention: add docstrings explaining deviations from expected patterns

rule: When code compares two values that seem equivalent on the surface (e.g., locationContext vs window.location), add a comment explaining why the comparison is necessary.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for subtle comparisons

rule: Add comments to explain non-standard patterns or conventions (e.g., sort direction encoding) that differ from the usual codebase patterns.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for non-standard patterns

rule: Add comments explaining non-obvious default values (e.g., `true`) so future readers understand the intent without digging into history.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for default values

rule: When code contains non-obvious defensive logic (e.g., forcefully killing a process on repeated stop requests), add a comment explaining why it is necessary.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention

rule: Add comments to non-obvious dependency injection module bindings so that readers can understand why a binding exists without tracing through the entire dependency graph.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: comment non-obvious DI bindings

rule: When the same authorization action is applied across multiple endpoints or methods, add a comment explaining why the duplication exists to help future developers understand the intent.
mode_rationale: Convention to comment duplicated auth logic

rule: Add a description comment to entity classes and data structures whose purpose is not immediately obvious from the name, especially integration-specific DTOs.
Add a comment explaining why a retry or reconnection loop exists, including the failure scenarios it handles (e.g. cursor closed, node failover).
mode_rationale: Documentation convention: explain why retry loops exist

rule: When a method returns different wrapper types (e.g. Some vs None) to signal different outcomes, add documentation explaining what each return value means.
mode_rationale: Single convention: document what different return values mean

rule: When a function's return value is not self-explanatory, either keep a clarifying comment or create a named type to make the return shape explicit.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for unclear return values

rule: When a design involves non-obvious resource allocation tradeoffs (e.g., volume sizing, memory splitting), document the behavior clearly for users.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation guideline for resource allocation decisions

rule: Document the observable behavior of notification and messaging abstractions: what medium they use (email, in-app popup, etc.), who receives them, and what triggers them.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention for notification abstractions

rule: In OpenAPI/Swagger specs, only document expected status codes that clients should handle; do not routinely document generic 500 errors unless existing endpoints already do so for consistency.
mode_rationale: Simple API documentation convention

rule: When adding or changing operational configuration (feature flags, cache TTLs, service parameters), document them in the relevant runbook so on-call engineers can find and adjust them.
mode_rationale: Convention to document operational config in runbooks

rule: Do not use unexplained magic numbers in code; when a reviewer has to ask 'why this number?', the value should be a named constant or accompanied by a comment explaining its derivation.
mode_rationale: Single readability convention for constants

rule: When code impersonates a user or uses elevated privileges, add an inline comment explaining why it is safe to do so and what authorization mechanism protects the call.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention to add inline comments for privilege escalation

rule: When an optional API field being absent causes destructive behavior (e.g., removing an existing value), document this explicitly in the API and add unit tests, because callers may assume absent fields are simply skipped.
mode_rationale: Single API documentation convention

rule: Add comments specifying the expected units (e.g., milliseconds, bytes, seconds) for numeric fields that represent time or measurements.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for numeric fields

rule: When introducing configurable timeouts that affect user-facing behavior, ensure public documentation exists so users know about the limits and how to adjust them.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation guideline about timeout visibility

rule: When implementing a workaround or non-obvious fix, add a comment explaining the background context, why the simpler approach was chosen, and any caveats for future maintainers.
mode_rationale: Simple commenting convention for workarounds

rule: Domain/interface modules must not reference API/web module types (e.g., DTOs). Mapping between domain and API models should live in the appropriate layer, and build tools like Bazel should enforce this boundary.
mode_rationale: Single architectural rule about dependency direction between layers

rule: Domain entities should not include JSON serialization mappings; coupling the domain to a specific serialized format constrains its evolution and creates backwards-compatibility concerns that belong in a separate mapping layer.
mode_rationale: Convention to keep serialization out of domain entities

rule: Create purpose-specific authorization roles for distinct use cases rather than repurposing existing roles with similar-but-different semantics, to avoid coupling and accidental permission drift.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence authorization design guideline

rule: When two code branches differ only in text or small values, use a conditional expression (ternary) to switch the differing parts rather than duplicating the entire block.
mode_rationale: Single DRY convention about using ternaries for differing values

rule: When multiple components or functions share the same data shape, define a shared interface or type alias rather than repeating the structure.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence DRY convention about type definitions

rule: New DynamicField field types must check whether the field is editable and only render interactive controls when editable; return null or a read-only view otherwise.
mode_rationale: Single constraint: DynamicField types must gate on editability

rule: Each class should use its own logger instance rather than accepting another class's logger as a parameter; log correlation should be done via shared context (e.g., DominoContext), not by sharing logger instances.
mode_rationale: Logger ownership convention

rule: Use early return (guard clause) patterns to exit functions early when preconditions are not met, instead of wrapping the entire function body in a conditional block.
mode_rationale: Single coding style preference for guard clauses

rule: When two methods return the same result, consolidate them into a single method to reduce confusion and maintenance burden.
mode_rationale: DRY principle for method consolidation

rule: When a method executes one query per item in a list (N+1 pattern), replace it with a batch query or join to avoid performance degradation as the list grows.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention about batching queries

rule: Do not maintain duplicate configuration keys that hold the same value; consolidate to a single canonical key used everywhere.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to avoid duplication

rule: Controllers should delegate to domain services for business logic like lookups and data assembly rather than implementing it directly.
mode_rationale: Single architectural boundary rule

rule: When fields are required for an operation but optional in the data model, declare them as non-optional in the operation's interface and validate their presence at the boundary, throwing if required fields are missing.
mode_rationale: Single type design convention for required fields

rule: Include units or type context in variable names (e.g., widthPx, timeoutMs) instead of relying on comments to convey that information.
mode_rationale: Naming convention: include units in variable names

rule: Error messages must accurately describe the specific operation that failed; do not copy-paste error messages between handlers without updating them to reflect the actual context.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: error text must describe the actual failed operation

rule: Error messages should accurately describe the failure condition — do not assume a specific cause (e.g., 'archived') when the check only verifies a broader condition (e.g., 'does not exist').
mode_rationale: Error handling convention for accurate error descriptions

rule: Ensure that exception types map to the correct HTTP status codes; IllegalArgumentException should produce a 400 (Bad Request), not a 500 (Internal Server Error).
mode_rationale: Simple rule about correct exception-to-status-code mapping

rule: Use getOrElse with an explicit error thrown for required values rather than silently returning None or a default, so missing data fails fast with a clear message.
mode_rationale: Fail-fast convention for required values

rule: Add explicit conditional guards before type-specific operations to make the code's intent clear and prevent unintended execution on wrong types.
mode_rationale: Defensive coding convention for type checks

rule: When a function should only handle specific types or cases, explicitly restrict and fail on unexpected inputs rather than silently returning nothing.
mode_rationale: Single defensive coding principle

rule: Export anything used outside a module through its index file so external consumers don't depend on internal module structure.
mode_rationale: Simple export convention for module boundaries

rule: When a type is used across module boundaries, export it from the shared package rather than re-defining or importing it from an internal path.
mode_rationale: Convention for cross-module type sharing

rule: Modal components should extend the base modal's prop interface (e.g., open, onClose) rather than redeclaring those props, reducing duplication and ensuring consistency.
mode_rationale: DRY convention for modal component prop interfaces

rule: When a trait or interface has multiple implementations, add a brief comment to each explaining its specific purpose and how it differs from the others.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation rule for interface implementations

rule: When .get on an Option is unavoidable, add a comment explaining why the value is guaranteed to exist at that point.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for forced unwraps

rule: Example manifests and sample configurations that use permissive security settings (e.g., wide-open NetworkPolicies) should include comments warning that production usage should be more targeted and restricted.
mode_rationale: Convention to comment on permissive security in examples

rule: When code relies on an assumption that may change in the future (e.g., only one active instance), add a comment documenting the assumption and what would need to change.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention for fragile assumptions

rule: When an API endpoint performs a soft delete, explicitly document that behavior in the API specification so consumers understand the data is not permanently removed.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation rule for soft-delete behavior

rule: When suppressing deprecation or compiler warnings in build files, document which specific deprecations are being silenced and why, so reviewers and future maintainers understand the trade-off.
Update the PR description with concrete testing evidence (e.g., YAML dumps, API responses) so reviewers and cross-team stakeholders can verify correctness.
mode_rationale: PR description convention for testing evidence

rule: When a parameter or code path exists for non-obvious reasons, write a detailed comment explaining the real-world scenario that necessitates it, not just what it does.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention about explaining real-world reasoning

rule: Every @ts-ignore or type suppression comment must include an explanation of what the type issue is and why the suppression is necessary.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence documentation convention for suppression comments

rule: When using undocumented or hard-to-find API endpoints, add a comment explaining where the endpoint was discovered and link to the closest available documentation.
mode_rationale: Single rule to comment on undocumented endpoint sources

rule: When a PR includes changes outside the scope of its Jira ticket, add a note in the PR description explaining what those changes do and why they are included.
mode_rationale: Simple PR hygiene convention

rule: When a data class has multiple optional fields where only certain combinations are valid, document the valid states explicitly via comments or a sealed trait hierarchy.
mode_rationale: Single documentation guideline for complex data classes

rule: TODOs must reference tracked tickets. Forward-looking assumptions must be documented inline. Interface-layer docs must not reference implementation details from other modules.
trigger: When adding TODO comments, writing docstrings on interface-layer classes, or making assumptions about current system behavior that may change
rationale: Untracked TODOs are never resolved. Undocumented assumptions become invisible tech debt. Cross-module implementation details in interface docs create coupling and break when internals change.
mode_rationale: Convention about TODO format and doc boundaries, not a workflow

rule: Domain events and UI-facing operations should use the application's canonical user ID, not IDP-specific identifiers (e.g., Keycloak IDs), to avoid leaking infrastructure details into the domain layer.
mode_rationale: Single convention to use canonical user IDs, not IDP-specific ones

rule: Use domain-level identifiers (e.g., DominoId) in service interfaces and keep persistence-specific details (e.g., MongoDB ObjectId) confined to the persistence implementation layer.
mode_rationale: Single architecture rule: use domain IDs in service interfaces

rule: Keep domain logic (validation, business rules, entity construction) in the domain layer, not in infrastructure or service implementation layers.
mode_rationale: Layered architecture convention: domain logic stays in domain layer

rule: Name domain model fields to reflect business concepts rather than implementation details, especially for fields that may surface in APIs.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for domain model fields

rule: Domain entities and business logic should not depend on web framework constructs (e.g., Play Forms, HTTP-specific libraries); isolate framework dependencies to the presentation or validation layer to avoid coupling the domain to a specific web technology.
mode_rationale: Layering rule about framework independence

rule: Use domain-specific prefixes for identifiers that could be ambiguous across contexts (e.g., prefer `dfsCommitId` over generic `commitId` when referring to the Domino File System commit).
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for ambiguous identifiers

rule: Do not include code (classes, files, endpoints) that has no current usages; add it in the PR where it is actually needed.
mode_rationale: Simple YAGNI principle about not adding unused code

rule: Reusable components must not depend on context values (e.g., currentProject) that may not exist in all usage contexts; pass required data explicitly via props instead.
mode_rationale: Single convention: pass data via props, don't depend on context in reusable components

rule: Avoid relying on closure values that may change between the time a hook captures them and the time they are used; prefer passing stable callback references instead.
mode_rationale: Single convention: prefer stable callback references over mutable closures in hooks

rule: When using elevated privileges (e.g., service principal) to check for blocking conditions, do not expose names or details of resources the user lacks permission to see in user-facing error messages.
mode_rationale: Single security rule about error message content

rule: Instead of commenting out code, create a new version of the variable or logic with the desired behavior and use it directly.
mode_rationale: Code cleanliness convention against commented-out code

rule: Before adding implicit formats, serializers, or other boilerplate to a class, check if the parent trait or class already provides them — avoid redundant declarations.
mode_rationale: Simple inheritance hygiene rule

rule: Only use Promise.all when resolving multiple concurrent promises; wrapping a single promise in Promise.all adds unnecessary complexity.
mode_rationale: Single code simplicity rule

rule: Wrapper or data classes should not extend RuntimeException unless they genuinely represent throwable errors.
mode_rationale: Single inheritance guideline for exception classes

rule: Do not mock table column definitions when testing table components, as this reduces the test to verifying a generic table rather than the actual component behavior; only mock specific components that cause test issues.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about what to mock

rule: Do not mark route parameters as required if the controller already handles the missing/empty case with a default value.
mode_rationale: Single API design rule about parameter optionality

rule: Do not allow a code path that enables functionality which has not been tested by QE; if a feature is not supported in MVP, the code should not permit it.
mode_rationale: Simple gate rule: no code paths for untested functionality

rule: Do not skip or comment out tests without documenting the reason and creating a Jira ticket to track re-enabling them.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: no skipped tests without reason and ticket

rule: Always double-quote shell variable expansions to prevent word splitting and glob expansion bugs.
mode_rationale: Single rule about quoting variable expansions

rule: Extract shared query filter clauses (e.g., isArchived checks) into reusable variables and compose them with query-specific criteria to avoid duplicating filter logic across methods.
mode_rationale: Single DRY principle for query filter reuse

rule: When the same permission or edit-eligibility check is repeated across components, extract it into a named helper function (e.g., `canEdit(row)`) to reduce duplication and improve readability.
mode_rationale: Single convention: extract repeated permission checks into named helpers

rule: When the same style override appears in multiple views, extract it into the shared component that owns the styling rather than duplicating it across consumers.
mode_rationale: Simple DRY principle for style overrides

rule: Implement a dry-run mode for destructive operations (cleanup jobs, bulk deletions) so operators can preview the impact before executing.
mode_rationale: Single design guideline for destructive operations

rule: When test code contains highly duplicated setup or assertion blocks, refactor them into shared helper methods to reduce noise and improve maintainability.
mode_rationale: Simple test code organization guideline

rule: Extract repeated logic within the same component into a shared helper or variable to reduce duplication and improve maintainability.
mode_rationale: Single DRY principle for component-level duplication

rule: Name data transfer objects to reflect their architectural layer and purpose; avoid calling objects 'DTO' if they are internal service-layer types rather than API request/response objects.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for data transfer objects

rule: Use ES module dynamic `import()` instead of CommonJS `require()` for conditional or lazy-loaded modules.
mode_rationale: Simple module system preference for ES imports

rule: Make data-testid attributes and accessibility labels dynamic to reflect component state (e.g., expanded vs collapsed) so tests and assistive tools can distinguish between states.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence accessibility/testing convention

rule: In E2E tests, navigate to a new URL within the existing browser session instead of opening a new browser window, unless the test specifically requires a fresh session.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about browser session reuse

rule: Within a single test scenario, use a consistent interaction approach (UI or API) rather than mixing both without justification.
mode_rationale: Simple testing consistency convention

rule: In E2E tests, add an explicit wait or assertion step (e.g., 'I wait to see' or 'I wait to click') before interacting with UI elements to avoid race conditions from asynchronous rendering.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention to wait before interacting

rule: When a component fetches data on mount, initialize the loading state to true immediately (or before the first render) to prevent a brief flash of the error or empty state before data arrives.
mode_rationale: UI convention: initialize loading=true to prevent flash of error state

rule: Use eager singleton binding (.asEagerSingleton()) for dependency injection modules to ensure only one instance is created.
mode_rationale: Single DI configuration convention

rule: Do not accept parameters that can be derived from other parameters; compute them inside the method to prevent caller misuse.
mode_rationale: Single convention: compute derivable values inside the method

rule: Before creating a new class, check if an identical or equivalent class already exists in another module and reuse or consolidate it to avoid duplication.
mode_rationale: DRY principle applied to cross-module class deduplication

rule: When a method duplicates the logic of an existing method with only minor argument wrapping, consolidate callers to use the canonical method instead of maintaining both.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to consolidate duplicate methods

rule: Avoid redundant operations on Option types such as `.getOrElse(None)` which is a no-op on Option values.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about removing no-op Option operations

rule: When a domain concept is renamed, remove all variations of the old term from the codebase to prevent confusion between the old and new mental models.
mode_rationale: Single convention: remove all old term variations after a rename

rule: When a single method already handles all cases correctly, do not add conditional branching that duplicates or wraps it unnecessarily.
mode_rationale: Code simplicity convention

rule: When a service already returns the needed type, use it directly rather than adding unnecessary conversion methods.
mode_rationale: Simplicity convention: don't add conversion methods when the type already matches

rule: Do not create empty containers that are immediately passed to a function for population; instead, capture the function's return value directly in the variable.
mode_rationale: Code style rule to avoid redundant temporaries

rule: Components should inject their own known values into callbacks rather than requiring parent components to pass curried functions.
mode_rationale: Single component design convention

rule: Use a route switch to encapsulate conditional page or view rendering rather than inline conditional logic in the component body.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture convention: use route switch instead of inline conditionals

rule: Push format conversion (e.g., toString, toHexString) into the domain type's case class so callers use named fields instead of scattering conversion logic across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Convention to push conversions into case classes

rule: Sensible default styles (like removing borders) should be built into the component itself rather than requiring every consumer to override them.
mode_rationale: Single component design principle

rule: When adding functionality to an existing component that doesn't align with its primary purpose, extract the new functionality into its own dedicated component to preserve single responsibility.
mode_rationale: Single convention: extract unrelated functionality into dedicated components

rule: Keep domain-specific code (e.g., governance logic) encapsulated within its own module rather than spreading it across unrelated sections of the codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization principle

rule: Keep domain-specific logic (e.g., secret-to-env-var conversion) encapsulated inside the plugin rather than leaking it to the orchestrator, so callers don't need to know internal details.
mode_rationale: Encapsulation convention to keep domain logic inside the plugin

rule: When a class already owns 'handles this thing' logic, new filtering conditions should be added to that class via a consistent interface (e.g., a handlesEvent method) rather than spreading the logic across callers.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence design principle about encapsulation

rule: Do not add more logic to already-large components; encapsulate new functionality within its own component or a dedicated hook to keep parent components manageable.

When a hook exposes state that depends on a configuration value (like 'required'), pass that configuration into the hook and encapsulate the derived logic there, rather than spreading it across consumers.
mode_rationale: Simple encapsulation guideline for hook logic

rule: When multiple operations are always performed together (e.g., fetch data + set state), encapsulate them into a single method to reduce coupling and improve readability.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention to group related operations

rule: When layout logic around a component is duplicated at multiple call sites, move that logic into the component itself so callers only pass dynamic content as children.
mode_rationale: Single convention: move duplicated layout logic into the component itself

rule: If a function is responsible for one side of a state transition (e.g., turning something off), it should also handle the symmetric side (turning it on) to keep related logic co-located.
mode_rationale: Code organization guideline to co-locate symmetric logic

rule: When a collection is filtered by the requesting user's permissions, encode that in the variable or field name (e.g., `accessibleHardwareTiers` instead of just `hardwareTiers`).
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention encoding permission context

rule: When a type belongs to one or more categories, define that relationship directly on the type rather than validating compatibility externally.
mode_rationale: Single architectural guideline about type-category relationships

rule: Always URL-encode user-controlled or dynamic URI components in redirect paths and auth callbacks to prevent injection and malformed URLs.
mode_rationale: Simple security rule to URL-encode dynamic URI components

rule: Always call throwIfNotAuthorized() (or the equivalent enforcement method) after performing a permission check to ensure authorization is actually enforced, not just evaluated.
mode_rationale: Single security rule: always call the enforcement method

rule: Every new endpoint that accesses a protected resource must include an explicit authorization/permission check, consistent with similar existing endpoints.
mode_rationale: Security convention applied passively to every new endpoint

rule: Authorization checks must be enforced on the server side; relying solely on UI restrictions (e.g., limiting dropdown options) leaves the API vulnerable to direct calls that bypass the UI.
mode_rationale: Single convention: always enforce auth on the server side

rule: Ensure IDE autoformatting is enabled so import statements follow the project's style conventions (e.g., single quotes, consistent spacing).
mode_rationale: Single convention: enable IDE autoformatting for import style consistency

rule: User-supplied secrets and tokens should be validated against a minimum entropy threshold to prevent trivially guessable values.
mode_rationale: Single validation rule for user-supplied secrets

rule: When different entity types share an implementation (e.g., service accounts implemented as users), ensure API endpoints enforce the correct permission model for each entity type.
mode_rationale: Single convention: enforce correct permissions per entity type

rule: Verify that callback functions passed as props or used as effect dependencies are memoized (e.g., with useCallback) to prevent unnecessary re-renders.
mode_rationale: Performance convention about memoizing callbacks

rule: When adding a new provider, verify it handles the same edge cases (e.g., missing branches) as existing providers to maintain consistent behavior.
mode_rationale: Single architectural guideline about provider consistency

rule: When implementing a feature that operates on project files, verify it works across all supported project types (e.g., DFS and GBP) rather than only the default type.
mode_rationale: Single principle: verify features across all supported project types
