rule: When setting initial/default values for async state (e.g., React state or query results), ensure the initial shape matches what the resolver actually returns to avoid runtime type mismatches.
mode_rationale: Single type consistency rule for async state

rule: Verify that status codes declared in the OpenAPI spec match what the server actually returns at runtime, especially when code generators may silently override spec values.
mode_rationale: Single convention: verify spec status codes match actual server responses

rule: Ensure all resources (RevWalk, TreeWalk, streams) are properly disposed even within Using blocks, since close() and dispose() may have different semantics.
mode_rationale: Single rule: ensure resources are properly disposed

rule: When adding new configuration defaults that relate to existing settings, verify the defaults are logically consistent (e.g., a max-active-runs limit should be at least as large as the max-items limit it relates to).
mode_rationale: Simple consistency check for related config defaults

rule: When setting explicit column widths in Ant Design tables, verify the total width does not exceed common viewport sizes; leave at least one column without a fixed width to absorb remaining space.
mode_rationale: Single layout guideline for Ant Design tables

rule: Do not throw exceptions in callbacks or handlers unless the caller explicitly catches them; uncaught throws in modal onOk or similar props silently fail or cause runtime errors.
mode_rationale: Single error-handling convention about throw/catch pairing

rule: Always verify that useEffect dependency arrays are correctly placed inside the hook call — dependencies placed outside the function body create malformed effects.
mode_rationale: React correctness rule for useEffect dependency placement

rule: When naming entity types in events or audit trails, be specific enough to distinguish the granularity level (e.g., a single setting vs. an entire config page).
mode_rationale: Naming convention about specificity in entity type names

rule: Include a correlation ID in error responses so operators can match the user-facing error to the corresponding entry in server logs.
mode_rationale: Simple convention for error responses

rule: Error log messages must include relevant entity IDs and the name of the operation that failed for effective debugging.
mode_rationale: Single logging content requirement

rule: Error messages should clearly describe what went wrong using complete, human-readable sentences rather than terse or ambiguous phrasing.
mode_rationale: Single convention about error message readability

rule: When removing or changing validation guards, ensure that unsupported operation paths still produce helpful error messages rather than silently passing invalid requests to downstream services.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about maintaining error messages

rule: When an operation can fail for multiple similar reasons, the error message should specify which state caused the failure rather than being vague.
mode_rationale: Single guideline: make error messages specific about which state caused the failure

rule: User-facing error and validation messages should start with a capitalized word.
mode_rationale: Simple formatting convention for error messages

rule: When catching or suppressing errors, explicitly consider whether the failure affects the user's workflow and provide appropriate feedback if it does.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline: provide user feedback when suppressed errors affect workflow

rule: When an error occurs in navigation or critical UI paths, provide a meaningful recovery experience (e.g. redirect to an error page) rather than just rendering an inline error message.
mode_rationale: UX convention: provide recovery paths on errors

rule: Do not add new ESLint rules as 'error' severity without a migration plan — it forces developers to use --no-verify or add excessive exceptions.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about ESLint rule severity levels

rule: Before broadly adopting react-query, establish a centralized pattern for defining query keys that accounts for varying parameters, provider placement across Scala and React pages, and caching scope.
mode_rationale: Multi-step planning procedure for react-query adoption involving key strategy, provider placement, and caching scope decisions

rule: Before adding a new audit event, consider how frequently the triggering action occurs (e.g., every file preview click) and assess whether the logging volume is acceptable for the system.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence reminder to consider logging volume before adding audit events

rule: When renaming fields in API response models, assess backward and forward compatibility risks — especially in deployments with multiple frontends that may temporarily run different versions.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline: assess compatibility risks when renaming API fields

rule: When introducing random delays or jitter in loops that process many items, calculate the aggregate worst-case time to ensure it remains acceptable at production scale.
mode_rationale: Single-check guideline to calculate aggregate worst-case time

rule: When creating similar page variants, explicitly evaluate whether duplicated components or a single component with conditionals is the better trade-off before proceeding.
mode_rationale: Requires explicit evaluation/decision process before proceeding with implementation

rule: When exposing internal service endpoints (hosts, ports) to user-accessible environments like workspaces, explicitly evaluate and document the security implications before proceeding.
mode_rationale: Requires explicit evaluation and documentation steps before exposing endpoints

rule: Add event listeners inside useEffect hooks and return a cleanup function to remove them on unmount; never add listeners directly in the render body of a function component.
mode_rationale: Single React lifecycle convention

rule: In audit/telemetry events, the target should be the entity that receives the verb's action (e.g., the file being downloaded), not the container or context (e.g., the repo).
mode_rationale: Simple modeling rule for audit event targets

rule: Name the exception parameter 'cause' (not 'ex') and pass it to the parent Exception constructor so the JVM exception chain is properly linked.
mode_rationale: Exception parameter naming convention

rule: When wrapping exceptions across SDK versions, define proper custom exceptions that set message and cause on the parent Throwable; never throw wrapper objects that lose the original stack trace.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about preserving exception cause chains

rule: Telemetry events should contain only data useful for analysis — exclude descriptions, large lists, and other fields that won't be queried or aggregated.
mode_rationale: Simple filtering rule for telemetry event payloads

rule: Review your PR diff to ensure no unintended files (especially those that should be gitignored) are included; discard changes to files unrelated to your feature.
mode_rationale: Simple hygiene rule about reviewing PR diffs for unintended files

rule: Do not include code in a PR that cannot be validated without the context of a future PR; defer it so reviewers can assess correctness with full context.
mode_rationale: PR hygiene rule about reviewable scope

rule: When adding new query parameters or feature inputs, update related existing test specs to exercise the new parameters rather than only testing them in isolation.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention to update existing test coverage

rule: Always include a fallback/wildcard case in pattern match expressions that throws an IllegalArgumentException or similar, so unrecognized values fail fast with a clear error instead of silently falling through.
mode_rationale: Simple coding convention to always include a fallback case

rule: Do not add new exceptions to the useEffect dependency lint rule; always include all dependencies, especially for simple value types like booleans.
mode_rationale: Simple React lint rule convention

rule: When using acronyms in code identifiers (class names, config keys, variables), add a comment explaining what the acronym stands for.
mode_rationale: Single convention: explain acronyms in comments

rule: Do not include unrelated changes to auto-generated files in a PR; if unavoidable, explain why the changes appeared.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about explaining generated file changes in PRs

rule: Disabled UI elements should display a message explaining why they are disabled and what the user can do to resolve the situation, enabling self-service without contacting support.
mode_rationale: Single UX convention for disabled elements

rule: In switch statements or if-else chains, explicitly match all known cases rather than using a meaningful value as the default/fallback; return the raw value or throw for truly unknown cases to avoid silently masking unexpected inputs.
mode_rationale: Single convention: explicitly match all known cases instead of using meaningful defaults

rule: Always define theme keys in both light and dark mode configurations, even when the values are identical, to ensure flexibility for future design changes.
mode_rationale: Single rule about always defining both light and dark theme keys

rule: When a method returns Option/None, explicitly handle the absent case — decide whether to throw an exception, return a default, or propagate the Option.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about handling absent values

rule: Avoid default parameter values in method signatures; prefer explicit arguments at call sites to make behavior clear and prevent hidden bugs.
mode_rationale: Simple coding convention about avoiding default parameter values

rule: Long-lived objects such as actors or background workers must have an explicit cleanup or shutdown mechanism to prevent memory leaks, rather than relying on implicit garbage collection.
mode_rationale: Simple best practice for long-lived objects

rule: Always set explicit timeout values (connection, socket, etc.) and configure retries for external HTTP clients to prevent requests from hanging indefinitely.
mode_rationale: Single configuration rule for HTTP clients

rule: Do not assume a value's type by elimination (e.g., 'if not string then object'); use explicit type guards or discriminated unions.
mode_rationale: Single convention about type narrowing approach

rule: Use explicit domain types (e.g., Seq[DominoId]) for collections of identifiers rather than leaving them untyped or using generic types.
mode_rationale: Single typing convention for ID collections

rule: In E2E tests, use explicit wait-then-act steps (e.g., wait to click) after any action that changes the visible page content, rather than immediately interacting with elements that may not yet be rendered.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about wait-then-act patterns

rule: Export error message strings as named constants rather than inlining them, enabling reuse and consistent error messaging across modules.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to export error messages as named constants

rule: Instead of exporting raw base URL paths for API endpoints, create and export specific named functions for each endpoint (e.g., `getArtifactUrnEndpointUrl`).
mode_rationale: API design convention for endpoint exports

rule: When a string literal is used in production code and also asserted in a unit test, export it as a named constant so both share a single source of truth.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to share string literals between production code and tests

rule: Ensure APIs intended for external or customer use are exposed on the public API surface, not accidentally placed behind private/internal-only routes.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence API routing convention

rule: Global authorization and shared state hooks should be provided through the centralized `useStore` rather than consumed as standalone hooks in individual components.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture convention for shared state access

rule: Custom hooks that perform async operations should expose `loading` and `hasLoaded` flags so consumers can render appropriate loading states.
mode_rationale: API design convention for async hooks

rule: Component prop types should extend or pick from the base library (Ant Design) types rather than defining loose, unconstrained prop interfaces.

When adding a new API endpoint call, extend or refactor an existing client class with compatible responsibilities rather than creating a new class responsible for a single endpoint; if reuse is not feasible, document why in a docstring.
mode_rationale: Single convention: extend existing clients rather than creating new single-endpoint ones

rule: Before creating a new UI component implementation, check if an existing one can be extended with the needed behavior to avoid maintaining multiple near-identical components.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to check for extensible existing components before creating new ones

rule: When adding new parameters to a service method, extend the existing method with default-valued parameters instead of creating a new method alongside it, to avoid API clutter and confusion for future developers.
mode_rationale: API design convention to avoid method proliferation

rule: When adding retry/wait logic to tests, extend the existing helper function rather than creating a new wrapper; tests that don't need retries can call the underlying API directly.
mode_rationale: Single convention about extending vs duplicating test helpers

rule: When you need a variant of an existing utility (e.g., a different ID type), extend the existing utility rather than creating a duplicate with a hardcoded copy of the same value.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention to extend rather than duplicate utilities

rule: When building a URL or configuration, use and extend existing utility functions rather than appending parameters inline in a consuming component.
mode_rationale: Single code reuse guideline

rule: When creating wrapper components for HTML elements, extend the native HTML prop types (e.g., React.DetailedHTMLProps<React.IframeHTMLAttributes<HTMLIFrameElement>, HTMLIFrameElement>) instead of manually listing individual props.
mode_rationale: Single typing guideline for wrapper components

rule: Order import statements with external/library imports first, followed by relative/local imports, separated by a blank line.
mode_rationale: Single convention: external imports first, then relative imports

rule: Do not hardcode values that may need to change; extract them into configuration settings so they can be adjusted without code changes.
mode_rationale: Single configuration convention

rule: Externalize polling intervals and timeout values as central configuration flags rather than hardcoding them, so they can be tuned without code changes.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence configuration convention for tunable values

rule: Do not hardcode navigation paths inside UI components; define routes in a centralized location and import them.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to centralize route definitions

rule: Action-oriented UI components (e.g., row actions, toolbar actions) should be defined in their own files because they tend to accumulate logic over time.
mode_rationale: Simple file organization guideline

rule: Do not mix analytics or tracking logic directly inside UI components; extract it into a dedicated hook or utility function to avoid duplication and keep components focused on rendering.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence separation-of-concerns guideline for UI components

rule: When a React component makes multiple API calls that control rendering, extract the data-fetching and state logic into a custom hook to keep the component focused on presentation and simplify testing.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about where to place data-fetching logic

rule: Move calculations and business logic out of React components into standalone utility functions that can be unit tested independently.
mode_rationale: Single principle about separating logic from components

rule: Move non-trivial inline calculations in components to a utility function to keep component code focused on rendering.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization convention for inline calculations

rule: Define semantic color constants (e.g., neutral, warning, success) instead of using inline hex or color values throughout component code.
mode_rationale: Single convention: define semantic color constants instead of inline hex values

rule: When multiple match/case branches share the same structure, extract the differing parts into a tuple or data object and handle the common logic once to reduce duplication.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention: deduplicate shared logic across match branches

rule: When multiple tests share the same assertion logic, extract a common assertion function and have each test call it with its specific parameters.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: DRY up repeated assertion logic into shared helpers

rule: When multiple tests perform similar verification, extract the shared logic into a common assertion function and have each test call it with its specific parameters, reducing duplication and improving maintainability.
mode_rationale: Testing best practice about reducing duplication via shared helpers

rule: When multiple test scenarios share identical setup steps, extract those steps into a shared fixture or Background block to reduce duplication and simplify maintenance.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about extracting shared setup, not a multi-step procedure

rule: Move inline boolean expressions into descriptively named variables to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention

rule: Extract complex boolean expressions into well-named intermediate variables or use pattern matching to make conditional logic self-documenting and easier to verify.
mode_rationale: Simple code clarity convention for complex conditionals

rule: When a component accumulates multiple derived calculations, extract them into a utility function or module to keep the component focused on rendering.
mode_rationale: Code organization: keep components focused on rendering

rule: Extract complex string interpolations or expressions into separate named variables to improve readability, especially when a similar pattern is already used nearby.
Move complex inline expressions into named utility functions or variables to improve readability and testability.
mode_rationale: Readability convention for inline complexity

rule: When a React component's render method grows complex, extract logical sections into dedicated sub-components for readability and reusability.
mode_rationale: Simple refactoring convention

rule: When a React component's render method grows complex, extract logically distinct sections into dedicated sub-components for better readability and maintainability.
mode_rationale: Standard refactoring guideline for React components

rule: Extract complex inline JSX expressions or conditional rendering blocks into named variables (e.g., `mainContent`) to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention for complex JSX

rule: When a React component contains complex logic (e.g., theme setup, side effects), extract it into a separate custom hook to improve readability and reusability.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization convention for extracting hooks

rule: When inline logic chains are hard to follow, extract them into a named private method to improve readability, even if the logic is only used once.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention about extracting inline logic

rule: When implementing multi-step business logic, refactor the code so that each documented rule maps to a clearly named method, making the code read like the requirements.
mode_rationale: Single refactoring convention to name methods after business rules

rule: When inline logic becomes hard to follow, extract it into a well-named method with scaladoc or doc comments explaining the business logic.
mode_rationale: Simple readability guideline about extracting complex logic

rule: Extract complex mapping or transformation logic out of form submit handlers into separate utility functions to keep the handler readable.
mode_rationale: Single refactoring convention for handler readability

rule: Abstract larger matching filters and complex query-building logic into named helper functions to improve readability and maintainability.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization rule about query abstraction

rule: When a JSX conditional involves multiple chained expressions, extract it into a descriptively named boolean variable defined before the return statement.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence readability convention for JSX conditionals

rule: When a JSX conditional involves multiple chained expressions, extract them into a descriptively named boolean variable defined before the return statement to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention for JSX conditionals

rule: When a render function or column renderer grows complex, extract it into its own standalone component rather than keeping it in a utility file.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline about extracting complex render logic

rule: When a column renderer or inline render function grows complex, extract it into its own component rather than keeping it in a utility file.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention for render complexity

rule: When a column renderer or inline JSX block grows complex, extract it into its own component rather than leaving it as a function in a utility file.
mode_rationale: Single code organization convention

rule: Complex rendering logic should not be defined as ad-hoc functions inside table column definitions or parent components; extract it into its own component for maintainability and testability.
mode_rationale: Single principle about component extraction for complex render logic

rule: Extract complex UI sub-sections (e.g. an inline editable table) into their own components, and define helper functions outside the component body to avoid unnecessary re-creation on every render.
mode_rationale: Component decomposition guideline

rule: Extract complex channel or subscription initialization (e.g., basicConsume) into a separate dedicated method rather than inlining it in the subscription setup.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention to extract complex logic into dedicated methods

rule: Move complex or hard-to-read ternary expressions into a named variable or a separate function to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Simple readability guideline for ternary expressions

rule: When a React component contains self-contained logic (e.g., side effects, state management), extract it into a separate custom hook for better separation of concerns and reusability.
mode_rationale: Single convention: extract self-contained logic into custom hooks

rule: Extract complex UI logic (e.g., floating controls with action handlers) into dedicated components using composition, keeping base implementations reusable and testable.
mode_rationale: Convention about component extraction for reusability

rule: Proactively extract sub-components (e.g., tab contents) into their own files before they grow too large, even if they are small initially.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization convention about component file extraction

rule: Split non-trivial sub-components into their own files rather than defining multiple components in a single file.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention about file structure

rule: When conditional branches only differ in text content, assign the text values to variables conditionally and write the JSX once instead of duplicating the entire block.
mode_rationale: Single DRY convention about extracting conditional text from duplicated JSX

rule: Move constant values outside of component functions and name them using UPPER_SNAKE_CASE (e.g., MAX_RETRY_COUNT).

rule: When the same literal value (e.g., a template string, magic number, or configuration key) appears in multiple places, extract it into a shared constant to ensure consistency and ease of maintenance.

rule: Move hardcoded constant values into companion objects, and reference existing shared constants from other modules rather than redefining them locally.
mode_rationale: Single rule: centralize constants in companion objects

rule: Drive hardcoded values (URLs, endpoints, magic strings) from constants or a config file rather than embedding them inline.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to avoid hardcoded values

rule: Significant constants should live in dedicated parameter or config files, not inline in generator or builder classes.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization rule about constant placement

rule: Default values and initial state objects should be extracted to named constants or a shared constants file rather than inlined in components.
mode_rationale: Single convention: extract default values to named constants

rule: Extract hardcoded default values and initial state into named constants or a shared initial-state definition to improve readability and reuse.

rule: When a section of JSX represents a distinct, self-contained UI block, extract it into its own component for clarity and testability.
mode_rationale: Simple component extraction guideline

rule: When the same logic appears in multiple methods, extract it into a shared helper function to reduce duplication.
mode_rationale: Single DRY convention about extracting shared logic

rule: When filtering, sorting, and pagination logic is duplicated across modules, extract it into a shared commons library and have modules extend it rather than re-implementing it.
mode_rationale: Simple DRY convention for shared logic

rule: When the same logic appears in multiple components, extract it into a shared custom hook to eliminate duplication and centralize behavior.
mode_rationale: Single DRY principle about shared custom hooks

rule: When the same logic appears in multiple React components, extract it into a shared custom hook to eliminate duplication and centralize maintenance.
mode_rationale: Single convention: extract shared logic into custom hooks

rule: When a code block is repeated across multiple call sites, extract it into a shared utility function to reduce duplication and improve maintainability.
mode_rationale: DRY convention: shared utility for repeated code blocks

rule: When the same logic is repeated across multiple components, extract it into a shared custom hook.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence DRY convention about custom hooks

rule: When the same logic appears in multiple places with only minor parameter differences, extract it into a shared utility function.
mode_rationale: Simple DRY convention

rule: When the same logic appears in multiple files, extract it into a shared utility or hook to avoid duplication.
mode_rationale: Single DRY principle for duplicated logic

rule: When the same logic appears in multiple places, extract it into a shared utility function and reuse it.
mode_rationale: Standard DRY convention

rule: When catching errors, extract and surface the actual error message rather than swallowing it or showing a generic message.
mode_rationale: Simple error handling convention about surfacing actual error messages

rule: Non-trivial filter/transform functions used inside components should be extracted into separate utility modules for reuse and testability.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention: extract filter logic for reuse

rule: When a form renderer uses a switch/case or conditional to render different input types, extract each form control type into its own component to keep the renderer manageable as new input types are added.
Move helper functions out of component files into dedicated util files to separate concerns and keep components focused on rendering.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention to separate helpers from components

rule: Move reusable helper and utility functions out of component files and into dedicated utility files to improve discoverability and reuse.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: move helpers to utility files

rule: Place helper functions and utilities in a dedicated utils file rather than co-locating them in the component file.

rule: Obtain entity IDs from API responses rather than parsing them out of URLs.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to get IDs from API responses not URL parsing

rule: Extract entity IDs from API response payloads rather than parsing them out of URLs, which is fragile and couples tests to URL structure.

rule: When test steps need entity IDs, extract them from API response bodies rather than parsing them out of URLs, which is fragile and couples tests to URL structure.

rule: Extract inline event handler functions into named functions wrapped with useCallback for improved readability and to avoid unnecessary re-renders.

rule: When a JSX block or expression grows complex, extract it into a descriptively named variable (e.g., `mainContent`) to improve readability and make the render tree scannable.

rule: Move complex inline expressions out of JSX and into named variables or functions defined before the return statement for readability.

rule: Extract complex inline logic into named functions to improve readability and reusability.
Large render blocks and complex JSX fragments within utility files should be extracted into their own separate components.

rule: Magic literals (strings, URLs, file paths) embedded in the middle of functions should be extracted to a config file and referenced from there.

rule: Magic values and literals embedded in the middle of functions should be moved to configuration files and referenced from there.

rule: When modifying an API or data model, take the opportunity to fix misleading names in the same area rather than perpetuating them further.

rule: Fix the root cause of TypeScript errors (e.g., make a prop required) rather than replacing one suppression mechanism with another (e.g., swapping @ts-ignore for a default value).
mode_rationale: Single guideline about fixing errors properly

rule: When fixing a styling issue that affects multiple instances of a component, apply the fix in the shared component definition rather than patching individual usages.
mode_rationale: Single rule: fix shared styling in the component definition, not per-usage

rule: When a code change broadens the scope of an operation (e.g., from template repos only to all repos), explicitly note the behavioral change and assess potential unintended consequences.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence review convention about scope changes

rule: When cherry-picking or backporting changes, call out any diffs that are not directly related to the stated PR objective so reviewers can confirm they are intentional.
mode_rationale: Simple PR hygiene convention

rule: Prefer separate, flat styled components over nesting styles within a single styled component when you control the child elements.
mode_rationale: Single style rule: prefer flat styled components over nested styles

rule: When storing polymorphic domain entities, put commonly-used fields for filtering and sorting at the top level, and nest type-specific definitions in dedicated sub-fields.
mode_rationale: Data modeling convention for polymorphic entities

rule: When business logic operates on mixed entity types (e.g. users and orgs) but downstream processing only needs one type, flatten to the common type (e.g. resolve orgs to users) as early as possible to simplify all downstream logic.
mode_rationale: Single principle: resolve mixed types to common type early

rule: Avoid deeply nested case/match expressions; refactor into smaller methods, for-comprehensions, or combinators to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Simple readability guideline about nesting depth

rule: Use flexible version constraints (>=) in requirements files rather than compatible release (~=) to avoid breakage when other dependencies or vulnerability fixes require newer versions.
mode_rationale: Single dependency versioning convention

rule: Action codes must follow the established naming convention of the codebase (e.g., 'project.view_goals') — match the pattern used by existing actions for consistency.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming convention for action codes

rule: Use parameter names that match the established API standard (e.g., Domino API Standard) rather than inventing new conventions, to maintain consistency across services.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to follow established API naming standards

rule: Use the codebase's established naming convention for optional values (e.g., `maybeFoo` prefix) rather than inventing new abbreviations like `Opt` or `optional`.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for optional value prefixes

rule: When adding permission checks, follow the existing authorization patterns in similar methods rather than inventing new approaches; consistency reduces review burden and prevents subtle security gaps.
Authorizer methods should follow the established interface pattern: expose specific `canX` methods that return an AuthorizationResult, keeping shared helpers private.
mode_rationale: Single convention: authorizers expose canX methods returning AuthorizationResult

rule: Follow established infrastructure conventions (e.g., client labels for network policies) to keep services loosely coupled, even when a more explicit approach seems correct for a specific case.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about adhering to existing infrastructure conventions

rule: Section-level actions (like 'Create' buttons) should follow the established page action design pattern rather than being embedded inline in child components.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: follow existing page action design patterns

rule: Name new classes following the established naming pattern in the codebase (e.g., use `XyzPersister` if that is the existing convention, not `XyzManager`).
mode_rationale: Naming consistency convention

rule: When an optimization is valid but diverges from the established pattern in the codebase, prefer following the existing convention for consistency unless the performance gain is significant.
mode_rationale: Simple consistency guideline for code style decisions

rule: New variables and constants should follow the naming conventions already established in the file (e.g., capitalization, casing style) for consistency.
mode_rationale: Single consistency rule about matching existing file conventions

rule: When adding new routes, follow the conventions established by existing endpoints in the same routes file rather than introducing new patterns.
mode_rationale: Convention to match existing endpoint patterns

rule: Custom HTTP headers should follow the established naming convention in the codebase (e.g., `X-Domino-Api-Key` capitalization style) rather than introducing inconsistent casing.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for HTTP headers

rule: Name styled-component files as styles.ts or styled.ts to match the existing project convention, even if an alternative convention is preferable.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: use styles.ts or styled.ts for styled-component files

rule: Import styled-components using the namespace convention `import * as Styles from './styled'` to follow established codebase conventions and enable easy visual search-and-replace.
mode_rationale: Import convention for styled-components

rule: Do not wrap components in an Ant Design Form unless there are corresponding Form.Item elements that provide validation — an empty Form with no Form.Items serves no purpose and misleads future readers.
mode_rationale: Single rule about Ant Design Form usage

rule: File uploads and other form actions must go through the same submit function used for form validation, otherwise required fields (like Reason for Change) can be bypassed.
mode_rationale: Simple rule that all form actions go through validation

rule: Align and format complex expressions (e.g., chained calls, multi-line parameters) so they are easy to scan and understand at a glance.
Wrapper and composed components must accept and forward data-test (or dataTest) props to their root container so Cypress tests can target them.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to forward data-test props for Cypress targeting

rule: Function names should not reference concepts from other architectural layers; a service-layer method should not mention web-layer concerns like cookies or HTTP headers.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention about layer-appropriate function names

rule: Utility functions should always return a consistent type (e.g., always a string) rather than sometimes returning undefined or null, to simplify consumption and avoid null checks downstream.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention about return type consistency

rule: When adding support for a new provider or integration, ensure all related feature gates and requirement checks include the new provider to maintain consistent behavior.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence consistency rule about feature gates

rule: Put changes to external service integrations (especially cloud provider APIs) behind feature flags/central config to allow rollback, since corner cases often surface only in production.
mode_rationale: Single convention about feature-flagging risky integrations

rule: When a feature depends on a feature flag, disable or hide the corresponding UI controls when the flag is off, rather than showing options that won't work.
mode_rationale: Single convention: hide controls when flag is off

rule: General-purpose UI components (e.g., DynamicFieldDisplay) should never be aware of or consume feature-specific React context providers; feature-specific state should be managed outside the general-purpose component boundary.
mode_rationale: Component boundary rule: no feature-specific context in general components

rule: Track tab navigation as a single generic event with a property indicating which tab was selected, rather than creating separate event names per tab.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for tab navigation events

rule: Use get-or-create patterns instead of separate get-then-create flows when a missing value should never cause a failure.
mode_rationale: Single error-handling pattern preference

rule: Control font-family at the global/theme level rather than setting it in individual component styles, so font changes (e.g., during a visual refresh) propagate automatically.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention: font-family at theme level only

rule: When a request spans multiple resources with varying permissions, return results for authorized resources and an empty set for unauthorized ones rather than failing the entire request with a 403.
mode_rationale: Single API design convention for mixed-permission responses

rule: When parsing or aggregating data, prefer returning partial results over throwing exceptions when possible, to avoid total failure on minor mismatches.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention to prefer partial results over throwing

rule: Split authorization checks to match the scope of each query — don't require broad permissions when the user only needs access to a narrow subset of data.
mode_rationale: Single principle about scoping authorization checks

rule: Apply feature flag checks at the specific endpoint or component boundary where the feature is used, not at a coarse wrapper level.
mode_rationale: Single convention: apply feature flags at specific boundaries, not coarse wrappers

rule: Use separate try-catch blocks for independent operations that require different rollback logic, rather than grouping them into a single block.
mode_rationale: Error handling scoping convention

rule: Organize analytics event type definitions by feature domain (e.g., environments) rather than by UI pattern (e.g., tabs), so related events are colocated and discoverable.
mode_rationale: File organization convention for analytics types

rule: When a function accumulates many parameters, group related parameters into a dedicated object to keep signatures manageable.
mode_rationale: Simple refactoring convention for parameter lists

rule: Declare related variables or fields adjacent to each other so their relationship is immediately visible.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization rule

rule: Combine related imports from the same package into a single import statement rather than separate lines.
mode_rationale: Import organization convention

rule: Place new tests in a dedicated test module rather than appending them to an unrelated test file, especially when they test a distinct feature area.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: place tests in appropriate dedicated modules

rule: When accessing a field assumed to always be present (e.g., via .get on an Option), add defensive handling or document the invariant, since 'always present' assumptions can silently break at runtime.
mode_rationale: Defensive coding guideline for accessing optional fields

rule: When performing division, verify that the divisor cannot be zero — even if current callers make it unlikely — to prevent runtime exceptions from unexpected inputs.
mode_rationale: Single defensive coding rule about checking divisors

rule: When parsing request bodies in controllers, defensively handle missing or malformed bodies (e.g., wrap in `Try(...).toOption`) to avoid unexpected 500 errors on requests that legitimately have no body.
mode_rationale: Single convention: defensively handle missing or malformed request bodies

rule: When a value is required for correct behavior, make it non-optional at the type level rather than silently accepting None and producing incorrect results.
mode_rationale: Single convention: make required values non-optional at the type level

rule: Validate that required fields exist before making API calls; if a field accessed via optional chaining (e.g., `file.name?.fileName`) may be undefined, either throw an error or skip the call rather than sending an invalid request.
mode_rationale: Simple validation guideline before making API calls

rule: When calling refetch after a state update, verify the new state has been applied before the request fires to avoid race conditions where the request uses stale state.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about verifying state before refetch

rule: When tests involve asynchronous operations like deletion or state transitions, consider potential race conditions and order operations to minimize timing-dependent failures.
mode_rationale: General awareness principle about ordering async operations

rule: When combining one-time DOM events (like onLoad) with asynchronous data resolution, verify the async data is available before making decisions, or handle the case where the event fires before data is ready.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence defensive coding guideline for async+DOM timing

rule: When managing pagination, sorting, or filter state that triggers data fetching, ensure that state changes during an in-flight request are not dropped or ignored -- do not gate state updates on a loading flag.
mode_rationale: Single principle about not gating state updates on loading flags

rule: When performing async operations (e.g., navigation followed by state updates), ensure the component is still mounted before updating state to avoid React warnings.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence React convention about async state safety

rule: When rendering data that is fetched asynchronously, guard against undefined or loading states to prevent displaying 'undefined' strings in the UI.
mode_rationale: Simple defensive coding convention for async data rendering

rule: Use template literals instead of string concatenation to build strings, and guard against potentially undefined values that would produce 'undefinedsome-str' when concatenated.
mode_rationale: Simple defensive coding convention

rule: Do not fire API requests when required filter parameters are undefined; guard the fetch to prevent unfiltered queries and race conditions with loading state.
mode_rationale: Simple guard pattern for API calls with missing parameters

rule: When a feature depends on multiple configuration flags, guard it behind ALL required flags (e.g., config.enabled && sshConfig.enabled), not just a subset.
mode_rationale: Single rule: check all required flags, not a subset

rule: Catch domain-specific not-found exceptions (e.g., UnknownDataPlaneIdException) inside cache loaders to prevent one bad key from breaking the loader for other entries.
mode_rationale: Simple error handling rule for cache loader resilience

rule: When accessing deeply nested response properties (e.g., err.response.data), add null/undefined guards or use optional chaining to prevent runtime errors.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence defensive coding convention

rule: Append '|| true' to non-critical cleanup commands in shell scripts so a cleanup failure does not mask the exit code of the preceding critical command.
mode_rationale: Simple shell scripting convention

rule: Expensive operations (feature flag lookups, external service calls) should be guarded behind early-return checks for empty input to avoid unnecessary work.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention for early returns

rule: Move feature flag checks and expensive service calls inside the branch that actually needs them, so they are skipped when the result would be unused (e.g., when a list is empty).
mode_rationale: Single performance convention for conditional logic

rule: Prevent form submission while asynchronous operations (e.g., adding a collaborator) are in progress to avoid partial or inconsistent data being submitted.
mode_rationale: Single guard rule for async form state

rule: Before initializing third-party SDKs, verify all required data dependencies have loaded successfully and use a module-level guard flag to ensure initialization runs exactly once, eliminating the need for defensive nullish chaining.
mode_rationale: Initialization safety convention

rule: When displaying conditional content based on async data, add a loading-state guard (e.g., `hasLoaded && !data`) to prevent a flash of the 'not found' view while data is still being fetched.
mode_rationale: Single guard condition pattern to prevent flash of not-found state

rule: When adding new functionality that depends on a feature flag, ensure the code behaves correctly when the flag is disabled — do not render or include flag-gated features unconditionally.
mode_rationale: Single convention about feature flag correctness

rule: Do not assume runtime metadata or configuration values will always be defined; guard against missing values with explicit checks or Option types.
mode_rationale: Simple null-safety convention

rule: Only add resource requests (e.g., GPU) to Kubernetes specs when the requested amount is greater than zero, matching the pattern used for scheduling workloads.
mode_rationale: Single guard condition convention for Kubernetes resource specs

rule: Analytics and tracking events must include comparison guards to ensure they only fire when the specific tracked state actually changes, not when unrelated state changes occur.
mode_rationale: Simple correctness convention for analytics events

rule: When calling setState inside useEffect, ensure the dependency condition is strong enough to prevent infinite render loops.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence React performance convention

rule: When a component conditionally renders nothing, ensure that its useEffect hooks do not continue triggering side effects like calling onChange handlers on the parent.
mode_rationale: Single convention about preventing side effects when component renders nothing

rule: When binding service classes in a Guice injection module, scope them as Singletons to avoid creating multiple instances of stateful or expensive service objects.
mode_rationale: Guice scoping convention for service classes

rule: When code looks up an entity that may not exist (deleted, archived, never created), handle the absence explicitly — clean up stale references, provide fallback behavior, or defend against null returns.
trigger: When fetching entities by ID that could be deleted, archived, or not yet created, or when processing collections that reference other entities
rationale: Unhandled absent entities cause NullPointerExceptions or stale data in indexes. Reviewers flagged cases where archived apps remained in search indexes and where missing instances or commits had no fallback handling.
mode_rationale: Defensive coding convention for null/missing entity handling

rule: When pattern matching on enum-like types (e.g., file change types), explicitly consider all variants including less obvious ones like DELETE to avoid silent bugs.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about exhaustive pattern matching

rule: When blocklisting or allowlisting file types, include all related format variants (e.g., all Office formats: doc, docx, ppt, pptx, xls, xlsx, rtf) rather than just the one that triggered the ticket.
mode_rationale: Single convention: include all related format variants in block/allowlists

rule: When calling paginated APIs, always implement pagination handling; do not rely on default page sizes, as they silently truncate results.
mode_rationale: Single rule about always implementing pagination handling

rule: When reading from a cache, handle the case where the expected entry is missing -- ensure the operation degrades gracefully and will be retried on the next cycle rather than failing silently.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention for cache reads

rule: Utility and resolver methods should handle rare edge cases (such as null returns for empty repositories) defensively, especially if they may be reused in broader contexts beyond their original caller.
mode_rationale: Single defensive coding convention

rule: When using string replacement or pattern matching on URLs or paths, consider edge cases where the target substring may appear multiple times and ensure the logic handles all occurrences correctly.
When deriving data from related entities, always consider the case where the relationship doesn't exist yet (e.g., no instances created) and implement explicit fallback behavior.
mode_rationale: Single defensive coding principle for missing relationships

rule: When a UI component can render with empty data, show an informative message instead of rendering an empty container (e.g. an empty collapse panel).
mode_rationale: UI convention for empty data states

rule: When fetching data in a view, always handle the error state using the module's existing error component (e.g., FlowsError) rather than ignoring failures silently.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use module's existing error component for fetch failures

rule: When refactoring Future/async error handling (e.g. replacing onComplete with map/recover), verify that all exception paths from upstream calls are still handled.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention for async refactoring

rule: When a parsing function can throw exceptions, ensure all call sites that invoke similar parsing handle the same exceptions consistently.
mode_rationale: Single convention: handle exceptions consistently across all call sites

rule: When performing cleanup or deletion of resources, handle possible failures in the deletion itself to avoid silent errors or orphaned state.
mode_rationale: Simple error handling guideline for cleanup paths

rule: When using try-catch-finally patterns, ensure that fatal errors are handled distinctly from non-fatal errors so that finally blocks do not execute unintended logic after catastrophic failures.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: distinguish fatal from non-fatal in catch handling

rule: Input normalization (e.g., URL decoding, trimming, case conversion) should be performed at the controller or entry-point level so that downstream functions receive consistent, already-normalized data.
mode_rationale: Single convention: normalize inputs at the entry point

rule: When async data is used to guard destructive actions (e.g., allowing deletion), handle loading and error states explicitly — a falsy default during fetch or on failure can incorrectly enable the action.
mode_rationale: Single convention: handle loading/error states when async data guards destructive actions

rule: Route-based access control and redirects should be handled at the router level rather than in individual components.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architecture convention about routing responsibility

rule: When an API endpoint references another entity (e.g., setting roles for a user), handle the case where that entity does not exist by catching NotFoundException and returning an appropriate error.
mode_rationale: Error-handling convention for referenced entity lookups

rule: When calling external services or APIs that can return null in edge cases (e.g., a repo with no commits), handle the null case defensively, especially if the method may be reused in broader contexts.
mode_rationale: Single defensive coding rule for external API nulls

rule: Do not assume optional or dynamically-provided values are always defined; add explicit checks or fallbacks for cases where the value may be absent.
mode_rationale: Simple defensive coding convention

rule: When performing optimistic UI updates, always implement a rollback or refetch strategy for when the backend operation fails.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention for optimistic UI patterns

rule: Never call .toString on an Option type to extract its value; use .getOrElse, pattern matching, or .map to avoid producing stringified wrapper values like "None" or "Some(value)" instead of the inner value.
mode_rationale: Single convention: never call .toString on Option; use getOrElse or pattern match

rule: When executing multiple sequential service calls that must all succeed for a consistent state, handle partial failures gracefully and ensure the UI reflects the actual state (e.g., refetch data after errors).
mode_rationale: Single guideline about graceful partial failure handling

rule: Formatting and display utility functions should gracefully handle undefined or null inputs, returning a sensible fallback instead of rendering 'Invalid date' or similar broken output.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention: formatters should handle null/undefined gracefully

rule: Hide pagination controls when there is insufficient data to paginate rather than showing an empty or non-functional paginator.
mode_rationale: Simple UI display convention

rule: After filtering children from a parent container (submenu, group), check if the parent has any remaining children and hide it if empty.
mode_rationale: Simple UI cleanup rule after filtering

rule: Do not expose internal implementation details (such as internal tag names, internal IDs, or system-level metadata) in public API documentation, as users may misuse or depend on them.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence API documentation rule

rule: When creating wrapper abstractions over third-party SDKs, use generic names (e.g., listObjects) rather than leaking vendor-specific version details (e.g., listObjectsV2) into public APIs.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for wrapper abstractions

rule: Static preset values, configuration objects, and constants that do not depend on props or state should be defined outside the React component to avoid unnecessary re-creation on each render.
mode_rationale: Single performance/organization convention

rule: Never mutate backend-fetched data when applying client-side filters; store the original data as an immutable source of truth and compute filtered views from it using separate filter state.
mode_rationale: Single architectural principle about data immutability

rule: Use imperative functions (e.g., a callable `fetchData`) for data refreshes instead of chaining multiple useEffect hooks with boolean flags, which causes unnecessary re-renders.
mode_rationale: Single preference for imperative fetch over chained useEffects

rule: Do not explicitly pass `true` to boolean JSX props; use the shorthand attribute name alone (e.g., `<Comp disabled />` instead of `<Comp disabled={true} />`).
mode_rationale: JSX style rule: use shorthand boolean props

rule: Import path aliases that reference a /dist directory should resolve to compiled output, not source code; mixing these causes broken React contexts and confusing build behavior.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence build configuration rule

rule: When a directory has an index.ts barrel file, import from the index rather than directly from the underlying module files to maintain proper encapsulation.
mode_rationale: Simple import convention for encapsulation

rule: Use components from the internal packages/ui library (e.g., Tag from packages/ui) instead of importing directly from Ant Design, as the internal components expose theme-aware props like color keys.
mode_rationale: Single import convention to use internal wrappers over Ant Design directly

rule: Maintain consistent import ordering: absolute/external imports should come before relative imports, and rely on auto-formatting tools like Prettier to enforce this.
When recording audit or tracking events, include all available identifying information (e.g., user IDs, entity IDs) even if not strictly required, to make events useful for debugging and auditing.
mode_rationale: Single logging completeness guideline

rule: When a useEffect callback dependency becomes stable (e.g., wrapped in useCallback with empty deps), add it to the dependency array rather than leaving it out, to keep the hook correct and avoid stale-closure bugs.
mode_rationale: Single React hooks convention

rule: Error messages should include domain-specific context (e.g., entity type, identifier) so that engineers debugging production issues can quickly identify which resource and operation failed.
Error log messages must include the operation that failed and relevant entity identifiers (e.g., modelId, userId) to enable effective debugging.
mode_rationale: Simple convention for error log content

rule: When defining status enums, always include an explicit error state and ensure error messages from downstream systems are passed through rather than silently dropped.
mode_rationale: Enum design convention for error states

rule: API schema fields, especially those with complex or non-obvious formats, should include inline examples to clarify expected values.
mode_rationale: Single API documentation convention

rule: When logging service operations, include the request filters and parameters in the log message so that issues can be diagnosed from logs alone.
mode_rationale: Single logging content convention

rule: When testing state-dependent logic, include negative test cases that verify the system does NOT perform an action when it shouldn't, not just that it performs correctly when it should.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about negative cases

rule: Include the test scenario ID in dynamically created resource names (e.g., workspace names) to ensure uniqueness and traceability when debugging test failures.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for test resource uniqueness and traceability

rule: API route parameter definitions should include $ref or enum constraints to enable automatic API request validation rather than accepting unvalidated strings.
mode_rationale: Single API validation convention

rule: Configuration variable names for durations should include the unit (e.g., `cacheTtlSeconds` instead of `cacheTtl`) to prevent ambiguity about whether the value is in seconds, milliseconds, or another unit.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for duration variables

rule: Initialize data structures with their intended values at declaration rather than creating empty structures and mutating them afterward.
mode_rationale: Simple coding convention about initialization style

rule: When transformation logic is only used in one place, prefer keeping it inline rather than extracting it into a separate method, to reduce indirection and keep the code easier to follow.
mode_rationale: Single code organization preference

rule: Docstrings in interface/public-API layers should describe contracts and behavior, not reference concrete implementation classes or execution-layer specifics that the interface has no knowledge of.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention for interface layers

rule: TypeScript interface and type names must start with an uppercase letter, following PascalCase convention.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for TypeScript types

rule: When adding cross-cutting policies or behaviors, use a no-op/null implementation for modules that should not be affected to prevent unintended side effects.
mode_rationale: Design pattern convention for cross-cutting concerns

rule: When iterating over items that require remediation or processing, extract the loop body into its own function with independent error handling so a failure on one item does not prevent processing the rest.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention for loop resilience

rule: When a periodic task has multiple independent stages, catch NonFatal exceptions in each stage independently so that a failure in one does not prevent the others from executing.
mode_rationale: Error-handling convention: catch exceptions per stage independently

rule: In e2e tests, ensure test entities (orgs, projects) are created by the appropriate user role so that the entity owner is not part of the group being tested, avoiding false positives from implicit owner permissions.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline about test entity ownership to avoid false positives

rule: When adding a new dependency to a BUILD file or build configuration, be prepared to explain why it is needed; unnecessary dependencies increase build times and coupling.
Don't wrap code in Try or similar error-handling constructs without a clear justification; reviewers will question unnecessary defensive coding.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to avoid unnecessary defensive wrapping

rule: When modifying behavior or APIs, verify that associated documentation (comments, docs, scaladoc) still accurately reflects the new behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple discipline rule about updating docs alongside code

rule: Avoid oversized components by extracting API calls and request logic into a container component or custom hook, keeping the presentational component focused on rendering.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about component size and separation

rule: API specifications should define the minimal contract; put validation logic and known-constant enforcement in application code rather than embedding it in the spec.
mode_rationale: Design principle about where validation logic belongs

rule: When copying code or comments from one location to another, update all references and descriptions to match the new context to avoid misleading future readers.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation hygiene rule

rule: Avoid adding computed values and derived state directly to React context providers; instead use separate hooks that consume the context, keeping the provider as a single source of truth without unnecessary growth and re-render propagation.
mode_rationale: React performance convention about context design

rule: Do not overload a DTO with fields that conceptually belong to a different entity; place each field on the DTO that owns that concept.
mode_rationale: Convention about DTO field ownership

rule: Avoid hardcoding version numbers in file names when the content is not version-specific; use generic names so the file doesn't need renaming with each product version.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention to avoid version numbers in filenames

rule: Lambda functions should be lightweight routing layers; push complex logic (config processing, branching) into downstream services or build steps that can be tested independently.
mode_rationale: Single architecture principle: keep lambdas as thin routing layers

rule: When renaming or changing default values, update all associated log messages and warnings to reflect the new names or behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple discipline rule about log message accuracy

rule: When a create operation involves copying multiple sub-resources, do not fail the entire operation if some items cannot be copied — instead track what succeeded and report failures with reasons.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention for batch operations

rule: When stripping or parsing delimiter characters (e.g., comment markers like `#`), handle the case where the delimiter appears inside quoted strings to avoid corrupting valid values.
mode_rationale: Single defensive-coding rule about delimiter handling in quoted strings

rule: When a data-fetching hook throws on error, ensure callers catch the error; otherwise an uncaught exception will crash the view.
mode_rationale: Simple error-handling rule about catching hook errors

rule: A help/question-mark icon should itself be the clickable link to documentation, not a hover trigger that reveals a separate link — reduce interaction steps.
mode_rationale: Simple UX convention about help icon interaction

rule: Do not expose internal retry/backoff handler callbacks to callers; instead add logging inside the polling function itself, so the retry library can be swapped without breaking the public API.
If a method's return value can never vary (e.g., always returns true), refactor the return type to reflect actual behavior (e.g., Unit/void) rather than suggesting a branching outcome.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about truthful return type signatures

rule: React hooks should live in their own dedicated files, not in util files; a file named *Util should contain pure utility functions, not hooks.
mode_rationale: File organization convention for React hooks

rule: Call React hooks at the top level of the component function, not inside JSX; inline hook calls in JSX break if early returns or conditional rendering change hook order.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence React rules-of-hooks convention

rule: Aria labels must be written in human-readable form for screen readers (e.g., 'Create comment tab'), not as IDs or machine-readable strings; they exist for accessibility, not as test selectors.
mode_rationale: Simple accessibility convention for aria label formatting

rule: When migrating to new icon components, apply color via a StyledComponent wrapper rather than passing color as a direct prop, as the new icon components may not accept color props the same way.
mode_rationale: Single rule to apply icon color via styled wrapper

rule: Use idiomatic collection operations like `drop(offset).take(limit)` for in-memory pagination instead of manual index arithmetic with `slice`.
mode_rationale: Coding style preference for idiomatic collection operations

rule: Use idiomatic empty collection expressions (e.g., List.empty or Nil in Scala) instead of constructing empty instances manually.
mode_rationale: Simple style preference for idiomatic Scala

rule: Use idiomatic Option combinators (map, getOrElse, flatMap) instead of verbose pattern matching or nested conditionals when transforming or defaulting optional values in Scala.
mode_rationale: Single convention: prefer Option combinators over verbose pattern matching

rule: In Scala, use case class val accessors (obj.key) instead of Java-style getter methods (obj.getKey); follow Scala naming conventions for accessors and mutators.
mode_rationale: Simple Scala naming convention

rule: Use idiomatic Scala collection methods like `.map(...).toMap` and `.collect` with pattern matching instead of imperative loop-and-accumulate patterns.
mode_rationale: Single style rule: use idiomatic collection methods instead of imperative loops

rule: When configuring lint ignore rules, ignore entire directories rather than listing individual files to reduce maintenance burden and avoid missing new files.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about lint ignore granularity

rule: Use imperative onSuccess/onFailure callbacks passed to hooks for showing toasts, rather than declaratively rendering toasts inside components based on state.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use imperative callbacks for toasts, not declarative rendering

rule: Number formatting, date formatting, and other localization concerns should be addressed as an integral app-wide solution rather than applied ad-hoc in individual components.
mode_rationale: Simple architectural guideline against ad-hoc localization

rule: All controller methods should extract DominoContext via `implicit val dc: DominoContext = request.dominoContext` and service method signatures should accept it as an implicit parameter.
mode_rationale: Architectural convention for controller method signatures

rule: Always import colors from the canonical colors.ts file, not from alternative files like colorsNew.ts, to ensure theme switching works correctly.
mode_rationale: Single import convention for color definitions

rule: Import shared modules from their canonical package (e.g., `@domino/core`, `@domino/core-ui`) to keep a single change point for future package extractions.
mode_rationale: Single import convention rule

rule: In a monorepo, import shared package components from their dist (built) files rather than directly from source to ensure consistent module resolution and avoid compilation issues.
mode_rationale: Single monorepo import convention

rule: Import modules from their source (src) paths, not from compiled dist/build output directories.
mode_rationale: Single convention: import from src paths, not dist/build output

rule: Ensure import statements reference the exact filename on disk, including casing and spelling, to prevent build errors and confusion.
mode_rationale: Single convention: import paths must match exact filename casing and spelling

rule: If a hook returns only a static constant that never changes, import the constant directly instead of consuming it through a hook.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention about avoiding hooks for static values

rule: Place important identifiers like IDs as the first (or near-first) parameters in method signatures so they are immediately visible.
mode_rationale: Simple parameter ordering convention

rule: Place all imports at the top of the file rather than inline so that all dependencies are visible in one place.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization convention about import placement

rule: When generating certificates for Kubernetes services, include the short hostname, namespace-qualified name, and full FQDN as principals to ensure resolution works across all contexts.
mode_rationale: Single rule about K8s certificate principals

rule: Debug log messages should include sufficient context (e.g., the object being operated on) so that logs are useful for troubleshooting without additional instrumentation.
mode_rationale: Logging quality convention

rule: Generate and include a correlation ID (e.g., UUID) in error responses so operators can correlate client-facing errors with server-side log entries for debugging.
mode_rationale: Single convention about including correlation IDs in errors

rule: Function and step names should include the domain or service they interact with (e.g., 'keycloak') to avoid ambiguity, even if it feels redundant — clarity for readers outweighs brevity.
mode_rationale: Naming clarity convention

rule: Name result types with enough domain context that they are meaningful when imported in other files — e.g., use 'UpsertThumbnailResult' instead of generic 'UpsertResult'.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for result types

rule: Include key entity identifiers (such as names, titles, and IDs) in log messages to make debugging easier when scanning logs for specific items.
mode_rationale: Single logging best practice

rule: Include parent resource identifiers in API route paths (e.g., /snapshot/:snapshotId/import-blobs/:taskId) for consistency and to enable authorization checks against the parent resource.
mode_rationale: Single API route design convention

rule: Include the acting principal (user identity) in log messages for operations that modify state, to support debugging and audit traceability.
mode_rationale: Single logging convention for audit traceability

rule: When modifying templates or configuration generators (e.g., nginx configs), include the fully rendered output in the PR description so reviewers can verify the final result.
mode_rationale: Simple PR description convention

rule: When wrapping errors from external APIs, include the original response body in the exception message to aid future debugging if the upstream API changes.
mode_rationale: Single error handling convention for external API responses

rule: Include a `DeletedAt` (or equivalent) timestamp field in entities that support soft deletion, so deletions are auditable and reversible.
mode_rationale: Simple data modeling convention

rule: Tests for new or changed functionality should be included in the PR itself, not deferred to a follow-up.
mode_rationale: Single convention: tests belong in the PR, not a follow-up

rule: When leaving TODO comments or task markers in code, include a ticket reference (e.g., Jira ID) so the work can be tracked.
mode_rationale: Single convention about TODO comment format

rule: When creating new entities, initialize numeric fields to their correct default values (e.g., views should be 0 for a newly created app) rather than leaving them null or undefined.
mode_rationale: Convention to set correct default values for new entities

rule: Shared resources like ActorSystem should be injected via dependency injection rather than instantiated within individual classes, to avoid creating redundant instances and ensure proper lifecycle management.
mode_rationale: DI convention for shared resources like ActorSystem

rule: When refactoring reduces a method to a trivial delegation, inline it at the call site rather than keeping a thin wrapper that adds no value.
mode_rationale: Simple refactoring guideline about eliminating thin wrappers

rule: Ensure inner component state and outer/parent state use the same type representations (e.g., both use undefined or both use empty string) to avoid subtle inconsistencies.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: inner and outer state should use matching type representations

rule: Integration tests should use real implementations (e.g., real persisters and repositories), not mocks; mocking in integration tests defeats their purpose and reduces coverage of real interactions.
When adding new ESLint rules, start them as warnings rather than errors to avoid forcing developers to use --no-verify or add excessive exceptions.
mode_rationale: Single convention: start new ESLint rules as warnings, not errors

rule: Invalid or unmatched routes should render a 404 page, not silently redirect to another section of the app.
mode_rationale: Simple routing convention: render 404 for invalid routes

rule: When tests are flaky or slow, investigate the root cause (unmocked API calls, missing test utilities, inefficient selectors) rather than simply increasing timeouts.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about debugging flaky tests properly

rule: Keep dependency injection framework imports (e.g., Guice) confined to injection/wiring modules rather than spreading them into domain or service classes, to avoid transitive dependency leakage.
mode_rationale: Architectural guideline: keep DI framework imports in injection modules only

rule: Do not introduce direct dependencies from implementation modules to the monolith server package; use adapter classes or named injection to keep implementation code decoupled and preserve the ability to eventually remove monolith dependencies.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention to avoid direct monolith dependencies

rule: Break tests that validate multiple independent behaviors into separate test cases so that failures can be isolated and one failing assertion does not mask others.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: one behavior per test case

rule: Define traits (interfaces) that are clean of external library dependencies so they can be shared across modules without pulling in transitive dependencies.
When a service restart triggers many concurrent operations (e.g., spawning actors for all active entities), stagger or jitter the initialization to avoid a thundering herd that spikes resource usage.
mode_rationale: Performance convention to stagger concurrent operations

rule: Use JSDoc block comment format (/** ... */) for component and function documentation so IDEs display it in hover hints.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation format convention

rule: When modeling JSON Schema enum fields, support JsValue (or equivalent generic JSON type) rather than only String, since enum values may be numbers or other non-string types.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use JsValue for enum fields

rule: Use the short syntax <> instead of React.Fragment when no key or attributes are needed.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use <> instead of React.Fragment when no key is needed

rule: Use label="value" notation for JSX string attributes instead of template literals or expressions wrapping plain strings.
mode_rationale: Simple JSX syntax style convention

rule: Every new configuration setting should have a clear justification; prefer simplicity and fewer settings unless there is a concrete scenario (e.g., user code causing false positives) that requires a runtime kill switch.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline about preferring simplicity in configuration

rule: When introducing batched database queries, document why the chosen batch size is safe (e.g., won't exceed MongoDB's 16MB BSON document limit) and consider how it scales for large deployments.
mode_rationale: Single documentation requirement when introducing batch queries

rule: Do not mark fields as optional without a clear reason; unnecessary optionals introduce null-checking burden and weaken type safety.
mode_rationale: Simple type design convention

rule: When a Kubernetes operator owns a CRD, the operator should own cleanup of all related resources; avoid coupling external services to trigger resource deletion.
mode_rationale: Architecture rule about K8s resource ownership

rule: Use kebab-case for data-test (data-testid) attribute values to maintain consistency across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for data-test attribute values

rule: Remove or update suppression annotations (like @unused) when the suppressed element is actually in use, to avoid misleading readers.
mode_rationale: Simple code hygiene convention about annotation accuracy

rule: When designing internal service APIs, place contextual parameters (IDs, metadata) in the same location (header or body) as similar parameters in the same request for consistency, preferring the simpler option.
mode_rationale: Single API design consistency convention

rule: When an API is defined in multiple spec formats (e.g., JSON and YAML), update all formats together to avoid drift between spec files.
mode_rationale: Single convention: update all spec formats together

rule: API definitions should only include fields and schemas relevant to the resource being exposed; remove references to unrelated features or legacy concepts.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline about API scope
