rule: When cache eviction relies on last-access timestamps, ensure the timestamp is updated throughout the resource's lifecycle (not just at startup) to prevent premature eviction of actively-used entries.
mode_rationale: Single caching convention about timestamp freshness

rule: Place feature components in modules that match their domain; do not add unrelated features to existing views just because it is convenient.
mode_rationale: Simple placement guideline for feature components

rule: When updating configuration values for a new version, ensure the same change is applied consistently across all version entries that should share the value.
mode_rationale: Single convention: apply config changes consistently across all relevant version entries

rule: When copying CRD or schema definitions across repositories, add a comment noting the source and ensure changes in the source are propagated to all copies.
mode_rationale: Single maintenance rule about cross-repo copies

rule: When updating shared configuration values like image tags, ensure the same value is updated in all repositories and charts that reference it to maintain congruence.
mode_rationale: Single awareness rule about shared config consistency

rule: Default or fallback values for component props (like labels) should be the responsibility of the consuming component, not embedded in shared utilities, to maintain separation of concerns.
mode_rationale: Single principle about separation of concerns for default values

rule: Domain entities and validation logic should not depend on web framework constructs (e.g., Play Forms); if the framework changes or the domain is reused in a non-web context, the domain should remain unaffected.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architectural boundary rule

rule: Do not embed feature-specific concepts (e.g., MLflow secret name overrides) into shared/generic domain code; find a way to use existing generic contracts instead.
mode_rationale: Architectural guideline about separation of concerns

rule: DynamicField component types should remain simple, reusable form controls; complex domain-specific components should be extracted into their own module rather than embedded as a field type.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architectural boundary rule

rule: When renaming a class or trait (e.g., Manager to Persister), update all corresponding filenames — including test and integration test files — to match the new name.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: rename files when renaming classes/traits

rule: When adding or changing fields in a backend API, verify whether frontend mocks, TypeScript types, or swagger docs also need updating to stay in sync.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence cross-layer sync convention

rule: When modifying API definitions, regenerate derived spec files using the project's designated tooling (e.g., `dev/bazel/update_api_specs.sh`) to keep generated artifacts consistent with source definitions.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about regenerating derived files after changes

rule: Do not spread entire objects into React hook dependency arrays when the individual properties are already listed as dependencies; remove redundant object-level entries to avoid unnecessary re-renders.
mode_rationale: Simple React performance convention for dependency arrays

rule: Break long lines into multiple lines for readability; do not let single expressions stretch beyond the project's line-length convention.
mode_rationale: Simple formatting convention: respect line-length limits

rule: When moving or refactoring imports, ensure `eslint-disable` comments remain attached to the specific import they were suppressing.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about maintaining eslint-disable comments during refactors

rule: Do not reset submitting or loading flags until all dependent async operations (including data refetches) have completed, to prevent users from operating on stale data.
mode_rationale: Simple state management rule

rule: Keep log messages concise by abbreviating well-known terms (e.g., 'repo' instead of 'repository'), especially when the message will include long values like URIs.
mode_rationale: Simple log message style convention

rule: Domain mapping code (e.g., converting template models to project models) should live in the service that owns the source domain, not in the consuming service, to avoid cross-service coupling.
mode_rationale: Architectural rule about where mapping code should live

rule: Keep PRs focused on the task at hand; avoid including unrelated reformatting or cosmetic changes that make the diff harder to review and may require additional reviewers.
mode_rationale: Single guideline: avoid unrelated formatting changes in PRs

rule: If a component is already a styled component, define all its styles (including fontSize) in the styled definition rather than passing them as inline style props.
mode_rationale: Simple styling rule: define all styles in styled definition, not inline props

rule: When refactoring test logic, update the test name to accurately describe the current behavior being tested; misleading test names obscure intent and cause confusion during failures.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about naming accuracy

rule: When changing the semantics of a value, update all variable and string references to reflect the new meaning — stale names mislead future readers.
mode_rationale: Naming accuracy convention post-refactor

rule: When leaving temporary workarounds or placeholders in test files, annotate them with a TODO label so they are trackable and not forgotten.
mode_rationale: Single convention about annotating workarounds with TODO

rule: Leverage the base component library's built-in props and APIs (e.g., icon prop on Ant Design SubMenu) rather than re-implementing equivalent functionality.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to use built-in props instead of reimplementing

rule: When adding a lifecycle state transition method like `pause`, always provide its complement (e.g., `resume`) rather than overloading an existing method like `start`.
mode_rationale: API design convention: always provide complementary lifecycle methods

rule: When adding non-standard implementations or workarounds, reference the tracking ticket (DOM number) in code comments so future readers understand the context and can trace the decision.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention to reference tickets in workarounds

rule: Lockfile changes (yarn.lock, package-lock.json) should always correspond to a dependency change in package.json; unexplained lockfile diffs should be investigated and removed if unnecessary.
mode_rationale: Simple validation rule for lockfile diffs

rule: When adding log statements with contextual data, include all relevant fields rather than a subset, so that logs provide complete diagnostic information.
mode_rationale: Simple logging completeness guideline

rule: When catching and handling an error, always log the original error so that if the assumption about the cause is wrong, engineers can diagnose the real issue from logs.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to always log original errors when catching

rule: Log a warning when the system encounters an unexpected but non-fatal state (such as an archived project being resolved at runtime) to aid debugging and provide visibility into edge cases.
mode_rationale: Single logging convention for unexpected states

rule: Scala imports should follow a consistent ordering convention (java/javax first, then scala standard library, then project imports) and group related imports together.
mode_rationale: Simple import ordering convention

rule: Make behavioral constants (e.g., batch sizes, delays, thresholds) configurable rather than hardcoded, so that risky changes can be reverted via configuration without requiring a full code rollback.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention for tuneable constants

rule: Extract hard-coded limits (e.g., max result size) into method parameters with sensible defaults so tests can set low values to verify limit enforcement without relying on large data sets.
mode_rationale: Testability convention for extracting parameters

rule: When naming a Map or dictionary variable, encode the key type in the name (e.g., `dataPlaneHwTierMap` when the key is a data plane ID) so readers immediately understand the lookup semantics.
mode_rationale: Naming convention: encode key type in Map variable names

rule: Methods that are not part of a class's public interface or trait contract should be marked private to limit the API surface.
mode_rationale: Simple encapsulation convention

rule: When implementing retry logic for opaque exceptions, match on specific known error messages rather than broad exception types to avoid retrying non-retryable failures.
mode_rationale: Defensive coding rule for retry specificity

rule: Ensure that type annotations match the actual data representation; avoid using narrower types (e.g., Int) when the source data uses a wider type (e.g., BigDecimal) to prevent silent data loss from implicit conversions.
mode_rationale: Single rule: match type widths to prevent data loss

rule: Extract inline event handler functions into memoized callbacks using useCallback to avoid unnecessary re-renders of child components.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention: memoize callbacks with useCallback

rule: Memoize filtering, searching, or other expensive computations inside table cell components to avoid re-running the same operation N times (once per row) on every re-render.
mode_rationale: Single performance guideline: memoize expensive operations in table cell renders

rule: Memoize the value object passed to React context providers to prevent unnecessary re-renders of all consumers on every parent render.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention for context providers

rule: Memoize objects and arrays passed as props to child components to prevent unnecessary re-renders caused by new reference creation on each render cycle.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention for React props

rule: Name methods to precisely reflect their behavior — a method that updates a resource should not be named `create`, and a method that creates or updates should be named `createOrUpdate`.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention about method accuracy

rule: Keep test fixture JSON files trimmed to the minimum data needed for the test scenario rather than using full production-sized responses.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about fixture data size

rule: Avoid adding dependencies on the `server` module from other modules; use narrower service interfaces (e.g., UserStateService) instead of reaching into server internals (e.g., UserPreferencesPersister).
mode_rationale: Single architecture principle: use narrow interfaces over server internals

rule: Internal implementation details such as API client instances and invokers should be declared as private to prevent unintended external coupling.
mode_rationale: Simple encapsulation convention

rule: When synchronizing upstream data with a local database, consolidate queries into bulk operations rather than performing per-record IO; consider strategies like remove-all-and-reinsert for simplicity.
mode_rationale: Performance guideline to consolidate IO

rule: When calling an RPC or service method, pass only the essential identifier and assemble the full request from available context on the receiving side, rather than forwarding redundant data the callee already has.
mode_rationale: API design convention to avoid passing redundant data

rule: Modals should call generic callbacks (onClose, onSuccess) provided by the parent, not directly trigger side effects like data refetching that are the parent's concern.
mode_rationale: Architectural convention about modal responsibility boundaries

rule: When renaming values, constants, or types, update all warning and log messages to reflect the new names so users see accurate information.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to update log messages during renames

rule: Consolidate all external data fetching needed for DTO construction in one place and pure attribute mapping in another; do not split data-fetching across both the service and the mapper.
mode_rationale: Single architectural convention about separation of mapping and fetching

rule: Dependencies on the monolith server module should live in the monolith-adapter module, not in the core implementation module, to maintain clean module boundaries.
mode_rationale: Module boundary convention

rule: When renaming a parameter in one file, update the corresponding parameter names in all related interfaces and adapters to maintain consistency across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for cross-layer parameter consistency

rule: When the same permission concept exists at both global and project scope, use the same name (e.g., 'QueryAuditEvents') to maintain alignment and reduce confusion.
mode_rationale: Naming consistency rule for permissions

rule: Domain interfaces should use domain IDs (e.g., DominoId) and keep persistence-specific details (e.g., MongoDB ObjectId) confined to the persister implementation layer.
mode_rationale: Layer boundary convention about domain vs persistence types

rule: Place methods specific to a single external provider (e.g., Bitbucket, GitHub) on that provider's implementation class rather than on the shared base client or trait.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architecture convention for provider abstractions

rule: React context providers should only contain state and logic that directly affects the provided values; extract formatting utilities, helper functions, and use-case-specific logic into separate hooks or utility modules to prevent provider bloat.
mode_rationale: Single architectural principle about provider scope

rule: Components added to reusable systems like DynamicField should be generic and simple; if a component is highly complex or domain-specific, extract it into its own module rather than embedding it in the reusable system.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline: keep reusable system components generic

rule: Ensure method/class documentation accurately describes what the code actually does — if validation happens in the controller, the service Scaladoc should not claim the service performs it.
mode_rationale: Documentation accuracy convention

rule: Shared/generic components (e.g., DynamicField) must not import or reference feature-specific types; use generics or a registration pattern to extend them without creating coupling.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture rule: shared components must not import feature-specific types

rule: When a method is used by multiple callers, keep it generic rather than embedding caller-specific concepts (like domain-specific labels) that tie it to a single use case.
mode_rationale: Simple design guideline about method generality

rule: Keep React state as close as possible to the component that uses it; do not lift state to a parent container unless the parent actually needs it.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence React state management guideline

rule: Do not wrap synchronous operations inside Future or async blocks when only the return type needs to be async; keep synchronous work outside the async boundary.
mode_rationale: Performance convention about unnecessary async wrapping

rule: Ensure data-test attributes and form names are consistent and clearly related to the component or feature they belong to.
mode_rationale: Single convention: data-test attributes should match component names

rule: Do not combine unrelated boolean conditions into a single validation variable; each validation check should test exactly one concern to avoid unexpected interactions when individual conditions change.
mode_rationale: Single convention about separating unrelated boolean conditions

rule: When adding a new constraint or default for a form field, ensure it is also reflected in the centralized validation rules constant so that UI validation and business logic stay consistent.
mode_rationale: Convention about co-locating validation logic

rule: Always include a descriptive label or title in print and log statements so that output can be easily identified when debugging.
mode_rationale: Logging convention: always include descriptive labels in output

rule: Follow the target language's established naming conventions (e.g., camelCase for Scala identifiers) consistently across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention to follow consistently

rule: Catch known exceptions at the service level and map them to domain-specific errors; use a global catch block at the API controller level to convert any unmapped exceptions to 500, rather than masking all exceptions in utility methods.
mode_rationale: Convention about where to catch and map exceptions in the stack

rule: Wrap expensive computations in lambdas or lazy values so they are only evaluated when actually needed.
mode_rationale: Single rule: wrap expensive computations in lazy/lambda

rule: Route components should use lazy loading so they are only fetched when the user navigates to that route, reducing the initial bundle size.
Keep execution headers and transport objects as lean as possible — remove redundant fields and avoid duplicating data already available through other fields.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to keep transport objects minimal and avoid duplication

rule: Grant permissions only to the roles that genuinely need them; service-level operations (e.g., archiving backing resources) should be restricted to service principals, not user-facing roles.
mode_rationale: Single convention: restrict permissions to roles that need them

rule: Do not manually override font properties (size, weight, line-height) that are already controlled by the design system's typography tokens.
mode_rationale: Single rule about not overriding design system typography

rule: Omit explicit type annotations when TypeScript can reliably infer the type; only annotate when inference fails or is ambiguous.
mode_rationale: Style convention to omit redundant type annotations

rule: Use Ant Design's built-in form capabilities (Form, Form.List, Form.Item shouldUpdate, onFinish handler) instead of building custom form state management with nested conditionals.
mode_rationale: Convention to prefer Ant Design built-in form capabilities

rule: Before creating custom layout workarounds, check if the shared component already supports your use case (e.g., nested elements for layout alternation in DynamicField).
mode_rationale: Single convention about checking existing component APIs before custom workarounds

rule: When a component is rendered per table row, fetch shared data once in the parent/wrapper component and pass results down, rather than making one request per row.
mode_rationale: Single performance guideline about where to fetch data

rule: Place UI state (e.g., search, filters) at the container level rather than in leaf components, anticipating future needs like pagination that would require lifting it later.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architecture guideline about state placement

rule: Create lightweight 'header' DTOs that return the minimal representation of an entity, so endpoints remain extensible without requiring new API calls or bloated full-entity fetches.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention for minimal entity representations

rule: When querying paginated APIs, limit the page size to the minimum number of records needed rather than fetching default or maximum page sizes.
mode_rationale: Performance convention for paginated API queries

rule: When implementing protocol-level behavior (HTTP caching, conditional requests, etc.), link to the relevant RFC or specification in code comments and add a docstring summarizing the behavior.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention to reference specs in comments

rule: Show a loading indicator while asynchronous data (e.g., dropdowns, selectors) is being fetched to give users feedback that content is on the way.
mode_rationale: Single UX convention about loading feedback

rule: Be aware that checking loading=false and data=undefined together can cause a brief flash of incorrect UI state before data arrives; guard against this race condition in data-fetching patterns.
mode_rationale: Single convention about guarding against loading/data race conditions

rule: Use locale-aware date formats (e.g., dayjs `l`/`lll` tokens or `toLocaleDateString` with options) instead of hardcoded format strings, so dates are consistent with the user's system locale across the UI.
mode_rationale: Formatting convention to use locale-aware dates

rule: Use locale-independent string methods (toUpperCase) instead of toLocaleUpperCase when transforming strings for API values, since locale-specific casing rules can produce unexpected results.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use locale-independent string methods for API values

rule: Add debug-level logging with the relevant metadata before returning a NotAuthorized result, so authorization failures can be diagnosed from logs.
mode_rationale: Simple logging convention: add debug logging before returning NotAuthorized

rule: When logging exceptions, pass the full exception object as a parameter (which includes the stack trace) and write a descriptive log message about the operation context, rather than just logging ex.getMessage.
mode_rationale: Simple logging convention: pass full exception object and describe context

rule: When a feature flag is removed and its behavior defaults to enabled, add a deprecation warning log on any endpoints or code paths that are now obsolete.
mode_rationale: Single convention to log deprecation warnings when removing flags

rule: When recording a failure or error condition, always emit a corresponding log statement so that failures are visible in logs, not just in internal data structures.
mode_rationale: Logging convention for failure visibility

rule: When performing pattern matching or inference logic, log which pattern was matched to aid debugging and observability.
mode_rationale: Single convention: log which pattern was matched for debuggability

rule: Log messages and status values should accurately describe the operation being performed (e.g., use 'Stopped' not 'Delete' when stopping a run).
mode_rationale: Simple accuracy rule for log messages

rule: Add a log message when changing or removing operational behavior (such as disabling health check pauses) so that on-call engineers and debuggers can understand the system's current mode from logs.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to log when operational behavior changes

rule: Log messages should include the relevant identifiers (controller name, operation name) and indicate the lifecycle phase (started, completed) to support debugging and tracing.
mode_rationale: Single logging convention about including identifiers and phase

rule: When triggering state transitions like restarts or redeployments, log the reason for the transition to aid debugging and operational tracing.
mode_rationale: Simple logging convention for state changes

rule: After destructive filesystem operations (rm -rf, mv), log which items were actually affected to aid debugging, since these operations often succeed silently even on nonexistent targets.
mode_rationale: Logging convention to record what was affected by destructive operations

rule: For security-sensitive validation failures, return a generic error message to the client (to avoid leaking information to attackers) but log the full details server-side for debugging.
mode_rationale: Single convention: generic response, detailed log

rule: Add initialization logging to services so that their deployment placement (which pod/node they run on) can be verified, especially when services are moved between modules.
mode_rationale: Logging convention: log init for deployment verification

rule: Add debug logging inside retry/polling loops to capture the current state on each iteration, aiding troubleshooting of intermittent failures.
mode_rationale: Single logging convention for retry/polling loops

rule: New controller and service methods should include debug or trace logging from the start, especially when test coverage is not yet in place.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to include logging from the start

rule: Routine operational messages should use DEBUG, not INFO. Warnings should only fire on genuinely unexpected conditions, and log messages must accurately reflect the condition that triggers them.
trigger: When adding or modifying log statements, especially when choosing between INFO/DEBUG/WARN levels or writing log message text
rationale: Overly verbose INFO logs create noise in production. Warnings on legitimate conditions cause alert fatigue. Inaccurate log messages mislead on-call engineers. Reviewers enforce logging discipline across multiple PRs.
mode_rationale: Simple style rule about choosing correct log levels

rule: Use lowercase for file names and kebab-case for directory names in the frontend codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention

rule: Don't add components to a shared abstraction (e.g., DynamicField) unless they genuinely fit the abstraction's purpose; forcing unrelated components into a pattern erodes the abstraction's meaning.
mode_rationale: Single convention: only add components that fit an abstraction's purpose

rule: When the same logical condition is expressed in multiple related template or view files, use the same form of the expression to avoid confusion.
mode_rationale: Simple consistency rule for repeated boolean expressions

rule: Method descriptions and doc comments within a file or module should follow the same punctuation and formatting conventions for consistency.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation consistency rule

rule: When modifying configuration that affects multiple services or codepaths (e.g., KubernetesConfigurationGenerator and WorkloadServiceHelper), ensure all consumers are updated to maintain exact parity.
mode_rationale: Single convention about keeping configs in sync

rule: When splitting mapper logic into smaller classes, maintain a clean dependency tree where shared utilities are leaf-level classes — child mappers should not reference the root mapper.
mode_rationale: Convention about clean dependency trees in mapper classes

rule: In a monorepo, an app's tsconfig or build config should not directly reference the internals of another package; packages should be consumed through their public API.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architecture rule about package boundaries

rule: When the scope of an API response field is uncertain, mark it as optional to avoid breaking changes if the field is removed or deferred in a future release.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to mark uncertain fields as optional

rule: When catching all errors as a best-effort cleanup strategy, make the intent explicit with comments or logging so reviewers and future maintainers understand the errors are intentionally swallowed.
mode_rationale: Single convention about documenting swallowed errors

rule: Component properties that have sensible defaults or are not always needed should be declared as optional rather than required.
mode_rationale: Single convention: default props with sensible values should be optional

rule: When constructing composite string keys or identifiers, include enough context in each segment so readers can tell what entity each part refers to without checking the surrounding code.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for composite key readability

rule: External service URLs and endpoints that may change per deployment should be exposed as configurable flags rather than hardcoded.
mode_rationale: Configuration convention for external URLs

rule: When exposing identifiers in API response models, make the ID type explicit (e.g., DominoId, MongoId, UUID) so consumers do not have to guess the format or provenance.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for explicit ID types in API models

rule: Design domain models and DTOs so that invalid combinations of fields cannot be constructed; validate at creation time rather than relying on downstream checks.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence domain modeling convention

rule: When fields are effectively required for an operation, make them non-optional in the type signature rather than leaving them as Option types that obscure which fields are truly required.
mode_rationale: Type design convention about optionality

rule: Trait fields that do not apply to all implementations should be declared as Optional rather than requiring implementations to use runtime exceptions (e.g., ???) as placeholders.
mode_rationale: Type design convention for trait fields

rule: When None and an empty collection (e.g., Some(Seq.empty)) have different business semantics, make the distinction explicit through helper methods, comments, or descriptive variable names so the code is self-documenting.
mode_rationale: Single convention about making None vs empty semantics self-documenting

rule: Operational parameters like cache settings, timeouts, and thresholds should be configurable via feature flags (CC Flags) rather than hardcoded, so they can be tuned without redeployment.
mode_rationale: Configuration convention for operational params

rule: When introducing polling or periodic checks against external services, expose the interval as a configurable parameter (e.g., via Central Config) rather than hardcoding it, enabling tuning without redeployment.
mode_rationale: Single guideline: expose polling intervals as configurable parameters

rule: When a component toggles between states (e.g., expand/collapse), its data-testid and accessible label should update dynamically to reflect the current state, improving both testability and screen-reader experience.
mode_rationale: Convention for keeping test IDs and labels in sync with component state

rule: Test step definitions should be explicit about what they do and named intuitively so other engineers will discover and reuse them; avoid combining unrelated behaviors into a single opaque step.
mode_rationale: Best practice for test step naming and reuse

rule: Extract hardcoded timeout values into named constants and expose them as optional props so consumers can configure them without modifying the component.
mode_rationale: Single API design guideline about exposing configuration

rule: When mapping request payloads to internal models, ensure all fields from the payload are mapped — do not silently drop fields (e.g., setting ssh = None when the payload includes SSH configuration).
mode_rationale: Single rule about not dropping fields during payload mapping

rule: When transforming a Map's entries, call .map directly on the Map with a partial function instead of converting to a List first with .toList.map.
mode_rationale: Scala idiom: avoid unnecessary .toList before .map on Maps

rule: Use common code comments or markers to flag configuration lines that must be updated when cutting a release branch, so they are easy to find and not missed.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use code comments to flag release-branch config lines

rule: API parameter types should match their actual usage (e.g., single-select UI means a singular parameter, not a sequence); expanding from singular to multi-valued later is a non-breaking change.
mode_rationale: Single API typing convention

rule: When handling external service errors, match on structured error codes (e.g., AWS error codes) rather than parsing exception message strings, which are fragile and may change.
mode_rationale: Simple error handling convention about matching on structured codes

rule: Do not return HTTP 404 for corrupted or invalid internal data — if the resource exists but contains bad data, that is a server error (5xx), not a 'not found' error.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about correct HTTP status mapping

rule: When pattern matching on related values, match them as a tuple in a single match expression rather than nesting matches, for conciseness and readability.
mode_rationale: Simple code style convention about tuple matching vs nested matches

rule: A value passed to a component should have a type the component actually accepts; never pass a mismatched type and rely on implicit coercion.
mode_rationale: Single type safety convention about matching component contracts

rule: Give configuration files meaningful, distinct names rather than generic names like application.conf; they can reference each other by resource classpath.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for configuration files

rule: Never leave data-test attributes empty; always provide a meaningful, descriptive test identifier.
mode_rationale: Single convention: always provide meaningful data-test attribute values

rule: Wrap debounce functions in useMemo (or useCallback) so the debounce reference is not regenerated on every re-render, which would break the debounce behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention for debounce in React

rule: Components that consume global store via hooks like useStore should be memoized to prevent unnecessary re-renders.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention for store consumers

rule: Method names should accurately reflect their actual scope and behavior; if a function only works for a specific provider or context, the name should indicate that restriction.
mode_rationale: Simple naming accuracy convention

rule: Method and variable names should accurately describe what they return — a method returning a count should include 'count' in its name, not just the entity name.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for method return type clarity

rule: When a query method filters out certain records (e.g., archived items), make the filtering behavior explicit in the method name to avoid ambiguity.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for query methods

rule: Method names should clearly communicate their behavior (e.g., include 'latest' if the method returns only the most recent result) so callers understand semantics without reading the implementation.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention

rule: When a method creates a domain object with additional validation or lookups beyond simple construction, its name should reflect that (e.g., 'createValidEnvironmentUpdate' rather than a generic name).
mode_rationale: Naming convention for methods that validate during construction

rule: Name methods with a leading verb (e.g., 'fetch', 'create', 'validate') to clearly indicate the action being performed.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: lead method names with a verb

rule: When defining metrics or timers, ensure each description accurately describes the specific time range or quantity being measured — do not copy-paste descriptions between metrics that measure different things.
mode_rationale: Simple accuracy rule for metric definitions

rule: Ensure metric names used in code match exactly between the metric definition and the metric recording call to avoid silent metric loss.
mode_rationale: Simple naming consistency rule for metrics

rule: When adding counters or metrics for one variant of a feature, ensure equivalent metrics exist for all variants to maintain observability parity.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline about observability parity

rule: When adding a new feature path that parallels an existing one, ensure it has equivalent observability (counters, metrics, logging) as the original path.
mode_rationale: Convention to maintain observability parity

rule: A microservice's local config file should only contain settings required to bootstrap the service; all other settings must be defined in the parent platform (nucleus) so they can be overridden via central config.
mode_rationale: Single architecture rule about config location

rule: When introducing a new shared policy, utility, or abstraction, migrate existing callers that should use it rather than leaving them on the old approach.
mode_rationale: Convention: migrate existing callers when introducing new shared abstractions

rule: Replace deprecated `javax.xml.bind` (and other javax packages) with their `jakarta` equivalents to stay current with the Jakarta EE migration.
mode_rationale: Single migration convention for package imports

rule: Avoid deep nesting selectors (e.g., `&&&&&`) to override library styles; prefer `!important` or targeted overrides when overriding Ant Design styles.
mode_rationale: Single convention: avoid deep nesting selectors to override library styles

rule: Avoid creating multiple similar DTOs (e.g., StorageDto, StorageInfoDto, StorageViewDto) when a single DTO with an additional field would be simpler and clearer.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture convention against DTO sprawl

rule: Feature flags with long lifespans should be referenced in as few places as possible to reduce state inconsistency risk and simplify future removal.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: reference long-lived feature flags in as few places as possible

rule: Keep the root of a feature directory shallow by nesting new component folders under an existing 'components' directory rather than creating new top-level folders.
mode_rationale: Simple directory structure convention

rule: Avoid accumulating @ts-ignore comments; fix the underlying type issue or, when suppression is unavoidable, document the specific error being suppressed.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: prefer fixing types over suppressing them

rule: When adding authentication or authorization wrappers to components, update existing tests to mock the auth layer to prevent unintended API calls during testing.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: mock auth layers when testing authenticated components

rule: In component unit tests, mock the custom hook rather than the underlying API calls — this avoids unnecessary async waits for mocked requests and keeps component tests focused on rendering logic, with hook logic tested separately.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about what to mock

rule: Use the roles-and-permissions system to model access control rather than writing ad-hoc conditional authorization checks; create tech debt tickets if refactoring is needed to unblock a release.
mode_rationale: Prefer existing authz system over ad-hoc checks

rule: When a domain concept (e.g., feature flags) appears in multiple event types, model it the same way (same target type, same field structure) for consistency and queryability.
mode_rationale: Consistency convention for domain concept modeling in events

rule: Use modern default imports (`import React from 'react'`) instead of namespace imports for React.
Place dependency injection bindings in the module they belong to (e.g., template bindings in TemplatesInjectionModule) rather than in a parent or sibling module to maintain cohesion.
mode_rationale: Single organization rule: place DI bindings in the owning module

rule: When using Docker BuildKit secrets, mount them directly as environment variables using the env option instead of reading secret files with cat.
mode_rationale: Single convention for how to handle Docker BuildKit secrets

rule: Keep React components thin by moving data-fetching, error handling, and state logic into context providers, exposing only methods and state to components.
mode_rationale: Single convention: keep components thin by moving logic to context providers

rule: When a callback is only used inside a useEffect, move the function definition inside the effect to avoid an unnecessary useCallback wrapper.
mode_rationale: Simple React convention to reduce unnecessary wrappers

rule: Extract hardcoded constants (URLs, config values, magic strings) into dedicated constants files rather than defining them inline in hook or component files.
mode_rationale: Single convention about constant placement

rule: Extract utility functions and helper logic out of component files into dedicated utils files to keep components focused and helpers reusable.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization convention

rule: Table column definitions, configuration objects, and other static data that do not depend on component props or state should be defined outside the component to avoid unnecessary re-creation on each render.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention about static data placement

rule: Test fixture creators and builders should live in the shared test utilities library (e.g., `testslib`) so they can be reused across test suites, not duplicated in individual spec files.
mode_rationale: Single test organization convention

rule: When refactoring code that moves responsibility from one class to another, ensure equivalent unit tests are created for the new class covering the same behavior.
mode_rationale: Single guideline: ensure tests follow moved logic

rule: When building abstractions that will serve multiple use cases, name them for the general capability (e.g., 'LogRetriever') rather than the first specific use case (e.g., 'BuildLogRetriever').
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for reusable abstractions

rule: Function and field names should match their domain-level meaning (e.g., use 'hub' when filtering by hub, not 'author') to avoid confusion between API concepts and internal filters.
mode_rationale: Naming convention about domain-level terminology

rule: If a file exports a React component, name it as a component (PascalCase, no 'Render' prefix or 'Util' suffix); file names should reflect what the module actually is, not how it was originally conceived.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for file names matching exports

rule: Name test fixtures to describe the data or resource they provide (e.g., `git_credentials`) rather than using generic or action-oriented names.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for test fixtures

rule: Replace magic numbers (especially z-index values, pixel sizes, and thresholds) with named constants, and add a comment explaining why the specific value was chosen.
mode_rationale: Simple code clarity rule about magic numbers

rule: When a method serves a specific use case within an overloaded domain concept, name it to convey that specific intent rather than reusing a generic name.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for specific method intent

rule: Name variables to reflect their lifecycle state (e.g., `persistedDeployment` instead of a generic name) so readers immediately know whether the value is in-memory, persisted, or pending.
mode_rationale: Naming convention about state clarity

rule: Never use .get() on Scala Option values; use .map/.getOrElse or pattern matching to safely handle the None case with a meaningful fallback.
Do not define React components inside another component's render body; the inner component is recreated on every re-render, causing unnecessary unmount/remount cycles and state loss.
mode_rationale: Single performance rule about React component definition placement

rule: When using styled-components, define all styles in the styled component definition rather than using inline `style` props — this keeps styling centralized and consistent.
Do not import components from another team's or domain's section directory; instead, extract shared functionality into a common reusable package to avoid unintended coupling and breaking changes.
mode_rationale: Simple import boundary rule

rule: Do not add eslint-disable comments to suppress the exhaustive-deps rule in useCallback or other hooks. Ignoring dependencies in useCallback is especially dangerous because the function will silently use stale values. Instead, fix the dependency array.
mode_rationale: Single lint rule enforcement convention

rule: Avoid merging to develop while dependencies point to a custom branch; pin dependencies to the develop version or merge a follow-up PR immediately after to correct the reference.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about dependency pinning before merging

rule: Always use state setter functions to update React state; never mutate state objects directly, as it leads to unexpected rendering behavior.
mode_rationale: Fundamental React convention about state immutability

rule: Avoid `@ts-ignore` and `as` type assertions as they suppress TypeScript's type checking and can mask real bugs that surface at runtime.
mode_rationale: Single rule about avoiding type suppression

rule: When logic is extracted into a new class, that class must have its own dedicated unit tests — do not rely on tests that existed for the original caller.
mode_rationale: Simple testing rule: extracted classes need their own unit tests

rule: When introducing a new function or API endpoint, add corresponding tests to verify its behavior rather than relying solely on tests for the old code path.
mode_rationale: Single rule: add tests when introducing new functions

rule: Do not use the explicit 'any' type in TypeScript; use proper types, generics, or 'unknown' to maintain type safety.
mode_rationale: Simple type safety rule to avoid 'any'

rule: When using styled-components, do not use `className` for styling — use styled-component composition instead. Reserve `className` as a last resort for deeply nested third-party components you cannot reach otherwise.
mode_rationale: Single styling convention for styled-components

rule: Do not leave commented-out code in the codebase unless it is tracked by a ticket; prefer removing it and referencing the ticket if the code will be needed later.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about removing commented-out code

rule: Do not use deprecated or legacy product terminology (e.g., 'Detwirl') in new code, variable names, route names, or comments — even when refactoring code that originally used those terms.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention to avoid legacy terms

rule: Never import from a package's dist/build output folder within the same package; use relative paths instead.
mode_rationale: Single import convention against dist folder imports

rule: Consolidate all imports from the same module into a single import statement; never have duplicate import lines from the same path.
mode_rationale: Simple import deduplication rule

rule: Do not commit empty or placeholder files (e.g., empty test files, scratch files) to the repository; remove them before merging.
mode_rationale: Simple repo hygiene rule

rule: Do not use eslint-disable comments to work around type compatibility issues; fix the types so the code is naturally compatible.
mode_rationale: Single convention about fixing types instead of disabling lint rules

rule: Never hardcode color values in components; always reference colors from the theme or design palette to ensure consistency and support theming.
mode_rationale: Convention to always reference theme colors

rule: Never hardcode documentation or support URLs; use the project's support utils, HelpLink component, or docs base path hook so links stay correct across environments.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use support utils and hooks for documentation URLs

rule: Route paths like '/admin' should not be hardcoded in component files; use constants or a route configuration to keep navigation paths centralized and maintainable.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to centralize route path constants

rule: Never import mock modules or test utilities in production source files; this causes build issues and increases bundle size. Duplicate the needed functionality as a utility instead.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: never import test utilities in production files

rule: Replace nested ternary expressions with switch statements or early returns for readability, especially when matching against enum or state values.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention for conditional expressions

rule: Generic infrastructure code (e.g., AWS SDK wrappers) should live in shared modules like common-core, not in domain-specific packages; replace domain-specific config objects with primitive parameters to remove coupling.
mode_rationale: Simple architectural convention about where generic code belongs

rule: Methods that don't depend on instance state should be made static by moving them to the companion object.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about static method placement

rule: When detwirling Scala templates, move data-fetching logic into dedicated API endpoints (e.g. v4 routes) so React components can own their own data fetching and default state, reducing coupling to Scala templates.
mode_rationale: Multi-step migration workflow: create API endpoints, move data-fetching logic, update React components to own data

rule: Side effects like changing user selection or UI state after an async operation must be placed in the success handler, not unconditionally after the operation completes.
mode_rationale: Single error-handling rule about side effect placement

rule: After a mutative action (restore, update, archive) on a detail view, refetch the current entity's data rather than navigating the user away from the page.
mode_rationale: Single behavioral convention for post-mutation UI updates

rule: API response type names must make sense for every endpoint that uses them; if a response is shared across endpoints, choose a generic name rather than one tied to a specific operation.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for shared API response types

rule: Methods that skip authorization checks should be named with a clear suffix like `withoutAuthz` to make the security implications obvious at the call site.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for security-sensitive methods

rule: Name binders, helpers, and utility methods after what they actually do, not what they were copied from or vaguely related to.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for utility methods

rule: Config key names should be explicit and self-descriptive so future readers can understand their purpose without looking up external documentation.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: config keys should be self-descriptive

rule: Name configuration objects with a suffix like Config or Options to distinguish them from React components (e.g. persistenceCheckboxConfig instead of persistenceCheckbox).
mode_rationale: Naming convention for config objects

rule: When introducing a new domain class that extends or enriches an existing one, prefix it with the parent context name and use richer types instead of raw strings.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for domain class hierarchy

rule: Name DTOs after the domain concept they represent (e.g., DataPlaneDto for workspace data plane config), not after implementation details or generic terms.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for DTOs

rule: Name error types and utility classes based on their general scope of applicability, not after the specific feature or module where they were first introduced, so they can be reused across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for reusable error types

rule: Name audit events, permissions, and action identifiers to describe the action's scope rather than coupling them to the current role that performs them, to avoid renames when access policies change.
mode_rationale: Naming convention to decouple names from current role assignments

rule: When a field contains items filtered by the requester's permissions, name it to convey that scope (e.g., `accessibleHardwareTiers` instead of `hardwareTiers`) so consumers understand the data is user-scoped.
mode_rationale: Naming convention: field names should convey permission scope

rule: Name fields precisely to indicate what they contain — e.g., use 'username' instead of a generic 'userId' when the field holds a username string, not an ID.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: field names should match what they contain

rule: When a file contains only one class or object, name the file after that class to make it easy to locate.
mode_rationale: Simple file naming convention

rule: When creating a new file for a specific feature, consider whether the file could host related functionality in the future and name it generically to avoid premature renaming.
mode_rationale: Single file naming convention

rule: Name filters, methods, and query objects to explicitly describe their actual behavior and scope (e.g., `OwnedAndCollaboratingProjects` instead of a generic name) to prevent incorrect assumptions.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for descriptive method names

rule: Name event handler functions after the semantic action they perform (e.g. onConfirm, onSave) rather than generic UI actions (e.g. onClose) when the handler represents a specific business action.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for event handlers

rule: Function names should clearly describe what the function returns or does; prefer specific names like `getFileAbsolutePath` over generic or ambiguous names.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for functions

rule: Choose identifier names that are specific to the domain concept they represent (e.g., `reasonForChangeId` over `eSigId`) so they don't conflict with future features that may share the broader concept.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention about domain-specific identifier names

rule: Use precise verbs that describe the actual operation — use 'unset' when clearing a value, not 'delete' which implies removing a resource entirely.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: use precise verbs matching the actual operation

rule: Name method parameters to describe their purpose or behavior (e.g., filterByUserAndOrgIds instead of userAndOrganizationIds) so callers understand how the argument is used without reading the implementation.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for method parameters

rule: Permission names should accurately reflect what they enforce — a permission that only checks authentication should be named 'requireAuthenticatedUser', not 'canListEndpoints', which implies broader authorization.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for permission identifiers

rule: When defining a props type for a React component, name it after the component (e.g., ConnectionInstructionsProps for ConnectionInstructions) rather than reusing or extending an unrelated type name.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for React props types

rule: Name creation request DTOs as `New[EntityName]` and update request DTOs as `Updated[EntityName]` to clearly distinguish their purpose in the API contract.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: New[Entity] for creation, Updated[Entity] for updates

rule: Name service interfaces based on their actual responsibility (e.g., 'ProjectRepository') rather than implementation details like authorization bypass (e.g., 'ProjectServiceWithoutAuthorization').
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming convention for service interfaces

rule: Name styled components after their semantic role or usage context (e.g., `IconDanger`, `IconFailure`) rather than generic or color-based names.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for styled components

rule: When testing an actual implementation, name the variable under test accurately (e.g., `authorizer`) instead of prefixing with `mock` when it is not a mock.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: don't prefix with mock when testing real implementations

rule: Test names should precisely describe the behavior and constraints being verified, including boundary conditions like 'to org that they are a part of'.
mode_rationale: Single convention: test names must precisely describe behavior and constraints

rule: Name utility methods to reflect their specific domain purpose (e.g., `composeOrdering` instead of `compose`) and add documentation explaining their intended use.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for utility methods

rule: Name variables and fields to accurately reflect their actual usage pattern — do not call something a 'cache' if it is not used for lookup-based retrieval.
mode_rationale: Single naming accuracy convention

rule: When a variable is referenced in multiple places, give it a descriptive name that conveys its semantic role (e.g., 'unarchivedResources' instead of a generic name) to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention about descriptive variable names

rule: Assign boolean literals to descriptively named variables before passing them as function arguments to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Readability convention for boolean arguments

rule: Define React context default values as a named constant and reuse it in both createContext and the provider return to ensure consistent shape and easier extension.
mode_rationale: React context pattern convention

rule: Use named constants (e.g., Status.CONFLICT) instead of magic numbers (e.g., 409) for HTTP status codes to improve readability and maintainability.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming convention for HTTP statuses

rule: When renaming or naming functions and events, ensure the name accurately represents the full scope of the behavior (e.g., plural if it handles multiple items).
mode_rationale: Single naming convention about accuracy

rule: Import style modules using namespace imports (`import * as Styles from './styles'`) so styled components are clearly identifiable as components in JSX.
mode_rationale: Single import convention for style modules

rule: Prefix OpenAPI schema names with their module namespace to prevent silent collisions where one module's schema definition masks another's.
mode_rationale: Single convention: prefix schema names with module namespace

rule: Never catch all Throwables; catch only NonFatal exceptions to avoid swallowing fatal JVM errors like OutOfMemoryError.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: catch NonFatal only, not all Throwables

rule: When adding retry logic, filter on specific exception types or messages rather than catching all exceptions, to avoid masking real intermittent failures.
mode_rationale: Single error-handling convention for retry logic

rule: Use the narrowest fixture scope possible (function over session) to avoid unnecessary environment setup when running subsets of tests.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about fixture scope

rule: Store values in the native type the UI component understands (e.g., Date objects for date pickers) and only transform to string/serialized format when sending to the backend.
mode_rationale: Single convention about storing native types and serializing at boundaries

rule: React hooks must only be called from React function components or custom hooks — never from plain utility functions, as this violates the Rules of Hooks and can cause crashes when hook call order changes at runtime.
Never commit valid JWT tokens, API keys, or other secrets to the codebase, even in test or mock files.
mode_rationale: Blanket security rule — same as no-secrets-in-source

rule: When a data fetch fails, show a meaningful error state rather than leaving the page blank — even if a toast notification has been shown.
mode_rationale: Single convention: show error state instead of blank page

rule: Never log authentication tokens, secrets, or credentials; review log statements to ensure sensitive values are not included.
mode_rationale: Security convention against logging secrets

rule: When an API requires a context object, use a proper default (e.g., DominoContext.uncorrelated) instead of passing null, even if the context is not currently used.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about proper defaults

rule: Sensitive credentials like API tokens must not be stored in MongoDB or other application databases; use a secrets manager (e.g., Vault) or pass them as one-time arguments.
mode_rationale: Clear security rule about credential storage

rule: When adding a new authentication mechanism, add dedicated test cases covering the new auth flow.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention for auth flows

rule: When adding a new configuration value, always provide a sensible default fallback in the application code and add the value to deployment chart configs; a missing config value should not cause the application to crash with an opaque error.
mode_rationale: Configuration convention requiring sensible defaults

rule: When adding new fields to a DTO, make them optional to avoid breaking changes for existing consumers.
mode_rationale: Single API compatibility convention

rule: When adding a new prop to a shared component, make it optional (with a default or callback) if not all consumers need it, to avoid breaking existing usages.
mode_rationale: Simple API design rule for shared component props

rule: When adding new fields to API responses, verify that existing clients using the API will not break — adding a new field to a response object is generally safe for loosely-typed clients but should be verified.
mode_rationale: Backward compatibility convention for API responses

rule: When adding new entries to the color palette or design tokens, ensure they are also added to the corresponding Storybook story.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to keep Storybook in sync when adding design tokens

rule: Core-ui packages should contain only foundational UI components with no API request logic; components that make API calls belong in the higher-level ui package to avoid cyclic dependencies when packages are split.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architecture boundary rule for core-ui packages

rule: Do not return the result of an assignment expression; split the assignment and return into separate statements for clarity.
mode_rationale: Simple code clarity rule

rule: Components should not rely on knowing the internal implementation details of callback props (e.g., assuming a callback will never throw) when determining control flow such as success/error feedback.
mode_rationale: Simple component design principle

rule: Do not mark test functions as async if they contain no await expressions; unnecessary async adds confusion and overhead.

Do not modify shared utility methods (e.g., adding case-insensitive matching) to fix a single call site; instead, create a separate method with a comment explaining why the relaxed behavior is needed.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention: don't modify shared methods for single call sites

rule: Do not catch exceptions only to rethrow them without adding context or handling; either handle the exception meaningfully, wrap it in the appropriate type (e.g., Left for Either), or let it propagate naturally.
mode_rationale: Single error-handling convention about meaningful catch blocks

rule: Do not import from a package's own public API barrel within that package's internal files; use direct relative imports instead.
mode_rationale: Single import rule to prevent circular dependencies

rule: Do not nest configuration providers inside feature components; instead, use prop composition (e.g., passing a ReactNode to a prop like emptyMessage) to achieve the same behavior without tight coupling.
Remove console.log and other console statements before merging; do not use console logging as a workaround for unused variable errors.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to remove console statements before merging

rule: When creating new classes based on existing ones, update class-level comments to reflect the new class's purpose rather than leaving copied comments from the source class.
mode_rationale: Convention to update comments when copying classes

rule: Do not merge unused methods or code; if it's needed for follow-up work, add it in the follow-up PR.
mode_rationale: Code hygiene rule against merging unused code

rule: Do not use deprecated HTML attributes like frameBorder; use CSS equivalents instead.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: use CSS instead of deprecated attributes

rule: Do not use the deprecated `margins` key in styled components; use the project's themeHelper or standard spacing utilities instead.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to avoid deprecated margins key

rule: Never modify antd library CSS classes from application code; instead, create wrapper components (e.g., IconWrapper) to apply custom styling without touching library internals.
mode_rationale: Single styling convention about not touching library CSS

rule: Do not add retry logic for operations that are already retried at a lower layer, as this compounds retry attempts and can cause excessive delays or cascading failures.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention: avoid compounding retries across layers

rule: Do not check the same condition twice in contiguous lines — consolidate duplicate conditional checks into a single block.
mode_rationale: Simple code style rule about consolidating conditions

rule: Log each error once at the appropriate level; do not duplicate error logging calls for the same error.
mode_rationale: Single rule: log each error once

rule: API specs should define a single canonical response code per operation; do not add a duplicate status code as a workaround — replace the incorrect one instead.
mode_rationale: Single API spec convention

rule: Do not duplicate URL path parameters in the request body — maintain a single source of truth for each value to avoid conflicts.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention about single source of truth

rule: Do not declare the same utility, constant, or type in multiple locations; consolidate into a single shared definition.
Do not leave empty or placeholder docstrings/comments; either write meaningful documentation or remove the block entirely.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation hygiene rule

rule: Do not use environment-specific values (such as internal AWS account IDs) as hardcoded fallback defaults in application code, because they mask real configuration propagation failures in other environments.
mode_rationale: Configuration convention against hardcoded env-specific values

rule: Do not explicitly import from index files (e.g., './module/index'); import from the directory path instead.
mode_rationale: Single convention: import from directory path, not explicit index files

rule: Avoid using explicit `return` statements in Scala; rely on expression-based control flow instead, as `return` has surprising semantics in Scala.
mode_rationale: Single Scala coding convention

rule: Do not place success-only logic in a finally block; put it in the try block after the operation that must succeed first.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention about correct placement of success logic

rule: Router components that are mounted at all times must not trigger global side effects like loaders or spinners scoped to a particular section.
mode_rationale: Single routing architecture rule

rule: Do not use workarounds like closing modals in unrelated lifecycle points; instead, restructure the data flow so state transitions are handled in the correct place.
mode_rationale: Single convention: restructure data flow instead of using lifecycle workarounds

rule: Shared/common components must not depend on ambient context like currentProject; instead accept the required data (e.g., projectId) as a prop so the component works in any scope.
mode_rationale: Convention to accept data via props not ambient context

rule: Shared or common components must not contain hardcoded options or test data; all values should be passed via props or configuration to ensure correctness across all consumers.
mode_rationale: Single convention: shared components must receive values via props, not hardcode them

rule: Do not reuse a permission-check or authorization method for unrelated purposes; extract a dedicated method so that future changes to one check do not silently break another.
mode_rationale: Principle against reusing methods for unrelated purposes

rule: Index files should only re-export from other modules; do not define function implementations directly in index files.
Do not add custom styles to shared UI components inline; if the component's design doesn't match requirements, file a ticket to update the shared component or discuss with UX.
mode_rationale: Single convention: don't add custom styles to shared components inline

rule: Internal infrastructure errors and retry failures should not bubble up to user-facing output; keep them in system logs only.
mode_rationale: Single convention: keep infrastructure errors in system logs, not user-facing output

rule: Never use an invalid path as a placeholder link href; use '#' with a TODO comment instead, or wait until the real URL is available.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about placeholder href values

rule: Do not allow language-specific sentinel values like the string "None" to propagate through the system as valid data; throw an exception or use a proper domain-level default instead.
mode_rationale: Simple rule against propagating language-specific sentinels as valid data

rule: Do not wrap static strings in JSX expression containers (`{'some string'}`); use the string directly in the JSX template.
mode_rationale: Simple syntax convention about static strings in JSX

rule: Do not prefix internal component names with third-party library names (e.g., 'Ant') as it couples the component identity to the underlying library.
mode_rationale: Naming convention: don't prefix components with library names

rule: New Storybook stories should not use the `.mdx` format; use the current CSF (Component Story Format) instead.
mode_rationale: Single convention about which Storybook format to use

rule: Avoid nesting CSS class selectors inside styled components; apply styles directly to a new or existing styled component instead.
mode_rationale: CSS-in-JS convention: avoid nested class selectors

rule: The theme should remain general-purpose; do not add component-specific one-off colors to the theme object — map to existing semantic tokens instead.
mode_rationale: Theme architecture convention against component-specific colors

rule: Remove developer names, nicknames, and personal debug markers from code before merging.
mode_rationale: Simple hygiene rule: no developer names in code

rule: Never send secrets in plain text over the network in execution payloads; use vault keys or secret references instead.
mode_rationale: Security convention to use vault references instead of plaintext secrets

rule: Do not add partial or poorly-implemented HATEOAS fields (like URI links) to API responses; remove unused API fields and add them properly when there is a concrete consumer need.
mode_rationale: Single API design rule: don't add partial HATEOAS fields without a consumer

rule: Do not add production API surface (methods, wrappers, interfaces) solely to support test code; move test-only utilities to the test module or use the underlying client directly in tests.
Do not use Promise.all when resolving a single promise — it adds unnecessary complexity with no concurrency benefit.
mode_rationale: Simple anti-pattern to avoid

rule: Do not use `Provider` for dependency injection unless there is an actual circular dependency; import the dependency directly for simpler, more readable code.
mode_rationale: Single dependency injection convention

rule: Use plain arrow function components (`const MyComponent = () => {}`) instead of typing with `React.FC` to follow project convention.
mode_rationale: Project convention for component typing style

rule: Do not include an 'all' value in REST API filter parameters; omitting the filter should return all results by default.
mode_rationale: Single API design convention about filter defaults

rule: Do not write comments that merely restate what the code already clearly says; remove extraneous comments that add no information beyond the code itself.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention to remove comments that restate what the code says

rule: Avoid adding a boolean toggle prop that only has effect when a companion prop is also provided; combine them into a single optional prop instead.
mode_rationale: API design rule to combine coupled boolean props

rule: Do not use optional chaining (?.) on a variable that has already been confirmed to be defined by a preceding conditional check (e.g., a ternary guard).
mode_rationale: Single code hygiene rule about unnecessary optional chaining

rule: Do not commit secrets, tokens, or credentials (e.g., in .npmrc) to version control; use a secrets management solution instead.
mode_rationale: Single security rule about not committing secrets

rule: Files within a package must not import from their own package's published name (e.g., `@domino/ui`); use relative imports instead to avoid circular dependencies and bundling issues.
Never suppress or skip useEffect dependency warnings; use a file-level variable or a ref to track mount status instead of an empty dependency array with missing deps.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about not suppressing useEffect dependency warnings

rule: Do not prefix variable names with underscores in frontend code; use descriptive names instead.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for frontend code

rule: Do not mark test functions as async unless the test body actually awaits something; unnecessary async adds confusion and may mask timing issues.
Do not wrap trivial computations (e.g., a simple boolean expression) in useMemo; reserve memoization for genuinely expensive calculations.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention about reserving memoization for expensive work

rule: When constructing URLs by concatenating a base URL and a path, normalize the result to prevent malformed URLs with double slashes or other artifacts.
mode_rationale: Defensive coding guideline for URL construction

rule: Omit the `={true}` assignment for boolean JSX props; the bare attribute name defaults to true.
mode_rationale: JSX syntax convention

rule: When an Ant Design table column simply displays the field value without custom formatting, use `dataIndex` alone and omit the `render` prop.
mode_rationale: Ant Design table simplification convention

rule: Each React component should live in its own file; do not export multiple components with similar interfaces but different implementations from the same file. Avoid prefixing component names with vendor library names (e.g., 'Ant').
mode_rationale: Simple file organization and naming convention

rule: Place feature components in descriptive subfolders (e.g., guardrails/project/models/) rather than dumping them at the module root.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use descriptive subfolders instead of dumping at module root

rule: Place entity classes in packages that reflect their source or ownership — entities retrieved from an external API belong in the upstream package, not alongside internal domain entities.
mode_rationale: Package organization convention based on entity source

rule: When multiple async operations are independent of each other, execute them in parallel rather than awaiting them sequentially to reduce latency.
mode_rationale: Single performance guideline about parallel execution

rule: When a method's responsibility is to validate an entity, pass the entire entity to it rather than having the caller decide which fields will be checked — the validation method should own that decision.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention for validation method signatures

rule: When logging exceptions, pass the exception object as a parameter to the logger method so the full stack trace is captured, rather than only logging the message string.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to pass exception objects to loggers

rule: Pass only the specific values a class needs (e.g., a boolean flag) rather than injecting an entire settings or configuration object, to reduce coupling and simplify testing.
mode_rationale: Dependency injection convention to reduce coupling

rule: When a component receives data from a single object, pass the object as one prop rather than spreading its fields, to keep the component's prop interface simple and explicit.
mode_rationale: Single convention about prop passing style

rule: Permission-denied error messages should clearly state what the user cannot do and direct them to contact an administrator for resolution, using a consistent format across the application.
mode_rationale: Error message convention for permission denials

rule: Database record definitions should clearly show what is stored without requiring developers to trace through codecs and external DTOs; do not reuse API DTOs as persistence record fields since changes in the API layer should not affect the persistence layer.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about persistence layer independence

rule: Write BDD/feature test descriptions as user stories (As a [role], I'd like to [action], So that [benefit]) to clarify intent and maintain a user-centric perspective.
mode_rationale: Test writing convention for BDD descriptions

rule: When depending on a branch reference (e.g., a Git branch for a package), pin it to the target branch (e.g., develop) before merging the PR to avoid shipping unstable references.
mode_rationale: Single convention: pin branch references to stable target before merging

rule: Pin exact versions for new dependencies in package.json rather than using semver ranges to ensure reproducible builds.
mode_rationale: Single dependency management rule

rule: Dependency injection bindings should be placed in the module that owns the feature (e.g., TemplatesInjectionModule), not in a parent or sibling module (e.g., ProjectsInjectionModule).
mode_rationale: DI placement convention

rule: In Scala, place utility functions that are specific to a class in its companion object rather than leaving them as loose functions or mixing them into unrelated classes.
mode_rationale: Single convention: Scala utility functions go in companion objects

rule: Custom React hooks should be organized in a dedicated `hooks` directory rather than placed alongside component files, to maintain a consistent and discoverable project structure.
mode_rationale: Simple file organization convention

rule: Methods should be placed in the service that owns the domain concept, not in a related but semantically incorrect service (e.g., launcher config belongs in the launcher service, not the launcher job service).
mode_rationale: Single convention: methods belong in the service that owns the domain concept

rule: New configuration settings should be added to the most relevant existing settings class rather than a generic top-level Settings file, using optional fields with sensible defaults to avoid touching application.conf.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about config class placement

rule: Test mock data should live in the shared mocks package rather than co-located with individual test files, so mocks can be reused across test suites.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence test organization convention

rule: In end-to-end and integration tests, poll on status changes instead of using fixed sleep/delay calls, which are flaky and waste time.
Test names should use positive statements that match what the test actually asserts (e.g., 'these providers are supported') rather than negative exclusions (e.g., 'all except X are supported').
mode_rationale: Naming convention: use positive statements in test names

rule: Ensure PR descriptions and summaries accurately reflect the actual code changes, including correct names for resources referenced.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation accuracy rule

rule: Avoid mapping the same source value to multiple DTO fields — each field should represent a distinct piece of data.
mode_rationale: Single data modeling rule against duplicate mappings

rule: Do not wrap calls in try/catch when the called method already handles exceptions internally — redundant error handling adds noise and can swallow useful context.
mode_rationale: Simple error-handling convention
