rule: Avoid duplicating information across telemetry event fields; if a value is already captured in one field (e.g., project as the location), do not repeat it in another field (e.g., affecting).
mode_rationale: Single convention about avoiding duplicate telemetry data

rule: Avoid setting both `data-test` and `testId` props on the same component; use only the one required by your testing framework.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: use one test selector prop, not both

rule: Avoid using the `return` keyword in Scala; it is a major code smell and should be reworked to use expression-based control flow instead.
mode_rationale: Scala style rule to avoid return keyword in favor of expression-based control flow

rule: Domain entities should not contain JSON serialization mappings; serialization concerns belong in the infrastructure or presentation layer to avoid constraining the domain with a specific serialized format.
mode_rationale: Layering rule keeping serialization out of domain

rule: Do not use fetch calls or state setters within boolean expressions; side effects should be in explicit control flow statements.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about side effect placement

rule: Do not place side effects inside render props, as they execute on every re-render; extract side effects into event handlers or lifecycle hooks instead.
mode_rationale: React convention to avoid side effects in render props

rule: Never invoke state setters or trigger API requests inside useMemo; useMemo is for derived computations only — side effects belong in useEffect or event handlers.
mode_rationale: Single React anti-pattern rule

rule: Do not silently mask parsing or authentication errors by falling through to an alternative method; surface the error as a BadRequest or Unauthorized response.
mode_rationale: Single convention: surface errors instead of silently falling through

rule: Do not skip or comment out tests without documenting the reason and creating a JIRA ticket to re-enable them.
mode_rationale: Simple rule requiring documentation and tickets for skipped tests

rule: Do not use React state for values that can be easily calculated or derived from existing state or props.
mode_rationale: Simple React convention about derived values

rule: Never call state setters directly in a React component's render body, as this can cause infinite re-render loops; use useEffect or event handlers instead.
mode_rationale: React safety rule against state setters in render body

rule: Service layer code should use storage-agnostic terminology (e.g., 'entity', 'record') rather than database-specific terms like 'document' or 'row', since the service layer should not know about the underlying storage implementation.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for service layer abstraction

rule: Use JSX interpolation with template expressions instead of the + operator for string concatenation inside JSX.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use template expressions instead of + in JSX

rule: Define styled components outside the React component function, never inline within JSX.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about styled component placement

rule: Do not place success-only logic in finally blocks; put it in the try block after the operation that must succeed, since finally runs regardless of whether an exception occurred.
Do not add production wrapper methods solely to support test code; instead, have tests use the underlying client or SDK directly.
mode_rationale: Single convention about production code purity

rule: Never throw errors from a React component's render function; it will trigger the error boundary at best or crash the UI at worst. Handle invalid states gracefully with fallback UI.
mode_rationale: React convention to never throw from render

rule: Do not add fields to a shared model that only apply to some variants; if not all instances of a type will have the field, it does not belong in the common model.
mode_rationale: Simple modeling rule about variant-specific fields

rule: Do not import from internal or transitive dependencies that are not explicitly listed in the package's package.json; create custom types or add the dependency explicitly instead.
mode_rationale: Simple dependency management rule

rule: Do not wrap synchronous operations in Future; use simpler return types like Option or Either when no async work is performed.
mode_rationale: Single convention to avoid async wrapping of synchronous code

rule: Do not pass props to a component that does not use them; only pass what the component's interface expects.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: don't pass unused props

rule: Do not wrap JSX in React.Fragment (or <Fragment>) when a shorthand fragment (<>) or no wrapper at all suffices.
mode_rationale: Single convention: avoid unnecessary Fragment wrappers in JSX

rule: Do not use template literal backticks when a plain string suffices; backticks should only be used when interpolation or multiline is needed.
mode_rationale: Style rule about when to use template literals

rule: Do not create wrapper functions that simply delegate to another function with the same signature; call the underlying function directly.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about avoiding unnecessary delegation

rule: Never call `.get` on an `Option` type; use pattern matching (`match { case None => ... case Some(v) => ... }`) or a safe alternative like `.getOrElse`/`.fold` to handle the empty case explicitly.
mode_rationale: Safety rule against unsafe Option.get calls

rule: Do not ship functionality that will not be tested by QE; if a feature is supported, it must work and have test coverage.
mode_rationale: Single rule about ensuring test coverage for shipped features

rule: Only include fields in wrapper/DTO types that are actually consumed by callers; remove fields carried over from the underlying SDK that have no current use.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to keep wrapper types minimal

rule: Do not pass arguments to a function that are not declared in its parameter list; relying on closure scope while passing a value positionally is misleading.
Do not define props in a component's interface that are never consumed by the component.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup rule about unused prop definitions

rule: Do not add props to a base or parent component that are only handled in a subclass or derived component; it is misleading for consumers who expect the prop to work at the base level. Scope such props to the component that actually implements the behavior.
mode_rationale: Single convention about prop scoping in component hierarchies

rule: Do not use useRemoteData (or similar data-fetching hooks) for mutations; these hooks are designed for initial page loads and refetching, not for write operations.
mode_rationale: Single convention: don't use data-fetching hooks for write operations

rule: Avoid wildcard imports (`import * as`) and duplicate imports from the same module; use named imports instead.
mode_rationale: Single import style convention

rule: Variables that are not React components should use camelCase naming, not PascalCase, to clearly distinguish them from components.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for non-component variables

rule: When changing shared configuration (e.g., environment images, tags), notify downstream test owners who may be affected by the change.
mode_rationale: Simple communication reminder

rule: Distinguish between null and empty collections (e.g., Seq.empty) in Scala; they are not equivalent, and confusing them causes incorrect mock behavior and subtle bugs.
mode_rationale: Single rule: distinguish null from empty collections in Scala

rule: In JSX, pass string literal props without curly braces (e.g., prop="value" not prop={"value"}) for cleaner syntax.
mode_rationale: Simple JSX syntax convention for string literal props

rule: Do not explicitly pass implicit parameters in Scala; let the compiler resolve them automatically to keep call sites clean.
mode_rationale: Scala style rule to let the compiler resolve implicits

rule: When wrapping a component, omit props from the exposed type definition that are managed internally and not intended to be controlled by the parent (e.g., disabled, options populated from a fetch).
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention for wrapper component type definitions

rule: When a value is Optional/None, omit it entirely rather than setting it to an empty string; use .map/.flatten to conditionally include only present values.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to omit None values rather than defaulting to empty strings

rule: Component names should omit generic suffixes like 'Page' or 'View' to match existing naming conventions in the codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention

rule: Place each class or major type definition in its own file, named after the class, to improve discoverability and keep files small.
mode_rationale: Simple file organization convention

rule: Each custom React hook should live in its own file rather than being co-located in a shared hooks utility file.
When a page has multiple independent API updates with different endpoints or payloads, use one form per update rather than nesting forms or sharing a single form across unrelated mutations.
mode_rationale: Architectural guideline about form-to-API mapping

rule: Record exactly one telemetry event per service operation — do not emit an event per item in a list result.
mode_rationale: Single cardinality rule for telemetry events

rule: Only add TODO comments for work that is planned within the current initiative or near-term roadmap; avoid speculative TODOs that may linger in the codebase indefinitely.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about when TODOs are appropriate

rule: Audit trails should only capture user-initiated actions, not system-driven state changes like automated job status transitions.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architectural guideline about audit scope

rule: The core routes file should only contain top-level base routes; feature-specific inner routes should be defined within their own module.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture rule about route file scope

rule: Do not set default values in OpenAPI route definitions unless you intend the API to use that value when the parameter is omitted, as per the OpenAPI spec.
mode_rationale: Single rule about OpenAPI default value semantics

rule: Ensure OpenAPI spec status codes match the actual runtime behavior of the API framework; if the framework always returns 200, document 200 rather than a theoretically correct but unimplemented status code.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to keep specs aligned with actual framework behavior

rule: Boolean props or parameters that modify default behavior should be optional with a sensible default value (typically false), rather than required.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention

rule: For internal methods, prefer adding an optional parameter to an existing function; for external-facing service APIs, keep separate methods to make the authorization context explicit.
mode_rationale: Single API design guideline about internal vs external method signatures

rule: When a function parameter has a sensible default value, make it optional with that default rather than requiring callers to always provide it.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention about parameter defaults

rule: Reorder early-return conditionals so that later branches don't need to re-check conditions already eliminated by earlier returns.
mode_rationale: Single code style convention for early returns

rule: When creating a resource that must be cross-referenced in another system, create the primary record first to obtain its ID before creating the secondary record that references it.
mode_rationale: Single convention: create primary record first to obtain ID before creating referencing record

rule: When chaining filter predicates, place the cheapest checks (e.g., simple equality or inequality) before more expensive ones to short-circuit evaluation early.
mode_rationale: Single convention: place cheapest filter predicates first for short-circuiting

rule: Absolute (package/library) imports must appear before relative imports in every file.
mode_rationale: Simple import ordering convention

rule: Keep imports organized and properly sorted in every file; disorganized imports signal careless code and make files harder to scan.
mode_rationale: Simple style convention for import ordering

rule: Module-scoped or session-scoped pytest fixtures that are shared across tests should be extracted into a separate conftest or fixture file rather than defined inside a specific test file.
mode_rationale: Test organization convention: shared fixtures go in conftest files

rule: Place new tests in a dedicated test module rather than appending them to an existing unrelated test file; group related tests together by concern.
mode_rationale: Test organization convention

rule: Import aliases for internal packages (e.g., @domino/ui/dist) should resolve to compiled output, not source code, to avoid breaking React contexts and causing build inconsistencies.
mode_rationale: Single build configuration convention

rule: Ensure the declared package in a source file matches its directory path to avoid confusing tooling and developers.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: package must match directory path

rule: Any list endpoint exposed through a public or external API must support pagination to prevent unbounded result sets; internal-only methods may defer pagination until exposure.
mode_rationale: Simple API design rule requiring pagination on list endpoints

rule: When aggregating results from multiple partitions or sub-queries, apply sorting and pagination to the combined result set rather than independently per partition.
mode_rationale: Single rule about where to apply pagination

rule: The totalCount field in paginated API responses must reflect the total number of items matching the filter in the database, not just the current page size.
Make internal limits (such as max result sizes) configurable via parameters with sensible defaults, so tests can set low values to verify limit enforcement without needing large datasets.
mode_rationale: Testing convention about configurable limits

rule: Pass actual runtime values as parameters to audit/event-tracking functions rather than relying on hardcoded defaults, so audit logs remain accurate even if defaults change.
mode_rationale: Single audit accuracy rule

rule: When caller already has the needed data (e.g., a Principal), pass it directly to downstream services rather than forcing a redundant database or API lookup.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention about avoiding redundant lookups

rule: When defining custom exception case classes, name the throwable parameter 'cause' and pass it to the Exception superclass constructor for proper exception chaining.
mode_rationale: Exception design convention for proper chaining

rule: When a component accepts color key props (e.g., text color and background color), pass the palette key string directly rather than resolving it to a color value in an intermediate variable.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about passing palette keys directly

rule: Components intended for reuse within a domain should receive configuration via props rather than reading from URL parameters, to avoid coupling to a specific routing context.
mode_rationale: Convention to decouple components from routing

rule: Always pass the caught error object to error utility functions like `getErrorMessage(err)` so they can extract meaningful information.
mode_rationale: Single error handling convention

rule: Pass function references directly as callbacks instead of wrapping them in unnecessary arrow functions.
mode_rationale: Performance convention: pass function refs directly instead of wrapping in arrow functions

rule: When a function or interface only needs a single field from a larger object, pass just that field rather than the entire object to reduce coupling and clarify dependencies.
mode_rationale: Simple coupling-reduction guideline about passing only needed fields

rule: Pass query parameters as explicit arguments in route definitions and controller methods rather than extracting them manually from the request, following the Play framework convention.
mode_rationale: Play framework convention for query parameter handling

rule: Pass select/dropdown options as a data prop (array of objects) rather than as JSX children to keep components declarative and data-driven.
mode_rationale: Simple component API convention

rule: PATCH endpoint descriptions should concisely state what happens to absent fields and call out any non-standard merge semantics (such as upsert behavior for nested collections).
mode_rationale: Single API documentation convention

rule: Use pattern matching (match/case) on enum or sealed-trait values instead of chained boolean conditions to make business rules explicit and exhaustively checked.
mode_rationale: Simple coding convention preferring pattern matching over boolean chains

rule: Use type-based pattern matching (e.g., matching on AnonymousPrincipal) instead of checking whether optional fields are defined, to make the intent clearer and leverage the type system.
mode_rationale: Single coding style preference for type-based matching

rule: When a user has permission to view a list of resources, the UI should either grant access to resource details or visually indicate (e.g., grey out links) that detail access requires a separate permission — never show clickable links that lead to blank or forbidden pages.
mode_rationale: UI convention about consistent permission-gated link behavior

rule: Add UI permissions and authorized actions in the correct scope — sidebar auth configs are only for sidebar menu visibility, not for feature-level permissions elsewhere in the app.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about where to place permission configs

rule: Tables should remember and restore the user's last sort/filter state across mounts rather than resetting to a default sort on every render.
mode_rationale: Simple UX convention: tables should remember sort/filter state across mounts

rule: When API and UI changes must ship together, coordinate the dependency version pinning — merging to develop triggers a new API version that requires a follow-up PR to pin.
mode_rationale: Multi-step coordination workflow: merge triggers version, then follow-up PR to pin

rule: Always use version specifiers (e.g., `>=`) when declaring Python dependencies in requirements files to avoid unexpected breakage from upstream changes.
mode_rationale: Single rule about version pinning in requirements files

rule: When upgrading a dependency to get new behavior, ensure the pinned version actually includes the feature you need by checking the dependency's changelog or release notes.
mode_rationale: Single practice: verify pinned version includes the needed feature

rule: Pure presentational components (containers, atoms) that are not connected to business logic should live in core-ui, not in feature-specific UI packages.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about where presentational components belong

rule: When adding domain attributes, ensure they are placed on the entity that actually owns the concept; avoid putting child-level attributes on a parent entity where they become ambiguous.
mode_rationale: Single domain-modeling convention about attribute ownership

rule: Authentication redirect logic should be placed inside the Route component that handles the protected path, not at the module top level, to prevent unintended redirects on unrelated routes.
mode_rationale: Simple architectural convention about auth redirect placement

rule: Authorization checks should be placed at the level that matches the granularity of the operation being authorized — if an action operates on a batch of entities, the auth check should cover the batch, not individual items.
mode_rationale: Principle about auth check granularity

rule: Tests that guard assumptions about a library's internal behavior should live in that library's test suite, not in downstream consumers — this prevents shipping bugs when the library changes.
mode_rationale: Simple test organization convention

rule: Business logic such as validation and URI construction belongs in the domain layer, not the infrastructure layer.
mode_rationale: Architectural layering convention

rule: Source files must live in the module directory that owns their domain concept, not in an adjacent or related module.
mode_rationale: Single file placement convention

rule: Helper functions should live in the module that owns their concern; do not add unrelated helpers to a generic helper file when a more specific test module or utility file is appropriate.
mode_rationale: Code organization principle about function placement

rule: Move generic utility code (e.g., AWS client wrappers) into common shared modules rather than domain-specific packages; remove domain-specific dependencies (like config objects) in favor of primitive parameters to keep the code reusable.
mode_rationale: Architectural convention for utility code placement

rule: When constructing or logging domain objects, place the entity ID as the first parameter (or immediately after the creator) so the most important identifier is immediately visible.
mode_rationale: Naming/ordering convention: put entity ID first in parameter lists

rule: Service implementations that depend on external communication (HTTP, gRPC, etc.) belong in the infrastructure layer, not in the server/application layer.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence layering convention for service implementations

rule: Ensure flags and configuration options are applied at the layer where they have effect (e.g., at the API call site), not at unrelated layers like permission checkers.
mode_rationale: Single convention: apply flags/config at the layer where they have effect

rule: Place conversion or mapping logic on the domain class or its companion object rather than in an unrelated service, so the logic is co-located with the type it transforms.
Provider-specific methods should live on the provider's own implementation class, not on a shared base client used by all providers.
mode_rationale: Single code organization rule about provider methods

rule: Components likely to be reused across modules should be placed in the shared packages/ui library rather than nested in a feature-specific directory.
mode_rationale: Single code organization rule about component placement

rule: Route-building and navigation helper functions should live in a shared utils or routes file for the feature area, not inline in individual components, so they can be reused from other entry points.
mode_rationale: Single file organization convention for route helpers

rule: When adding a new tag, field, or classification to an entity, plan a migration to backfill the value on all existing records, not just newly created ones.
mode_rationale: Single reminder to backfill existing records when adding fields

rule: Use plural nouns for REST API resource paths (e.g., /endpoints/ not /endpoint/) to maintain consistency with REST conventions and existing APIs.
mode_rationale: Single REST naming convention

rule: When polling for async task status, use a stable identifier (e.g., entity ID) as the task key so that polling can resume after a page refresh, rather than relying on ephemeral in-memory state.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use stable IDs for polling keys, not ephemeral state

rule: When different entity subtypes have distinct sets of fields, use polymorphism (separate subclasses) instead of making all fields optional on a single type; this enforces correctness at compile time.
mode_rationale: Design preference for type modeling

rule: When backporting or cherry-picking changes across branches, always port the associated tests to maintain test parity.
mode_rationale: Single convention: always port tests alongside backported code

rule: Use positive logic for boolean permission variables (e.g., canCreate, canEdit) rather than negative logic (e.g., isDisabled, isOverLimit) to improve readability and reduce inversion errors.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention preferring positive boolean names

rule: Name boolean props using positive framing (e.g., `keepValuesOnSubmit`) rather than double negatives (e.g., `doNotResetValuesOnSubmit`) to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Naming convention to avoid double negatives in boolean props

rule: Shell scripts should prefer POSIX-compliant syntax (e.g., `.` instead of `source`, `[ ]` instead of `[[ ]]`) and pass shellcheck validation to ensure compatibility across environments.
mode_rationale: Single convention about shell script compatibility

rule: Keep all changes in a PR related to the stated purpose; unrelated changes should go in a separate PR to keep reviews focused and diffs auditable.
mode_rationale: Process convention about keeping PRs focused

rule: Comments explaining algorithmic choices should be specific about what the algorithm optimizes for, not vague about what it does.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention about comment specificity

rule: Test names should precisely describe the scope being tested so readers can understand coverage without reading the test body.
mode_rationale: Testing convention for descriptive test names

rule: Extract sub-components (e.g., tab contents) into their own files early, even if they are small, to prevent a single file from growing unwieldy.
mode_rationale: Single convention: extract sub-components into their own files early

rule: In Scala, use == instead of .equals() for equality checks; == is null-safe and more idiomatic, improving readability.
mode_rationale: Simple Scala idiom preference

rule: Use Ant Design's Flex component for layout instead of creating custom divs with flexbox styles.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use Ant Design Flex component instead of custom flexbox divs

rule: Use Ant Design Form, Form.List, and shouldUpdate instead of building custom form state management with nested conditionals.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use AntD Form over custom state management

rule: Use the Ant Design Theme Provider to customize component appearance instead of manually overriding styles in styled-components.
mode_rationale: Single convention about using theme provider over manual style overrides

rule: Use existing API endpoints to update state rather than manually constructing state changes in the frontend; pass undefined for unused params to reuse a single API call.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence architecture convention to use existing APIs

rule: Use Array.some() instead of manual iteration or reduce when checking whether any element in a collection satisfies a condition.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence coding convention about which array method to use

rule: Prefer async/await with try/catch over .then/.catch promise chains for consistency across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Single coding style preference for async patterns

rule: Use Jest automock for module mocking in tests instead of manually mocking each dependency.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about mocking approach

rule: When a parent already manages a form field's state, use the `value` prop instead of `defaultValue` to make the component fully controlled and avoid needing to sync state manually.
mode_rationale: Simple React component pattern convention

rule: When adjusting visual layout such as spacing, indentation, or dividers, prefer CSS rules on the appropriate container or group elements rather than restructuring component hierarchy or adding JavaScript logic.
mode_rationale: Convention: use CSS for layout/spacing instead of JS restructuring

rule: In Python, prefer @dataclass (or NamedTuple) for simple data containers over custom classes with manual __init__ methods; keep data structures explicit and avoid hiding logic behind wrapper classes.
mode_rationale: Python convention for data containers

rule: Use the framework's default timeout values in tests unless there is a specific, documented reason to override them; arbitrary custom timeouts add unnecessary divergence.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use framework defaults

rule: Prefer explicit, typed data contracts (defined case classes/interfaces) over storing arbitrary JSON blobs, as defined contracts are easier to work with in code, tests, and database inspection.
mode_rationale: Simple preference for typed contracts over loose JSON

rule: Import UI components from the Domino design system library rather than directly from Ant Design; document the reason if a direct Ant import is necessary due to missing design system support.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention for design system usage

rule: Use non-optional collection types (e.g., Map instead of Option[Map]) with empty defaults unless None carries distinct semantic meaning different from an empty collection.
mode_rationale: Type design convention: use empty collections over Option[Collection]

rule: Use ES module imports (import/export) instead of CommonJS require; use .mts file extensions when needed to enable ESM syntax in TypeScript.
mode_rationale: Single import style convention

rule: Use existing utility classes like AutoRecordMapperCodec for serialization instead of writing manual mapping code, unless there is a specific technical reason not to.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use existing serialization utilities

rule: Prefer existing library constructs or standard language utilities for string parsing and URL splitting over manual implementations, which reduces the need for additional unit tests.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use library utilities instead of manual implementations

rule: Before writing custom utility code, check if an existing library already in the project can handle the same task — it will be documented, tested, and maintained externally.
mode_rationale: Convention to check for existing libraries before writing custom code

rule: Prefer explicit if-statement null checks over TypeScript non-null assertion operators (!) to make safety guarantees visible in the code.
mode_rationale: Single convention: if-checks over ! assertions

rule: Use fake implementations (in-memory, lightweight stand-ins) instead of mocks in integration and unit tests for more realistic behavior and easier maintenance.
mode_rationale: Single testing strategy convention

rule: Use built-in framework components (e.g., react-router's Redirect) instead of writing custom replacements unless you have a documented reason the built-in cannot satisfy the requirement.
Use Array.reduce() or other functional methods instead of declaring mutable variables with let and imperatively building up values.
mode_rationale: Single coding style preference for functional patterns

rule: Use `unknown` instead of `any` when a value is simply passed through without being accessed — it provides type safety while still allowing forwarding.
mode_rationale: Type safety convention for passthrough values

rule: Use React Router hooks instead of the legacy withRouter HOC; higher-order components are no longer necessary with modern React Router.
mode_rationale: Simple modernization convention preferring hooks over HOCs

rule: Use custom hooks close to where cross-cutting logic is needed instead of prop-drilling event handlers through multiple component layers that don't need to know about them.
mode_rationale: Architectural preference for hooks vs prop-drilling

rule: Prefer immutable array methods like `slice` over mutating methods like `splice`, even when working with copies, to signal immutable intent and prevent accidental mutation bugs.
mode_rationale: Simple coding convention: use slice over splice to signal immutable intent

rule: Use imperative functions (e.g., extracted fetchData callbacks) for data refresh instead of chaining boolean state flags through multiple useEffects, which causes unnecessary re-renders.
mode_rationale: Single React pattern preference for data fetching

rule: Use TypeScript interface with extends instead of redefining identical types; share type definitions from a common package rather than duplicating across app and package boundaries.
mode_rationale: Single TypeScript convention about type reuse

rule: In TypeScript, prefer 'interface' over 'type' for defining component prop shapes and object structures, as interfaces are more extensible and produce clearer error messages.
mode_rationale: TypeScript convention preferring interface over type for objects

rule: Use native JavaScript methods (e.g., Array.find, Array.includes) instead of utility library equivalents (Ramda, lodash) when the native API provides the same functionality.
mode_rationale: Simple preference for native JS over lodash/Ramda

rule: Use the nullish coalescing operator (??) instead of logical OR (||) for default values to avoid incorrectly treating falsy values like 0 or empty string as missing.
mode_rationale: Single operator preference rule

rule: Use optional parameters (param?: Type) instead of explicit union types (param: Type | undefined) for cleaner, more idiomatic TypeScript signatures.
mode_rationale: TypeScript idiom convention for optional params

rule: Use concise entity type names (e.g., 'user' instead of 'userAccount') in APIs and domain models unless the longer name disambiguates between genuinely different concepts.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for concise entity types

rule: Favor template literal string interpolation over string concatenation for readability and consistency.
Use string literal union types with a Map (and the `UnionToMap` type utility from `ui/utils/typescriptUtils`) instead of TypeScript enums, which are considered an antipattern in this codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use string literal unions with Map instead of TS enums

rule: Prefer structured or typed data access over manual string parsing; string parsing is fragile and should be a last resort with a clear comment explaining why.
mode_rationale: Single convention preferring typed access over string parsing

rule: Use the project's structured audit event system instead of ad-hoc logger.info calls for actions that need to be auditable, ensuring consistent format and queryability.
mode_rationale: Logging convention to use structured audit system

rule: Use styled components instead of inline styles, reference colors from the theme rather than hardcoding hex values, and use named constants for magic numbers in calculations.
mode_rationale: Simple style convention: styled components with theme refs over inline styles

rule: Let API utility functions throw exceptions and handle errors with try/catch or .catch() at the call site rather than passing onSuccess/onError callback parameters.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention preferring try/catch over callbacks

rule: In UI tests, prefer async waitFor assertions over hardcoded setTimeout delays; fixed delays are flaky and slow, while waitFor polls until the condition is met.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention for async assertions

rule: In pattern matches or switch statements, let removed or legacy cases fall through to the default/wildcard handler rather than listing them explicitly.
mode_rationale: Single convention: let removed cases fall through to default handler

rule: Boolean fields and variables should use the 'is' prefix (e.g., isWorkloadServiceExecution) to make their boolean nature immediately clear at the call site.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for booleans

rule: Prefix class and type names with their owning module to prevent confusion with similarly-named concepts from other modules during cross-module refactors.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for cross-module clarity

rule: When modifying or replacing an API endpoint, keep the old endpoint functional for backward compatibility, especially when external consumers or agents may depend on it.
mode_rationale: Single backward compatibility guideline

rule: When replacing icons from one library (e.g., AntD) with another (e.g., FontAwesome), the aria-label must match the original to avoid breaking existing tests.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to maintain aria-labels during icon migration

rule: When modifying code near eslint-disable comments, verify the directive still applies to the correct line and does not break the original suppression intent.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence caution about eslint-disable comments

rule: When wrapping exceptions in custom exception types, always pass the original exception as the cause parameter to the parent Exception constructor to preserve the full stack trace.
When migrating between SDK versions behind a feature flag, keep the old implementation unchanged so it remains a valid fallback; do not modify the old path with new SDK calls.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about keeping old code unchanged behind feature flags

rule: Prefer async/await with try/catch over .then()/.catch() promise chains to maintain a consistent async error-handling style across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Single style convention about async error handling

rule: When a parameter represents a boolean flag, use an explicit Boolean type with a sensible default (e.g., `true` or `false`) rather than Option[Boolean] where None could be confused with the negative case.
mode_rationale: Simple type design convention for boolean parameters

rule: Use a boolean for binary on/off states rather than creating an enum with only two values; only introduce an enum when there are concrete plans for additional states beyond enabled/disabled.
mode_rationale: Simple API design guideline for binary state representation

rule: Use shell built-ins like `export -p` instead of manually parsing and reconstructing environment variables, as manual parsing almost always has edge cases.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about using builtins over manual parsing

rule: Replace tuples with named case classes when passing multiple related values together, especially at call sites where the meaning of each position is unclear.
mode_rationale: Simple Scala convention to use named types over positional tuples

rule: In Scala, prefer case classes over regular classes with @unused annotations; case class constructor parameters are automatically public vals, eliminating the need for boilerplate getters and suppression annotations.
Prefer classes over singleton objects for service-like components so they can be easily mocked and tested in unit tests.
mode_rationale: Simple design preference for testability

rule: Use Scala's collect with a partial function instead of chaining filter and map when both selecting and transforming collection elements.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence Scala idiom preference

rule: Move stateless methods and constants to the companion object rather than keeping them on the case class instance.
mode_rationale: Simple Scala convention about method placement

rule: If a field is required, make it non-optional and enforce it at the type/compiler level rather than throwing runtime errors when it is missing.
mode_rationale: Type system convention favoring compile-time enforcement

rule: When adding behavior to a shared/base component for a targeted fix, prefer creating a wrapper component that composes the original rather than modifying the shared component directly, to limit blast radius.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention about wrapper components for hotfixes

rule: When a class needs specific implementations injected, prefer injecting the concrete types directly rather than using @Named annotations on the interface, reserving the interface binding for the facade that other consumers should use.
mode_rationale: Simple DI convention about injection style

rule: When a function or hook accepts many arguments, request a single config object parameter to improve readability and extensibility.
mode_rationale: Single API design convention

rule: Declare values that do not change as constants (const) rather than variables to communicate intent and prevent accidental mutation.
mode_rationale: Simple variable declaration convention

rule: Dependencies should be injected via the constructor rather than instantiated or resolved inside the class body.
mode_rationale: Single dependency injection convention

rule: When a default value is available (e.g., from a theme provider), use it directly instead of wrapping the component in a higher-order component like withTheme.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to avoid unnecessary HOC wrapping

rule: When objects are being drilled through several layers of classes to reach their point of use, prefer a dependency injection framework (e.g., Guice) over manual parameter passing to keep constructors clean and avoid coupling between layers.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention favoring DI over parameter drilling

rule: Use object destructuring when accessing multiple properties from props or other objects for cleaner, more readable code.
mode_rationale: Simple style preference for object destructuring

rule: Use direct imports (e.g., import package.ClassName) instead of qualified references through intermediate objects when the direct import is available.
mode_rationale: Import style convention preferring direct imports

rule: When updating a record, use a direct update operation rather than a delete-then-insert pattern, which is less atomic and can cause transient inconsistencies.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use direct update instead of delete-then-insert

rule: When filtering API results by an entity's domain type, use an enum-based query parameter (e.g., `?projectType=Template`) rather than a boolean toggle (e.g., `?includeTemplateApps=false`), as enums are more extensible, declarative, and consistent with domain modeling.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention about filter parameter types

rule: Use existing accessor methods on domain objects (e.g., d.getTemplateType) instead of manually unwrapping optional fields, to reduce boilerplate and avoid null-handling bugs.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use accessor methods instead of manual unwrapping

rule: Before implementing custom logic, check if an existing service method or API already provides the needed functionality and prefer reusing it.
mode_rationale: Single guideline to check for existing APIs first

rule: Check for and use existing data-fetching hooks (e.g., useRemoteData) before writing custom fetch logic in components.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to check for existing hooks before writing new fetch logic

rule: Use explicit boolean props rather than optional function props to control whether UI elements are shown, as optional functions are less verbose and harder to reason about.
mode_rationale: Single React prop design convention

rule: Use `getOrElse` with an `IllegalStateException` containing a clear message instead of calling `.get` on Options, even in cases that should never fail, to make debugging easier.
mode_rationale: Single error handling convention for Options

rule: In BUILD files where some source files need different targets, prefer explicit file lists over glob() patterns to prevent new files from silently landing in the wrong target.
mode_rationale: Single build convention: prefer explicit lists over globs

rule: Prefer importing individual classes by name over wildcard imports when the number of imported members is small, for clarity.
mode_rationale: Simple import style convention

rule: Use explicitly named parameters rather than generic container objects (e.g., baseOperationParams) when the caller must know which fields are set; wrap generic helpers with specific-named facades for clarity.
mode_rationale: API design convention about parameter clarity

rule: Use Array.filter instead of Array.reduce when the intent is to filter elements from a collection; it is more readable and expresses intent directly.
mode_rationale: Simple code style rule about array method readability

rule: Prefer flat tuple-based pattern matching over nested match/case blocks to improve readability and reduce indentation.
mode_rationale: Single Scala style preference: flat tuple matching over nested match blocks

rule: Use >= rather than ~= for Python dependency pinning in requirements files to avoid breakage when transitive dependencies shift, unless a known incompatibility requires a tighter bound.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use >= instead of ~= for Python dependency pinning unless needed

rule: When a method operates on a single ID, refactor it to accept a list of IDs (or use parametric polymorphism) to support both single and batch use cases.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention for method signatures

rule: Use Scala for-comprehensions with filter/yield to combine collections instead of manual nested matching with headOption.
mode_rationale: Single Scala style preference for collection operations

rule: When processing an Optional value with a side effect, prefer forEach over getOrElse to eliminate the Option wrapper and produce cleaner, more idiomatic code.
mode_rationale: Idiomatic Scala style guideline for processing Optional values with side effects

rule: Use map, flatMap, and other functional combinators on Either/Option/Try types instead of manually matching on left/right or some/none when the transformation is straightforward.
mode_rationale: Code style preference for functional types

rule: When adding new logic to a class component, convert it to a functional component rather than continuing to extend the class.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to convert class components when touching them

rule: Name boolean flags with general, intent-based names like `isEnabled` rather than implementation-specific names like `isInScope` that leak internal details.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for boolean flags

rule: When fixing a bug for a specific case, look for a generic solution that handles the entire class of problems rather than patching only the immediate instance.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about fix scope

rule: Prefer a generic, homogeneous representation for API response types over proliferating per-variant subtypes — work with design to simplify requirements if needed.
mode_rationale: Single API design preference for generic response types

rule: Simplify Option handling using combinators like .filter and .getOrElse rather than verbose if/else chains; e.g., use s3DefaultBucket.filter(_.nonEmpty).getOrElse(throw ...).
mode_rationale: Scala idiom preference for Option combinators

rule: Prefer if/ternary expressions over chaining && expressions when it avoids the need for non-null assertions (!).
mode_rationale: Simple code style preference for conditionals

rule: Use explicit if statements instead of complex ternary or inline conditional expressions for better readability.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use if statements over complex ternaries

rule: Use useMemo to create a debounced handler and useCallback for the change handler instead of wiring debounce logic through useEffect.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence preference for useMemo/useCallback debounce pattern

rule: Use java.time.Instant instead of legacy date types for timestamps in Scala/Java code for smaller memory footprint, built-in comparison support, and modern API compatibility.
mode_rationale: Single type choice convention for timestamps

rule: Use java.time.Instant for new code instead of legacy DateTime types; only use legacy DateTime when maintaining consistency within an existing file that already uses it.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use modern time API for new code

rule: When an established library already provides a method for an API call, use it instead of writing custom HTTP requests, so you can reuse existing error handling and warning logic.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention to use existing library methods

rule: Use list comprehensions instead of imperative for-loops with append when transforming a collection into a new list in Python.
mode_rationale: Simple Python style preference

rule: Import from lodash-es instead of lodash to enable tree-shaking and reduce bundle size in ES module-based builds.
mode_rationale: Single import rule: use lodash-es for tree-shaking support

rule: Use the Ant Design Menu's `onMenuClick` prop with menu item keys instead of wrapping individual buttons inside menu items; this makes the entire menu item area clickable and simplifies the handler logic.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use onMenuClick prop instead of buttons inside menu items

rule: Use `mockResolvedValue` instead of `mockImplementation` with fake timers when testing async operations, to keep tests simpler and avoid unnecessary timer complexity.
mode_rationale: Simple preference for one mocking approach over another

rule: When adding new feature permissions, create a dedicated role rather than adding them to an existing broad role, to avoid granting permissions to users who do not need them.
mode_rationale: Security convention for permission scoping

rule: Prefer built-in browser/platform APIs (e.g., URLSearchParams) over third-party libraries (e.g., qs) when the native API covers the use case.
mode_rationale: Convention to prefer built-in APIs over third-party libraries

rule: Use Option types to represent optional updates instead of pairing a value field with a boolean flag; this eliminates invalid states and reduces the API surface.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence API design convention for optional values

rule: When extending an API endpoint, prefer making existing parameters optional rather than adding new parameters to achieve the same result.
mode_rationale: Simple API extension convention

rule: Before creating a new utility function, check whether adding a parameter to an existing function achieves the same result with less surface area.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline to extend existing functions before creating new ones

rule: Use PATCH-based partial updates instead of PUT with full-object replacement to avoid concurrency issues where simultaneous requests overwrite each other's changes.
In Scala, use pattern matching on Option[Boolean] instead of nested conditionals to make the intent of each branch explicit and exhaustive.
mode_rationale: Scala idiom preference for explicit pattern matching

rule: Use pattern matching instead of .collect on a single Option value; .collect is idiomatic for filtering and mapping collections, not single values, and pattern matching is clearer.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence coding style preference for pattern matching over .collect

rule: In Scala, prefer `match` with exhaustive cases over nested `if`/`for-comprehension` chains for authorization and type-dispatching logic, as it improves readability and ensures exhaustiveness.
mode_rationale: Scala style convention for control flow

rule: Use pattern matching (match/case) instead of conditional checks with .get() when parsing or validating results in Scala.
mode_rationale: Style preference for pattern matching over .get() in Scala

rule: Prefer positive test assertions that verify expected behavior over negation tests that wait for something to not happen, as negation tests incur timeout costs on every run and may pass incorrectly if a different failure occurs.
mode_rationale: Testing style preference, single guideline

rule: Structure boolean expressions with the positive/enabling condition first to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Single readability convention for boolean expressions

rule: Reusable components should receive data via props rather than reading route params directly, since they may be rendered in different routing contexts.
mode_rationale: Single convention: reusable components receive data via props, not route params

rule: Implement UI and styling in React components rather than server-side Scala templates to keep rendering logic consistent and maintainable.
mode_rationale: Single architectural preference for React over server templates

rule: Use react-router's Link component instead of native anchor tags for internal app navigation to enable client-side routing and avoid full page reloads.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use Link over anchor tags

rule: Use ReactNode instead of more restrictive types (like string) for component props that accept renderable content, to allow consumers to pass any valid React element.
mode_rationale: Single typing convention for renderable content props

rule: When transforming and filtering a collection, prefer a single `reduce` pass over chaining `map` and `filter` to avoid unnecessary intermediate arrays.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention for collection transformations

rule: When a component accepts a customizable trigger or child element, use a render prop that injects callbacks (e.g., onClick) rather than accepting a Function Component, to allow consumers to pass additional properties.
mode_rationale: Component API design convention expressible in one sentence

rule: Make component props required rather than optional when the component depends on them; this surfaces missing data at compile time instead of failing silently at runtime.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention for prop optionality

rule: Use explicit TypeScript return type annotations on functions instead of casting the return value, which provides better type safety and cleaner code.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence TypeScript convention for return type annotations

rule: When filtering data displayed in tables, prefer server-side filtering via API calls over client-side filtering to handle large datasets and reduce client memory usage.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention for data filtering

rule: For API-backed tables, plan for server-side pagination and filtering rather than filtering results locally, as endpoints will eventually support pagination.
mode_rationale: Simple architectural guideline for API-backed tables

rule: When a shared wrapper component exists (e.g., TooltipWrapper), use it instead of reimplementing tooltip behavior inline.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use existing shared wrappers instead of reimplementing

rule: API route segments should use the simplest, most intuitive name (e.g., `/vanity/` instead of a verbose alternative) and defer complex naming redesigns to the new API.
mode_rationale: API naming convention

rule: Use the simpler `get` method for required configuration values instead of manual error handling; it throws an exception with the key name if the value is missing.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use get method for required config

rule: Do not suppress harmless information (like execution IDs) from API responses in the name of minimal exposure when it adds complexity without real security benefit.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention about information exposure

rule: Use 'sortBy' rather than 'sortField' as the query parameter name for sort operations in REST APIs for clarity and consistency.
mode_rationale: Simple API naming convention

rule: Use more specific pattern matches (e.g., Some(value) instead of raw Option) to simplify downstream code and avoid unnecessary map/foreach calls.
mode_rationale: Simple coding style preference for pattern matching

rule: For service-to-service authorization, check against specific fine-grained permissions rather than broad superuser checks to limit the scope of service accounts.
mode_rationale: Single security convention about fine-grained permission checks

rule: Use standard platform mechanisms (e.g., classpath includes) instead of file-manipulation workarounds to manage configuration layering.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention to use platform-native config mechanisms

rule: Use the standard canonical API (e.g., getAccessToken()) for token acquisition instead of internal-specific token acquirers unless there is a documented reason to diverge.
mode_rationale: Single guideline to use canonical API for token acquisition

rule: Use styled components for styling instead of CSS class names when possible to keep styles co-located and type-safe.
mode_rationale: Simple style preference for co-located, type-safe styling

rule: Create separate styled components for child elements rather than using CSS selectors nested inside a parent styled component.
mode_rationale: Styling convention for child element components

rule: When styling UI components, prefer using theme helpers and design tokens over hardcoded values so that styles remain consistent and centrally maintainable.
mode_rationale: Styling convention: use design tokens over hardcoded values

rule: When a function returns a tuple, destructure it into named variables at the call site for readability instead of accessing elements by index.
mode_rationale: Single readability convention

rule: When a value is already typed (e.g., via a typed parameter or return type), let TypeScript infer the type instead of adding redundant explicit annotations.
mode_rationale: TypeScript convention to avoid redundant type annotations

rule: Use unit tests instead of end-to-end system tests when the behavior can be verified without exercising the full application, as e2e tests are expensive to run and maintain.
Prefer useMemo with JSX over defining inner render functions for memoizable UI fragments within React components.
mode_rationale: Performance convention preferring useMemo over render functions

rule: API responses should include user IDs (in addition to or instead of usernames) because IDs are more stable and usable for programmatic consumers and UI clients.
mode_rationale: Single API design convention preferring stable identifiers

rule: Use `userEvent` instead of `fireEvent` for simulating user interactions in tests, as it more closely mirrors real browser behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple preference for one API over another in tests

rule: Use val instead of var in Scala when the variable is assigned once and does not need to be reassigned.
mode_rationale: Scala immutability convention

rule: When working with Option types in Scala, prefer pattern matching on the value rather than using `.isDefined` checks followed by `.get`, as it leads to safer and more readable code.
mode_rationale: Scala idiom preference expressible in one sentence

rule: Combinator functions like And/Or should accept variadic arguments rather than requiring callers to wrap arguments in a Set or List, to improve ergonomics at the call site.
mode_rationale: Single API ergonomics guideline

rule: Use purpose-built wrapper components (e.g., TooltipWrapper) instead of lower-level renderer helper functions for better composability and consistency.
mode_rationale: Single convention about component composition pattern

rule: Name test helper functions that verify conditions with an `assert_` prefix so their intent is immediately clear.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for test helpers

rule: When wrapping one entity as another (e.g., exposing a dataset as a datasource), verify that the original entity's access control invariants are maintained in the new abstraction.
mode_rationale: Single security convention about maintaining access control through abstractions

rule: When adding cross-module functionality, ensure dependency direction flows from specific feature modules toward generic/core modules, never the reverse.
mode_rationale: Single rule about dependency direction flowing specific-to-generic

rule: API endpoints should reflect the actual domain model relationships rather than creating shortcuts that flatten or skip entities, so the API remains easy to reason about as the domain evolves.
mode_rationale: Simple API design principle about reflecting domain relationships

rule: When capturing errors, store both the error code (if available) and the error message to enable better debugging and user feedback.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence error handling convention

rule: Do not rename established API method names without strong justification; inconsistent naming across versions creates confusion for consumers and breaks conventions.
mode_rationale: Naming convention about API stability

rule: When adding new conditional checks to an existing guard clause, preserve the original conditions rather than replacing them — combine old and new conditions together.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about combining guard conditions

rule: When creating new code paths that replace or parallel existing ones, audit the original path for validation steps (guardrails, permission checks) and ensure they are carried over.
mode_rationale: Single refactoring principle to carry over validation

rule: When refactoring code that removes logging statements, add equivalent warn/debug logs in the new code path to maintain observability.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to maintain logging during refactors

rule: When moving or refactoring code, verify that all existing test assertions are preserved in the new location — don't silently drop test conditions.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention: don't drop assertions when moving code

rule: Place public methods above private methods in class files to maintain a consistent top-down readability order.
mode_rationale: Simple ordering convention

rule: General-purpose components should live at a shared/global level, not scoped inside a feature module; start local only if the component needs refinement or design review before promotion.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about where general-purpose components should live

rule: When adding try/catch blocks to prevent crashes, also provide user-visible feedback (e.g., a toast or error message) so the user knows something went wrong rather than silently swallowing the error.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: show user feedback instead of silent catch

rule: When the frontend needs a derived value (e.g., provider name from a URI), include it as an explicit field in the API response rather than requiring consumers to parse or compute it, especially when the mapping already exists on the backend.
mode_rationale: Single API design convention to expose derived values explicitly

rule: When a display value may be absent, provide a meaningful human-readable fallback (e.g., derived from another field) instead of an empty string, especially for audit trails and user-facing logs.
mode_rationale: Single display convention for absent values

rule: Define pure utility functions (like sorting or data transformation) outside the component body and have them fully encapsulate their logic, including any copying or spreading of data.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: define pure functions outside components

rule: Push sorting, limiting, and pagination logic into database queries instead of fetching all records and processing in application memory, especially for endpoints that serve growing datasets.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention for pagination

rule: When a code change resolves the type error that a @ts-ignore or @ts-expect-error was suppressing, remove the suppression comment so TypeScript can resume checking that line.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention: clean up suppression comments when the underlying issue is fixed

rule: Mock data should use realistic example values that reflect the actual shape and format of real data (e.g., realistic IDs) to make tests and development more representative.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline for test data quality

rule: Test helper functions that handle failures should call record_failure (or equivalent) so that support bundles and diagnostic artifacts are captured for debugging.
mode_rationale: Convention for test helpers to capture diagnostic artifacts

rule: When adding user-identifying information (usernames, emails) to telemetry or analytics events, verify whether those fields need to be added to the redacted fields list to prevent PII leakage to third-party analytics platforms.
mode_rationale: Single security/privacy rule

rule: Instead of commenting out code to disable functionality, create a new variable or configuration that achieves the desired state cleanly, keeping the codebase free of dead commented-out blocks.
mode_rationale: Code hygiene rule: create clean alternative instead of commenting out

rule: When commenting out code or disabling tests due to a known issue, include a reference to the tracking Jira ticket so future readers know why it was disabled and when it can be re-enabled.
mode_rationale: Documentation convention for disabled code

rule: Reference related entities by their primary key (ID) rather than by name or other mutable fields to avoid synchronization complexity if renaming is ever supported.
mode_rationale: Single rule about using IDs instead of mutable fields

rule: After a state-restoring mutation (e.g., restore, unarchive), refetch the current entity's data and stay on the detail page rather than navigating the user back to the list view.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence UX/data-fetching guideline

rule: When fixing a bug, add a regression test that reproduces the original failure scenario to prevent the bug from reoccurring.
mode_rationale: Simple practice: add a regression test when fixing a bug

rule: Do not allow invalid string representations (like the string "None" from an Option's toString) to propagate through the system; validate and throw at the boundary instead.
mode_rationale: Validation convention: throw at boundary for invalid string representations

rule: When removing a feature or component, find and remove all related references, types, and dead code paths rather than leaving orphaned remnants.
mode_rationale: Single cleanup convention about thorough dead code removal

rule: If a function parameter is always called with the same value, remove the parameter and inline the constant to reduce unnecessary abstraction.
mode_rationale: Simple refactoring principle about constant params

rule: When removing a code step or block, also remove any associated comments that reference the removed code to avoid stale or misleading documentation.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup rule when removing code blocks

rule: Remove all println, console.log, or other debug output statements before submitting code for review; use structured logging instead if runtime output is needed.
mode_rationale: Basic hygiene rule to remove debug output before merge

rule: When refactoring removes all usages of a component, delete the component code rather than leaving it as dead code in the codebase.
mode_rationale: Cleanup convention when refactoring removes usages

rule: When adding a precondition check that prevents a state from being reached, remove or simplify downstream code that handled that now-impossible state.
mode_rationale: Simple refactoring convention about cleaning up unreachable code

rule: When migrating functionality to a new implementation, remove the old files if they are no longer referenced; verify by deploying after removal.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup convention during migrations

rule: Remove unused code paths (e.g., HTTP method mappings, enum cases) rather than keeping them for hypothetical future use; they can always be added back when actually needed.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline to remove unused code

rule: Remove fields from an entity or record that do not apply to that entity's type; storing inapplicable fields (always None/null) creates confusion and violates the principle that data models should accurately represent their domain.
mode_rationale: Data modeling convention about field relevance

rule: Do not pass empty no-op functions as event handlers when the framework or parent component already handles the behavior (e.g., form item onChange).
mode_rationale: Simple convention to avoid no-op handlers

rule: When changing code behavior (e.g., changing a default value), update or remove comments that described the previous behavior so they don't become misleading.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation hygiene guideline

rule: Do not ship half-implemented architectural patterns (e.g., HATEOAS URI fields); either implement them properly or remove them entirely and add them back when there is a real need.
mode_rationale: Single quality convention against shipping incomplete patterns

rule: After refactoring code, check whether existing eslint-disable or lint suppression comments are still needed and remove them if the underlying issue is resolved.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: clean up suppressions after refactoring

rule: When migrating between SDK versions behind a feature flag, keep the v1 code path unchanged so it can serve as a reliable rollback; only modify the v2 path.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about not modifying v1 code paths during migration

rule: When refactoring code that includes validation logic, verify that validation is still enforced somewhere in the new code path rather than accidentally dropping it.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about not dropping validation

rule: Before adding cross-module imports, verify the import graph to ensure no circular dependencies are introduced; if a cycle already exists, flag it for resolution rather than deepening it.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: verify import graph before adding cross-module imports

rule: React hooks that fetch data and set state on error must handle failure states carefully to avoid triggering infinite re-render loops.
mode_rationale: Convention about careful state handling in hooks

rule: Use data validation logic to prevent state combinations that lead to unrecoverable situations, rather than restricting actions per-role in ways that overfit specific requirements.
mode_rationale: Single design principle about preferring data validation over role-based action restrictions

rule: API parameters should only affect the dimension they describe; if a parameter like `limit` changes query semantics beyond just limiting results, create a separate endpoint instead of overloading the existing one.
mode_rationale: Design principle about API parameter scope — one sentence guideline

rule: Review error messages and log strings for typos before committing, as these are user-facing or debugging-critical text.
mode_rationale: Simple quality convention: check error/log strings for typos

rule: Name React props to clearly indicate the type of value they accept — boolean-sounding names (e.g., `isVisible`) should not accept components, and component-accepting props should use names like `renderX` or `xComponent`.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming convention for React props

rule: When defining status enums, include an explicit error state and ensure the corresponding error message is propagated so consumers can understand what went wrong.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention for status enums

rule: When adding a new field to a domain entity, ensure it is also added to all related models that derive from or are created from that entity (e.g., adding a field to Run also requires adding it to AppVersion).
mode_rationale: Single consistency rule for domain model changes

rule: When renaming a parameter in one file, update the corresponding parameter names in all related interfaces, traits, and adapter files to keep the codebase consistent.
mode_rationale: Single convention: keep parameter names consistent across interfaces when renaming

rule: When replacing a configuration flag, add a release note announcing the replacement, document the migration path, and schedule removal for the next minor release so customers have time to migrate.
mode_rationale: Multi-step workflow: add release note, document migration path, schedule removal

rule: When a required resource identifier is missing or invalid, return an appropriate 4xx HTTP status code (400 or 404) instead of allowing a 500 internal server error.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline about returning correct HTTP status codes

rule: Always use proper TypeScript types for data structures; avoid loose typing (e.g., any, unknown, or untyped objects) that could mask real errors.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use proper TypeScript types, avoid any/unknown

rule: Never check a Promise for truthiness directly — a Promise object is always truthy. Await the Promise or use `=== true` on the resolved value to get the correct result.
mode_rationale: Single rule about Promise truthiness pitfall

rule: When a resource's cleanup requires more than `close()` (e.g., `dispose()`), ensure it is explicitly called since `Using` blocks only invoke `close()`/`release()`.
mode_rationale: Resource cleanup convention

rule: When composing user-provided configuration with system-required configuration, ensure critical system arguments are positioned last or otherwise protected so user input cannot override them.
mode_rationale: Single security guideline about config composition order

rule: System tags and internal metadata should be modified using superuser/service credentials, not user credentials, and scoped so regular users cannot override them.
mode_rationale: Security convention about credential scoping for system tags

rule: Boolean component props should have explicit default values (typically `false`) so callers don't need to specify them when the default behavior is desired.
mode_rationale: API design convention for boolean prop defaults

rule: Always provide a default value for environment variable expansions in shell scripts (e.g., ${VAR:-default}) to prevent failures when the variable is unset.
mode_rationale: Shell scripting safety convention

rule: Set default values for hook return values and API responses so consumers don't need defensive optional chaining throughout the component.
mode_rationale: Defensive coding convention

rule: When authorization or data loading can result in an empty UI (e.g., empty navbar), provide user-visible feedback explaining why nothing is shown.
mode_rationale: Single UX guideline about empty state visibility

rule: When a form or modal depends on pre-loaded data, show an error message and prevent the user from interacting with an empty or broken form if the data load fails.
mode_rationale: Single convention: show error and block interaction when data load fails

rule: When a utility method accepts optional parameters but most callers always provide values, offer a strongly-typed overload that takes non-optional parameters for convenience and type safety.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention about convenience overloads

rule: When renaming or restructuring fields in a persisted data model (e.g., MongoDB documents), provide a runnable migration script for upgrading existing deployments.
mode_rationale: Multi-step workflow: assess impact, write migration script, test on existing data, deploy

rule: When a mutation affects multiple entities, expose an onSuccess callback so parent components can coordinate follow-up actions like refetching related data.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence API design convention about onSuccess callbacks

rule: When implementing features that reference an external provider (e.g., GitHub), verify the behavior is correct for all supported providers (e.g., Bitbucket, GitLab) or scope the feature to the applicable provider only.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about multi-provider correctness

rule: Only use Provider-based (lazy) dependency injection when resolving circular dependencies; use direct injection for all other cases.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about when to use lazy DI

rule: Prefer unit tests and API-level system tests over expensive E2E/UI tests for backend logic like permissions; reserve E2E tests for scenarios that genuinely require browser-level validation.
mode_rationale: Single test pyramid guideline

rule: Place data detection and validation logic on the backend where the authoritative information exists, rather than implementing complex client-side heuristics to infer server-side state.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture guideline to keep validation on the backend

rule: Operational troubleshooting details, deployment procedures, and system interaction workflows belong in runbooks or operational documentation, not embedded in source code comments.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about where operational docs belong

rule: In authorization-related classes and methods, place the principal or identity parameter at the top of the parameter list for consistency.
mode_rationale: Simple parameter ordering convention for auth code

rule: For REST PATCH/PUT endpoints, place the data being updated in the request body (or query params), not as path parameters; path segments should identify the resource, not carry update values.
mode_rationale: Single convention: update data goes in body, not path segments

rule: When querying metrics or data for a specific entity version, filter by the exact entity identifier rather than an alias like 'latest' that may include data from other versions.
mode_rationale: Single principle: filter by exact entity ID, not aliases like 'latest'

rule: Review hardcoded timeout and wait values critically; suspiciously long durations (e.g., one hour) should be justified or reduced, even if inherited from existing code.
mode_rationale: Simple code review convention

rule: Before adding a database query to enforce a constraint, verify the constraint isn't already enforced by the UX or calling code — skip the query if it adds latency without user-visible benefit.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline about avoiding unnecessary DB queries when constraints are already enforced

rule: When binding a service or scheduler in a shared injection module, verify which deployment instances actually need it to avoid unnecessary resource consumption.
mode_rationale: Simple check: verify which deployments need a service before binding

rule: When modifying behavior gated by a feature flag, verify that the change doesn't unintentionally expand the flag's scope to cover additional cases beyond its original intent.
mode_rationale: Single convention to verify feature flag boundaries

rule: Raise specific, named exception types rather than generic ones so callers can catch and handle them precisely.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use named exception types instead of generic ones

rule: Use unique data identifiers (e.g. object keys) as React component keys instead of array indices when the data provides a natural unique key.
mode_rationale: React key assignment convention

rule: Always include all referenced values in React useEffect dependency arrays, especially after stabilizing callbacks.
mode_rationale: Simple React convention about dependency arrays

rule: Read shared application state from the centralized store (useStore) rather than deriving it locally or passing it through props when the store already provides it.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to read shared state from the centralized store

rule: Write BDD test step definitions in natural, readable language that clearly conveys the action and expected outcome without ambiguity.
Align and format complex expressions or chained operations for readability, especially in functional pipelines.
mode_rationale: Formatting convention for readability

rule: Test data should match realistic API input values (e.g., use the actual casing the API sends) to catch case-sensitivity bugs.
mode_rationale: Testing convention: use realistic API values in test data

rule: Rebase or merge from the base branch before pushing to ensure your PR does not accidentally revert changes recently landed by other engineers.
mode_rationale: Single rule: rebase/merge before pushing to avoid reverting others' work
