rule: Metadata such as failure reasons should be recorded only at the final status transition, not at every intermediate stage, to avoid noise and redundancy.
mode_rationale: Single convention: record metadata at final transition only

rule: Record telemetry and audit trail events after the operation succeeds, not before, to avoid logging events for operations that actually failed.
mode_rationale: Single timing rule for when to log telemetry events

rule: Use reduce when building a new value from a collection; reserve forEach for intentional side effects like RPC calls where no return value is needed.
mode_rationale: Simple coding style convention

rule: When the same logic is repeated across call sites, encapsulate it as a method on the owning domain class rather than duplicating it inline.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to encapsulate repeated logic in domain methods

rule: When a client class accumulates many injected dependencies for different concerns (e.g., JWT signing, config management, API calls), refactor it into a minimal API client plus dedicated helper classes (e.g., a separate JWT signer) so the client can be reused across contexts without pulling in unnecessary dependencies.
mode_rationale: Multi-step refactoring workflow: identify concerns, extract helpers, slim down the client

rule: Reference defined enum values rather than checking string literals to ensure type safety and catch errors at compile time.
mode_rationale: Single convention: reference enum values not string literals

rule: When documenting external resources like cron jobs or infrastructure components in code comments, reference them by name rather than inlining their configuration details, which can silently drift out of date.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention to reference by name

rule: When using well-known specifications (e.g., JSON Schema meta-schemas), reference the official spec rather than creating a custom copy.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to reference official specs

rule: After triggering an async action (start, stop, update), refresh the relevant data so the UI reflects the new state, including updating action buttons and status indicators.
mode_rationale: Simple UI convention to refresh data after mutations

rule: When introducing new domain exceptions (e.g., AccessDeniedException), add them to the centralized error handler so they are consistently mapped to the correct HTTP status across all endpoints.
mode_rationale: Convention to register new exceptions in the central handler

rule: When creating an actor system, always register a shutdown hook to ensure graceful shutdown, following the established pattern used elsewhere in the codebase.
mode_rationale: Single convention for actor system lifecycle

rule: When implementing a plugin or field registry pattern, centralize the registry keys to prevent collisions between independently registered components, especially when registration order is non-deterministic.
mode_rationale: Single convention about centralizing registry keys to prevent collisions

rule: When adding new optional fields to persisted data models (e.g., BSON/JSON documents), add a test that verifies successful deserialization of existing records that include the new field to protect against future breakage.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to add deserialization tests when adding optional fields to persisted models

rule: When validating file paths for security, also reject null bytes to prevent null byte injection attacks that can bypass path traversal checks.
mode_rationale: Single security rule about null byte validation

rule: Use standardized code comments to mark lines that must be updated when cutting a release branch, so they are easy to find and not missed.
mode_rationale: Single convention about marking release-sensitive lines

rule: Remove commented-out lines left behind during refactoring and audit surrounding components for the same legacy pattern (e.g., legacy icon imports) to track follow-up cleanup.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup convention for refactoring leftovers

rule: Do not add validation checks that do not affect correctness — they add maintenance burden and complexity without preventing real bugs.
mode_rationale: Single principle against unnecessary validation checks

rule: Remove unnecessary encode/decode round-trips and other debugging artifacts before merging; if the operation has no functional purpose, simplify it.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup rule before merging

rule: When a file format or technology is no longer supported (e.g., MDX in Storybook), remove the files rather than refactoring them into the deprecated format.
After resolving merge conflicts, check for and remove duplicate imports — consider enabling compiler flags that treat duplicate imports as errors.
mode_rationale: Single convention: check for duplicate imports after merge conflict resolution

rule: Do not use `.map()` that returns the same value unchanged; it adds unnecessary iteration with no transformation.
mode_rationale: Single rule: no no-op .map() calls

rule: After refactoring return types (e.g., from throwing to Option), remove now-unnecessary error-handling wrappers like Try that were left over from the previous implementation.
mode_rationale: Cleanup convention to remove stale wrappers after refactoring

rule: Tests that have been skipped for an extended period (months or more) with no plan to re-enable should be deleted rather than carried forward as dead code.
mode_rationale: Simple test hygiene rule about long-skipped tests

rule: If code introduces a dependency or integration that isn't tested or currently intended for use, either remove it or add tests — don't ship dead code that may silently fail in production.
mode_rationale: Single rule against shipping untested dead code

rule: When a field's context already implies its type, use the shorter name to reduce redundancy (e.g., `pvcName` instead of `persistentVolumeClaimName` when inside a PVC-related object).
mode_rationale: Simple naming brevity convention

rule: Do not duplicate cleanup or state-reset logic in both catch/then blocks and a finally block; let the finally block handle it once.
mode_rationale: Single code organization rule about finally blocks

rule: Do not add conditional checks for values that are already guaranteed to be truthy by the surrounding control flow.
mode_rationale: Code hygiene rule against unnecessary conditionals

rule: Do not specify both a fixed height and a min-height on the same element when the min-height is redundant.
mode_rationale: CSS hygiene rule about conflicting properties

rule: Remove default values for props or parameters that are always explicitly provided by callers, as dead defaults obscure the actual data flow.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup convention for unused defaults

rule: Code comments should not restate information that is already obvious from the surrounding context; keep comments concise and additive.
mode_rationale: Simple commenting convention to keep comments concise

rule: If a variable is already defaulted to a non-nullable value (e.g., `= []`), do not use optional chaining (`?.`) on it — the guard is redundant and misleads readers about nullability.
mode_rationale: Code clarity rule about unnecessary guards

rule: Do not wrap a value that is already an Option in another Option and then flatten; access the Option value directly to avoid unnecessary nesting.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to avoid unnecessary Option nesting

rule: Do not mix in a trait that is already included in the parent type's definition, as it adds noise without changing behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple Scala convention about redundant trait mixins

rule: Do not call .toString or other type conversion methods on values that are already the target type — check the declared type of upstream variables before converting.
mode_rationale: Simple convention against unnecessary type conversions

rule: Remove documentation files that have been superseded by newer docs or tooling changes to avoid misleading developers.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation hygiene rule

rule: When a referenced Jira ticket is closed as 'won't do', remove associated TODO comments, placeholder types, and dead code paths that existed only for that ticket.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup convention for dead ticket references

rule: Review and remove TODO comments that are no longer valid before merging; stale TODOs create confusion about whether work is still pending.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: clean up outdated TODO comments before merge

rule: Remove CI pipeline parameters that are only needed for integration testing before merging to the main branch.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup rule for CI parameters

rule: Do not write tests that don't assert meaningful behavior; if the test scenario cannot actually occur given the API contract, the test is unnecessary.
mode_rationale: Single testing hygiene rule

rule: Do not add dependency injection annotations (such as @Named) unless they are required to disambiguate bindings; unnecessary annotations add noise and mislead readers.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to avoid noisy DI annotations

rule: Do not apply `distinct` to queries on fields that are already guaranteed to be unique (e.g., primary keys), as it adds unnecessary overhead.
mode_rationale: Simple performance guideline about redundant queries

rule: Don't add fields (like createdAt) that serve no functional purpose and complicate the implementation — if the data is only useful for debugging, rely on logs or audit trails instead.
mode_rationale: Design guideline to avoid unnecessary fields

rule: Do not wrap components in React fragments when there is only a single child element; fragments are only needed to group multiple sibling elements without adding a DOM node.
Do not add explicit return values or unnecessary expressions at the end of Scala methods that return `Unit`.
mode_rationale: Scala style convention for Unit methods

rule: Avoid unnecessary wrapper elements (e.g., extra span or div) in JSX when the child element can stand alone.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: avoid unnecessary wrapper divs/spans in JSX

rule: Remove code that has no effect or is no longer needed rather than leaving it in place; verify that leftover imports, hooks, or statements are still required.
Delete code that is not actively used instead of leaving it in the codebase undocumented; unused code adds maintenance burden and confusion without providing value.
mode_rationale: Single convention: delete unused code instead of keeping it around

rule: Verify that new exports, components, and utility functions are actually imported and used somewhere; remove any dead code before merging.
mode_rationale: Simple hygiene rule: verify exports are used, remove dead code

rule: Remove unused functions and imports before submitting a PR; dead code can cause CI build failures and adds confusion.
mode_rationale: Code hygiene rule to remove dead code

rule: Do not include files that are not imported or referenced anywhere in the codebase; verify that new or modified files are actually used before submitting a PR.
mode_rationale: Simple code hygiene rule about unused files

rule: When a function argument is unused, remove it from the function signature rather than adding an eslint-disable comment to suppress the warning.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: remove unused args rather than suppressing lint

rule: Remove unused method parameters (such as implicit DominoContext) rather than keeping them speculatively for possible future use; reduce boilerplate over speculative compatibility.
When renaming a class, update all related names (events, messages, methods, variables) that reference the old name to maintain naming consistency across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Naming consistency convention when renaming

rule: When refactoring removes or changes the behavior of a function, rename it to accurately reflect the new behavior and avoid confusing future readers.
When renaming a field or method during a refactor, update all call sites to use the new name rather than adding compatibility aliases that obscure the actual type.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence refactoring convention

rule: When displaying optional values in user-facing contexts (e.g., audit trails), resolve None/null to a human-readable default like "Unassigned" using a helper function.
mode_rationale: Single display convention for null/absent values

rule: The `useX` naming convention is reserved for React hooks; do not use it for component props, as it misleads readers into thinking it is a hook.
mode_rationale: Naming convention reserving useX for React hooks

rule: When a search or filter parameter changes, reset the pagination offset to zero because the previous page position may no longer exist in the filtered result set.
mode_rationale: Single behavioral rule: reset offset on filter change

rule: When fetching data that sets multiple state variables, ensure error handlers also reset dependent state (e.g., flags and content) to prevent stale values from a previous successful fetch from persisting.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to reset dependent state in error handlers

rule: When creating a versioned record, resolve all runtime-dependent fields (e.g., environment ID, commit ID) at creation time and persist the resolved values to ensure reproducibility.
mode_rationale: Single architectural rule about versioned records

rule: API response type names should make sense for every endpoint that uses them; avoid naming a shared response after a single operation (e.g., 'SagasStartedResponse' is wrong for a stop endpoint).
mode_rationale: Naming convention for shared API response types

rule: Remove or restrict interfaces from shared modules when direct injection should be prevented; keeping implementation classes in the implementation module (rather than exposing them via an interface module) ensures consumers use the intended service facade.
mode_rationale: Convention about controlling injection scope via interface visibility

rule: When conditionally rendering in React, return null rather than an empty wrapper element like <span> or <div> to avoid polluting the DOM.
mode_rationale: Simple React convention for conditional rendering

rule: API and wrapper methods should return typed data structures rather than pre-serialized strings; let callers decide how to format or log the data. If the return value is not needed, return Unit.
mode_rationale: API design convention about return types

rule: If a method mutates a builder or performs side effects and callers discard the return value, the return type should be Unit — a non-Unit return type implies a pure transformation the caller should use.
mode_rationale: Single convention about return type accuracy

rule: Reuse existing shared mock data from centralized mock directories (e.g., api/__mocks__) instead of creating duplicate mocks within individual component directories.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about mock reuse

rule: Before defining an ad-hoc type or interface, check if an existing domain type (e.g., ArtifactFile) already models the data; reuse it to reduce duplication and drift.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to check for existing types before defining new ones

rule: Use existing authorization primitives (e.g., PrivateRoute's built-in 403 handling) instead of implementing custom authorization checks that duplicate framework functionality.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use built-in auth primitives instead of custom duplicates

rule: Before creating a new UI component, check whether an existing component with similar functionality already exists in the codebase and can be reused or extended.
mode_rationale: Single convention about component reuse

rule: When a common/shared component already exists (e.g., a Link component), use it instead of creating a local duplicate with equivalent functionality.
mode_rationale: Simple DRY convention for shared components

rule: Before creating a new constant, check if an existing one already serves the purpose; if a default constant's value is insufficient, increase it rather than introducing a confusingly-named duplicate.
Use existing shared utility functions and hooks (e.g., date formatting helpers like `getFromNowString` from `useDate`) instead of reimplementing the same logic in new components.
Before implementing new data-fetching or state logic, check if an existing hook or utility already provides the needed functionality and use it instead of duplicating logic.
mode_rationale: Simple reuse convention

rule: Use existing shared library utilities (e.g., domino-pytk) for common operations like resource fetching and data traversal instead of writing custom implementations.
mode_rationale: Single rule: prefer existing shared utilities over custom implementations

rule: Before creating new mock data or test fixtures, check for existing mock generators and data factories in the codebase and reuse them to avoid duplication.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to check for existing test fixtures before creating new ones

rule: When obfuscating or redacting sensitive values, use existing platform redaction mechanisms (e.g., the redacted method on telemetry events) rather than rolling custom obfuscation logic.
mode_rationale: Security convention: use platform redaction, not custom logic

rule: Reference route paths from the canonical exported PATHS constant rather than hardcoding path strings, to ensure consistency and catch breakage at compile time.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use PATHS constant instead of hardcoded route strings

rule: Before creating inline styles or new styled components, check if an existing shared styled component already handles the same styling (e.g., shared StyledTag with margin).
mode_rationale: Simple rule to check for existing shared styled components before creating new ones

rule: Use the project's existing shared components (modals, buttons, etc.) rather than creating ad-hoc replacements, to maintain visual and behavioral consistency across the app.
mode_rationale: Convention to use shared components for consistency

rule: Before writing custom polling or wait logic in tests, check if an existing helper method already handles the use case (e.g., wait_for_status) to avoid duplicating logic and keep tests concise.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to check for existing helpers before writing new ones

rule: When an endpoint returns data filtered by user permissions, explicitly verify that unauthenticated or non-user callers cannot access restricted data, and raise security concerns in review if unclear.
mode_rationale: Security convention: verify access control on filtered endpoints

rule: When modifying shared utility code (e.g., error handling utilities), run the broader test suite to catch regressions in all dependent sections.
mode_rationale: Single testing guideline about test scope for shared code

rule: When modifying shared utility code (e.g., error handling utilities), run the full end-to-end test suite to catch unintended regressions across features that depend on the utility.
mode_rationale: Single rule to run full e2e suite when modifying shared utilities

rule: When introducing new configuration values that affect routing or paths, default to the safe/backward-compatible value first and change it in a follow-up PR to prevent breaking production.
mode_rationale: Single rule about defaulting to backward-compatible values

rule: Follow the Databricks Scala style guide for curly brace usage in multi-line expressions and control structures.
mode_rationale: Simple style convention: follow Databricks Scala guide for braces

rule: Review conditional branches and guard clauses to identify code that can never be reached due to earlier conditions, and remove any debugging or test code that was accidentally left in.
mode_rationale: Code hygiene rule: remove dead code and debugging artifacts

rule: When migrating or refactoring, remove color constants, styled components, or other exports that are no longer referenced anywhere in the codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple cleanup convention

rule: When adding new endpoints or refactoring, remove code paths that are no longer needed rather than leaving them in place.
mode_rationale: Basic code hygiene convention

rule: If a feature (like a lease holder check) was designed for a use case that doesn't exist in the current PR, remove it to reduce complexity.
mode_rationale: Single convention: remove unused features to reduce complexity

rule: Do not fetch data that is never used in a component. When multiple sibling components within the same form need the same data, lift the fetch to a shared parent or context instead of duplicating the request.
mode_rationale: Data fetching convention about lifting shared data

rule: Do not include unused fields in data structures or event payloads; remove fields that are not actively consumed.
mode_rationale: Single code hygiene convention about removing unused fields

rule: When removing all usages of a module during a refactor, also delete the now-unused source file to prevent dead code from accumulating.
mode_rationale: Single cleanup convention during refactors

rule: Do not connect or inject a global store into a component that does not use it; remove unnecessary store dependencies.
mode_rationale: Convention to remove unnecessary store dependencies

rule: Remove state variables (and their setters) that are declared but never read, as they add unnecessary complexity and can mislead future readers.
mode_rationale: Simple code cleanup convention

rule: Test specs should not contain unused test fixtures, setup data, or helper objects; clean up any test data that is no longer exercised by assertions.
mode_rationale: Simple test hygiene convention

rule: Do not include code in a PR that cannot be exercised or validated until a future PR; defer it to keep the PR reviewable and testable.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to defer unexercisable code to future PRs

rule: Do not add validation checks that guard against states which are technically valid and do not affect correctness, as they add maintenance burden and complexity without meaningful safety benefit.
mode_rationale: Single guideline against unnecessary validation

rule: When a class or method's responsibilities narrow or change, update its name to reflect the current behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention to keep names aligned with behavior

rule: When adding a new method with overlapping scope, rename the existing ambiguous method to be more specific so callers don't accidentally use the wrong one.
mode_rationale: Naming convention when adding overlapping methods

rule: Do not merge PRs with temporary or unpinned dependency versions; re-pin versions once the upstream branch or package is merged.
mode_rationale: Single rule about dependency version hygiene

rule: When migrating to a new build tool (e.g., tsc to Vite), replace all relevant scripts (build, watch, type-check) with the new tool's equivalents rather than leaving legacy scripts alongside the new ones.
mode_rationale: Single convention about complete migration

rule: When a conditional structure checks against multiple discrete values and is likely to grow, use an array with .includes() instead of if/else or switch chains.
mode_rationale: Refactoring guideline for extensible conditionals

rule: When a function has many Optional parameters, replace them with a typed configuration/filter object to improve readability and prevent positional argument errors.
mode_rationale: API design convention for parameter clarity

rule: Replace magic numbers with named constants (e.g., `MAX_GOALS_SHOWN`) and reuse the constant wherever the same value appears.
Replace hardcoded sleep calls with condition-based polling or explicit state checks, since sleeps provide no guarantee the awaited condition is met.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to avoid flaky sleep-based waits

rule: Even endpoints intended for broad access should verify the caller is at least an authenticated Domino user, to prevent anonymous access to internal data.
mode_rationale: Simple security rule about minimum authentication

rule: When a function only makes sense with a value present, accept a required parameter instead of an Option so the caller is forced to validate before calling.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use required params when a value must be present

rule: New external provider integrations (e.g., BitBucket, GitHub) must include system tests that exercise the real API endpoints, not just unit tests with mocks.
mode_rationale: Single testing requirement for external integrations

rule: Do not mark API fields as Optional when the enclosing context guarantees they will be present; align nullability with the actual domain invariants.
mode_rationale: API design rule about nullability alignment

rule: Never add frontend routes under reserved backend path prefixes like /cloud-admin to avoid routing conflicts.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to avoid routing conflicts with reserved paths

rule: When a picker or selector depends on a parent entity (e.g., project), reset its selected value when the parent ID changes to prevent stale selections.
mode_rationale: Convention to reset child state when parent changes

rule: Always reset loading/fetching state (e.g., setIsFetching(false)) in catch blocks to prevent the UI from being stuck in an infinite loading state when an API call fails.
mode_rationale: Single rule about resetting state in catch blocks

rule: Do not resolve PR review comments until the underlying issue has actually been fixed in a commit.
mode_rationale: Simple PR workflow convention

rule: Resolve colors from the theme at the component that owns the rendering and pass them as props, rather than wrapping components in withTheme or resolving colors deep in helper functions.
mode_rationale: Single convention about where to resolve theme colors

rule: Do not leave unresolved TODO comments in code that ships; either address them in the current PR or remove them and track the work in a ticket.
mode_rationale: Single code hygiene rule about TODO comments

rule: Place resource identifiers like projectId in the URL path rather than the request body to follow RESTful API conventions.
mode_rationale: RESTful convention about placing identifiers in URL paths

rule: Never remove pagination or use unbounded loops that pull all data from a paginated API; always respect client-specified page sizes and limits to avoid performance degradation at scale.
mode_rationale: Single performance rule about honoring pagination constraints

rule: Do not lint, format, or modify files that are not maintained by your team; ignore them in your tooling configuration instead.
mode_rationale: Single principle about not modifying other teams' files

rule: When naming methods like `map`, respect the established functional programming contract — `map` on a type should return the same type; use a more specific name if the method does something different.
mode_rationale: Naming convention about established method semantics

rule: Do not conflate HTTP authentication/authorization errors (401, 403) with feature-disabled states; each status code has specific meaning and should be handled distinctly to avoid incorrect application behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about using HTTP status codes correctly

rule: Do not remove classes or files solely because they have no explicit callers; frameworks like Play may discover and wire them implicitly (e.g., *Actions classes), and removing them can silently break functionality.
mode_rationale: Convention about understanding framework conventions before deleting

rule: Service implementations that make HTTP calls or depend on external infrastructure belong in the infrastructure layer, not in the domain or server layer.
mode_rationale: Single principle: HTTP/infra code belongs in infrastructure layer

rule: Files and route references must be placed in the correct domain module; do not add cross-module references that violate module ownership.
mode_rationale: Single architectural boundary rule

rule: Do not import from a module's internal file paths; expose what is needed through the module's public `index` file and import from that single entry point.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention for module import boundaries

rule: Domain/interface modules must not reference API/web module types; mapping between domain and API models should flow in the correct dependency direction as enforced by the build system.
mode_rationale: Single architectural rule about dependency direction

rule: Application-level configuration files (e.g., tsconfig.json in apps/) should not reference internal packages directly; keep dependency boundaries clean and revert accidental config changes.
mode_rationale: Single config hygiene rule for monorepos

rule: Use hyphens (kebab-case) instead of camelCase for casing in REST API URL paths.
mode_rationale: Single REST API URL casing convention

rule: Do not use an '/all' suffix on REST collection endpoints; the collection URL itself should return all resources.
mode_rationale: Single REST API naming convention

rule: Fields that are optional in REST API DTOs for backward compatibility should be required in the domain model; keep the distinction clear and plan to version the API to tighten optional fields.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about field optionality across layers

rule: After updating dependencies (e.g., domino-api), restart the dev server before committing so the lock file reflects the correct resolved state; stale lock changes will break CI.
mode_rationale: Single operational rule about lock file consistency

rule: API endpoints should follow RESTful conventions — use resource-oriented paths (e.g., GET /v4/workspaces) rather than action-oriented or verbose custom paths.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for REST endpoints

rule: In RESTful APIs, name schema/DTO types as entities (e.g., 'NewComputeTier') not actions (e.g., 'ComputeTierCreation'), and ensure the name is appropriate for all operations that use it.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for REST schema types

rule: GET APIs should not use request bodies; use query parameters instead, or change the HTTP method to POST if a body is required.
mode_rationale: Single REST convention about GET request bodies

rule: Design API endpoints using RESTful resource hierarchy patterns (collection/id/collection) and model capabilities as subresources rather than ad-hoc endpoints.
mode_rationale: Simple API design convention for resource paths

rule: When wrapping a base library component (e.g., Ant Design), explicitly restrict the exposed prop types to only those the design system supports rather than passing through the full base type.
mode_rationale: Single rule about restricting exposed props on wrapper components

rule: Event streaming or cursor-based loops must include retry logic so that transient failures (e.g. cursor closure, node failover) are automatically recovered without dropping events.
mode_rationale: Resilience convention: streaming loops must include retry logic

rule: Use a structured retry policy with backoff and max attempts instead of infinite loops for error recovery and reconnection logic.
In E2E tests, use a retry-with-timeout pattern (poll until a condition is met within a deadline) instead of a fixed sleep, so tests are both faster on success and resilient to timing variation.
mode_rationale: Testing convention: poll-until-condition instead of fixed sleep

rule: When an API endpoint always returns null/empty on success, return HTTP 204 No Content instead of advertising a nullable response schema.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use 204 when endpoint returns nothing on success

rule: Validate user-supplied identifiers (IDs, enums) at the controller layer and return a 400 Bad Request with a descriptive message rather than letting invalid input propagate and produce a 500.
mode_rationale: Single validation convention at controller layer

rule: Authentication and authorization failures should return 401/403, not 400; distinguish auth errors from input validation errors with appropriate HTTP status codes.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about correct HTTP status code usage

rule: When an entity is in a transitional state (e.g., building), return an empty or default response from informational endpoints rather than throwing a 500 error.
mode_rationale: Single error-handling convention for transitional states

rule: Plugin interface methods should return focused, single-purpose types (e.g., Seq[V1EnvVar] from getEnvironmentVariables) rather than mixed types, so the interface remains extensible for future plugins.
mode_rationale: Architecture guideline about plugin return types being single-purpose

rule: Return the result of a function call directly instead of assigning it to an intermediate variable that is immediately returned.
mode_rationale: Simple code style rule to avoid unnecessary intermediate variables

rule: Prefer returning a result type over throwing exceptions, letting callers decide how to handle the error.
mode_rationale: Single error-handling style convention

rule: When a function already computes or retrieves a value that callers need, return it directly rather than forcing callers to re-derive it separately.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about function return values

rule: Async data-fetching functions should return their results so callers can use them directly, rather than relying on state updates and extra useEffects to chain dependent calls.
mode_rationale: Single convention about async data flow in React

rule: Functions should return computed results rather than requiring callers to pass in empty containers to be filled.
mode_rationale: Function design convention about immutability

rule: When building a reusable component, make opinionated behaviors optional via props so the component can serve different use cases without forcing all consumers into one behavior.
mode_rationale: Single design principle about making component behaviors optional via props

rule: Generic, reusable UI components (e.g., cards, modals) should be placed in the shared packages/ui library, not in app-specific module directories.
mode_rationale: Code organization convention for shared UI components

rule: When data has already been retrieved in the current scope, reuse the existing reference rather than making a redundant fetch call.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention about avoiding redundant fetches

rule: When adding new API-based test steps to a feature file, check whether they can replace existing UI-based or duplicated steps in the same file to reduce redundancy.
mode_rationale: Testing convention: consolidate duplicate step definitions

rule: When a custom hook has been created for a concern (e.g., theme access), use it consistently across the codebase instead of falling back to older patterns.
mode_rationale: Simple consistency rule: use the team's custom hooks

rule: Use the project's existing useDate utilities for date formatting and localization instead of writing custom date logic, to ensure consistent locale-aware formatting.
mode_rationale: Convention to use project's useDate utilities over custom logic

rule: Before writing custom error extraction logic, check if the codebase already provides a utility (e.g., safeGetErrorMessage) for safely extracting error messages from API responses.
mode_rationale: Convention to check for existing utilities first

rule: When an existing field already holds the data you need, override or delegate to it rather than adding a redundant parameter that duplicates information.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to override existing fields rather than adding redundant parameters

rule: Before writing new handler logic, check if an existing handler can be reused or extended with minor changes to avoid duplication.
mode_rationale: Simple DRY convention for handlers

rule: Before writing custom null/error handling logic, check for existing utility helpers in the codebase that already handle the same edge case (e.g., K8s API null responses).
mode_rationale: Simple convention to check for existing utilities before writing new ones

rule: Before creating custom UI elements, check if an existing component in the shared library (e.g., ActionDropdown.Button) already provides the needed functionality.
mode_rationale: Single guideline to check for existing components first

rule: When implementing paginated API responses, use the existing PaginatedResponse traits from the shared utilities rather than creating ad-hoc pagination wrappers.
mode_rationale: Single rule to use shared PaginatedResponse traits

rule: When an existing parameter or request class already models the inputs you need, reuse it rather than passing individual parameters — this simplifies method signatures and keeps the API consistent.
mode_rationale: API design convention about parameter reuse

rule: Before creating custom styled elements (e.g., styled anchor tags, custom link wrappers), check if an existing shared component already provides the needed functionality.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: check for existing shared components before creating custom ones

rule: When persisting user preferences or state to storage, use the existing Preferences utility (or UiUx state API) rather than writing custom localStorage logic.
mode_rationale: Single convention about using existing Preferences utility

rule: Use existing shared components (e.g., SpinnerWrapper for loading states) instead of re-implementing conditional rendering logic inline.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to prefer existing shared components over inline reimplementation

rule: Use existing shared constants (e.g., NOT_AVAILABLE_PLACEHOLDER) instead of defining inline literals for common placeholder or sentinel values.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use shared constants over inline literals

rule: Revert exploratory or debugging changes to shared config files (e.g., package.json) that did not resolve the issue before merging.
Keep pull requests focused by reverting changes that are not directly related to the PR's purpose, even if they are minor or seemingly harmless.
mode_rationale: PR hygiene convention about keeping changes focused

rule: When modifying cache keys or the data a cache stores, evaluate whether cache invalidation logic also needs to change and get sign-off from the cache owner.
mode_rationale: Simple review guideline when modifying cache keys

rule: Review logging statements to ensure they use appropriate log levels; remove debug/trace logging or bump it down from info level before merging to production.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to verify appropriate log levels

rule: Scrutinize yarn.lock or package-lock.json changes in PRs to ensure they are intentional and necessary, not accidental artifacts.
mode_rationale: Simple PR review guideline

rule: Review log statements to ensure they do not inadvertently log authentication secrets, tokens, or sensitive headers.
mode_rationale: Single security review guideline

rule: Authorization should run at the top of a service method before any business logic executes, and must cover all code paths — avoid conditional auth that can be bypassed if no condition matches.
mode_rationale: Single convention: auth checks run first, covering all paths

rule: After modifying API dependencies or package versions, run the project's code-generation or update scripts to ensure all downstream package.json files and generated types stay in sync.
mode_rationale: Single-action reminder to run scripts after dependency changes

rule: When modifying or replacing DOM elements in shared components, run the e2e test suite and perform a visual check to catch regressions in downstream consumers.
mode_rationale: Multi-step procedure: run e2e suite then perform visual check when modifying shared DOM elements

rule: When adding or changing behavior (e.g., aria labels) on a shared component, run integration/E2E tests (e.g., cucu tests) to ensure the change does not break consumers that rely on specific selectors.
mode_rationale: Single testing reminder, not a multi-step procedure

rule: When adding a new Bazel project/module, run the project generation script (e.g., dev/bazel/gen_bazelproject.py) so that IDE tooling like IntelliJ picks up the new target.
mode_rationale: Procedural step that must be invoked when adding Bazel modules to update IDE tooling

rule: Always use optional chaining or a guard when accessing array elements by index, unless you can guarantee the element exists.
mode_rationale: Simple defensive coding rule

rule: Enum values in Scala should use camelCase to match the project's naming conventions, even though other languages may use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for Scala enum values

rule: Scala for-comprehensions with Futures execute sequentially (flatMap), not in parallel; assign Futures to vals before the for-comprehension if parallel execution is needed.
mode_rationale: Performance convention about Future parallelism in for-comprehensions

rule: Documentation in an interface module should not reference implementation-layer concepts or types from other modules; move implementation-specific edge case descriptions closer to where those details are resolved.
mode_rationale: Single documentation scoping principle

rule: Do not enable background services (watchers, schedulers, periodic jobs) in all pod types when they are only needed in a specific one (e.g., dispatcher) — this avoids unnecessary k8s API load, heap/CPU waste, and future side-effect multiplication bugs.
mode_rationale: Single guideline: only enable background services in the pod types that need them

rule: Use the correct semantic HTML element for interactive controls; do not use a Link component when the behavior is that of a button.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about correct HTML element usage

rule: Do not reuse the same endpoint for admin and regular user access when the response shape, visible fields, or permission model differs — create distinct endpoints to avoid leaking admin-only data.
mode_rationale: Single convention: don't reuse endpoints when response shapes differ by role

rule: Place DTOs in packages that reflect their boundary context (e.g., `api.models` for REST API DTOs, `remotehub.models` for external service mapping DTOs) so reviewers can quickly identify which compatibility constraints apply.
mode_rationale: Package organization convention for DTOs by boundary

rule: Place internal service models in the service interface package, not in the API models package; the api.model package should only contain API model entities.
mode_rationale: Single package organization convention

rule: Keep business logic (e.g., how to create/add entities) in container components or hooks, and keep presentation components free of domain-specific logic so they remain reusable and single-purpose.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention about component responsibilities

rule: Files named styles.tsx should only contain styled-components; if a file contains a standalone component with its own logic, it should be extracted to its own file.
mode_rationale: File organization rule keeping styles and components separate

rule: When the logic for create and update operations diverges significantly, split them into separate methods rather than using a single method with conditional branches.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: split divergent create/update logic into separate methods

rule: When a single integration has fundamentally different implementations (e.g., cloud vs. datacenter), use separate feature flags for each so they can be enabled, disabled, and debugged independently.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence feature flag scoping convention

rule: Module-scoped or broadly-shared test fixtures should be defined in dedicated fixture files, not inline in specific test files, to clarify ownership and reusability.
mode_rationale: Single test organization convention

rule: Extract styled components out of feature component files and into a dedicated styles file to keep component files focused on logic and rendering.
mode_rationale: Single convention: extract styled components into a dedicated styles file

rule: When introducing a v2 implementation alongside a legacy version, create a separate test spec file for the new version rather than adding v2 tests to the existing legacy spec file.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to separate v2 tests from legacy specs

rule: Disentangle unrelated concerns (e.g., client generation, dependency updates, feature logic) into separate PRs so each change is easier to review and reason about in history.
mode_rationale: PR hygiene convention

rule: Validation helpers and required-field definitions are not domain entities; extract them into a dedicated file (e.g., ValidationHelpers) scoped to the package that uses them, keeping domain entity files focused on business concepts.

A service method for a create or update operation should encapsulate its full lifecycle (database persistence and external resource management) rather than splitting responsibilities across the controller and service layers.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: service methods own the full create/update lifecycle

rule: The service layer should not reference REST API DTOs; returning API-specific types from service methods couples all internal consumers to the API definition and propagates changes unnecessarily.
mode_rationale: Single architecture rule: service layer must not reference API DTOs

rule: Services should return Optional values and let the caller decide how to handle absence, rather than throwing exceptions internally — the correct failure mode (IllegalArgument vs IllegalState) depends on the caller's context.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about service return types

rule: Prefer setting default values inline at the destructuring site rather than using separate conditional logic or fallback assignments later in the function body.
mode_rationale: Single code style convention

rule: For non-paginated endpoints, set the internal query limit high enough that it is effectively unreachable, to prevent items from being silently dropped from the response.
mode_rationale: API design convention about internal query limits

rule: Components consumed by multiple modules should be placed in packages/ui rather than owned by a single module, so changes driven by one module's requirements don't break others.
mode_rationale: Architectural convention about shared component ownership

rule: When state can be accessed by multiple threads, add explicit synchronization — protect both reads and writes using a dedicated lock object or volatile + synchronized pattern.
mode_rationale: Single rule: add explicit synchronization for shared mutable state

rule: Always provide default values for environment variables in shell scripts using the `${VAR:-default}` syntax to prevent unbound variable errors.
mode_rationale: Single shell scripting convention

rule: When an API call fails silently (e.g., fetching data that populates a list), show a toast notification so the user understands why the UI is empty rather than leaving them without explanation.
mode_rationale: Single convention: show a toast when API calls fail silently

rule: Prefer a single map operation over separate map and zip calls when transforming collections, to reduce intermediate allocations and improve readability.
mode_rationale: Simple performance/readability convention

rule: Review compound boolean conditions for redundancy; e.g., `(loading && !hasLoaded) || !hasLoaded` reduces to just `!hasLoaded`.
mode_rationale: Code style rule about simplifying boolean logic

rule: Audit events should designate one primary target entity and use the 'affecting' field for secondary entities, following the single-target policy.
mode_rationale: Audit event design convention

rule: Define configuration defaults in one canonical location (e.g., application.conf) and remove duplicate defaults from code to avoid divergence.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence DRY convention for configuration defaults

rule: Keep JSX component props and exported TypeScript interfaces sorted alphabetically for readability and consistent diffs.
mode_rationale: Single ordering convention for readability

rule: Organize import statements with absolute (third-party/library) imports first, followed by relative (local) imports.
mode_rationale: Simple import ordering convention

rule: In E2E tests, use the most specific selector available (e.g., `I wait to see the checkbox "Practitioner"`) rather than generic text matching, to make tests more resilient and intention-clear.
mode_rationale: Simple guideline about selector specificity in E2E tests

rule: When a component has multiple conditional branches based on feature flags, consider splitting it into separate components — one per flag state — to reduce complexity and make cleanup easier when the flag is removed.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence component organization convention for feature flags

rule: Method and function arguments in Scala should start with a lowercase letter, following standard Scala naming conventions.
mode_rationale: Simple Scala naming convention

rule: Document public methods with scaladoc (or the language-appropriate doc comment format) rather than plain inline comments above the method.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention about using doc comments

rule: Add scaladocs or doc comments to non-trivial private methods, especially when their role in the request lifecycle is not immediately obvious from the signature.
mode_rationale: Single convention: add docs to non-trivial private methods

rule: Audit trail events should be scoped to intentional user actions, not fired for every internal system call that happens to share a code path — verify the intended scope and consider performance of event fan-out.
mode_rationale: Single scoping principle for audit event emission

rule: Split authorization checks so each query only requires the permissions it actually needs, rather than demanding broad authorization for narrowly scoped operations.
mode_rationale: Principle of least privilege for authorization

rule: Ensure implementation changes are scoped precisely to what the ticket describes — do not apply fixes to both success and error paths when the ticket only asks for the failure case.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about keeping changes scoped to ticket requirements

rule: Move CI setup steps (like tool installation) into the specific jobs that use them rather than defining them globally, to prepare for extraction and keep CI configuration clearly separated by concern.
mode_rationale: Single CI organization principle

rule: Move computations inside the conditional branch where their result is actually used, rather than computing eagerly outside the branch.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention about lazy evaluation placement

rule: Audit or telemetry event schemas should be scoped to the correct domain entity — do not extend an event for one concept (e.g., BundlePolicy) with fields belonging to a different concept (e.g., Bundle state) even if they are related.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention about keeping event schemas scoped to their domain entity

rule: When adding feature flags or runtime configurations, verify that they are scoped to the correct entity types and not inadvertently enabled for all execution types.
mode_rationale: Single verification rule for feature flags

rule: When watching Kubernetes resources, use field selectors to limit the watch to only the specific resources you need, reducing memory footprint and API load.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use field selectors to limit k8s watches

rule: Leader election should be scoped per service mode (e.g. nucleus mode) rather than electing across all pods and modes, for predictability in where background tasks run.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention for leader election scope

rule: Create mutable data structures (buffers, accumulators) inside the function that owns them rather than allocating them externally and passing them in unused.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about data structure allocation locality

rule: When a function's arguments and return types do not make its scope obvious, prefix the function name with the domain or subsystem it belongs to.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention: prefix with domain when scope isn't obvious from types

rule: Rollback and cleanup logic must be conditional on the operation type — only delete a resource during rollback if the current operation created it, to avoid accidentally deleting pre-existing resources during an update failure.
mode_rationale: Single conditional rule for rollback behavior

rule: When fixing routing issues, scope changes to the affected section's inner routers rather than restructuring the entire top-level routing.
mode_rationale: Simple scoping convention for routing fixes

rule: Name configuration settings and variables to clearly indicate which domain entity they belong to, preventing ambiguity with similarly named concepts elsewhere.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for config settings

rule: When applying string transformations like encoding or replacement to URLs or paths, verify the transformation only affects the intended segment and does not corrupt other parts of the string.
mode_rationale: Simple correctness guideline for string operations

rule: Scope CSS and styled-component overrides to the specific element or variant that needs them; avoid broad selectors that unintentionally affect unrelated components.
Apply theme or style overrides at the most local scope possible rather than modifying global providers, to avoid unintended side effects.
mode_rationale: Single convention: apply theme overrides at local scope, not global

rule: Reserve SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE naming for true constants; use standard camelCase for variables whose values change at runtime.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for constant vs runtime variable casing

rule: When renaming an API field or attribute, search the codebase for the old name to ensure no usages are hidden behind an `any` type that would silently break at runtime.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: search for old name behind any types when renaming API fields

rule: Add section header comments in test scenarios to organize test steps into logical groups, making test failures easier to triage.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention for test organization

rule: Frameworks should default to the most restrictive security posture (e.g., deny anonymous access) so developers must explicitly opt in to less secure behavior rather than accidentally forgetting to opt out.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence security convention about default-deny posture

rule: Security-critical permission scopes and access controls must have dedicated test coverage to catch missing or incorrect permissions.
mode_rationale: Single rule that permission logic must have test coverage

rule: Props that control authorization or permissions should be required, not optional with permissive defaults, to prevent accidental privilege escalation when a parent forgets to pass the prop.
mode_rationale: Single rule: auth props must not be optional

rule: Use self-closing JSX syntax (`<Component />`) for components with no children instead of explicit open/close tags.
mode_rationale: Simple JSX syntax style rule

rule: Use color tokens that match the semantic purpose of the element — e.g., use border color tokens for grid lines and borders, not text color tokens.
mode_rationale: Convention about matching token purpose to element purpose

rule: Split functions that both transform data and make API calls into separate concerns: dedicated mapping functions for value transformations and a single function for the network request.
mode_rationale: Single separation-of-concerns convention

rule: Keep authorization logic (who can access) separate from feature flag or custom feature logic (what is enabled); do not mix them in the same provider or component.
mode_rationale: Architectural separation principle, not a procedure

rule: Keep authorization (authZ) and authentication (authN) as separate concerns; place authN-related logic in the authn-adapter module, not in the authZ interface.
mode_rationale: Single architectural separation-of-concerns rule

rule: Feature-specific specializations of a shared component should live in their own file, not inside the base component's file.
mode_rationale: Simple file organization convention for component specializations

rule: Do not embed configuration or feature-flag values in domain data objects; keep configuration concerns separate from the data model.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence separation of concerns guideline

rule: Each domain entity (apps, model APIs, etc.) should have its own dedicated configuration rather than reusing another entity's config, to maintain clear boundaries and avoid coupling.
mode_rationale: Architecture guideline for config boundaries

rule: Configuration retrieval functions should return raw config values without embedding permission checks; authorization logic should live in its own layer or service.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence separation of concerns principle

rule: Do not merge context objects that have different lifecycles or scopes; a per-request context (DominoContext) and a per-operation context (authorization context) should remain separate parameters even if it looks redundant.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about not merging different-scoped contexts

rule: Do not combine create and update semantics in a single endpoint; separate them so users understand what action they are performing and avoid surprising side effects like an edit silently triggering a new resource creation.
mode_rationale: API design convention to keep create and update distinct

rule: When an entity requires non-trivial or custom serialization logic, keep the serializer in a separate class (e.g., implementing a Serializer trait) rather than coupling it directly to the domain event or entity class.
mode_rationale: Single separation-of-concerns guideline

rule: Isolate dependency or API client changes into a separate PR and merge it promptly to minimize merge conflicts with concurrent work.
mode_rationale: Simple PR scoping convention

rule: Do not overload status enums with derived or computed conditions (e.g., 'expiringSoon'); instead, use a separate boolean field alongside the canonical status to avoid confusion in filtering and API responses.
mode_rationale: Single API design rule against overloading status enums

rule: Domain models should store only IDs for referenced entities; enriched objects with full details belong in the API DTO layer, not the core model.
mode_rationale: Architectural guideline about model layer separation

rule: Do not embed domain-specific logic (e.g., HTML escaping for files) in a generic controller that serves multiple resource types; instead, place that logic in the appropriate domain service layer.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention: domain logic belongs in service layer, not generic controllers

rule: API request functions should not handle error notification concerns; error alerting and UI feedback should be the responsibility of the consuming component or hook.
mode_rationale: Simple separation of concerns rule for API error handling

rule: Extract interactions with external services (e.g., Vault, secret stores) into separate classes rather than mixing them into the main service implementation, for better organization and testability.

Internal service-to-service callbacks and endpoints should be defined on internal API surfaces, not exposed through public APIs, to maintain clear API boundaries and avoid leaking implementation details.
mode_rationale: API boundary convention for internal vs public endpoints

rule: Reusable picker/select components should not include their own label; consumers should wrap them in a form item and control labeling externally.
mode_rationale: Component API design convention

rule: Split configuration into a managed/standardized file and a separate user-customizable file so custom settings layer on top without conflicting with managed defaults.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture convention for config file separation

rule: Keep mock data in a separate file from Storybook story definitions to maintain clean separation of concerns.
mode_rationale: File organization convention for Storybook

rule: Service method signatures should separate pagination, sorting, and filtering into distinct parameters rather than bundling them into a single ambiguous object.
When a function call or method signature has multiple parameters, place each parameter on its own line for readability.
mode_rationale: Single formatting convention for function parameters

rule: Files named 'util' should contain pure utility functions, not React components. Move components to their own files with component-appropriate names.
mode_rationale: File organization convention: utils vs components

rule: Never obscure secret values with placeholder strings in API responses; instead, use separate response types that omit the secret field entirely, and split retrieval into metadata-only and secret-value methods to minimize secret exposure.
mode_rationale: Security convention about using separate response types to avoid exposing secrets

rule: In mapper and serialization tests, define expected input and output as separate class-level fixtures so the test body is a single assertion line, making it clear what is being tested versus what is test data.
mode_rationale: Simple test structure convention

rule: When you suspect a component is unused, verify thoroughly and create a separate ticket for its removal rather than deleting it inline with an unrelated change.
mode_rationale: Single process convention about safe component removal

rule: Use separate try-catch blocks for independent operations that require different rollback logic, rather than grouping them in a single try-catch where partial failure handling becomes ambiguous.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use separate try-catch blocks for independent rollback logic

rule: Place TypeScript type and interface definitions in a dedicated types file (e.g., types.ts), not in a utils file which should contain runtime logic.
mode_rationale: File organization convention for type definitions

rule: Do not reuse a UI default config value as a security validation limit; introduce a separate config key for the maximum allowed value.
mode_rationale: Single security convention about config separation

rule: Avoid overloading domain types to represent unrelated concepts; each enum or sealed trait should model a single coherent domain category.
mode_rationale: Modeling convention about type cohesion

rule: Do not nest unrelated feature toggles inside each other's conditional logic; each feature's visibility should be controlled independently.
mode_rationale: Single principle about independent feature toggle control

rule: When multiple API calls depend on each other or must not run concurrently, ensure they are properly awaited and executed sequentially to prevent race conditions.
mode_rationale: Single concurrency guideline about sequential awaits

rule: Place serialization logic in the domain type's companion object or a dedicated serializer in the same namespace, rather than in consuming services.
mode_rationale: Code placement rule: serialization lives in companion object or dedicated serializer

rule: Place data classification or detection logic (e.g., whether a file is truncated) on the backend where the information is already known, rather than reimplementing complex detection heuristics on the frontend.
mode_rationale: Single architectural guideline about where to place detection logic

rule: When the API supports pagination, sorting must be done server-side; client-side sorting only operates on the current page and produces incorrect results.
mode_rationale: Pagination correctness convention

rule: Only allow service or superuser accounts to perform actions on behalf of other users; regular user requests must always use the authenticated principal's own identity to prevent impersonation risks.
mode_rationale: Single authorization rule about impersonation boundaries

rule: Services should have clear responsibility boundaries; avoid adding methods that bypass the service's core abstraction layer (e.g., direct DB queries in a search service meant to abstract over Elasticsearch).
mode_rationale: Single architecture principle about service boundaries

rule: Always set the name property on Ant Design Form.Item components so the form can properly track and manage field values.
mode_rationale: Simple Ant Design Form.Item convention

rule: Only update state or flags after the associated asynchronous operation (e.g., remote call, database write) completes successfully, not before.
mode_rationale: Single defensive coding rule for async state updates

rule: When multiple components use the same value-to-element mapping, extract the dictionary into a shared constant and reuse it.
mode_rationale: Convention to extract shared mappings into constants

rule: Common/shared components should not force consumers to wrap them in specific context providers; document the requirement at minimum, and prefer encapsulating the dependency internally.
mode_rationale: Architecture convention for shared component dependencies

rule: In a monorepo, shared third-party packages used by a UI library should be declared as peer dependencies and installed at the root, not as direct dependencies of the library package.
mode_rationale: Dependency declaration convention for monorepos

rule: Route-related code used across apps should live in the shared packages directory (e.g., packages/ui/src/core/routes), not in individual app modules.
mode_rationale: Single convention about where shared route code should live

rule: Create shared, reusable test data factory functions in a common test utilities module instead of duplicating test object construction across individual test files.
mode_rationale: Convention to centralize test data construction

rule: Place reusable test utilities in the shared tests-lib module rather than duplicating them in individual adapter or service test directories.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence code organization convention about where to place test utilities

rule: Shell-based plugins and scripts should declare and verify required tool availability before execution, rather than assuming tools like grep are present.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about verifying tool availability in shell scripts

rule: When a user lacks permissions, display a 403 Forbidden page rather than silently redirecting to an unrelated route.
mode_rationale: UX convention for handling unauthorized access

rule: Ensure the initial loading state displays a loader or skeleton; do not rely on checking whether data is empty, as that conflates 'loading' with 'no results'.
mode_rationale: Simple UX convention to display loaders during initial fetch

rule: When a user action triggers multiple sequential API calls, the UI must indicate that work is in progress so the user knows something is happening.
Return a spinner or empty placeholder while async data is loading rather than rendering the component with incomplete or undefined data.
mode_rationale: Simple UI convention for async data loading

rule: Return boolean expressions directly (e.g., `return x && list.includes(x)`) instead of wrapping them in if/else blocks.
mode_rationale: Simple code style convention

rule: When all callers can provide a required value, make the parameter mandatory instead of optional — eliminate None/null handling branches that add unnecessary complexity.
mode_rationale: Simple design convention about eliminating unnecessary optionality

rule: Use Set equality (converting both sides to Sets) instead of verbose manual key-by-key comparison when checking if two collections contain the same elements.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use Set equality for collection comparison

rule: When a collection is already filtered to matching items, use headOption (or equivalent) instead of an additional find call.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention to avoid redundant collection operations

rule: When handling multiple authentication or configuration types, match on the type discriminator field rather than checking which optional fields are present.
mode_rationale: Simple coding convention for type dispatch

rule: When an index file only re-exports a default export, use the shorthand `export { default } from './Module'` instead of importing and re-exporting separately.
mode_rationale: Simple code style convention for re-export shorthand

rule: When a framework requires boilerplate for the common case (e.g., actions that don't need a context parameter), provide a simplified method signature to avoid unnecessary dependencies and placeholder underscores.
mode_rationale: Single API design principle to reduce boilerplate

rule: Extract common variables from complex match/case expressions to reduce the number of cases and improve readability.
mode_rationale: Single refactoring guideline: extract shared variables from match arms

rule: When combining multiple Option[Seq] values, use getOrElse(Seq()) to unwrap and concatenate, then convert back to Option based on emptiness, rather than nesting match/fold expressions.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence Scala idiom for combining Option[Seq] values

rule: Remove redundant null/undefined checks when the subsequent condition already handles the falsy case.
mode_rationale: Single rule: remove redundant null checks

rule: Use authorization APIs directly instead of wrapping them in redundant conditional expressions that add no logic.
mode_rationale: Simple code style rule about avoiding redundant conditionals around auth APIs

rule: When a constructor or factory method already handles edge cases (e.g., single-element sequences), remove redundant conditional branching that duplicates that logic.
mode_rationale: Single convention: remove redundant conditionals when constructors handle edge cases

rule: Use built-in default-value mechanisms (e.g., dict.get with a default) to eliminate unnecessary conditional branches.
mode_rationale: Simple code style preference for defaults over conditionals

rule: When a conditional branch sets a value and returns, drop the else clause and let code fall through to improve readability.
mode_rationale: Simple readability convention about early returns

rule: Use a single button with a `loading` prop to indicate async operations instead of swapping between two different button components based on state; this keeps the UI consistent.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use loading prop instead of swapping button components

rule: Each React component file should contain only one component to maintain clear separation and discoverability.
mode_rationale: Simple file organization convention for React components

rule: Maintain exactly one entry per dependency in package.json; avoid duplicate entries or stray empty lines.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to avoid duplicate dependency entries

rule: Use a single Ant Design form instance per logical form scope; multiple instances fragment state management and bypass validation enforcement.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence Ant Design form convention

rule: Define mock return values once per test setup; avoid overriding fixture-level mocks in individual test cases, as the override semantics can be surprising and fragile.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about mock setup location

rule: Place modal instances at the table level rather than inside each table row; row actions should only trigger the modal, not own their own instance.
Avoid chaining multiple .filter() calls that iterate the same list repeatedly; use .reduce(), function composition, or a transducer to derive results in a single pass.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention: use single-pass iteration instead of chained .filter() calls

rule: In recursive functions, use a mutable accumulator as either a parameter or a return value, not both — dual use leads to duplicate entries and confusing ownership semantics.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about accumulator usage in recursive functions

rule: Split components that handle multiple distinct actions (e.g., start AND stop) into separate, simpler components with a single responsibility.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to split multi-action components

rule: Each authorizer should only enforce its own concern; do not duplicate authorization checks (e.g., anonymous user checks) that are already handled by other authorizers protecting the same endpoint.
mode_rationale: Single principle about authorizer scope

rule: When the same value (e.g., page size) is used in multiple places, reference a single shared constant to ensure consistency and prevent drift.
mode_rationale: Convention to reference a single shared constant

rule: Prefer a single source of truth for state with transformations between external and form representations, rather than syncing multiple independent state stores.
Define dependency versions in a single variable and reference it everywhere to keep related tool versions synchronized and avoid drift.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: one variable for shared version strings

rule: API clients with rate limiting must be injected in singleton scope so all threads share the same instance and respect the rate limit collectively.
mode_rationale: Simple dependency injection convention

rule: When a user explicitly chooses to force an operation, bypass expensive pre-validation checks (e.g., full repo clones) that exist to protect against accidental overwrites.
mode_rationale: Performance convention for force operations

rule: Do not add a validation query to reject invalid IDs if the downstream logic already handles them gracefully (e.g., returns no results); save the extra service call.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention to skip redundant validation when downstream handles it

rule: Do not wrap text in internationalization or whitelabel translation functions when the text is not subject to whitelabeling; use the string directly.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about avoiding unnecessary i18n wrapping

rule: Delete operations on persisted entities should set an archived or soft-delete flag rather than performing hard deletes, and reviewers should verify the flag is actually being set in the implementation.
mode_rationale: Single convention for delete operations on entities

rule: Include a `deletedAt` timestamp field on entities that support soft deletion to maintain an audit trail of when records were removed.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to include deletedAt on soft-deletable entities

rule: When adding sort functionality to an endpoint, verify the sort field matches the product requirement (e.g., 'last started') and document which field is being sorted on.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to verify sort fields match requirements

rule: Prefer spec-first API design (manually defining the OpenAPI/Swagger YAML) over code-first generation, as it makes code reviews easier and provides an extra layer of validation.
mode_rationale: Simple preference for spec-first over code-first approach

rule: Return distinct, resource-specific authorization error messages that identify which resource the user lacks access to, rather than generic 'not authorized' responses.
mode_rationale: Simple convention for resource-specific auth error messages

rule: Error messages for state-management code should distinguish between possible invalid states rather than using generic messages.
mode_rationale: Single error-handling convention

rule: Use specific, domain-qualified names instead of generic terms that could be confused with broader concepts.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention favoring domain-specific names

rule: When a component file contains multiple distinct components or variants (e.g., controlled and uncontrolled versions), split them into separate files.
When a component file contains utility functions, constants, and sub-components alongside the main component, split them into separate files (utils, sub-component, main component).
mode_rationale: Simple organizational rule: separate utils, sub-components, and main component

rule: Split components that grow too long or mix multiple concerns into smaller, focused components; extract injected logic into dedicated child components.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline about component decomposition

rule: Split E2E test specifications into multiple files when cumulative wait times and execution durations risk exceeding CI timeout limits.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about test file size management

rule: Be aware that the spread operator only performs a shallow clone; nested objects retain the same references, which can break immutability guarantees.
mode_rationale: Awareness rule about shallow clone behavior

rule: Define static constants (values that never change between renders) outside the React component body to avoid unnecessary re-creation on every re-render.
mode_rationale: Simple React performance convention

rule: When a simpler API shape (e.g., single-value parameter) satisfies current requirements and expanding it later (e.g., to multi-value) is not a breaking change, ship the simpler version first.
mode_rationale: Single API design principle to ship the simplest version first

rule: Always use strict equality (`===`) instead of loose equality (`==`) for comparisons, unless there is an explicit reason to coerce types.
mode_rationale: Single coding convention: use === over ==

rule: Move styled-component definitions into a separate styles file rather than co-locating them in the component file.
mode_rationale: File organization convention for styled-components

rule: Define styled components outside the React component body to prevent them from being regenerated on every re-render.
mode_rationale: Single performance convention identical to define-styled-components-outside-jsx

rule: Do not silently swallow errors with only console.error; surface failures to the user through UI feedback (toast, banner, or inline error) especially when the failure affects visible state.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about showing errors in UI instead of swallowing them

rule: Mutable state accessed by concurrent callers must be properly synchronized using atomic references or other concurrency primitives; a plain mutable variable is not safe.
mode_rationale: Single concurrency safety rule

rule: When protecting shared mutable state with a lock, synchronize on both read and write paths — a write lock alone does not prevent stale reads from other threads.
mode_rationale: Single rule: synchronize both read and write paths for shared state

rule: When processing all entities (e.g., for indexing or batch operations), use lazy/streaming collections instead of loading everything into memory at once to avoid OOM issues at scale.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention about lazy streaming

rule: When operating on resources that can have multiple instances (e.g., jobs in a project), target by a unique identifier (e.g., execution ID) rather than a broad parent identifier (e.g., project name).
