mode_rationale: Simple convention: use unique IDs over broad parent identifiers

rule: When suppressing compiler warnings, target the specific warning being suppressed rather than using broad suppression annotations that may hide future issues.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about targeted warning suppression

rule: When code needs future revisiting, create a Jira ticket and reference it in the comment rather than leaving open-ended TODO comments that will be forgotten.
mode_rationale: Single process convention for tracking debt

rule: Ensure that the test description accurately reflects what the test actually validates; if it claims to test a behavior change, the test must trigger that change and assert the result.
When testing multiple expected failures, assert each one individually rather than relying on a loop that short-circuits on the first failure.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about individual assertions

rule: When making API fields optional, write at least two tests: one that modifies all optional fields and one that modifies none, to verify correct behavior at both boundaries.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about boundary coverage

rule: When introducing a replacement for existing code (new API spec, new module, etc.), create a ticket to track removal of the old version so it doesn't get forgotten.
mode_rationale: Single convention: create a ticket when introducing replacements for old code

rule: CRUD integration tests should include explicit authorization test cases to verify that unauthorized users cannot access or modify protected resources.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about auth coverage

rule: When a method delegates to a dependency, write a unit test that verifies the dependency is called with the correct arguments, not just that the method succeeds.
mode_rationale: Single testing guideline about verifying dependency arguments

rule: Critical business logic (e.g., cache refresh, data synchronization) must have dedicated tests even when mocking is difficult; find a way to test it rather than skipping coverage.
mode_rationale: Convention that critical logic must have test coverage

rule: When a dependency can throw and is caught with NonFatal, write a test verifying the caller degrades gracefully (e.g., returns a default or partial result) rather than propagating the failure.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention for graceful degradation

rule: When new behavior is introduced that was previously disallowed (e.g., starting an older version stops a newer one), add explicit tests that codify and document this newly permitted behavior.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention for behavioral changes

rule: In polymorphic data models, place commonly-used fields for filtering and sorting at the top level and nest type-specific definitions in dedicated fields to enable efficient querying and pagination.
mode_rationale: Single data modeling convention for polymorphic schemas

rule: Add trace-level logging to all client/integration methods, including the method name and all parameters, to aid debugging of cross-service calls.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: add trace logging with method name and params to client methods

rule: When removing or renaming fields from serialized models, assess whether the change could break serialization compatibility and create a separate ticket to track the migration if so.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about tracking breaking changes separately

rule: Tag E2E feature files and scenarios with component tags (e.g., @projects, @dfs, @workspace) and team tags (e.g., @team-develop) so tests can be triggered selectively by PR scope and owned by the responsible team.
mode_rationale: Single test organization convention for tagging

rule: When code is gated behind feature flags or central config, create cleanup tasks to delete it once the flag is removed or the code is no longer used.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: create cleanup tasks for flag-gated dead code

rule: When a behavior depends on integration between multiple systems (e.g., browser requests through middleware), acknowledge unit test limitations and ensure an integration or e2e test ticket exists rather than forcing the assertion into the wrong test layer.
mode_rationale: Single testing principle about choosing the right test layer

rule: Keep test-only utilities, injection modules, and fixtures in test source directories rather than in production source sets.
mode_rationale: Straightforward code organization rule

rule: When a field accepts multiple value types (e.g., not just strings), add test cases covering non-string and non-default value types to ensure correct handling.
mode_rationale: Simple testing guideline about covering multiple types

rule: API controller methods should log success at info level and errors at error level, including structured context (userId, entity IDs, request parameters) in both cases; extract a reusable executeOperation helper to reduce verbosity.
mode_rationale: Multi-step procedure: log at specific levels, include structured context, extract reusable helper

rule: When implementing rollback or cleanup stacks, write tests that verify execution order and behavior when individual rollback callbacks throw errors.
mode_rationale: Testing guideline about what to cover, not a step-by-step procedure

rule: Style Ant Design components directly via their component API or styled-components wrappers rather than targeting internal class names like .ant-input or .ant-input-affix-wrapper with nested selectors.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to avoid targeting internal Ant Design class names

rule: Query methods that filter, scope, and sort results should have tests verifying each dimension: correct entity type, correct scope (e.g., project), correct status filtering, and correct sort order.
mode_rationale: Single guideline about what dimensions query tests should cover

rule: When passing callbacks to hooks, wrap them in useCallback to produce a stable reference, even if the consuming hook doesn't currently depend on reference stability — treat hooks as black boxes.
mode_rationale: Simple React convention about stable callback references for hooks

rule: Use a stable unique identifier (name, id, or link) as the React key prop instead of array index to avoid rendering bugs on reorder or deletion.
mode_rationale: Simple React rendering best practice

rule: Avoid using inline object literals like `{}` as default values in React props or hooks, as they create a new reference on every render and can cause unwanted re-renders or effect re-executions.
mode_rationale: Simple React convention about avoiding inline object literal defaults

rule: Avoid using mutable object references as useEffect dependencies; extract the specific primitive value you depend on into a variable and use that as the dependency instead.
mode_rationale: Single coding convention about React hook dependencies

rule: Service accounts should go through standard authorization checks (e.g., read access) rather than bypassing auth; add the service account to the authorizer if it lacks access.
mode_rationale: Single rule about how service accounts should be authorized

rule: REST API endpoints returning collections must use a consistent wrapper format with totalCount, paginationDetails, and items array, even when pagination is not needed, so all Domino APIs share the same structure for consumers.
mode_rationale: Simple API response format convention

rule: Side effects like changing user selection or UI state after an async operation must be gated on the success path, not executed unconditionally after the operation completes.
mode_rationale: Single convention: gate side effects on the success path of async operations

rule: State variables should be named to accurately reflect all the data they contain; a state object holding pagination, sorting, and filter state should not be named just 'pagination' as it misleads readers about its scope.
mode_rationale: Naming accuracy rule for state objects

rule: Terminate background polling as soon as the UI state it serves is no longer relevant, not just when the underlying process completes.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention about polling lifecycle

rule: In audit trails and event logs, store only the immutable identifier (e.g., userId) rather than mutable display values (e.g., userName) to avoid stale data; do not pass unused fields as they confuse future readers.
mode_rationale: Logging convention to store immutable identifiers in audit trails

rule: Sensitive configuration values such as signing keys, credentials, and tokens must be stored in vault or Kubernetes secrets, not in plaintext configuration files.
mode_rationale: Simple security convention for sensitive values

rule: Always capture the return value of `setTimeout` (the timeout ID) and use it with `clearTimeout` for proper cleanup; cancel the timeout when the async operation completes.
mode_rationale: Single cleanup convention for timeouts

rule: Assign the return value of `setTimeout()` (the timeout ID) to the variable, not the delay duration; mixing these up makes `clearTimeout` silently fail.
mode_rationale: Simple correctness rule about setTimeout assignment

rule: Storybook stories must not include custom inline styles or workarounds that make components appear to have capabilities they don't actually have — this creates false expectations for consumers.
mode_rationale: Convention about Storybook story fidelity

rule: When wrapping an input stream in a data object, store a factory function (lambda) to regenerate the stream on each access rather than holding a single stream reference that can only be read once.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence pattern for re-readable stream wrappers

rule: When using utility functions that may return a promise or a resolved value, add explicit strict checks rather than relying on truthy/falsy coercion, as a Promise object is always truthy.
mode_rationale: Single convention about strict checks on async returns

rule: Extract repeated string literals (especially query field names and filter keys) into named constants at the class or module level.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization convention

rule: Use enums for fields with a fixed set of valid values and domain-specific types instead of raw Strings to reduce validation code and improve readability.
mode_rationale: Single typing convention for DTO fields

rule: Use Ant Design Form instances and structured arrays of objects for form state instead of loose individual state variables.
mode_rationale: Convention to use Form instances instead of loose state variables

rule: Format log messages with clearly labeled key=value pairs using string interpolation, especially for optional/collection fields, to make logs parseable and readable.
mode_rationale: Logging format convention for key=value pairs

rule: When a shared UI component (e.g., a table) needs style adjustments, make the change in the shared component itself rather than applying CSS overrides from consuming components.
mode_rationale: Simple ownership rule for style changes

rule: Use styled-component props for dynamic styling instead of toggling CSS class names.
mode_rationale: Single convention for dynamic styling approach

rule: Use the function form for styled-components attrs (`() => ({})`) instead of the object form, per the updated API convention.
mode_rationale: API convention for styled-components attrs syntax

rule: Files containing only styled-components should be named `styled-components.tsx` rather than generic names like `styles.tsx`.
Use styled components instead of inline styles, especially when the same styles are repeated across multiple components.
mode_rationale: Simple styling convention to prefer styled components

rule: Use styled-components instead of plain CSS classnames; use data-test attributes for test selectors rather than CSS classes.
mode_rationale: Style convention for CSS approach and test selectors

rule: When a styled component accepts a color prop, type it to accept design token keys rather than raw hex values to maintain consistency with the design system.
mode_rationale: Type convention for color props to accept token keys not hex

rule: Import styled-component files using `import * as Styled` for consistency across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Simple import convention for styled-component files

rule: Model child entities as subresources (e.g., /deploymentTargets/{id}/resourceConfigurations) when they need independent CRUD operations, rather than embedding them only in the parent.
mode_rationale: Single API design convention for child entity endpoints

rule: Input components should support a controlled mode where the parent provides the value via props, not only an uncontrolled internal-state mode.
mode_rationale: Design guideline for input component API surface

rule: When gating UI elements behind permission checks, also suppress the underlying API requests to avoid unnecessary errors and unauthorized network calls.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to suppress network calls alongside UI gating

rule: When async operations fail in a global store action, capture the error in the store's error state (e.g., via `setError`) so the UI can display it, rather than silently swallowing it.
mode_rationale: Simple error handling convention for store actions

rule: When a data fetch or operation fails, show an error state to the user with a clear recovery action (e.g., refresh the page) rather than silently swallowing the failure.
mode_rationale: Single convention about showing error states with recovery actions

rule: When a multi-step operation partially succeeds, surface the failure to the user as a warning with clear guidance on what to do manually, rather than silently swallowing the error.
mode_rationale: Single error-handling convention

rule: When changing API method signatures or adding new parameters, update the corresponding scaladoc/swagger documentation in the same PR to describe the new behavior.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence documentation convention to keep docs in sync

rule: When removing a property from an API schema, also remove it from the required array to avoid invalid specs.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention about keeping API spec fields in sync

rule: Tab components should persist their active tab in the URL and support setting the initial tab from URL parameters for shareability and navigation.
mode_rationale: Single convention about persisting tab state in URL parameters

rule: New or modified integration endpoints (especially third-party provider integrations) must have corresponding system tests that exercise the real endpoint.
mode_rationale: Single convention: new integration endpoints must have system tests

rule: Table empty state components should include a maxContentWidth prop to prevent the empty message from stretching across the full table width.
mode_rationale: Simple UI prop convention for table empty states

rule: When introducing temporary code or workarounds, add a comment with the follow-up ticket number so the cleanup does not get lost.
mode_rationale: Simple comment convention for temporary code

rule: Adding a new shared library or provider (e.g., React Query) to a high-level component should be discussed at the team/architecture level first to agree on conventions, key management, and integration strategy.
mode_rationale: Process convention: discuss new shared library adoption at team level first

rule: Telemetry events must include the complete set of entity identifiers for the target; if the event targets a versioned resource, include both the resource ID and the version ID.
mode_rationale: Single convention: always include complete entity identifiers in telemetry events

rule: Telemetry events must target the correct entity type — if the operation acts on a Model API, extend the Model API event base, not the Model API Version event base.
mode_rationale: Simple correctness rule for telemetry event targeting

rule: Add telemetry events to CRUD methods so that create, read, update, and delete operations are observable in production.
mode_rationale: Single observability convention for CRUD methods

rule: Use template literals for string concatenation and guard against undefined values, since undefined + 'string' produces 'undefinedstring'.
mode_rationale: Simple defensive coding convention

rule: When adding dependencies on external resources (secrets, services, configs), verify the system behaves gracefully when the dependency is absent, not just when it is present.
mode_rationale: Single testing principle: verify behavior when dependencies are absent

rule: When modifying shared layout components, verify that changes render correctly under all active themes and feature flag configurations.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention for shared layout components

rule: Add tests for all significant code paths and features, not just the primary one; if a module handles multiple cases (e.g., both 'target' and 'location'), each should have dedicated test coverage.
When writing tests for access-controlled features, include test cases for all relevant user roles including admin users.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about role coverage

rule: When multiple code paths can trigger similar behavior, make test assertions specific enough (e.g., checking message content or parameters) to verify the expected path was exercised, not just that a side effect occurred.
mode_rationale: Single testing principle about assertion specificity

rule: Do not change HTML element types (e.g., replacing a div with a different element) in fragile layout areas without thorough testing.
mode_rationale: Caution rule for HTML element type changes in fragile areas

rule: When a boolean flag controls state transitions (e.g., promoting/unpromoting), write tests for both directions to ensure the flag doesn't incorrectly trigger behavior for the reverse transition.
mode_rationale: Simple testing guideline for boolean flags

rule: When adding validation logic, include tests for both the happy path (valid input passes) and the error path (invalid input is rejected).
mode_rationale: Single convention: test both valid and invalid inputs for validators

rule: Add tests for boundary and edge-case inputs (e.g., values below minimums, empty ranges) to ensure correct behavior at the extremes of input space.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about edge-case coverage

rule: Include test cases for records that should be excluded from query results (e.g., archived, deleted, or inactive entities) to verify filtering logic.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to include exclusion cases

rule: Name test classes to clearly indicate which specific implementation they are testing, so the purpose remains unambiguous when implementations change.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for test classes

rule: Test cleanup code (stopping services, deleting resources) must be placed in a try/finally block so that cleanup runs even when the test fails, preventing resource leaks in CI.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: cleanup belongs in finally blocks

rule: Add unit tests for functions containing complex filter or predicate logic, especially when the correctness of negation or matching is non-obvious.
mode_rationale: Single rule: add unit tests for complex filter/predicate logic

rule: Add dedicated serialization tests for any non-trivial custom serialization logic to verify the output matches expectations.
mode_rationale: Simple rule: add tests for non-trivial custom serialization

rule: Write test descriptions, variable names, and fixture data in English to maintain consistency and readability across the team.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about test language

rule: When a change touches multiple modules, verify each module has an existing spec and add relevant tests in every affected spec, not just one.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to ensure test coverage across all affected modules

rule: When reviewing tests, verify that both positive (valid input producing expected output) and negative (invalid input returning undefined/error) cases are covered.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about positive and negative cases

rule: When adding new components that make API calls, ensure a test suite exists and mock external calls to prevent unintended network requests in tests.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to ensure tests exist and mock external calls

rule: When introducing new allowed behaviors or wrapping service calls in NonFatal recovery, add explicit tests for those specific scenarios.
trigger: When adding new state transitions, changing allowed behaviors between entities, or adding error recovery wrappers around service calls
rationale: New behaviors without tests are invisible regressions waiting to happen. Reviewers specifically asked for tests covering new allowed version transitions and fallback behavior when taxonomy service fails.
mode_rationale: Testing convention: cover new behaviors with explicit tests

rule: When writing tests for serialization or type conversion logic, include edge case tests that verify correct behavior across type boundaries (e.g., retrieving an integer value as a Double).
mode_rationale: Single testing guideline about edge cases

rule: When changing file-handling code, verify behavior with file names containing spaces and special characters, and understand existing workarounds (like double-encoding) before modifying them.
mode_rationale: Single convention: test file handling with special characters

rule: When the implementation checks for a non-empty condition or distinct code path, ensure there is a corresponding test case that exercises that branch.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about branch coverage

rule: When testing new fields or computed values, assert that the values are correct, not just that they exist or are non-null.
mode_rationale: Testing convention: assert correctness, not just non-null

rule: When removing or modifying feature flags, verify that newly possible feature combinations (previously blocked by the removed flag) are tested.
mode_rationale: Simple testing reminder when modifying feature flags

rule: When a feature is gated behind a config flag, add a unit test that verifies the correct behavior when the flag is disabled, not just when it is enabled.
mode_rationale: Testing convention for feature flag coverage

rule: When adding feature-flagged functionality, verify the behavior with the flag both enabled and disabled, especially when multiple flags interact.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention for flag-gated features

rule: When adding filter-based endpoints, write tests that verify each filter value returns the correct subset, and that omitting the filter returns all expected results.
mode_rationale: Single testing guideline for filter-based endpoints

rule: When fixing flaky or failing tests, ensure the fix does not reduce coverage or hide potential bugs in the system under test; prefer more specific test inputs over broadening acceptance criteria.
mode_rationale: Single principle about preserving coverage when fixing tests

rule: When code correctness depends on the execution order of steps (e.g., items added to a script), add tests to verify the ordering is maintained.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to verify execution order when correctness depends on it

rule: Tests should verify that system invariants and constraints hold (e.g., result stays within documented limits), not just check the computed output value.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about verifying constraints

rule: Write explicit tests for operations on missing or non-existent entity IDs to verify the system handles them gracefully.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to cover missing entity scenarios

rule: Update tests should modify a value, then assert that only the targeted field changed and all other fields remain unchanged.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention for update assertions

rule: Tests should explicitly assert that excluded or filtered-out items are absent from results, not just verify the count and contents of included items.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about asserting exclusions

rule: When adding a new authorized action or permission check, add a corresponding test case in the authorizer spec.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to add tests when adding permissions

rule: When adding a new conditional branch or edge case in service logic, add a corresponding test case that exercises that condition.
mode_rationale: Single testing guideline to cover new branches

rule: When adding a new field to a domain mapping or data transformation, update or add a test that verifies the field is correctly set.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about covering new fields

rule: When adding new public methods or services, add corresponding test cases to cover their behavior.
mode_rationale: Single convention: add tests when adding public methods

rule: When introducing nullable or optional update fields, add explicit tests for the None/null case to verify the field is not inadvertently modified.
mode_rationale: Single convention: test the None/null case for optional fields

rule: When configuration (e.g., env vars) is conditionally included, write both a positive test (present when condition is met) and a negative test (absent when condition is not met).
mode_rationale: Convention about testing both branches of conditional config

rule: Add dedicated unit tests for repository query methods (sort order, filters) even if they are implicitly covered by higher-level integration tests.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about dedicated tests for repository queries

rule: Tests for infrastructure configuration (e.g., Kubernetes ingress annotations) should assert on actual generated values for security-relevant fields like auth-url and auth-signin, not just verify the keys exist.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to assert on actual values, not just key existence

rule: When a security-sensitive behavior (e.g., disabling SSL verification) is conditionally applied, add a unit test that verifies the behavior is only active for the intended case.
mode_rationale: Testing convention for security-conditional behavior

rule: Remove mock setup for code paths that the test asserts are never reached, making the test more realistic and its intent clearer.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention about minimal mock setup

rule: When handling file or directory paths, verify behavior with special characters (spaces, question marks, etc.) in every segment of the path.
mode_rationale: Simple testing guideline for path handling

rule: BDD/Cucumber step definitions should be descriptive enough to understand when listed outside their specific test scenario, such as in step catalogs or documentation.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for BDD step definitions

rule: Write test step definitions that clearly describe their action; avoid 'clever' steps that bundle multiple behaviors in non-obvious ways, as future authors won't reuse them.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence guideline about test step clarity

rule: When building UI components that display variable-length content (file names, paths, nested structures), verify rendering behavior with edge cases like deeply nested paths and long names.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention about edge case verification

rule: When moving or refactoring files, verify that associated unit tests exist and are migrated along with the code.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to migrate tests alongside refactored code

rule: When a test checks for the presence of unwanted resources or states, ensure it actually fails (e.g., raises an exception or calls pytest.fail) when the condition is detected, rather than silently passing.
mode_rationale: Testing correctness rule for failure assertions

rule: Theme object entries for color attributes should reference existing semantic palette tokens, not non-standard hex values, to maintain consistency across the application.
mode_rationale: Simple convention for theme color references

rule: Only use themeHelper within the scope of a styled component; when used outside, it returns a function instead of the resolved style value.
mode_rationale: API usage gotcha for themeHelper scope

rule: Write OpenAPI operation descriptions in third person (e.g., 'Gets all Deployment Target Types') rather than imperative form (e.g., 'Get all Deployment Target Types').
mode_rationale: Simple style convention for OpenAPI description phrasing

rule: When adding a security bypass or workaround, the comment must explain the full scope of the bypass, who it affects, the security implications, and the tracking ticket for the permanent fix.
mode_rationale: Convention about documenting security workarounds

rule: When a domain-specific exception is needed, throw it at the origin point rather than catching a generic exception and re-wrapping it upstream.
mode_rationale: Simple exception-throwing convention

rule: Leave a TODO comment when introducing temporary code or workarounds that should be removed later, so future developers know it is intentional debt.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention about marking temporary code

rule: When leaving a TODO or referencing future work in code, include concrete examples or link to a ticket so the intent is actionable by anyone who encounters it later.
mode_rationale: Single documentation convention for TODOs

rule: Add TODO labels to mark known issues or temporary workarounds in test code so they can be tracked and addressed later.
mode_rationale: Single test annotation convention

rule: Place case classes at the top level of the file or in a dedicated model file rather than nesting them inside companion objects, which forces confusing self-imports.
mode_rationale: Simple code organization preference for case class placement

rule: Do not force all API collection responses to include a totalCount; only include it when the pagination strategy (e.g., page-number-based) requires it, since cursor-based pagination does not need it and computing totalCount can be expensive.
mode_rationale: Single convention: only include totalCount when pagination strategy requires it

rule: Add trace-level logging at service entry points to aid debugging without cluttering production logs.
mode_rationale: Simple logging convention

rule: In data-fetching hooks that feed tables or lists, track a hasLoaded state to distinguish 'never loaded' from 'loaded but empty', preventing a flash of the empty state on initial render.
mode_rationale: Single UI pattern to distinguish never-loaded from empty

rule: When code references an API without a proper schema, create a tracking ticket to add the missing schema rather than leaving untyped workarounds in place.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention to create tickets for missing schemas

rule: When an operation can partially succeed, return what was included and what failed to include with reasons, rather than failing the entire request or silently dropping items.
mode_rationale: Design principle for API response handling, not a multi-step procedure

rule: Do not leave tests commented out or skipped without filing a tracking ticket to ensure they are re-enabled or properly removed.
mode_rationale: Simple rule requiring tickets for skipped tests

rule: Never leave SSL verification disabled without a tracked ticket for re-enabling it; disabling SSL opens the door to man-in-the-middle attacks and data exposure.
mode_rationale: Security convention to track SSL verification disabling

rule: Every TODO comment in the codebase must reference a tracked ticket (e.g., Jira) so it does not get lost or forgotten.
mode_rationale: Simple rule requiring ticket references on TODOs

rule: Add TypeScript types to all component props and data structures, even when using mocked data during development.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to always add TypeScript types to props and data

rule: Types and interfaces should be moved to a dedicated `types` file rather than defined inline in utility or component files.
mode_rationale: Code organization rule: move types to dedicated file

rule: Use the same domain terminology at all layers of the application (ubiquitous language from DDD); don't invent new names for concepts that already have established terms in documentation and user-facing surfaces.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention about domain terminology

rule: In Scala pattern matches and destructuring, replace named bindings that are never used with underscore (_) to signal intent and avoid dead-code warnings.
mode_rationale: Scala naming convention for unused bindings

rule: Before adding authentication redirects or route guards to a React component, verify the component's mounting lifecycle — if it is always mounted while the app is active, the guard may unintentionally affect unrelated routes.
mode_rationale: Architectural awareness check before adding route guards

rule: When using spacing utility functions like getMargin, verify the multiplier logic (e.g., getMargin(16) may compute 16 * BASE, not 16px) to avoid unintended values.
mode_rationale: Single caution: verify multiplier logic in spacing utilities

rule: Always verify that the rowKey property in table components is bound to a truly unique field to prevent rendering bugs and key collisions.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to verify uniqueness of row keys

rule: When adding behavior gated by a feature flag, add unit tests that validate the code path under the new flag to prevent regressions from untested branches.
mode_rationale: Simple testing guideline for feature-flagged code

rule: New service classes with non-trivial logic should have a corresponding unit test Spec class.
mode_rationale: Testing convention: new service classes need unit tests

rule: When modifying or renaming a shared configuration file, audit and update every service that includes or depends on that file to avoid runtime failures.
mode_rationale: Simple reminder to audit consumers when modifying shared config

rule: When adding new build tools or CLI utilities, update the relevant architecture documentation (wikis, ADRs) so the team understands how the new tool fits into the system.
mode_rationale: Single convention about keeping docs in sync

rule: Update/PATCH endpoints should not mark all fields as required; an update should allow modifying only a subset of fields.
mode_rationale: Single rule about PATCH field requirements

rule: When adding new customer-facing features or modifying visibility/access logic, update the corresponding runbook documentation so operations teams can troubleshoot issues.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to update runbooks with new features

rule: When adding or modifying fields in a public API contract, check whether existing system or integration tests need to be updated to cover the new field.
mode_rationale: Single testing maintenance convention

rule: Name non-function constants (arrays, config objects, column definitions) using UPPER_SNAKE_CASE to distinguish them from functions and variables.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence naming convention

rule: Variable names should precisely describe what they hold; rename variables when their meaning drifts from the actual content (e.g., use env_image instead of env_uri when the value is a container image reference).
mode_rationale: Naming convention to keep variable names aligned with their content

rule: Define Ant Design Menu items using the items array configuration (MenuItem[]) instead of the deprecated JSX children pattern (<Menu.Item>).
mode_rationale: Single convention about preferred Ant Design API pattern

rule: Prefer API-based setup steps over UI-based steps in end-to-end tests to reduce flakiness and speed up test execution, unless the test specifically validates UI-only behavior.
mode_rationale: Single preference for API-based over UI-based test setup

rule: Never hardcode the product name 'Domino' in UI strings; always use the white-label brandedText helper so the name is substituted correctly in white-labeled deployments.
mode_rationale: White-label convention to never hardcode product name

rule: Use debug level for verbose or frequently-executed logging (such as mapping/inference results); reserve info level for significant state changes or business events.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use debug for verbose logging, info for significant events

rule: Use aria-label attributes instead of custom testId attributes for element identification in tests, which simultaneously improves accessibility and eliminates the need for custom test step definitions.
mode_rationale: Testing convention for element identification

rule: When a component already supports a prop (e.g., dataTest for test IDs), use it directly instead of wrapping the component in an extra DOM element just to attach the attribute.
mode_rationale: Convention about using component APIs correctly

rule: When checking existence or fetching multiple records, use a single bulk query with $in or similar operators and do in-memory diffing, rather than issuing one query per item.
mode_rationale: Performance convention preferring bulk queries

rule: Expose service endpoints through the shared API package (@domino/api) rather than making ad-hoc HTTP calls from individual components.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use the shared API package

rule: Import from the package's canonical alias (e.g., ui/) rather than reaching into the dist build output (e.g., @domino/ui/dist/).
mode_rationale: Simple import convention: use aliases not dist paths

rule: Define colors in the project's centralized color constants file (e.g., `colors.ts`) rather than hardcoding hex values in components.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about where to define colors

rule: Leverage the framework's centralized error handler (e.g., ApiServerErrorHandler) for exception-to-HTTP-status mapping instead of duplicating exception handling in individual controllers.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use framework error handler

rule: Before hardcoding navigation paths, check for existing centralized route helpers (e.g., core/routes.ts) and use them to avoid duplication and ensure consistency.
mode_rationale: Convention to use existing route helpers over hardcoded paths

rule: In Scala, place constants and static-like values in a companion object rather than in the class body to separate instance state from shared values.
mode_rationale: Scala code organization convention

rule: Use px units for margin and padding values to stay consistent with the rest of the codebase, unless there is a specific reason to use relative units.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence CSS units convention

rule: Use color tokens from the correct semantic category — text color tokens for text, background tokens for backgrounds, border tokens for borders — even if the hex values happen to match.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about matching token category to usage context

rule: Use PUT for update/replace operations on existing resources rather than POST, which implies creation; choosing the correct HTTP verb makes the API more expressive and RESTful.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use PUT for updates, POST for creation

rule: Return the semantically correct HTTP status code for each operation — use 200 OK for retrievals/updates and 201 Created only when a new resource is actually created.
mode_rationale: Simple API convention for correct HTTP status codes

rule: When only the count or existence of records is needed, use a count query at the persistence layer instead of fetching and counting full objects.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence performance convention for count-only queries

rule: Write Storybook stories in Component Story Format 3 (CSF3), not MDX, which is no longer supported in Storybook 8.
mode_rationale: Single convention about Storybook story format

rule: Events must extend the correct trait or interface (e.g., AuditableEvent) to be processed by downstream handlers; inheriting from a base class alone may not activate all intended behaviors.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about extending the correct trait for event processing

rule: Feature flags used during development should be removed once the feature is tested and shipped; do not accumulate permanent development flags.
mode_rationale: Simple hygiene rule to remove flags after shipping

rule: Choose polling intervals appropriate to the operation's expected duration; a 15-second default is too long for user-facing progress — prefer shorter intervals (e.g., 2-5 seconds) for tasks the user is actively waiting on.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use shorter polling intervals for user-facing progress

rule: Mock data used in tests or storybook should be typed using the types generated from the API schema, not left untyped or manually typed.
mode_rationale: Simple typing convention for mock data

rule: Functions that return color values should have their return type annotated with the Palette or color token type, not a generic string.
mode_rationale: Simple typing convention: annotate color return types with Palette type, not string

rule: Use type-safe enum mapping builders (e.g., StrictEnumBiMapBuilder) to validate bijection between related enums at class-load time, ensuring new enum values are never silently missed.
mode_rationale: Convention to use type-safe enum mapping builders

rule: Dynamic UI text (e.g., 'Login with SSO') must be conditional on whether the referenced feature is actually enabled, to avoid misleading users.
mode_rationale: Simple UX convention to make dynamic text conditional on feature state

rule: Properties prefixed with an underscore (_) signal private/internal use; do not access underscore-prefixed properties from outside the owning class or module.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention about underscore-prefixed properties

rule: Verify that test fixtures, hooks, and framework callbacks actually execute at the expected lifecycle phase; unused teardown logic that never runs is a silent bug.
mode_rationale: Single awareness guideline about test fixture execution

rule: When emitting events or metrics, be aware that empty-string defaults (via `.getOrElse("")`) can change how monitoring tools like New Relic index fields — an empty string may omit the key entirely, which is often the desired behavior for optional fields.
mode_rationale: Single convention: be aware of empty-string defaults in metrics/events

rule: Verify that the router uses exclusive matching (e.g., Switch) before adding a catch-all/404 route, otherwise it will render alongside every other route.
mode_rationale: Single guard rule before adding catch-all routes

rule: When two callbacks or state setters appear to do the same thing, unify them into one; when an expression is repeated, extract it into a named constant.
mode_rationale: Single convention: deduplicate callbacks and extract repeated expressions

rule: When multiple code paths log similar errors, unify them with a shared log message prefix so that searching logs and creating alerts does not require matching multiple distinct strings.
mode_rationale: Single logging convention for searchability

rule: When two components have similar implementations, create a single reusable component that can be customized via props rather than maintaining parallel implementations.
mode_rationale: Single reusability guideline about merging similar components

rule: Define union types for known sets of values instead of using the loose `string` type in interfaces and function signatures.
mode_rationale: Simple typing convention: use union types for known value sets instead of string

rule: Include a unique scenario ID or test identifier in dynamically created test resource names (e.g., workspace names) to avoid collisions and improve traceability.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for test resources

rule: Include the unit of measurement as a suffix in constant names (e.g., COUNTDOWN_MS, TIMEOUT_SECONDS) so the unit is unambiguous at the call site.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for constants with units

rule: Bug fixes in serialization or mapping logic should include a unit test verifying the correct behavior, even if it requires adjusting method visibility.
Mapping functions that transform between domain types should have simple unit tests to verify the correctness of the mapping.
mode_rationale: Single testing convention: write unit tests for type mapping functions

rule: Utility and validation functions (especially those handling user input like CIDR parsing) must have unit tests.
mode_rationale: Simple rule that utility functions need tests

rule: Add unit tests to illustrate expected inputs and outputs, especially for non-trivial processing logic, so reviewers and future developers can quickly understand the behavior.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to illustrate behavior

rule: Add unit tests for newly introduced or modified logic paths, especially when handling new data variants or edge cases.
mode_rationale: Basic testing expectation for new code paths

rule: New or modified service adapter files should have accompanying unit tests.
mode_rationale: Simple testing convention for adapter files
Placeholder/unclassified pattern with no rule content

rule: Do not leave Futures unyielded at the end of a test block; the test framework may not wait for them, causing silently passing tests.
mode_rationale: Testing convention to not leave Futures unyielded

rule: When adding a new feature or field, ensure all exposed API versions (v1, v4, public) are updated to include it.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to keep all API versions in sync

rule: When adding a new field to a model or DTO, audit and update all validation checks that compare versions or detect changes, and add tests for the new field in those checks.
mode_rationale: Multi-step procedure: add field, audit all validation checks, update each, add tests

rule: When reverting or modifying dependency files, ensure version numbers are updated to the correct target value before merging the PR.
mode_rationale: Simple pre-merge check for correct dependency versions

rule: When the filtering, querying, or API capabilities of a model or endpoint change, update its documentation (scaladoc, JSDoc, comments) to reflect the new behavior.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation convention for API changes

rule: Name update request DTOs using the pattern 'Updated[EntityName]' (e.g., UpdatedResourceConfiguration) to clearly distinguish them from creation and read models.
mode_rationale: Single naming pattern for update DTOs

rule: When adding or changing fields in a backend API, verify whether frontend mocks, TypeScript types, or Swagger/OpenAPI docs also need to be updated to stay in sync.
mode_rationale: Reminder to check related contracts when changing APIs

rule: When a function's behavior is extended (e.g., adding a filter parameter), update its doc comment to reflect the new functionality.
mode_rationale: Simple documentation maintenance convention

rule: Run IDE project generation commands when adding new build targets to keep the development environment in sync.
mode_rationale: Simple reminder to regenerate IDE config

rule: When a method's parameter list or behavior changes, update the method name to accurately reflect the new contract.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention about method name accuracy

rule: When modifying the semantics of API parameters (e.g., making fields optional or changing nullability), update the corresponding scaladoc/documentation to describe the new behavior (e.g., 'Ignore if None, Unassign if Some(None)').
mode_rationale: Simple rule to keep docs in sync with code changes

rule: Do not update local state or flags until the corresponding remote/async operation completes successfully, to avoid inconsistent state on failure.
mode_rationale: Single rule about ordering state updates after async ops

rule: When a type's fields or semantics change, update the type name to reflect the new shape so API consumers are not misled.
mode_rationale: Single naming guideline to rename types when shape changes

rule: Entities that may require deep linking should be represented in the URL path or query params, not solely as internal component state.
mode_rationale: Single architectural convention about URL state

rule: Always URL-encode user-supplied values when constructing query strings to prevent injection and encoding issues.
mode_rationale: Single convention: always URL-encode user-supplied query values

rule: When determining active navigation state, use prefix matching (e.g., startsWith) rather than exact URL equality to correctly highlight parent menu items for nested/detail routes.
mode_rationale: Single convention about using prefix matching for nav state

rule: When a function or hook accepts several parameters, group them into a single options/params object to improve readability and reduce confusion at call sites.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to group parameters into an options object

rule: Use service principal abstractions (e.g., ServicePrincipalMaker) instead of directly injecting superuser credentials, making it easier to remove privileged access later.
mode_rationale: Single architectural convention about privilege abstraction

rule: Do not attach click events to non-interactive elements like `<span>`; use a button component (e.g., InvisibleButton) for proper keyboard and screen reader accessibility.
mode_rationale: Simple accessibility rule about interactive elements

rule: Use dedicated accessor methods (e.g., getTemplateType) to simplify handling of optional fields rather than manually unwrapping Option types.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use accessor methods to simplify optional handling

rule: Use precise terminology for cryptographic concepts — 'signingKey' refers to the private key used to sign, not the public key used to verify.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for cryptographic concepts

rule: Metric and counter names must use the correct domain term for the scope they cover — e.g., use "runs" not "jobs" when the metric tracks all run types, since "jobs" is a sub-category of runs.
mode_rationale: Single convention: match metric names to the correct domain scope

rule: UI labels and copy must use the correct domain term for the entity being displayed (e.g., 'file' vs 'artifact') to avoid confusing users.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use correct domain terms in UI copy

rule: Use the `ActionDropdown.Button` component for dropdown actions on table rows instead of building custom dropdown triggers.
mode_rationale: Convention to use existing dropdown component

rule: Access other modules' services through adapter interfaces rather than taking direct dependencies on their internal implementations, preserving module boundary separation.
mode_rationale: Simple architecture convention for module boundaries

rule: When a telemetry field would contain a large or unbounded list, record an aggregate (e.g. count) instead of the full list.
mode_rationale: Single convention to use counts instead of full lists

rule: Use ampersand (&) nesting in styled-components to increase CSS specificity and avoid style conflicts.
mode_rationale: Simple CSS convention for styled-components specificity

rule: When using Ant Design Form components, let the Form manage field state rather than using it purely as a layout/styling wrapper with separate state management.
Use Ant Design's `Flex` component for flex layouts instead of creating custom styled divs with flexbox properties.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use Ant Design Flex instead of custom styled divs

rule: Use AntD Form.List for dynamic array fields instead of implementing custom add/remove logic.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use Form.List for dynamic arrays

rule: Use Ant Design Form's built-in submit handler and form change events rather than manually wiring up submit and change logic.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to use Ant Design's built-in form handlers

rule: Style customizations should go through the Ant Design Theme Provider rather than manually overriding component styles in styled-components or CSS.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to use Theme Provider for customizations

rule: Replace growing if/else or switch chains with a list and Array.includes() to keep conditional structures from expanding unboundedly.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to replace growing conditionals with array lookup

rule: Use Array.isArray() to check if a value is an array instead of other type-checking approaches.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use Array.isArray() for array checks

rule: When an API spec represents a collection of schemas, model it as an array with typed items rather than nesting objects with redundant wrapper properties.
mode_rationale: Simple modeling convention for API spec collections

rule: Use Array.some() instead of filter-and-check-length when you only need to know whether any element matches a condition.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention preferring one array method over another

rule: Use arrow function expressions for component definitions and handlers to maintain a consistent code style across the codebase.
mode_rationale: Code style convention for function syntax

rule: When updating a single field on a database record, update only that field directly rather than reading the entire object into memory, modifying it, and saving it back, to avoid overwriting concurrent updates to other fields.
mode_rationale: Single best practice about updating database fields atomically

rule: Use the automock functionality instead of defining manual mock files for components and modules.
mode_rationale: Simple testing preference for automock over manual mocks

rule: Use already-available contextual data (e.g., `principal.eventUser`) instead of making additional service calls to fetch the same information.
mode_rationale: Simple performance convention about avoiding redundant fetches

rule: When you only need part of a composed component, use the exposed base component directly rather than hiding UI elements of the higher-level wrapper.
mode_rationale: Architecture guideline preferring base components over hiding parts

rule: Use word-break: break-word instead of break-all to avoid cutting words in the middle when wrapping text.
mode_rationale: Single CSS convention: break-word over break-all

rule: Name feature flags for the broad capability they gate, not for a specific sub-feature, so the same flag can cover future related work without proliferating flags.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for feature flags

rule: Field names within audit event targets must use camelCase to follow the established convention.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for audit event field casing

rule: Enum values in Scala should follow camelCase naming convention to stay consistent with the codebase style.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for enums

rule: TypeScript utility and helper files should follow camelCase naming conventions (e.g., getTheme.ts, not get-theme.ts) to match the project's frontend naming standards.
Avoid accessing tuple fields by positional index (`_1`, `_2`); use a named case class instead for readability and type safety.
mode_rationale: Readability convention preferring named fields over tuple indices

rule: In Scala, prefer case classes with public vals over regular classes with private fields and explicit Java-style getter methods.
mode_rationale: Simple Scala idiom preference

rule: When matching string input to an enum, use enumeratum's withNameInsensitiveOption instead of manually converting to uppercase before matching.
mode_rationale: Scala convention for enum matching with enumeratum

rule: When implementing search or filter functionality on user-provided strings, normalize to lowercase before comparison to avoid case-sensitivity bugs.
mode_rationale: Simple defensive coding rule for string comparison

rule: Documentation and help links should use centralized support utilities (e.g., HelpLink component or docs base path hook) rather than hardcoded URLs, so links update automatically when docs locations change.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use centralized utilities for doc links

rule: Use the classnames library for conditional CSS class composition in React components, and forward className props to allow styled-component wrapping.
mode_rationale: Simple convention about using classnames library and forwarding className props

rule: Class names should clearly communicate their purpose and domain concept; avoid vague, cryptic, or 'weird' names that force readers to inspect the implementation to understand intent.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for class clarity

rule: When displaying code snippets in the UI, use a CodeEditor component (read-only) rather than plain text elements, to provide proper syntax highlighting and visual context.
mode_rationale: Simple UI component choice convention

rule: When filtering and transforming a collection simultaneously, use `.collect` with pattern matching instead of chaining `.filter` and `.map` separately.
mode_rationale: Single Scala idiom preference for filter+map

rule: Use `command -v` instead of `which` to check for executable availability in shell scripts, since `command` is a POSIX shell builtin guaranteed to be present.
mode_rationale: Simple portability convention for shell scripts

rule: Do not rename default exports from the shared component library or use underlying framework props (e.g., Ant Design) when the wrapper component defines its own prop types.
mode_rationale: Single convention about respecting library APIs

rule: When URL-encoding path elements, encode all special characters comprehensively using a standard library function (e.g., URLEncoder.encode), not just specific characters like spaces.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use standard library for full URL encoding

rule: Use the `ConfirmationModal` component for confirmation dialogs with warning boxes instead of building custom modal layouts with warning elements.
mode_rationale: Convention to use existing confirmation component

rule: Use 'preview' instead of 'beta' for pre-release API path segments to maintain consistent terminology across the platform.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use 'preview' not 'beta' for pre-release API paths

rule: Use the same constant reference (alias or fully-qualified name) consistently across implementation and test code to avoid confusion about whether different names refer to the same value.
mode_rationale: Naming consistency convention for constant references

rule: Use consistent separators and delimiters within related data structures — mixing semicolons and commas in the same context signals unintentional inconsistency.
mode_rationale: Simple consistency convention for separators

rule: Deprecation notices in API docs should use a consistent format: bold 'DEPRECATED' label, specify the version(s) where deprecation begins, and direct users to the replacement endpoint.
mode_rationale: Simple formatting convention for deprecation notices

rule: When adding documentation comments, use the same style (e.g., scaladoc) as other files in the package for consistency.
mode_rationale: Documentation consistency convention

rule: Related API endpoints (e.g., list and get-by-id) should use the same DTO models to avoid divergent representations of the same resource.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence API design convention for DTO consistency

rule: API route descriptions and parameter docs should use consistent casing, spacing, and terminology (e.g., 'data plane' as two words, 'ID' fully capitalized) matching the variable names they describe.
mode_rationale: Naming/style convention for API docs consistency

rule: Name wrapper types using consistent Request/Response pairs — if input types are named 'Request', output types should be named 'Response' (not 'Result') to match SDK v2 conventions.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for API wrapper types

rule: Declare fixed configuration values (timeouts, limits, thresholds) as constants rather than variables to communicate immutability.
mode_rationale: Simple immutability convention for config values

rule: Reference defined constants instead of duplicating magic strings in test files to keep tests in sync with production code.
mode_rationale: Single convention about referencing constants

rule: Use context managers (with-blocks) when temporarily changing state (e.g., window size, config overrides) so the original state is always restored, even on failure.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use with-blocks for temporary state changes

rule: Use the semantically correct ARIA role for the element's function (e.g., use 'menuitem' for items inside a menu, not a generic role).
mode_rationale: Single accessibility convention

rule: Use the 'affecting' field for data types that are related but not the direct object of the audit event; reserve 'target' for the direct object of the verb.
mode_rationale: API convention for audit event field semantics

rule: Test fixtures should use the semantically correct domain type for each entity rather than a convenient substitute that doesn't match the real domain model.
mode_rationale: Simple rule about using semantically correct types in test fixtures

rule: Use 403 Forbidden (not 401 Unauthorized) when a request will never succeed regardless of credentials; 401 implies retrying with valid creds would work.
mode_rationale: Single rule: 403 vs 401 usage

rule: Expected operational behavior like rollbacks should be logged at info level, not warn; reserve warn/error for genuinely unexpected conditions.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence logging convention

rule: When serving files to the browser, set the content type to the correct MIME type for the format (e.g., `text/xsl` for XSL files) rather than defaulting to `text/plain`, so the browser can render or highlight the content appropriately.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use correct MIME types when serving files

rule: Use NavTabs for full-page tab navigation and Segmented controls only for toggling between views within a section.
mode_rationale: Single convention: NavTabs for page navigation, Segmented for in-section toggles

rule: Use a <button> element (not an <a> or link) when the element triggers an action rather than navigating to a URL; if the link-styled button pattern recurs, create a dedicated button variant.
mode_rationale: Simple HTML semantics convention

rule: Use `2>/dev/null` to suppress stderr output silently; do not use `2>&1` when the intent is to discard errors rather than merge them with stdout.
mode_rationale: Single shell scripting convention

rule: Configuration keys must be defined with their actual expected type (e.g., Int for numeric values), not as strings that require downstream parsing.
mode_rationale: Single convention: define config keys with actual expected types, not strings

rule: Use encodeURIComponent only on individual path segments or query values, never on full URLs or paths containing slashes.
mode_rationale: Single rule about encodeURIComponent scope

rule: Use the CSS `gap` property for spacing between flex/grid children instead of margin-based approaches, and consider adding a linting rule to enforce consistent gap usage.
mode_rationale: Simple CSS layout convention

rule: Follow the Databricks Scala style guide and add curly braces around multi-line expressions for clarity and consistency.
mode_rationale: Scala style convention following Databricks guide

rule: Set SVG fill attributes to `currentColor` so the icon inherits its color from the parent element's font color, making icons reusable across different color contexts.
mode_rationale: Simple SVG convention for color inheritance

rule: Use dedicated data-test attributes with descriptive, component-scoped names (e.g., data-test="billing-tag-add-button") as stable selectors for tests instead of relying on CSS classes or DOM structure.
mode_rationale: Simple selector convention for test stability

rule: Use debug log level for routine, high-frequency operations like indexing individual records; reserve info level for significant state changes or events operators need to see.
mode_rationale: Simple log level selection guideline

rule: Reference defined constant values (e.g., EventTarget.Project) instead of raw string literals in tests and production code to catch refactoring errors at compile time.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use constants over string literals

rule: Choose descriptive, self-documenting variable names that communicate purpose; avoid generic or abbreviated names that require additional context to understand.
Name function parameters to describe their purpose (e.g., `defaultResult` or `defaultRedirect`) rather than using generic names like `fn` that give no indication of the parameter's role.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention

rule: Use the project's named color tokens (e.g., border-specific palette colors) instead of raw hex values or ad-hoc color strings, even if surrounding legacy code uses hardcoded values.
mode_rationale: Convention to use named color tokens

rule: Import UI components from the internal design system wrapper library rather than directly from the underlying component library (e.g., Ant Design) to maintain consistency and enable centralized customization.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence rule about importing from design system wrappers

rule: Use the iconSize prop with predefined iconSizes values from the design system instead of inline size overrides.
When a utility function exists for a layout calculation (e.g., `getMargin`), use it instead of writing inline calculations to maintain consistency and single-source-of-truth.
mode_rationale: Convention to reuse existing layout utilities

rule: Use the design system's RouteLink component instead of plain anchor tags or generic Link components to get consistent navigation styling.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use RouteLink instead of raw anchors

rule: Always use typography styles from the design system (e.g., Storybook typography foundations) instead of custom font styles; if no existing token matches, discuss with UX before creating custom styles.
mode_rationale: Style convention about typography tokens

rule: Use `dict.get(key, default)` with an appropriate default value instead of separate null-checking logic to simplify data access in Python.
mode_rationale: Python idiom to simplify null checks with dict.get defaults

rule: When a component maps between a fixed set of values (e.g., type to icon), use a dictionary/map rather than conditional logic to make the mapping explicit and easy to extend.
mode_rationale: Code pattern preference for value mapping

rule: Use domain-level abstractions (e.g., DominoId.newInstance) instead of implementation-specific types (e.g., MongoDB ObjectId) outside of persistence code to maintain proper layering.
mode_rationale: Architectural layering convention: use domain types outside persistence code

rule: Name variables and parameters after the domain concept they represent in the current service, not after terminology borrowed from other services or systems.
mode_rationale: Naming convention about domain-local terminology

rule: Prefix class names with the domain context (e.g., ModelImageBuildCancelRequest instead of BuildCancelRequest) when the short name is ambiguous outside the immediate package.
mode_rationale: Naming convention to avoid ambiguous class names

rule: Throw domain-specific exception types (e.g., GenAIApiException.HttpNotFound) instead of generic exceptions when mapping API error responses, so the controller can translate them to correct HTTP status codes.
mode_rationale: Convention to throw typed exceptions instead of generic ones

rule: Use domain-specific types (e.g., DominoId) instead of raw primitives (e.g., String) even when implicit conversions would make primitives compile, to preserve type safety and intent.
When defining a set of related string or numeric constants, use an enum (or a const object with an extracted type) rather than loose constants, and tie the type to the enum keys for type safety.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to prefer enums over loose constants

rule: When a value has more than two meaningful states (e.g., waiting, requested, completed), use an enum or sealed type rather than a boolean, and name it to capture the intent of each transition.
mode_rationale: Simple type design convention

rule: Use existing environment variables for version strings and configuration values rather than hardcoding them, especially in CI scripts where values are already exported.
mode_rationale: Convention to use environment variables over hardcoded strings

rule: Use the canonical package alias paths (e.g., @domino/core-ui/Link) instead of deep relative imports to keep imports consistent and refactor-friendly.
mode_rationale: Simple import convention

rule: Never hardcode the application name (e.g., 'Domino'); use the getAppName utility with whitelabelSettings from useStore to support whitelabelling.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use getAppName utility instead of hardcoded strings

rule: Before implementing custom behavior (e.g., manually destroying inactive tabs), check if the component library already provides a prop or built-in feature for it.
mode_rationale: Single convention: check for built-in library features before implementing custom behavior

rule: Use existing utility functions (e.g., getAppName, getTemplateType) instead of manually handling raw values or reimplementing logic that a utility already encapsulates.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: use existing utils instead of hardcoding or reimplementing

rule: Before implementing common UI patterns like loading spinners, check for existing shared wrapper components (e.g., SpinnerWrapper) that handle the pattern consistently.
mode_rationale: Single-sentence convention to check for existing components first

rule: Prefer existing utility hooks (e.g., useInterval) over manual setInterval/setTimeout with useEffect cleanup to reduce boilerplate and avoid common timer bugs.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to prefer existing hooks

rule: Replace deeply nested map/flatMap/match chains with for-comprehensions for readability, especially when chaining multiple Optional value extractions.
mode_rationale: Scala readability convention for chained Optional extractions

rule: When a function may not handle all input cases, return a functional error type like Try or Either instead of throwing exceptions for unmatched cases.
mode_rationale: Simple error handling convention

rule: Replace bare `.get` calls on Option/nullable values with `getOrElse` throwing an `IllegalStateException` with a clear message describing the unexpected state, even for cases that should never occur.
mode_rationale: Error handling convention: use getOrElse with descriptive exception instead of bare .get

rule: In React function components, access the theme via hooks (e.g., useTheme) instead of injecting it as a prop via the withTheme HOC, which is being deprecated.
mode_rationale: Single convention about using hooks over deprecated HOC patterns

rule: Always use the IconResolver abstraction for rendering icons rather than importing icon components directly, to ensure consistent icon handling across the application.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use IconResolver abstraction for icons

rule: Use idiomatic Option combinators like filter, map, and getOrElse instead of verbose if/else chains when working with optional values in Scala.
mode_rationale: Simple Scala convention: use filter/map/getOrElse over if/else

rule: Always use curly braces in Scala control structures and expressions, following the Databricks Scala style guide.
mode_rationale: Scala style convention per Databricks guide

rule: Write Storybook stories using CSF3 format instead of deprecated MDX format to stay current with the framework and avoid migration debt.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use CSF3 instead of deprecated MDX format

rule: When writing new components or code, use the latest agreed-upon terminology rather than legacy names to avoid immediate tech debt from planned renames.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention about terminology

rule: When comparing or sorting date values from API responses, parse them into proper date objects using dayjs rather than comparing raw strings, to avoid bugs if the backend changes the date format.
mode_rationale: Single convention: parse dates with dayjs instead of comparing raw strings

rule: For upsert operations, rely on the database's upsert return value to determine if a record was created or updated, rather than performing a separate existence check followed by an insert/update — the latter introduces race conditions under concurrency.
mode_rationale: Single rule preferring upsert return value over check-then-act

rule: Define button and element styles as their own styled components rather than nesting class selectors inside a parent styled component; reserve parent-level nesting only for state-dependent styles like hover.
mode_rationale: CSS-in-JS style convention about component structure

rule: Use purpose-built components (e.g., help link component for support/help links) instead of generic HTML elements to ensure consistent behavior and styling.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use purpose-built components

rule: Use the modal component's default body styling instead of adding custom padding or spacing overrides.
mode_rationale: Convention against overriding modal defaults

rule: Use the default import syntax (`import React from 'react'`) rather than named or namespace imports for React, as modern React and bundler configurations support this simpler form.
mode_rationale: Simple import style convention for React

rule: Use defined constant sets (e.g., `terminalStatuses`) for state checks instead of hardcoding individual enum values, so new states are automatically handled.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use constant sets instead of hardcoded enum values

rule: Name components specifically enough to distinguish them from other similar components in the codebase (e.g., `PlatformNavBar` instead of generic `TopBar` when multiple top bars exist).
mode_rationale: Naming convention for component specificity

rule: Name filter parameters to clearly indicate what entity or level they apply to, especially when multiple filters exist at different levels of a hierarchy.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for filter parameters

rule: Service interface methods should have documentation explaining their purpose, and parameters should use descriptive names (not generic names like 'body').
mode_rationale: Naming and documentation convention for service interfaces

rule: When a variable refers to one of several possible resources of the same kind (e.g., S3 buckets), include a qualifier in the name to clarify which resource it refers to.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for disambiguation

rule: Place shared code in packages that reflect the owning module (e.g., domino.projects), not generic catch-all names like 'common', unless the code truly belongs in a shared common library.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for package organization

rule: Name classes and types to reflect their specific scope and domain context rather than using generic names.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention

rule: Write test names as human-readable behavioral descriptions (e.g., 'should return empty list when no items match') rather than mirroring method names (e.g., 'should testGetItems').
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for tests

rule: Use palette colors (e.g., palette.textPrimary, textSecondary) and themeHelper for spacing instead of raw values or system token colors, as system tokens are reserved for specific cases.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use palette colors and themeHelper, not raw values

rule: When migrating to design tokens, verify that the token type matches the CSS property — do not use a shadow token where a color value is expected.
mode_rationale: Simple convention: match token type to CSS property

rule: Use theme tokens (spacing, border radius, colors) instead of hardcoded values in styled components to maintain design system consistency.
mode_rationale: Simple styling convention: use theme tokens instead of hardcoded values

rule: Prefer defining default values via destructuring assignment in component props rather than using wrapper utilities like withDefaultProps.
mode_rationale: Single convention: prefer destructuring defaults over wrapper utilities

rule: Import classes directly rather than using intermediate package-qualified access (e.g., `import events.AuditTrailV2Transformer` should be `import AuditTrailV2Transformer`).
mode_rationale: Single-sentence import style convention

rule: Use TypeScript discriminated unions and type guards (e.g., checking a `type` field) to narrow types instead of manual type assertions or casting.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use discriminated unions instead of type assertions

rule: Import from the built dist output of internal packages rather than reaching into source files directly.
mode_rationale: Import convention for internal packages

rule: Use clearly distinct variable and directory names in tests to avoid confusion when the same value is reused in different contexts, even if it is technically correct.
mode_rationale: Simple naming convention for test variables

rule: UI copy must use the correct domain-specific action verb for each entity type (e.g., 'unregistered' for data plane storages, not 'completed or shutdown') to avoid confusing users.
mode_rationale: Single naming convention for domain-specific UI copy

rule: Methods that return multiple related fields should use a named domain object (case class) rather than tuples or generic types, so each field's purpose is clear.
mode_rationale: Convention to use named domain objects over tuples

rule: Use domain-specific helper methods (e.g., `isGitBased`, `isDfsProject`) instead of reimplementing the same logic inline; this avoids drift and keeps business logic centralized.
mode_rationale: Simple convention to use existing helper methods instead of inline logic

rule: Name shared resources (collections, queues, tables) with domain-specific prefixes to avoid ambiguity when multiple subsystems use similar concepts.
mode_rationale: Naming convention for shared resource disambiguation

rule: Use dynamic import() instead of require() for conditional module loading in ES module codebases.
mode_rationale: Single convention: use dynamic import() instead of require() in ESM codebases

rule: Components should reference the dynamic color palette rather than implementing hardcoded dark/light theme branches.
mode_rationale: Simple rule to reference dynamic palette

rule: Use the design system's shadow/elevation tokens for box shadows instead of raw CSS values to maintain visual consistency.
mode_rationale: Simple style rule: use shadow tokens not raw CSS

rule: When conditional branches are mutually exclusive, use if/else rather than separate if statements to make the logical relationship explicit and prevent unintended fall-through.
mode_rationale: Simple coding style convention

rule: Leverage audit trail entity preprocessors (e.g., `UserNameFetchingEntityPreprocessor`) to resolve entity names from IDs automatically, rather than manually injecting and calling persisters.
mode_rationale: Single convention to use built-in preprocessors
